Recurrent non-coding mutational hotspots drive chromosomal rearrangement and disease progression in multiple myeloma

Multiple Myeloma Research Program, Perlmutter Cancer Center, NYU Langone Medical Center, New York, NY, USA

Published

July 1, 2026

Abstract

Background Most somatic variants occur in non-coding DNA regions, yet their contributions to multiple myeloma progression and the mutational processes shaping them remain underexplored. Systematic analysis of the non-coding genome can provide insights into the mechanisms driving genomic instability and disease progression.

Results Using whole-genome sequencing data from 294 tumor-normal pairs across stages of multiple myeloma progression, we characterize the distribution of recurrent mutations and identify foci of mutation at 307 “mutationally enriched regions” (MERs) located near 220 genes. MERs are enriched for active chromatin features and span 5.38 Mb of DNA with a 4.32-fold enrichment in tumor mutational burden. Mutations at MERs are characterized by mutational signatures associated with lineage-specific processes of off-target AID and SBSblood. MERs could be clustered into four groups, including intergenic, intronic, UTR, and protein coding MERs, with a subset of 50 MERs associated with enhancers/super-enhancers (E/SE) that are active at the germinal center or plasma cell stages of B-cell differentiation. We find that these regions frequently exhibit clustered mutation patterns (kataegis and omikli) and are significantly enriched near structural variant breakpoints. Notably, chromosomal translocations occur in close proximity to these regions, with recurrent involvement of loci such as MYC, a key oncogene driving multiple myeloma progression.

Conclusions We identify recurrent non-coding mutational hotspots in multiple myeloma and highlight their association with clustered mutagenesis and structural variation. These findings support a model in which localized mutational processes contribute to the emergence of chromosomal rearrangements and shape the non-coding mutational landscape during multiple myeloma progression.

Keywords

Cancer Biology, Multiple Myeloma, Non-coding, Genome Instability, Enhancers, Structural Variants

  • 11 The genome topography at MERs is in an accessible and active conformation

Notebooks

  • Article Notebook

Data Availability

The newly sequenced data used in the study has been deposited to the International Cancer Genome Consortium’s Accelerating Research in Genomic Oncology file repository in collaboration with the P-1000. All sequencing data from previously published studies are maintained at the European Genome-Phenome Archive (EGAD00001003309, EGAD00001006119, EGAD00001006896, EGAD00001006363, EGAD00001011132); NCBI’s Database of Genotypes and Phenotypes (phs000348.v2.p1 and phs000748.v7.p4, and Sequence Read Archive (PRJNA694128).

Code Availability

All whole-genome sequencing data was preprocessed and analyzed for somatic mutations using our open-source pipeline, the MPG1000 (https://github.com/pblaney/mgp1000/tree/daytona). Additional tools used to analyze and visualize output from the pipeline were written in R as an open-source package devgru (https://github.com/pblaney/devgru/tree/daytona). Data analysis and visualization for this study was performed in R version 4.1.0. Software included for specialized analysis were executed using container-based environments in Docker and Singularity. The code utilized for the analyses in this manuscript can be found at https://github.com/pblaney/myelomaNoncodingGenome.

1 Environemnt Setup

2 Functions

Load in necessary functions

Code 
# Read in metadata file and convert to formatted data.table
read_metadata_file <- function(metadata_file) {

    # Read MGP metadata
    metadata_df <- fread(file = metadata_file, sep = "\t", header = T)

    metadata_df$study <- factor(x = metadata_df$study, levels = c("CoMMpass Trial NCT01454297, IA19",
        "Chapman et al., Nature 2011", "Maura et al., Nat Commun 2019", "Landau et al., Nat Commun 2020",
        "Maura et al., Clin Cancer Res. 2021", "Oben et al., Nat Commun 2021", "Liu et al., Nat Commun 2021",
        "Maura et al., Nat Cancer 2023", "Kazandjian et al., Clin Cancer Res. 2024",
        "Newly Sequenced - MSKCC Patients", "Newly Sequenced - Polyethnic-1000"))

    metadata_df$dataset_id <- factor(x = metadata_df$dataset_id, levels = c("phs000748.v7.p4",
        "phs000348.v2.p1", "EGAD00001003309", "EGAD00001006119", "EGAD00001006896",
        "EGAD00001006363", "PRJNA694128", "EGAD00001011132", "Newly Sequenced - MSKCC Patients",
        "Newly Sequenced - Polyethnic-1000"))

    metadata_df$disease_stage <- factor(x = metadata_df$disease_stage, levels = c("MGUS",
        "SMM", "MM", "RRMM", "PCL", "WM"))

    metadata_df$sex <- factor(x = metadata_df$sex, levels = c("Female", "Male"))

    metadata_df$self_identified_race <- factor(x = metadata_df$self_identified_race,
        levels = c("White", "Black or African American", "Hispanic", "Asian"))

    metadata_df$igh_translocation <- factor(x = metadata_df$igh_translocation, levels = c("t(11;14)",
        "t(4;14)", "t(14;16)", "t(14;20)", "t(6;14)", "t(8;14)", "Not Detected"))

    metadata_df$genomic_hrd_status <- factor(x = metadata_df$genomic_hrd_status,
        levels = c("yes", "no"))

    metadata_df$super5_cluster_id <- factor(x = metadata_df$super5_cluster_id, levels = c("EUR Dominant",
        "AFR Dominant", "AMR Dominant", "EAS Dominant", "SAS Dominant", "Highly Admixed"))

    return(metadata_df)
}

# Create a table to describe each series in the whole dataset
summary_table <- function(metadata) {

    # Create table with summary metrics for each series of patients
    dataset_summary_table <- c()
    for (i in 1:length(levels(metadata$dataset_id))) {

        # Column for total patients and samples
        total_patients <- metadata %>%
            dplyr::filter(dataset_id == levels(metadata$dataset_id)[i] & !is.na(patient)) %>%
            dplyr::distinct(patient) %>%
            dplyr::count()

        total_samples <- metadata %>%
            dplyr::filter(dataset_id == levels(metadata$dataset_id)[i] & !is.na(sample)) %>%
            dplyr::distinct(sample) %>%
            dplyr::count()

        # Column for covearge metrics in normal and tumors
        mean_normal_cov <- metadata %>%
            dplyr::filter(dataset_id == levels(metadata$dataset_id)[i]) %>%
            dplyr::distinct(patient, coverage_normal) %>%
            dplyr::reframe(mean_cov = round(mean(na.omit(coverage_normal)), digits = 0))

        range_normal_cov <- metadata %>%
            dplyr::filter(dataset_id == levels(metadata$dataset_id)[i]) %>%
            dplyr::distinct(patient, coverage_normal) %>%
            dplyr::reframe(range_cov = round(range(na.omit(coverage_normal)), digits = 0))

        normal_cov_and_range <- str_c(mean_normal_cov, " (", range_normal_cov$range_cov[1],
            "-", range_normal_cov$range_cov[2], ")")

        mean_tumor_cov <- metadata %>%
            dplyr::filter(dataset_id == levels(metadata$dataset_id)[i]) %>%
            dplyr::distinct(patient, coverage_tumor) %>%
            dplyr::reframe(mean_cov = round(mean(na.omit(coverage_tumor)), digits = 0))

        range_tumor_cov <- metadata %>%
            dplyr::filter(dataset_id == levels(metadata$dataset_id)[i]) %>%
            dplyr::distinct(patient, coverage_tumor) %>%
            dplyr::reframe(range_cov = round(range(na.omit(coverage_tumor)), digits = 0))

        tumor_cov_and_range <- stringr::str_c(mean_tumor_cov, " (", range_tumor_cov$range_cov[1],
            "-", range_tumor_cov$range_cov[2], ")")

        dataset_summary_table <- rbind(dataset_summary_table, tibble(`Dataset Identifier` = levels(metadata$dataset_id)[i],
            Patients = total_patients, Samples = total_samples, `Coverage\nin Normal` = normal_cov_and_range,
            `Coverage\nin Tumor` = tumor_cov_and_range))
    }

    # Calculate the mean of the total for T/N coverage
    mean_norm_cov_of_total <- metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, coverage_normal) %>%
        unique() %>%
        dplyr::reframe(mean_norm_cov = mean(coverage_normal))
    mean_norm_cov_of_total <- round(mean_norm_cov_of_total$mean_norm_cov, digits = 1)

    mean_tumor_cov_of_total <- metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, coverage_tumor) %>%
        dplyr::filter(!is.na(coverage_tumor)) %>%
        unique() %>%
        dplyr::reframe(mean_tumor_cov = mean(coverage_tumor))
    mean_tumor_cov_of_total <- round(mean_tumor_cov_of_total$mean_tumor_cov, digits = 1)

    # Add total count row to summary table
    dataset_summary_table <- rbind(dataset_summary_table, tibble(`Dataset Identifier` = "Total",
        Patients = sum(dataset_summary_table$Patients$n), Samples = sum(dataset_summary_table$Samples$n),
        `Coverage\nin Normal` = mean_norm_cov_of_total, `Coverage\nin Tumor` = mean_tumor_cov_of_total))

    # Add row for high-coverage data only high_cov_df <- metadata %>%
    # dplyr::select(patient, sample, coverage_normal, coverage_tumor) %>%
    # dplyr::filter(!is.na(coverage_tumor)) %>% dplyr::filter(coverage_normal >
    # 15 & coverage_tumor > 15) mean_norm_of_high_cov <- high_cov_df %>%
    # dplyr::select(patient, coverage_normal) %>% unique() %>%
    # dplyr::summarise('mean_norm_high_cov' = mean(coverage_normal))
    # mean_norm_of_high_cov <- round(mean_norm_of_high_cov$mean_norm_high_cov,
    # digits = 1) mean_tumor_of_high_cov <- high_cov_df %>%
    # dplyr::select(sample, coverage_tumor) %>%
    # dplyr::filter(!is.na(coverage_tumor)) %>% unique() %>%
    # dplyr::summarise('mean_tumor_high_cov' = mean(coverage_tumor))
    # mean_tumor_of_high_cov <-
    # round(mean_tumor_of_high_cov$mean_tumor_high_cov, digits = 1)
    # dataset_summary_table <- rbind(dataset_summary_table, tibble('Dataset
    # Identifier' = 'High-coverage', 'Patients' =
    # length(unique(high_cov_df$patient)), 'Samples' =
    # length(unique(high_cov_df$sample)), 'Coverage\nin Normal' =
    # mean_norm_of_high_cov, 'Coverage\nin Tumor' = mean_tumor_of_high_cov))

    # Make the base table
    dataset_summary_table_plot <- ggtexttable(x = dataset_summary_table, rows = NULL,
        theme = ttheme(tbody.style = tbody_style(fill = c("grey95", "grey90"), hjust = 1,
            x = 0.99), colnames.style = colnames_style(size = 12, fill = NA)))

    dataset_summary_table_plot <- dataset_summary_table_plot %>%
        table_cell_font(row = 2:tab_nrow(dataset_summary_table_plot), column = 1,
            face = "bold", size = 11) %>%
        tab_add_hline(at.row = 1:2, row.side = "top", linewidth = 4, linetype = 1,
            linecolor = "#8000FF") %>%
        tab_add_hline(at.row = max(tab_nrow(dataset_summary_table_plot)) - 1, row.side = "bottom",
            linewidth = 4, linetype = 1, linecolor = "#8000FF") %>%
        tab_add_hline(at.row = max(tab_nrow(dataset_summary_table_plot)), row.side = "bottom",
            linewidth = 4, linetype = 1, linecolor = "#8000FF")

    return(dataset_summary_table_plot)
}

coverage_bar_plotter <- function(coverage_metrics, normal_or_tumor) {

    if (normal_or_tumor == "normal") {
        sorted_coverage_metrics <- coverage_metrics %>%
            dplyr::select(patient, dataset_id, coverage_normal) %>%
            unique() %>%
            dplyr::arrange(coverage_normal)

        sorted_coverage_metrics <- sorted_coverage_metrics %>%
            dplyr::mutate(cov_type = case_when(coverage_normal >= 15 ~ "High-coverage",
                coverage_normal < 15 ~ "Low-coverage"))

        hi_cov_cutoff <- sum(sorted_coverage_metrics$cov_type == "High-coverage")

        study_cov_plot <- ggplot() + geom_col(aes(x = seq(1, nrow(sorted_coverage_metrics),
            1), y = sorted_coverage_metrics$coverage_normal, fill = sorted_coverage_metrics$cov_type),
            width = 1) + geom_vline(xintercept = length(unique(sorted_coverage_metrics$patient)) -
            hi_cov_cutoff, linewidth = 0.5, color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
            labs(x = "Patients", y = "Coverage in Normal") + scale_y_continuous(position = "right",
            expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, round(max(sorted_coverage_metrics$coverage_normal),
                digits = 0) + 1), breaks = c(0, 5, 15, 30, 60, 90, round(max(sorted_coverage_metrics$coverage_normal),
                digits = 0))) + scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.02, 0.02), breaks = c(1,
            250, 500, 750, 1000, length(unique(sorted_coverage_metrics$patient)))) +
            scale_fill_manual(name = "", values = c("black", "gray")) + theme(legend.position.inside = c(0.2,
            0.8), legend.text = element_text(size = 12), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
            axis.text = element_text(size = 12)) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
            x = length(unique(sorted_coverage_metrics$patient)) - (hi_cov_cutoff/2),
            y = round(max(sorted_coverage_metrics$coverage_normal), digits = 0) *
                0.8, label = ">=15x", size = 4.5)

    } else if (normal_or_tumor == "tumor") {
        sorted_coverage_metrics <- coverage_metrics %>%
            dplyr::select(patient, dataset_id, coverage_tumor) %>%
            unique() %>%
            dplyr::arrange(coverage_tumor)
        sorted_coverage_metrics <- sorted_coverage_metrics %>%
            dplyr::mutate(cov_type = case_when(coverage_tumor >= 15 ~ "High-coverage",
                coverage_tumor < 15 ~ "Low-coverage", is.na(coverage_tumor) ~ "Low-coverage"))

        hi_cov_cutoff <- sum(sorted_coverage_metrics$cov_type == "High-coverage")

        study_cov_plot <- ggplot() + geom_col(aes(x = seq(1, nrow(sorted_coverage_metrics),
            1), y = sorted_coverage_metrics$coverage_tumor, fill = sorted_coverage_metrics$cov_type),
            width = 1) + geom_vline(xintercept = nrow(sorted_coverage_metrics) -
            hi_cov_cutoff - sum(is.na(dataset_metadata$coverage_tumor)), linewidth = 0.5,
            color = "black", linetype = "dashed") + labs(x = "Samples", y = "Coverage in Tumor") +
            scale_y_continuous(position = "right", expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0,
                round(max(sorted_coverage_metrics$coverage_tumor, na.rm = T), digits = 0) +
                  1), breaks = c(0, 5, 15, 30, 60, 90, round(max(sorted_coverage_metrics$coverage_tumor,
                na.rm = T), digits = 0))) + scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.02, 0.02),
            breaks = c(1, 250, 500, 750, 1000, 1250, nrow(sorted_coverage_metrics) -
                sum(is.na(dataset_metadata$coverage_tumor)))) + scale_fill_manual(name = "",
            values = c("#8000FF", "gray")) + theme(legend.position.inside = c(0.2,
            0.8), legend.text = element_text(size = 12), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
            axis.text = element_text(size = 12)) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
            x = nrow(sorted_coverage_metrics) - (hi_cov_cutoff/2) - sum(is.na(dataset_metadata$coverage_tumor)),
            y = round(max(sorted_coverage_metrics$coverage_tumor, na.rm = T), digits = 0) *
                0.8, label = ">= 15x", size = 4.5)
    }

    return(study_cov_plot)
}

extract_model_pvalue <- function(lm_model_summary) {
    f <- lm_model_summary$fstatistic
    p <- pf(f[1], f[2], f[3], lower.tail = F)
    attributes(p) <- NULL
    return(p)
}

# TODO: complete the functionalization of this take fishhook results in
# data.table form and ggplotify the QQ plot
geom_fishhook_qq <- function(fishhook_results, plot_title = "", hightlight_vector = NULL,
    label_vector = NULL, label_x_nudge = 0, manual_lambda = NULL) {
    # Transform the observed P-values to -log10
    neg_log10_obs_p <- -log10(as.numeric(fishhook_results %>%
        dplyr::select(p) %>%
        purrr::as_vector()))

    # Create -log10 of the expected uniform distribution as the null hypothesis
    n_obs <- length(neg_log10_obs_p)
    neg_log10_exp_p <- -log(1:n_obs/n_obs, 10)

    # Create DT for plotting that is sorted from smallest to largest
    qq_plot_data <- data.table(expected = base::sort(neg_log10_exp_p), observed = neg_log10_obs_p[base::order(neg_log10_obs_p)])

    # Set max for plot using the largest value on either axis
    if (max(qq_plot_data$expected) > max(qq_plot_data$observed)) {
        max_xy <- max(qq_plot_data$expected)
    } else {
        max_xy <- max(qq_plot_data$observed)
    }

    # TODO: lambda calculation is inconsistent with the output from fishHook,
    # need to address this for now, set the lambda using the value from the
    # fishHook output
    if (!is.null(manual_lambda)) {
        lambda <- manual_lambda
    } else {
        # Subsample if over 100k obs for lambda calculation
        if (nrow(qq_plot_data) > 100000) {
            subsample <- pmin(pmax(0, 50000/nrow(qq_plot_data)), 1)
            qq_data_for_lambda_eval <- qq_plot_data[sample(nrow(qq_plot_data), subsample *
                nrow(qq_plot_data)), ]
        } else {
            qq_data_for_lambda_eval <- qq_plot_data
        }

        # Calculate the lambda i.e. genomic inflation factor
        lambda <- lm(expected ~ observed - 1, qq_data_for_lambda_eval)$coefficients
    }

    # Build ggplot piece by piece start with the base of plot with reference
    # line for good quality lambda
    base_plot <- ggplot(qq_plot_data) + geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, alpha = 0.7,
        linewidth = 3, color = "gray70")

    # Add layer of QQ points and highlight points of interest, if provided
    if (!is.null(hightlight_vector)) {
        simple_qq_plot <- base_plot + scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(x = expected,
            y = observed), color = rev(hightlight_vector), alpha = 0.7, pointsize = 3.5)
    } else {
        simple_qq_plot <- base_plot + scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(x = expected,
            y = observed), alpha = 0.7, pointsize = 3.5)
    }

    # Add label points of interest, if provided
    if (!is.null(label_vector)) {
        labeled_qq_plot <- simple_qq_plot + ggrepel::geom_label_repel(aes(x = expected,
            y = observed, label = rev(label_vector)), min.segment.length = 0, max.overlaps = 100,
            nudge_x = label_x_nudge, na.rm = T)
    } else {
        labeled_qq_plot <- simple_qq_plot
    }

    # final layer to the plot with lambda regression fit + text annotation
    final_qq_plot <- labeled_qq_plot + geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = lambda,
        alpha = 0.9, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,
        max_xy + 0.5), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,
        max_xy + 0.5), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + labs(title = plot_title, x = expression("Expected -log"[10] *
        "(" * italic("P") * ")"), y = expression("Observed -log"[10] * "(" * italic("P") *
        ")")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", label = paste0("lambda=", round(lambda,
        digits = 3)), x = 0.87 * max_xy, y = 0.04 * max_xy, size = 6, color = "red") +
        theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), plot.title = element_text(size = 20,
            hjust = 0.5))

    # Output the plot
    return(final_qq_plot)
}

# Plot individual SBS96 deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input output is the input
# for this
plot_sbs96 <- function(sbs_counts) {

    # Transpose the SBS count matrix and prep for easy ggplotting
    transpose_sbs <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(sbs_counts)) %>%
        tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "trinuc_context")
    transpose_sbs <- transpose_sbs %>%
        dplyr::rename(count = colnames(transpose_sbs)[2])

    transpose_sbs$pct_sbs <- (transpose_sbs$count/sum(transpose_sbs$count))

    # Set the trinucleotide order via factor
    transpose_sbs$trinuc_context <- factor(transpose_sbs$trinuc_context, levels = transpose_sbs$trinuc_context)

    # Set COSMIC standard color scheme
    col_96 <- c(rep("#5ABCEB", 16), rep("#050708", 16), rep("#D33C32", 16), rep("#CBCACB",
        16), rep("#ABCD72", 16), rep("#E7C9C6", 16))

    # Now let's make the SBS96 bar plot
    sbs96_barplot <- ggplot2::ggplot(transpose_sbs) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = trinuc_context,
        y = pct_sbs), fill = col_96, width = 1) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,
        0), limits = c(0, max(transpose_sbs$pct_sbs) * 0.1 + max(transpose_sbs$pct_sbs)),
        labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) + ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0,
        0)) + ggplot2::labs(x = "Trinucleotide Context", y = "Single Base Substitutions (%)") +
        ggplot2::theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
            size = 5, color = col_96, face = "bold"), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
        ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(0.5, 16.5, 32.5, 48.5, 64.5, 80.5),
            xmax = c(16.5, 32.5, 48.5, 64.5, 80.5, 96.5), ymin = max(transpose_sbs$pct_sbs) *
                0.025 + max(transpose_sbs$pct_sbs), ymax = max(transpose_sbs$pct_sbs) *
                0.1 + max(transpose_sbs$pct_sbs), fill = col_96[c(1, 17, 33, 49,
                65, 81)]) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = c(8, 24, 40, 56,
        72, 88), y = max(transpose_sbs$pct_sbs) * 0.06 + max(transpose_sbs$pct_sbs),
        label = c("C>A", "C>G", "C>T", "T>A", "T>C", "T>G"), size = 4, color = c("black",
            "white", "black", "black", "black", "black"))

    return(sbs96_barplot)
}

# TODO: FUNCTIONALIZE FOR ggGenomicsTracks, parametrize the number of chroms?
# Need tick mark breaks at the midpoint of the chromosome
linearized_hg38_end_breaks <- c()
linearized_hg38_strip_fill <- c()
linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks <- c()
for (i in 1:24) {
    # Get end-to-end breaks for each chromosome
    linearized_hg38_end_breaks[i] <- sum(hg_seqlengths()[1:i])

    # Set alternating strip colors for fill
    if (i%%2 == 1) {
        linearized_hg38_strip_fill[i] <- "gray40"
    } else {
        linearized_hg38_strip_fill[i] <- "gray80"
    }

    # Get midpoint of range
    chrom_midpoint <- round(hg_seqlengths()[i]/2, digits = 0)
    # Now add the midpoints to each linear chrom after the first
    if (i == 1) {
        linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[i] <- chrom_midpoint
    } else {
        linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[i] <- sum(hg_seqlengths()[1:i - 1], chrom_midpoint)
    }
}
linearized_hg38_limits <- c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks))

# TODO: need to functionalize this for use in ggGenomicTracks
gr_absolute_ranges <- function(input_gr, cpus = 1, ref_seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]) {

    # Set parallel cores parameter
    message("Setting parallel cores to ", cpus, " ...")
    doParallel::registerDoParallel(cores = cpus)

    # GenomicRanges::absoluteRanges() function does not scale to 'large
    # genomes, aka human genome Let's pararllelize the function across the
    # chromosomes in the input GR
    chrom_iter_list <- as.character(unique(levels(GenomeInfoDb::seqnames(input_gr))))

    final_abs_ranges <- foreach::foreach(x = 1:length(chrom_iter_list), .combine = rrbind,
        .packages = "gUtils") %dopar% {

        # Grab the ranges per chromosome
        input_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(input_gr)
        ranges_per_col <- input_dt %>%
            dplyr::filter(seqnames == chrom_iter_list[x])

        # Set base linear value depending on the chromosome
        base_linear_val_idx <- which(chrom_iter_list[x] == names(ref_seqlengths))
        base_linear_val <- sum(as.numeric(ref_seqlengths[1:base_linear_val_idx -
            1]))

        # Now add the genomic coordinates to the base linear value to get the
        # linear start and end
        linear_start <- as.numeric(base_linear_val + ranges_per_col$start)
        linear_end <- as.numeric(base_linear_val + ranges_per_col$end)

        # Return output of the foreach loop, data.table of absolute linear
        # coordinates
        abs_linear_coords <- data.table::data.table(start_abs_linear = linear_start,
            end_abs_linear = linear_end)
        abs_linear_coords
    }

    # Add the absolute linear coordinates to the input GR
    gr <- input_gr
    gr$start_abs_linear <- final_abs_ranges$start_abs_linear
    gr$end_abs_linear <- final_abs_ranges$end_abs_linear

    # Output input GR with absolute linear coordinates
    return(gr)
}

p_star <- function(p_vals) {
    pstar_vals <- dplyr::case_when(p_vals < 0.0001 ~ "****", dplyr::between(x = p_vals,
        left = 0.0001, right = 0.001) ~ "***", dplyr::between(x = p_vals, left = 0.001,
        right = 0.01) ~ "**", dplyr::between(x = p_vals, left = 0.01, right = 0.05) ~
        "*", p_vals > 0.05 ~ "ns")
    return(pstar_vals)
}
p_signif_codes <- "Signif. codes: **** <0.0001, *** 0.001, ** 0.01, * 0.05, ns >0.05"


# Run DESeq2 on featureCounts data
deseq2_exe <- function(x_counts_input, y_counts_input, x_conditions_input, y_conditions_input,
    gencode_gene_list = "gencode.v42.primary_assembly.basic.annotation.gtf.gz") {

    # Read in two sets of raw count matrix from featureCounts output
    x_counts <- read.table(file = x_counts_input, header = F)
    y_counts <- read.table(file = y_counts_input, header = F)

    # Add proper column names and convert to matrix
    colnames(x_counts) <- read_delim(file = x_counts_input, delim = "\t", col_names = T,
        show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
        colnames() %>%
        str_remove(pattern = "\\#")
    x_counts <- tibble::column_to_rownames(x_counts, var = "GENE")

    colnames(y_counts) <- read_delim(file = y_counts_input, delim = "\t", col_names = T,
        show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
        colnames() %>%
        str_remove(pattern = "\\#")
    y_counts <- tibble::column_to_rownames(y_counts, var = "GENE")

    # Read in two sets of conditions names
    x_conditions <- read.table(file = x_conditions_input, header = F, sep = "\t",
        col.names = c("sample", "condition", "type"))
    x_conditions <- tibble::column_to_rownames(x_conditions, var = "sample")

    y_conditions <- read.table(file = y_conditions_input, header = F, sep = "\t",
        col.names = c("sample", "condition", "type"))
    y_conditions <- tibble::column_to_rownames(y_conditions, var = "sample")

    # Create condition comparison matrix and factorize condition matrix
    # variables
    y_vs_x_conditions <- rbind(x_conditions, y_conditions)
    y_vs_x_conditions$condition <- factor(y_vs_x_conditions$condition, levels = c(x_conditions[1,
        1], y_conditions[1, 1]))
    y_vs_x_conditions$type <- factor(y_vs_x_conditions$type)

    # Create count comparison matrix
    y_vs_x_counts <- cbind(x_counts, y_counts)

    # To refine analysis, restrict the counts matrix to CDS genes in GENCODE
    # v42 GTF and then remove any mitochondrial genes
    gencode_genes <- import(gencode_gene_list)
    gencode_genes <- unique(gencode_genes$gene_name)
    gencode_genes <- gencode_genes[!str_detect(string = gencode_genes, pattern = "^MT-")]

    y_vs_x_counts <- y_vs_x_counts %>%
        dplyr::filter(rownames(y_vs_x_counts) %in% gencode_genes)

    # Quick first hard filter to remove genes with little expression across all
    # samples Remove all genes with mean expression <
    # median(all_per_gene_exp_mean) / 10
    per_gene_exp_mean <- y_vs_x_counts %>%
        dplyr::mutate(exp_mean = round(rowSums(y_vs_x_counts)/ncol(y_vs_x_counts),
            digits = 0)) %>%
        dplyr::select(exp_mean) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        as.numeric()

    y_vs_x_counts <- y_vs_x_counts %>%
        dplyr::filter(rowSums(y_vs_x_counts)/ncol(y_vs_x_counts) > round(median(per_gene_exp_mean)/10,
            digits = 0))

    # Convert counts matrix and conditions file into DESeq2 object
    y_vs_x_counts_deseq_obj <- DESeq2::DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = y_vs_x_counts,
        colData = y_vs_x_conditions, design = ~condition)

    # Run DESeq2
    y_vs_x_counts_deseq_obj <- DESeq2::DESeq(object = y_vs_x_counts_deseq_obj)

    return(y_vs_x_counts_deseq_obj)
}


# Modification of the gUtils function gr.collapse,
gr_collapse = function(gr, pad, strand_ignore) {
    tmp = gr.findoverlaps(gr + pad, gr + pad, ignore.strand = strand_ignore)
    m = rep(FALSE, length(gr))
    m[tmp$query.id[tmp$query.id == (tmp$subject.id - 1)]] = TRUE

    ## will not collapse if two intersecting ranges are in the wrong 'order'
    ## (ie not increasing (decreasing) on pos (neg) strand
    m[which((strand(gr)[-length(gr)] == "+" & (start(gr)[-length(gr)] > start(gr)[-1])) |
        (strand(gr)[-length(gr)] == "-" & (end(gr)[-length(gr)] < end(gr)[-1])))] = FALSE

    m = as(m, "IRanges")

    if (length(m) > 0) {
        end(m) = end(m) + 1
        tmp = cbind(start(gr)[start(m)], end(gr)[start(m)], start(gr)[end(m)], end(gr)[end(m)])
        s = pmin(start(gr)[start(m)], end(gr)[start(m)], start(gr)[end(m)], end(gr)[end(m)])
        e = pmax(start(gr)[start(m)], end(gr)[start(m)], start(gr)[end(m)], end(gr)[end(m)])
        return(GRanges(seqnames(gr)[start(m)], IRanges(s, e), strand = strand(gr)[start(m)],
            seqlengths = seqlengths(gr)))
    } else {
        return(gr[c()])
    }
}

# my function fill gaps in a genomic variable using a median of values in a
# window around the gaps
impute_gaps <- function(input_table, column_with_gaps, ref_data) {

    # Set output clone so input is not manipulated and find gaps
    output_table <- input_table
    gaps_to_impute <- which(is.na(input_table[[column_with_gaps]]))

    for (i in 1:length(gaps_to_impute)) {
        # TODO: functionalize this and make better with GR window selection
        # instead of just number of rows
        founder <- GenomicRanges::follow(x = dt2gr(input_table[gaps_to_impute[i]]),
            subject = dt2gr(ref_data))

        padding <- 25000
        if (is.na(founder)) {
            founder_segment <- dt2gr(input_table[gaps_to_impute[i]])
            founder_segment <- founder_segment + padding
            founder_segment <- gr.findoverlaps(query = founder_segment, subject = dt2gr(ref_data),
                scol = column_with_gaps, return.type = "data.table")
            if (length(founder_segment) > 0) {
                output_table[gaps_to_impute[i]][[column_with_gaps]] <- mean(founder_segment[[column_with_gaps]])
            } else {
                padding <- padding * 10
                founder_segment <- founder_segment + padding
                founder_segment <- gr.findoverlaps(query = founder_segment, subject = dt2gr(ref_data),
                  scol = column_with_gaps, return.type = "data.table")
                output_table[gaps_to_impute[i]][[column_with_gaps]] <- mean(founder_segment[[column_with_gaps]],
                  na.rm = T)
            }

        } else {
            output_table[gaps_to_impute[i]][[column_with_gaps]] <- mean(ref_data[founder -
                1:founder + 1][[column_with_gaps]], na.rm = T)
        }

    }
    return(output_table)

}

# Gather gene sets for investigation TODO: functionalize this for use in devgru

get_gene_set <- function(path_to_file, file_format, has_header = T, gene_set_name = NULL) {

    output_gene_set <- NULL
    if (file_format %in% c("COSMIC", "cosmic")) {
        message(paste0("Building COSMIC gene set at ", path_to_file, " ..."))
        cosmic_source_id <- str_remove(string = basename(path_to_file), pattern = ".tsv.gz")
        cosmic_gene_set <- data.table::fread(file = path_to_file, sep = "\t", header = has_header,
            stringsAsFactors = F)
        # Add the source to the columns
        cosmic_gene_set$source_id <- cosmic_source_id
        # Add user-defined gene set name
        if (!is.null(gene_set_name)) {
            cosmic_gene_set$gene_set <- gene_set_name
        }
        message(paste0(cosmic_source_id, " gene set built succsessfully ..."))
        message("This file is unfiltered ...\nRecommend review ...")
        # Preview
        pillar::glimpse(dplyr::slice_head(cosmic_gene_set, n = 3))
        # set output
        output_gene_set <- cosmic_gene_set

    } else if (file_format %in% c("txt", "tsv", "txt.gz", "tsv.gz")) {
        message(paste0("Building custom gene set at ", path_to_file, " ..."))
        custom_source_id <- str_remove(string = basename(path_to_file), pattern = paste0(".",
            file_format))
        custom_gene_set <- data.table::fread(file = path_to_file, sep = "\t", header = has_header,
            stringsAsFactors = F)
        # Add the source to the columns
        custom_gene_set$source_id <- custom_source_id
        # Add user-defined gene set name
        if (!is.null(gene_set_name)) {
            custom_gene_set$gene_set <- gene_set_name
        }
        message(paste0(custom_source_id, " gene set built succsessfully ..."))
        message("This file is unfiltered ...\nRecommend review ...")
        # Preview
        pillar::glimpse(dplyr::slice_head(custom_gene_set, n = 3))
        # set output
        output_gene_set <- custom_gene_set

    } else if (!file_format %in% c("COSMIC", "cosmic", "txt", "tsv", "txt.gz", "tsv.gz")) {
        stop(paste0("\nFile format:\n", file_format, "\nnot recognized ..."))
    }

    # Output
    return(output_gene_set)
}

# Set MERs were interested in with name of mapped gene
trx_starburst <- function(sv_bp, mer_of_interest, mapped_gene, proximal_dist_threshold = 2500000,
    table_position_x = 14, nudge = 0) {

    # Calculate the distances to the region of interest
    dist_to_moi <- gr.dist(gr1 = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(sv_bp)), gr2 = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(e_se_mers[mer_id ==
        mer_of_interest])), ignore.strand = T)

    # Build DT of inter-chromosomal SV pairs and their distances
    sv_inter_pairs <- data.table::data.table(pair_id = sv_bp$sv_id, distance = c(as.vector(dist_to_moi)),
        is_proximal = c(!is.na(dist_to_moi)), chrom = factor(gr2dt(sv_bp)$seqnames,
            levels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:24])), nearest_gene = sv_bp$nearest_gene)

    # Find all proximal and distal BPs, keep those within 1MB of M.O.I.
    close_prox_sv_inter_pairs <- data.table::data.table()
    for (j in 1:length(unique(sv_inter_pairs$pair_id))) {

        query_pair <- sv_inter_pairs %>%
            dplyr::filter(pair_id == unique(sv_inter_pairs$pair_id)[j])
        if (sum(query_pair$is_proximal) > 0) {

            if (query_pair$distance[which(!is.na(query_pair$distance))] <= proximal_dist_threshold) {
                proximal_index <- which(query_pair$is_proximal == TRUE)
                distal_index <- which(query_pair$is_proximal == FALSE)
                close_prox_sv_inter_pairs <- gUtils::rrbind(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs,
                  data.table::data.table(pair_id = query_pair$pair_id[proximal_index],
                    distance = query_pair$distance[proximal_index], chrom_of_partner = query_pair$chrom[distal_index],
                    nearest_gene_of_partner = query_pair$nearest_gene[distal_index]))
            }
        }
    }

    # Find top TRX partner genes
    ordered_partner_chroms <- close_prox_sv_inter_pairs %>%
        dplyr::group_by(chrom_of_partner) %>%
        dplyr::count() %>%
        dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
        dplyr::ungroup() %>%
        dplyr::mutate(Proportion = round(n/nrow(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs), digits = 2)) %>%
        dplyr::filter(n > 1)

    ordered_partner_genes <- close_prox_sv_inter_pairs %>%
        dplyr::group_by(nearest_gene_of_partner, chrom_of_partner) %>%
        dplyr::filter(chrom_of_partner %in% ordered_partner_chroms$chrom_of_partner) %>%
        dplyr::count() %>%
        dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
        dplyr::ungroup()

    gene_count_string <- c()
    for (i in 1:nrow(ordered_partner_chroms)) {
        genes_per_chrom <- ordered_partner_genes %>%
            dplyr::filter(chrom_of_partner == ordered_partner_chroms$chrom_of_partner[i]) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(gene_count_string = paste0(nearest_gene_of_partner, " (",
                n, ")"))
        gene_count_string[i] <- paste(genes_per_chrom$gene_count_string, collapse = ", ")
    }

    ordered_partner_chroms$gene_count_string <- gene_count_string
    ordered_partner_chroms <- ordered_partner_chroms %>%
        dplyr::select(chrom_of_partner, Proportion, gene_count_string) %>%
        dplyr::rename(Chrom = chrom_of_partner, Gene = gene_count_string)

    # Make table to go along with starburt pplot
    partner_table <- tibble::tibble(x = table_position_x, y = max(ordered_partner_chroms$Proportion) +
        nudge, tb = list(ordered_partner_chroms))

    # Create starburst plot of top frequent TRX
    starburst_plot <- ggplot(data = as.data.frame(table(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs$chrom_of_partner))) +
        ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = Var1, y = Freq/nrow(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs),
            fill = Var1), width = 1) + geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5) + ggpp::geom_table(data = partner_table,
        aes(x = x, y = y, label = tb)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Translocation\nPartner\nChromosome",
        values = paletteer::paletteer_d("ggsci::default_igv")[1:24]) + labs(title = paste0(mapped_gene,
        " (+", round(proximal_dist_threshold/10^6, digits = 1), "Mbp Flank)")) +
        coord_polar() + theme(legend.position = "bottom", axis.line = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_blank(), axis.title = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
        x = c(0.5), xend = c(0.7, 0.625), y = c(max(as.data.frame(table(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs$chrom_of_partner))[,
            2]/nrow(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs))/2, max(as.data.frame(table(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs$chrom_of_partner))[,
            2]/nrow(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs))), yend = c(max(as.data.frame(table(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs$chrom_of_partner))[,
            2]/nrow(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs))/2, max(as.data.frame(table(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs$chrom_of_partner))[,
            2]/nrow(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs)))) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
        x = c(23.85, 24.2), y = c(max(as.data.frame(table(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs$chrom_of_partner))[,
            2]/nrow(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs))/2, max(as.data.frame(table(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs$chrom_of_partner))[,
            2]/nrow(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs))), label = c(round(max(as.data.frame(table(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs$chrom_of_partner))[,
            2]/nrow(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs))/2, digits = 2), round(max(as.data.frame(table(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs$chrom_of_partner))[,
            2]/nrow(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs)), digits = 2)))

    # return both plots
    return(starburst_plot)
}

# functions to get SV BP counts per around GR of interest
proximal_bp_dist <- function(sv_bp_gr, gr_of_interest, proximal_dist_threshold = 5000000) {

    # Calculate the distances to the region of interest
    dist_to_moi <- gr.dist(gr1 = sv_bp_gr, gr2 = gr_of_interest, ignore.strand = T)

    # Build DT of inter-chromosomal SV pairs and their distances
    sv_inter_pairs <- data.table::data.table(chrom = factor(gr2dt(sv_bp_gr)$seqnames,
        levels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:24])), start = GenomicRanges::start(sv_bp_gr),
        end = GenomicRanges::end(sv_bp_gr), bp_abs_linear = sv_bp_gr$start_abs_linear,
        nearest_gene = sv_bp_gr$nearest_gene, pair_id = sv_bp_gr$sv_id, distance = c(as.vector(dist_to_moi)),
        is_proximal = c(!is.na(dist_to_moi)))
    # find directionality of distance Find midpoint of MER and use this to find
    # directionality of BPs
    abs_range_ref <- elliplot::midpoint(c(gr_absolute_ranges(gr_of_interest)$start_abs_linear,
        gr_absolute_ranges(gr_of_interest)$end_abs_linear))[[1]][2]

    sv_inter_pairs$proximal_bp_directionality <- dplyr::case_when(sv_inter_pairs$is_proximal &
        sv_inter_pairs$bp_abs_linear > abs_range_ref ~ "+", sv_inter_pairs$is_proximal &
        sv_inter_pairs$bp_abs_linear < abs_range_ref ~ "-", !sv_inter_pairs$is_proximal ~
        "NA")

    # Find all proximal and distal BPs, keep those within 1MB of M.O.I.
    close_prox_sv_inter_pairs <- data.table::data.table()
    for (j in 1:length(unique(sv_inter_pairs$pair_id))) {

        query_pair <- sv_inter_pairs %>%
            dplyr::filter(pair_id == unique(sv_inter_pairs$pair_id)[j])
        if (sum(query_pair$is_proximal) > 0) {

            if (query_pair$distance[which(!is.na(query_pair$distance))] <= proximal_dist_threshold) {
                proximal_index <- which(query_pair$is_proximal == TRUE)
                distal_index <- which(query_pair$is_proximal == FALSE)
                close_prox_sv_inter_pairs <- gUtils::rrbind(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs,
                  data.table::data.table(chrom_proximal = query_pair$chrom[proximal_index],
                    start_proximal = query_pair$start[proximal_index], end_proximal = query_pair$end[proximal_index],
                    bp_abs_linear_proximal = query_pair$bp_abs_linear[proximal_index],
                    pair_id = query_pair$pair_id[proximal_index], distance_proximal = query_pair$distance[proximal_index],
                    directionality_proximal = query_pair$proximal_bp_directionality[proximal_index],
                    chrom_distal_partner = query_pair$chrom[distal_index], nearest_gene_distal_partner = query_pair$nearest_gene[distal_index]))
            }
        }
    }
    return(close_prox_sv_inter_pairs)
}

# window density calculation
window_density <- function(data_to_count, window_size, bin_size) {

    window_mid <- window_size/2
    window_start <- -window_mid

    count_per_bin_df <- data.table()
    for (i in 1:(window_size/bin_size)) {

        if (i == 1) {
            bin_start <- window_start
            bin_end <- window_start + (bin_size * i)
        } else {
            bin_start <- window_start + (bin_size * (i - 1))
            bin_end <- window_start + (bin_size * i)
        }

        count_per_bin_df <- gUtils::rrbind(count_per_bin_df, data.table(bin = paste0(bin_start,
            " - ", bin_end), midpoint_for_plot = elliplot::midpoint(c(bin_start,
            bin_end))[[1]][2], count = sum(dplyr::between(x = as.numeric(paste0(data_to_count$directionality_proximal,
            data_to_count$distance_proximal)), left = bin_start, right = bin_end))))
    }
    return(count_per_bin_df)
}

# TODO: maybe generalize a bit
epilogo_prop_per_region <- function(roi, roi_id) {
    # Find overlapping regions
    epilogo_per_roi <- gr.findoverlaps(query = roi, subject = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(mm_epilogos)),
        scol = colnames(mm_epilogos)[-c(1:3)], return.type = "data.table")
    # normalize each row to equate a proportion of chromatin state to each
    # segment
    normalized_epilogo_signal <- t(apply(as.matrix(epilogo_per_roi %>%
        dplyr::select(colnames(mm_epilogos)[-c(1:3)])), 1, ohenery::normalize))

    # Find the sum proportion of all columns then divide by the number of rows
    # to get the proportion of all chromatin states for the region
    epilogo_roi_prop <- colSums(normalized_epilogo_signal)/nrow(normalized_epilogo_signal)
    epilogo_roi_prop_dt <- data.table(state = names(epilogo_roi_prop), prop = epilogo_roi_prop,
        roi = roi_id)
    epilogo_roi_prop_dt$state <- factor(epilogo_roi_prop_dt$state, levels = chromhmm_metadata$long_name)
    return(epilogo_roi_prop_dt)
}

# Data needed for ggGenomicTracks
hg38_ref_cytobands <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "cytoband_autosome_sex_chroms.hg38.bed"),
    has_header = F, additional_col_names = c("name", "gieStain"))
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

# Build the genomic coordinate x-axis for the locus of interest DT for
# continuous x-axis name, labels, limits, and breaks
get_locus_axis <- function(input_window, input_flank) {
    # Find width of desired window (native width plus flank applied to both
    # ends)
    window_width <- GenomicRanges::width(parse.gr(input_window) + (input_flank *
        2))

    # Get labels and name if total window width is >10kbp, will use Mb if total
    # window is <= 10kbp will use bp
    if (window_width > 10000) {
        window_axis_name <- paste0(stringr::str_split(string = input_window, pattern = ":")[[1]][1] %>%
            stringr::str_replace(pattern = "chr", replacement = "Chr "), " (Mbp)")
        window_axis_labels <- scales::label_comma(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 0.000001)

    } else if (window_width <= 10000) {
        window_axis_name <- paste0(stringr::str_split(string = input_window, pattern = ":")[[1]][1] %>%
            stringr::str_replace(pattern = "chr", replacement = "Chr "), " (bp)")
        window_axis_labels <- scales::label_comma(accuracy = 1)
    }

    # Get limits
    window_limits <- stringr::str_remove(string = input_window, pattern = "chr[\\dXY]+:") %>%
        stringr::str_split(pattern = "-", simplify = TRUE)
    window_axis_limits <- c(as.numeric(window_limits[1, 1]) - input_flank, as.numeric(window_limits[1,
        2]) + input_flank)

    # Build the continuous genomic coordinate x-axis for the locus of interest
    locus_x_axis_params <- list(name = window_axis_name, labels = window_axis_labels,
        limits = window_axis_limits)
    return(locus_x_axis_params)
}

# @author Ricardo Saporta
# https://stackoverflow.com/questions/20249653/insert-layer-underneath-existing-layers-in-ggplot2-object
# ggplot_base : Plot object insert_layer_after : Position where to insert new
# layers, relative to existing layers ...  : additional layers, separated by
# commas (,) instead of plus sign (+)
insert_gg_layer <- function(ggplot_base, insert_layer_after = 0, ...) {

    if (insert_layer_after < 0) {
        insert_layer_after <- insert_layer_after + length(ggplot_base$layers)
    }

    if (!length(ggplot_base$layers)) {
        ggplot_base$layers <- list(...)
    } else {
        ggplot_base$layers <- append(ggplot_base$layers, list(...), insert_layer_after)
    }
    return(ggplot_base)
}

locus_viewer_string <- function(input_window, input_flank = 0) {
    # Collect the parts of the viewer style string
    viewer_strings <- stringr::str_split(string = gUtils::gr.string(devgru::gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::parse.gr(input_window) +
        input_flank)), pattern = ":") %>%
        BiocGenerics::unlist() %>%
        stringr::str_split(pattern = "-") %>%
        BiocGenerics::unlist()

    pretty_locus_string <- paste0(viewer_strings[1], ":", humanize::number_as_comma(as.numeric(viewer_strings[2])),
        "-", humanize::number_as_comma(as.numeric(viewer_strings[3])), "\n(", humanize::number_as_comma(as.numeric(viewer_strings[3]) -
            as.numeric(viewer_strings[2])), " bp)")
    return(pretty_locus_string)
}

# default view is this is top track and the locus zoom trace is below
geom_single_chrom_ideogrom <- function(data = NULL, cytoband_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
    flank = 0, zoom_trace_fill_color = "#FFA319") {

    # TODO: this requies loading from scratch each time, too slow for now, let
    # user load GTF as GRanges obj anyway if(!is.null(data)) { # Quickset the
    # data like a regular ggplot geom genes_gtf <- data } else if(is.null(data)
    # & !is.null(gtf_file)) { # Read in the GTF data genes_gtf <-
    # devgru::read_gtf_file(gtf_file_path = gtf_file) }

    # First subset to the chromosome of WoI
    coi <- stringr::str_split(string = window_of_interest, pattern = ":")[[1]][1]
    coi_query <- hg38_ref_cytobands %Q% (seqnames == coi)

    # Generate name of view Chr CHROM (STARTCYTO - ENDCYTO)
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)
    cytoband_overlap <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = devgru::gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank), subject = hg38_ref_cytobands, scol = "name")
    # Account for small regions i.e. only 1 name and for large regions, i.e.
    # many names per cytoband covered
    if (length(cytoband_overlap) == 1) {
        cytoband_text <- cytoband_overlap$name[which.min(GenomicRanges::start(cytoband_overlap))]

    } else if (length(cytoband_overlap) > 1) {
        cytoband_text <- paste0(cytoband_overlap$name[which.min(GenomicRanges::start(cytoband_overlap))],
            "-", cytoband_overlap$name[which.max(GenomicRanges::end(cytoband_overlap))])
    }
    # Combine chromosome name and cytoband text
    ideogram_title <- stringr::str_replace(string = locus_x_axis$name, pattern = "\\(.*\\)",
        replacement = paste0("(", cytoband_text, ")"))

    # Build the base single chromosome ideogram
    ideogram_base <- ggplot2::ggplot() + ggbio::layout_karyogram(data = coi_query,
        cytobands = T, geom = NULL) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(position = "top",
        labels = scales::label_comma(accuracy = 1, scale = 0.000001, suffix = " Mb"),
        expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::labs(title = ideogram_title) + ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text.y = element_blank(), plot.title = element_text(size = 12,
            hjust = 0.5), axis.line = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
        axis.ticks.length.x = unit(2, units = "mm"), plot.margin = unit(c(3.5, 3.5,
            0.05, 3.5), "mm"))

    # Add locus highlight and zoom trace to WoI plus flank on both ends of
    # genomic interval of WoI
    locus_zoom_coordinates <- c(locus_x_axis$limits[1], 0, max(GenomicRanges::end(coi_query)),
        locus_x_axis$limits[2])

    gg_ideogram_track <- ideogram_base + ggplot2::geom_polygon(ggplot2::aes(x = locus_zoom_coordinates,
        y = c(-2, -10, -10, -2)), fill = ggplot2::alpha(colour = zoom_trace_fill_color,
        alpha = 0.5)) + ggplot2::geom_rect(ggplot2::aes(xmin = locus_x_axis$limits[1],
        xmax = locus_x_axis$limits[2], ymin = -2, ymax = 10), fill = ggplot2::alpha(colour = zoom_trace_fill_color,
        alpha = 0.95))

    # Output plot
    return(gg_ideogram_track)
}

# adds slim bar of window coordinates and scale
geom_locus_scale <- function(window_of_interest = NULL, flank = 0) {
    # Get the axis of the locus view
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)

    # use this to make segment plot By default this will have the scale on the
    # far right and will be scaled based on width of view
    loi_query <- devgru::gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)

    # Find the scale and appropriate abbrevation to use from total window width
    locus_scale <- dplyr::case_when(dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 50, right = 200) ~ 10, dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 201, right = 1000) ~ 40, dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 1001, right = 5000) ~ 200, dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 5001, right = 25000) ~ 1000, dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 25001, right = 100000) ~ 5000, dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 100001, right = 500000) ~ 15000, dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 500001, right = 1000000) ~ 50000, dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 1000001, right = 5000000) ~ 250000, dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 5000001, right = 20000000) ~ 500000, dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 20000001, right = 100000000) ~ 2500000, dplyr::between(x = GenomicRanges::width(loi_query),
        left = 100000001, right = 200000000) ~ 5000000)

    locus_scale_abbrv <- dplyr::case_when(locus_scale %in% c(10, 40, 200) ~ paste0(locus_scale,
        " bp"), locus_scale %in% c(1000, 5000, 15000, 50000, 250000, 500000) ~ paste0(locus_scale/1000,
        " kb"), locus_scale %in% c(2500000, 5000000) ~ paste0(locus_scale/1000000,
        " Mb"))

    # Build the locus scale track Base plot will be barbell plot from
    # right-hand end of the WoI
    scale_base <- ggplot2::ggplot() + ggplot2::geom_segment(ggplot2::aes(x = GenomicRanges::end(loi_query) -
        locus_scale, xend = GenomicRanges::end(loi_query) - locus_scale, y = 1.2,
        yend = 1.8), linewidth = 0.8) + ggplot2::geom_segment(ggplot2::aes(x = GenomicRanges::end(loi_query),
        xend = GenomicRanges::end(loi_query), y = 1.2, yend = 1.8), linewidth = 0.8) +
        ggplot2::geom_segment(data = gr2dt(loi_query), ggplot2::aes(x = GenomicRanges::end(loi_query) -
            locus_scale, xend = end, y = 1.5, yend = 1.5), linewidth = 0.8)

    # Add text annotations
    gg_scale_track <- scale_base + ggplot2::geom_text(ggplot2::aes(x = GenomicRanges::end(loi_query) -
        locus_scale, y = 1.5, label = locus_scale_abbrv), size = 3.4, nudge_x = locus_scale/2,
        nudge_y = 0.8) + ggplot2::geom_text(ggplot2::aes(x = GenomicRanges::start(loi_query),
        y = 1.5, label = locus_viewer_string(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)),
        hjust = -0.03, size = 3.5) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name,
        labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,
            0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 3), expand = c(0.02,
        0.02)) + theme(axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3), axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0.05, 0.05, 0.05, 0.05), "mm"))

    # Output plot
    return(gg_scale_track)
}

geom_gene_track <- function(data = NULL, gtf_file = NULL, genes_of_interest, window_of_interest = NULL,
    flank = 0, exon_fill_color = "darkred", gene_name_side = "five_prime", gene_label_nudge = 100,
    gene_label_size = 4, x_axis_theme = "full") {

    # TODO: this requies loading from scratch each time, too slow for now, let
    # user load GTF as GRanges obj anyway if(!is.null(data)) { # Quickset the
    # data like a regular ggplot geom genes_gtf <- data } else if(is.null(data)
    # & !is.null(gtf_file)) { # Read in the GTF data genes_gtf <-
    # devgru::read_gtf_file(gtf_file_path = gtf_file) }

    # TODO: add sanity check of genes of interest

    # First subset to gene transcript structure records for query and then
    # subset to window of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic interval
    # of WoI
    goi_query <- complete_gene_segments_gr %Q% (gene_name %in% c(genes_of_interest)) %&%
        (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)

    # Restrict the beginning and end of the GoI window if the genes extend out
    # of the window If GoI start is less than the WoI start (i.e.
    # out-of-bounds), rrestrict start
    if (sum(GenomicRanges::start(goi_query) < GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)) > 0) {
        goi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = goi_query, start = GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
            flank))
    }
    # If GoI end is greater than the WoI end (i.e. out-of-bounds), restrict end
    if (sum(GenomicRanges::end(goi_query) > GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)) > 0) {
        goi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = goi_query, end = GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
            flank))
    }

    # Convert GoI query GR to DT
    goi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(goi_query)

    # Recode GoI strand orientation for gggenes
    goi_query_dt$orientation <- dplyr::case_when(goi_query_dt$strand == "+" ~ 1,
        goi_query_dt$strand == "-" ~ 0)

    # Set the aesthetic of strand orientation arrows
    intron_arrows <- grid::arrow(type = "open", angle = 45, length = unit(x = 3,
        units = "mm"))

    # Dynamic set of color for gene, default same color, can provide user set
    # and also a random set equal to number of genes
    if (exon_fill_color == "random") {
        exon_fill_color <- randomcoloR::distinctColorPalette(k = length(genes_of_interest))
    }

    # Build gene track plot Starting with gene base - exons and UTRs
    gene_base <- ggplot2::ggplot() + gggenes::geom_gene_arrow(data = goi_query_dt %>%
        dplyr::filter(segment == "exon"), ggplot2::aes(xmin = start, xmax = end,
        y = gene_name, fill = gene_name, forward = orientation), fill = exon_fill_color,
        color = exon_fill_color, arrowhead_height = unit(9, "mm"), arrowhead_width = unit(7,
            "mm"), arrow_body_height = unit(7, "mm")) + ggplot2::geom_segment(data = goi_query_dt %>%
        dplyr::filter(segment %in% c("three_prime_utr", "five_prime_utr")), ggplot2::aes(x = start,
        xend = end, y = gene_name, yend = gene_name), linewidth = 5)

    # Introns To handle multiple genes with different strand orientation, add
    # each orientation type in separate layers Start by pulling down the intron
    # segments
    intron_coords <- goi_query_dt %>%
        dplyr::filter(segment == "intron")

    # First check if there are single or mixed orientations If single, only
    # need to add one intron arrow layer
    if (length(unique(goi_query_dt$orientation)) == 1) {
        # Check for positive strand orientation (rightward facing)
        if (unique(goi_query_dt$orientation) == 1) {
            full_gene <- gene_base + ggarchery::geom_arrowsegment(data = intron_coords,
                ggplot2::aes(x = start, xend = end, y = gene_name, yend = gene_name),
                linewidth = 0.7, arrow_positions = 0.5, arrows = intron_arrows)

            # Check for negative strand orientation (leftward facing)
        } else if (unique(goi_query_dt$orientation) == 0) {
            full_gene <- gene_base + ggarchery::geom_arrowsegment(data = intron_coords,
                ggplot2::aes(x = end, xend = start, y = gene_name, yend = gene_name),
                linewidth = 0.7, arrow_positions = 0.5, arrows = intron_arrows)
        }

        # If mixed orientation, need to add two separate intron arrow layers
    } else if (length(unique(goi_query_dt$orientation)) == 2) {
        # Add positive strand orientation (rightward facing)
        forward_gene <- gene_base + ggarchery::geom_arrowsegment(data = intron_coords %>%
            dplyr::filter(orientation == 1), ggplot2::aes(x = start, xend = end,
            y = gene_name, yend = gene_name), linewidth = 0.7, arrow_positions = 0.5,
            arrows = intron_arrows)
        # Then add the negative strand orientation (leftward facing)
        full_gene <- forward_gene + ggarchery::geom_arrowsegment(data = intron_coords %>%
            dplyr::filter(orientation == 0), ggplot2::aes(x = end, xend = start,
            y = gene_name, yend = gene_name), linewidth = 0.7, arrow_positions = 0.5,
            arrows = intron_arrows)

    }

    # Annotate with gene names 5 prime vs 3 prime end To handle multiple genes
    # with both a default location for the gene name and for user-defined
    # location, link each gene of interest within the WoI with the gene name
    # side and orientation

    # First, check if there is a single side to use or if user provided set of
    # different sides to place gene names TODO: need to add sanity check that
    # if more than 1 side provided, must match length of GoI
    if (length(gene_name_side) == 1 | length(gene_name_side) == length(genes_of_interest)) {
        # Start with creating simple DT of label and y-axis discrete location
        # (same as gene name)
        gene_name_text <- data.table(label = genes_of_interest, y_axis = genes_of_interest,
            side_of_anno = paste0(gene_name_side, "_utr")) %>%
            dplyr::left_join(y = goi_query_dt %>%
                dplyr::rename(label = gene_name, side_of_anno = segment), by = c("label",
                "side_of_anno")) %>%
            dplyr::select(label, start, end, y_axis, side_of_anno, orientation)
        annotated_gene <- full_gene

        # Add each layer depending on gene name side + orientation type
        # combination Five prime side for positive strand orientation
        # (rightward facing)
        if ("five_prime_utr" %in% gene_name_text$side_of_anno & 1 %in% gene_name_text$orientation) {
            annotated_gene <- annotated_gene + geom_text(data = gene_name_text %>%
                dplyr::filter(side_of_anno == "five_prime_utr" & orientation == 1),
                aes(x = start, y = y_axis, label = label), fontface = "italic", size = gene_label_size,
                nudge_x = -gene_label_nudge)
        }

        # Five prime side for negative strand orientation (leftward facing)
        if ("five_prime_utr" %in% gene_name_text$side_of_anno & 0 %in% gene_name_text$orientation) {
            annotated_gene <- annotated_gene + geom_text(data = gene_name_text %>%
                dplyr::filter(side_of_anno == "five_prime_utr" & orientation == 0),
                aes(x = end, y = y_axis, label = label), fontface = "italic", size = gene_label_size,
                nudge_x = gene_label_nudge)
        }

        # Three prime side for positive strand orientation (rightward facing)
        if ("three_prime_utr" %in% gene_name_text$side_of_anno & 1 %in% gene_name_text$orientation) {
            annotated_gene <- annotated_gene + geom_text(data = gene_name_text %>%
                dplyr::filter(side_of_anno == "three_prime_utr" & orientation ==
                  1), aes(x = end, y = y_axis, label = label), fontface = "italic",
                size = gene_label_size, nudge_x = gene_label_nudge)
        }

        # Three prime side for negative strand orientation (leftward facing)
        if ("three_prime_utr" %in% gene_name_text$side_of_anno & 0 %in% gene_name_text$orientation) {
            annotated_gene <- annotated_gene + geom_text(data = gene_name_text %>%
                dplyr::filter(side_of_anno == "three_prime_utr" & orientation ==
                  0), aes(x = start, y = y_axis, label = label), fontface = "italic",
                size = gene_label_size, nudge_x = -gene_label_nudge)
        }
    }

    # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)

    # Build the track
    gg_gene_track <- annotated_gene + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name,
        labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,
            0.01)) + ggplot2::labs(y = "Genes") + ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "none",
        panel.background = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5), axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), plot.margin = unit(c(0.1, 5, 3, 5), "mm"))

    # Add x-axis theme
    if (x_axis_theme == "full") {
        gg_gene_track <- gg_gene_track + theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
        gg_gene_track <- gg_gene_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "blank") {
        gg_gene_track <- gg_gene_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
    }

    # Output plot
    return(gg_gene_track)
}

geom_bed_track <- function(data = NULL, bed_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = NULL, aes_fill_column = NULL, legend_title = NULL,
    aes_fill_colors = NULL, tornado_sort = F, bar_height = 5, x_axis_theme = "full") {
    # TODO: multiple forms of input, like point to diff file types and use
    # readers (VCF, MAF, etc)

    # TODO: this requies loading from scratch each time, too slow for now, let
    # user load GTF as GRanges obj anyway if(!is.null(data)) { # Quickset the
    # data like a regular ggplot geom bed_gr <- data } else if(is.null(data) &
    # !is.null(bed_file)) { # Read in the GTF data bed_gr <-
    # devgru::read_bed_file(gtf_file_path = bed_file) }

    # First, subset to region of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic
    # interval of WoI
    roi_query <- data %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)

    # Restrict the beginning and end of the GoI window if the genes extend out
    # of the window If GoI start is less than the WoI start (i.e.
    # out-of-bounds), rrestrict start
    if (sum(GenomicRanges::start(roi_query) < GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)) > 0) {
        roi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = roi_query, start = GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
            flank))
    }
    # If GoI end is greater than the WoI end (i.e. out-of-bounds), restrict end
    if (sum(GenomicRanges::end(roi_query) > GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)) > 0) {
        roi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = roi_query, end = GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
            flank))
    }

    # Convert RoI query GR to DT
    roi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(roi_query)

    # If there are many regions around specific locus, sort them by size with
    # largest at top of track and smallest at bottom, like a tornado
    if (tornado_sort) {
        tornado_sort_dt <- roi_query_dt %>%
            dplyr::arrange(width)
        tornado_sort_dt$sample <- factor(tornado_sort_dt$sample, levels = unique(tornado_sort_dt$sample))
        plot_dt <- tornado_sort_dt
    } else {
        plot_dt <- roi_query_dt
    }

    # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)

    # Set the colors if not provided or no fill column
    if (!is.null(aes_fill_column) & !is.null(aes_fill_colors)) {
        region_fill_colors <- aes_fill_colors
    } else if (!is.null(aes_fill_column) & is.null(aes_fill_colors)) {
        # If colors not specified, set them here
        region_fill_colors <- randomcoloR::distinctColorPalette(k = plot_dt %>%
            dplyr::select(eval(aes_fill_column)) %>%
            dplyr::n_distinct())
    }

    # If provided, color bars by some grouping variable
    if (!is.null(aes_fill_column)) {
        gg_region_track <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = plot_dt, aes(xmin = start, xmax = end,
            y = .data[[aes_y_column]], fill = .data[[aes_fill_column]], color = .data[[aes_fill_column]])) +
            gggenes::geom_gene_arrow(arrow_body_height = unit(bar_height, "mm"),
                arrowhead_height = unit(bar_height, "mm"), arrowhead_width = unit(0,
                  "mm")) + ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(name = eval(legend_title),
            values = region_fill_colors) + ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(guide = "none",
            values = region_fill_colors) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name,
            labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,
                0.01)) + ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
            axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 1.25,
                color = "black"), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"),
            plot.margin = unit(c(0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"))

    } else if (is.null(aes_fill_column)) {
        gg_region_track <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = plot_dt, aes(xmin = start, xmax = end,
            y = .data[[aes_y_column]])) + gggenes::geom_gene_arrow(arrow_body_height = unit(bar_height,
            "mm"), arrowhead_height = unit(bar_height, "mm"), arrowhead_width = unit(0,
            "mm")) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name, labels = locus_x_axis$labels,
            limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
            axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5,
                angle = 0, vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 1.25,
                color = "black"), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"),
            plot.margin = unit(c(0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"))
    }

    # Add x-axis theme
    if (x_axis_theme == "full") {
        gg_region_track <- gg_region_track + theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
        gg_region_track <- gg_region_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "blank") {
        gg_region_track <- gg_region_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
    }

    # Output plot
    return(gg_region_track)
}

# TODO: incorporate this to plotting tracks Remove first tick mark from y axis
# https://stackoverflow.com/questions/66279940/remove-first-label-of-y-axis
breaks_minus_one <- function(x) {
    x <- scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)
    x[x <= 0] <- NA
    x
}

geom_count_mutations <- function(data = NULL, maf_file = NULL, vcf_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column, aes_fill_color = "gray70", x_axis_theme = "full") {
    # TODO: multiple forms of input, like point to diff file types and use
    # readers (VCF, MAF, etc)

    # First, subset to mutations of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic
    # interval of WoI
    moi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)

    # Convert RoI query GR to DT
    moi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(moi_query)

    # Count the number of mutations at single locus using chromosome, start
    # site, and reference allele
    moi_query_counts <- moi_query_dt %>%
        dplyr::group_by(seqnames, start, .data[[aes_y_column]]) %>%
        dplyr::count()

    # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)

    # Create plot
    gg_mutation_count_track <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = moi_query_counts) + ggplot2::geom_segment(aes(x = start,
        xend = start, y = 0, yend = n), color = "gray10", alpha = 0.65, linewidth = 0.5) +
        ggplot2::geom_point(aes(x = start, y = n, size = n), shape = 21, fill = aes_fill_color,
            color = aes_fill_color) + ggplot2::scale_size(guide = "none") + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name,
        labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,
            0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 1),
        limits = c(0, max(moi_query_counts$n) + 1), expand = c(0.03, 0.03), breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) +
        ggplot2::labs(y = "Mutation\nCount") + ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5,
            angle = 0, vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 1.25,
            color = "black"), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"), plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,
            0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"))

    # Add x-axis theme
    if (x_axis_theme == "full") {
        gg_mutation_count_track <- gg_mutation_count_track + theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
        gg_mutation_count_track <- gg_mutation_count_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "blank") {
        gg_mutation_count_track <- gg_mutation_count_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
    }

    # Output plot
    return(gg_mutation_count_track)
}

# columns exepect within muts file: start, VAF, seqnames
geom_vaf_landscape <- function(data = NULL, mut_file = NULL, vcf_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
    flank = 0, point_size = 3, contrast = T, x_axis_theme = "full") {
    # TODO: multiple forms of input, like point to diff file types and use
    # readers (VCF, MAF, etc)

    # First, subset to mutations of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic
    # interval of WoI
    moi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)

    # Convert RoI query GR to DT
    moi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(moi_query)

    # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)

    # Create plot
    gg_vaf_track <- ggplot2::ggplot(moi_query_dt) + ggpointdensity::stat_pointdensity(aes(x = start,
        y = VAF), method = "kde2d", size = point_size) + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density",
        colours = c(paletteer::paletteer_c(palette = "grDevices::Lajolla", n = 15))) +
        ggplot2::labs(y = "VAF") + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name,
        labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,
            0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0.01,
        0.01), breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) + ggplot2::theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 1.25,
            color = "black"), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"), plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,
            0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"))

    # Add background
    if (contrast == T) {
        gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track + theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray50"))

    } else if (contrast == F) {
        gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track + theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA))
    }

    # Add x-axis theme
    if (x_axis_theme == "full") {
        gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track + theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
        gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "blank") {
        gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
    }

    # Output plot
    return(gg_vaf_track)
}

geom_epilogo_col <- function(data = NULL, epilogo_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
    flank = 0, x_axis_theme = "full", contrast = T) {
    # TODO: # For alternate route of reading files from scratch and not already
    # as obj mm_epilogos <- aggregate_these(path_to_files =
    # '~/morganLab/mgp1000/development/nonCoding/epiLogo/results/',
    # pattern_to_grab = '*_mm_epilogos.*.txt.gz', delim = '\t', has_header = F)
    # chromhmm_metadata <- fread(file = 'nonCoding/epiLogo/metadata.tsv', sep =
    # '\t', header = T) colnames(mm_epilogos) <- c('chrom', 'start', 'end',
    # chromhmm_metadata$long_name)

    # First, subset to epilogos of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic
    # interval of WoI
    eoi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)

    # Restrict the beginning and end of the GoI window if the genes extend out
    # of the window If GoI start is less than the WoI start (i.e.
    # out-of-bounds), rrestrict start
    if (sum(GenomicRanges::start(eoi_query) < GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)) > 0) {
        eoi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = eoi_query, start = GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
            flank))
    }
    # If GoI end is greater than the WoI end (i.e. out-of-bounds), restrict end
    if (sum(GenomicRanges::end(eoi_query) > GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)) > 0) {
        eoi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = eoi_query, end = GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
            flank))
    }

    # Get midpoint of EoI query GR ranges then convert to DT
    eoi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(gUtils::gr.mid(eoi_query))

    # Melt the EoI DT
    eoi_query_melt <- data.table::melt(eoi_query_dt, id.vars = c("seqnames", "start",
        "end"), measure.vars = colnames(eoi_query_dt)[6:length(colnames(eoi_query_dt))],
        variable.name = "chromhmm_state", variable.factor = T, value.name = "bits",
        value.factor = F)

    # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)

    # Remove interactions that are outside the window
    eoi_query_melt_bound <- eoi_query_melt %>%
        dplyr::filter(start > locus_x_axis$limits[1] & end < locus_x_axis$limits[2])

    # Order the ChromHMM states
    eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state <- stringr::str_replace_all(string = eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state,
        pattern = "_", replacement = " ")
    eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state <- factor(eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state,
        levels = rev(c("Heterochromatin Low Signal", "H3K27me3 Repressed", "H3K9me3 Repressed",
            "Weak Transcription", "Transcription Elongation", "Transcription Transition",
            "Weak Enhancer", "Strong Enhancer2", "Strong Enhancer1", "Poised Promoter",
            "Weak Promoter", "Active Promoter")))

    # Color blind friendly
    chromhmm_state_colors <- c("#ff6db6", "#ffb6db", "#b66dff", "#920000", "#924900",
        "#db6d00", "#006ddb", "#6db6ff", "#b6dbff", "#009292", "#004949", "#000000")

    # c('#260F99','#6551CC','#BFB2FF', '#0F6B99','#51A3CC','#B2E5FF',
    # '#6B990F','#A3CC51','#E5FFB2', '#99540F','#CC8E51','#FFD8B2')

    # Create plot
    gg_epilogo_track <- ggplot(eoi_query_melt_bound) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = start,
        y = bits, fill = chromhmm_state), na.rm = T, width = 200) + ggplot2::labs(y = "Epilogos") +
        scale_fill_manual(name = "ChromHMM\nStates", values = chromhmm_state_colors) +
