57.1 (12)

60.5 (52)

59.8 (98)

56.4 (22)

Hyperdiploidy

 yes

36.8 (7)

53 (44)

44.9 (70)

59 (23)

 no

63.2 (12)

47 (39)

55.1 (86)

41 (16)

Summary table that details key genomic and demographic metadata variables for all patients stratified by disease stage. Admixture cluster refers to the assignment of each patient to a continental ancestry grouping inferred from genomic ancestry. Age is describe using the median with the range in parenthesis. The variables sex, admixture cluster, self-identification, IgH translocation, and hyperdiploidy are described using the percentage of patients per disease stage with raw count in parenthesis. MGUS, monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance; SMM, smoldering multiple myeloma; MM, multiple myeloma; RRMM, relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma; EUR, European ancestry; AFR, African ancestry; AMR, American ancestry; EAS, East Asian ancestry; SAS, South Asian ancestry.

4 Identification of SNV and InDel enrichment, Part 1

Let’s analyze the mutations

Code 
# Read in genes
genes <- get_genes_shortcut(gtf_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.108.gtf.gz"))

# Read in dataset-wide MAF file including all variants (no filtering based on
# consequence, which happens with maftools::read.maf)
muts <- read_maf_file(maf_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "mgp1000.hq.union.consensus.somatic.snv.indel.maf.gz"))
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns
muts$VAF <- muts$t_alt_count/muts$t_depth

# Gather high-coverage, high-quality samples
muts_in_hq_patients <- muts %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample))

# Collapse all mutations per patient, remove any duplicated mutations (i.e.
# shared across timepoints) Column 13 is Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode
muts_hq <- muts_in_hq_patients[!duplicated(gUtils::gr2dt(muts_in_hq_patients[, 13]))]

# Separate SNVs and InDels
snv_muts_hq <- muts_hq %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV")
indel_muts_hq <- muts_hq %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("deletion", "insertion"))

# How many SNVs and InDels before removing Silent mutations
print("Total SNVs and InDels before filtering out silent mutations ...")
## [1] "Total SNVs and InDels before filtering out silent mutations ..."
length(snv_muts_hq)
## [1] 1957997
length(indel_muts_hq)
## [1] 480318

# Convert mutations to DT
snv_muts_hq_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(snv_muts_hq)
indel_muts_hq_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(indel_muts_hq)

# print('SNVs per sample ... ') snv_muts_hq_dt %>%
# dplyr::group_by(Tumor_Sample_Barcode) %>% dplyr::count(name = 'total_snvs')
# %>% dplyr::ungroup() %>% dplyr::reframe('median_snvs_per_sample' =
# median(total_snvs), 'range_snvs' = range(total_snvs)) print('Median InDels
# per sample ... ') indel_muts_hq_dt %>% dplyr::group_by(Tumor_Sample_Barcode)
# %>% dplyr::count(name = 'total_indels') %>% dplyr::ungroup() %>%
# dplyr::reframe('median_indels_per_sample' = median(total_indels),
# 'range_indels' = range(total_indels))

# simplify mut DT for plotting downstream
snv_muts_hq_dt_for_plot <- snv_muts_hq_dt[, .(seqnames, start, end, Hugo_Symbol,
    Variant_Classification, Variant_Type, Consequence, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2,
    VAF, Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode)]
indel_muts_hq_dt_for_plot <- indel_muts_hq_dt[, .(seqnames, start, end, Hugo_Symbol,
    Variant_Classification, Variant_Type, Consequence, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2,
    VAF, Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode)]

# Add metadata to mut DT
snv_muts_hq_dt_for_plot <- snv_muts_hq_dt_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata, by = c("sample", "patient"))

indel_muts_hq_dt_for_plot <- indel_muts_hq_dt_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata, by = c("sample", "patient"))

muts_hq_dt_for_plot <- gUtils::rrbind(snv_muts_hq_dt_for_plot, indel_muts_hq_dt_for_plot)

5 The landscape of the non-coding genome in myeloma is characterized by a discrete set of mutationally enriched regions, Part 1

We are searching the entire genome (ocean) for enrichment (trophy fish) beyond coding regions. Here, fishy fishy …

Code 
# Read in WGS calling BED
eligible_bed <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "wgs_calling_regions.hg38.bed"))
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

# total eligible size to start
message("Eligible genome size start: ", sum(GenomicRanges::width(eligible_bed)))
## Eligible genome size start: 2923745819

# First, to focus the analysis and remove potential confounders, specifically
# those due to low-complexity repeat regions, imbalance of calls on sex
# chromosomes, and immunoglobulin regions (functional and non-functional), the
# eligible region will be subset to remove these regions. This will properly
# reduce both the background regions to test against (eligible) and the
# hypothesis regions (tiles) before the run to instantiate the Fish obj

# WindowMasker/SDust low-complexity, repeat region filter BED
wm_sdust_mask_bed <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "windowMaskerSDust.hg38.bed.gz"),
    additional_col_names = "wm_sdust_id")
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns
# WM/SDust mask size
message("WM/SDust mask size start: ", sum(GenomicRanges::width(wm_sdust_mask_bed)))
## WM/SDust mask size start: 1074813116


# IG regions and genes filter BED
# (https://github.com/ferrannadeu/IgCaller/blob/master/IgCaller_reference_files/hg38/dicts/AllRegionsAndGenes_hg38.bed).
# This also includes non-functional IG genes
# (https://www.genenames.org/data/genegroup/#!/group/350)
ig_region_mask_bed <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "igFunctionalAndNonFunctionalGenesAndRegions.hg38.bed"),
    additional_col_names = "ig_region_id")
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns
# Add 250 padding to both ends of IG regions
ig_region_mask_bed <- ig_region_mask_bed + 250
# IG regions mask size
message("IG regions mask size start: ", sum(GenomicRanges::width(ig_region_mask_bed)))
## IG regions mask size start: 3994620

# Reduce the background eligible BED by the WM/SDust mask, sex chromosomes, IG
# regions, and exons
eligible_bed <- eligible_bed %_% wm_sdust_mask_bed
eligible_bed <- eligible_bed %_% ig_region_mask_bed
eligible_bed <- eligible_bed %Q% (!seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY"))
# final eligible size
message("Final eligible genome size end :", sum(GenomicRanges::width(eligible_bed)))
## Final eligible genome size end :1760671127


# # For WGS, the eligible region is a set of 10kbp tiled windows where each
# successive window is shifted # 500bp of the previous (i.e. each tile 10kbp
# window overlaps the next by 9.5kbp) tiles_set_init <- gUtils::gr.tile(gr =
# seqinfo(eligible_bed), width = 10000) # Since we want to shift the 10kbp
# tiles by 500 each set, we need [(10000 / 500) - 1] number of steps (i.e.
# window / shift - 1) # As the initiating set of tiles will be the same as the
# 20th set tile_set <- GenomicRanges::GRangesList(tiles_set_init) for(i in
# 1:19) { # First, add 500bp to each side of the tile (shifting it) and trim
# the ends to ensure they don't # exceed the seqlength tiles_step <-
# suppressWarnings(tile_set[[i]] %+% 500) tiles_step <-
# #GenomicRanges::trim(tiles_step) tile_set[[i+1]] <- tiles_step } #
# Concatenate the tile steps set into single set of tiles, and fix seqinfo
# final_tiles <-
# GenomicRanges::sort.GenomicRanges(gUtils::grl.unlist(tile_set)) final_tiles
# <- gr_refactor_seqs(input_gr = final_tiles) # Remove any tiles less than
# 10kbp final_tiles <- final_tiles[!final_tiles@ranges@width < 10000,] #
# Annotate each tile with the nearest gene # TODO: gather annotations for
# super-enhancers, B-cell TF binding sites, etc. as these can be added to #
# tile metadata in same fashion of genes final_tiles$nearest_gene <-
# genes$gene_name[nearest(x = final_tiles, subject = gr.stripstrand(genes))]

final_tiles <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "genome_tiles.rds"))
# Collect bait (i.e. covariates) for the best model to catch the trophy fish
# (drivers)

# From Gajic et al Nat. Comm. 2022 Covariate set (“covariome”) to
# comprehensively capture the contribution of background genomic features to
# the neutral mutation density.  Replitome - replication timing across 96 cell
# lines, from Roadmap Epigenomics Project Encodome - 15 ChromHMM states across
# 127 cell lines and tissues, from ENCODE Seqome - sequence context (mono, di
# and tri), These are binned numeric values that were calculated using hg19
# data, they will be lifted over for this analysis

##################### Replication timing

# replication_timing_hg19 <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path,
# 'reptome_fish.rds')) # Original data is hg19 so let's liftOver liftchain <-
# rtracklayer::import.chain(paste0(base_data_path, 'hg19ToHg38.over.chain'))
# replication_timing_hg38 <- rtracklayer::liftOver(x =
# gUtils::gr.chr(replication_timing_hg19$data), chain = liftchain) # Unlist
# output to get GR replication_timing <-
# gUtils::grl.unlist(replication_timing_hg38) # Extract the covariate data GR
# and convert to good seqinfo replication_timing <- gr_refactor_seqs(input_gr =
# replication_timing) # Save copy of hg38 version of full reptome
# #saveRDS(object = replication_timing, file = 'reptome_fish.hg38.rds') Subset
# to top 10 PCs
replication_timing <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "reptome_fish.hg38.rds"))
replication_timing <- replication_timing[, 4:13]
replication_timing_covar <- Cov(data = replication_timing, field = colnames(GenomicRanges::mcols(x = replication_timing)))

################### Sequence context

# seq_context_hg19 <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, 'seqome_fish.rds')) #
# Original data is hg19 so let's liftOver seq_context_hg38 <-
# rtracklayer::liftOver(x = gUtils::gr.chr(seq_context_hg19$data), chain =
# liftchain) # Unlist output to get GR seq_context <-
# gUtils::grl.unlist(seq_context_hg38) # Extract the covariate data GR and
# convert to good seqinfo seq_context <- gr_refactor_seqs(input_gr =
# seq_context) # Save copy of hg38 version of full seqome #saveRDS(object =
# seq_context, file = 'seqome_fish.hg38.rds') Subset to top 10 PCs
seq_context <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "seqome_fish.hg38.rds"))
seq_context <- seq_context[, 4:13]
seq_context_covar <- Cov(data = seq_context, field = colnames(GenomicRanges::mcols(x = seq_context)))

################### ChromHMM states

# chrom_states_hg19 <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path,
# 'epigenetic_covariate_fish_top200.rds')) # Original data is hg19 so let's
# liftOver chrom_states_hg38 <- rtracklayer::liftOver(x =
# gUtils::gr.chr(chrom_states_hg19$data), chain = liftchain) # Unlist output to
# get GR chrom_states <- gUtils::grl.unlist(chrom_states_hg38) # Extract the
# covariate data GR and convert to good seqinfo chrom_states <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(input_gr = chrom_states) # Save copy of hg38 version of full
# encodome #saveRDS(object = chrom_states, file = 'encodome_fish.hg38.rds')
# Subset to top 10 PCs
chrom_states <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "encodome_fish.hg38.rds"))
chrom_states <- chrom_states[, 4:13]
chrom_states_covar <- Cov(data = chrom_states, field = colnames(GenomicRanges::mcols(x = chrom_states)))

# With the bait (covariates) loaded, now we can catch the trophy fish!
covars <- c(replication_timing_covar, chrom_states_covar, seq_context_covar)

# Creating an empty FishHook object covars_bait <- Fish(hypotheses =
# final_tiles, mc.cores = 1) covars_bait$merge(covars)

# export to HPC for run saveRDS(object = covars_bait, file = 'covars_bait.rds')
# saveRDS(object = final_tiles, file = 'genome_tiles.rds') saveRDS(object =
# eligible_bed, file = 'eligible_region.rds')

# Split events for various models Pan-Myeloma saveRDS(object = snv_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/panMyeloma/mgp1000_snv_mutations.rds') saveRDS(object =
# indel_fish, file = 'fishHookModels/panMyeloma/mgp1000_indel_mutations.rds')

# Separate SNVs and InDels to be used in fishHook non-coding analysis
snv_fish <- snv_muts_hq %Q% (Variant_Classification != "Silent")
indel_fish <- indel_muts_hq %Q% (Variant_Classification != "Silent")

# Disease Stage specific
mgus_snv_fish <- snv_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()))
mgus_indel_fish <- indel_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()))
# saveRDS(object = mgus_snv_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/diseaseStageSpecific/mgus_snv_mutations.rds') saveRDS(object
# = mgus_indel_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/diseaseStageSpecific/mgus_indel_mutations.rds')

smm_snv_fish <- snv_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "SMM") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()))
smm_indel_fish <- indel_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "SMM") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()))
# saveRDS(object = smm_snv_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/diseaseStageSpecific/smm_snv_mutations.rds') saveRDS(object =
# smm_indel_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/diseaseStageSpecific/smm_indel_mutations.rds')

mm_snv_fish <- snv_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MM") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()))
mm_indel_fish <- indel_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MM") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()))
# saveRDS(object = mm_snv_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/diseaseStageSpecific/mm_snv_mutations.rds') saveRDS(object =
# mm_indel_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/diseaseStageSpecific/mm_indel_mutations.rds')

rr_snv_fish <- snv_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "RRMM") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()))
rr_indel_fish <- indel_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "RRMM") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()))
# saveRDS(object = rr_snv_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/diseaseStageSpecific/rr_snv_mutations.rds') saveRDS(object =
# rr_indel_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/diseaseStageSpecific/rr_indel_mutations.rds')

# # Molecular Subtype specific t11_14_snv_fish <- snv_fish %Q%
# (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
# dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == 't(11;14)') %>% dplyr::select(sample) %>%
# purrr::as_vector())) t11_14_indel_fish <- indel_fish %Q%
# (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
# dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == 't(11;14)') %>% dplyr::select(sample) %>%
# purrr::as_vector())) #saveRDS(object = t11_14_snv_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/molecularSubtypeSpecific/t11_14_snv_mutations.rds')
# #saveRDS(object = t11_14_indel_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/molecularSubtypeSpecific/t11_14_indel_mutations.rds')
# t4_14_snv_fish <- snv_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in%
# unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == 't(4;14)')
# %>% dplyr::select(sample) %>% purrr::as_vector())) t4_14_indel_fish <-
# indel_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
# dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == 't(4;14)') %>% dplyr::select(sample) %>%
# purrr::as_vector())) #saveRDS(object = t4_14_snv_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/molecularSubtypeSpecific/t4_14_snv_mutations.rds')
# #saveRDS(object = t4_14_indel_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/molecularSubtypeSpecific/t4_14_indel_mutations.rds')
# no_trx_snv_fish <- snv_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in%
# unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == 'Not
# Detected') %>% dplyr::select(sample) %>% purrr::as_vector()))
# no_trx_indel_fish <- indel_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in%
# unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == 'Not
# Detected') %>% dplyr::select(sample) %>% purrr::as_vector())) #saveRDS(object
# = no_trx_snv_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/molecularSubtypeSpecific/no_trx_snv_mutations.rds')
# #saveRDS(object = no_trx_indel_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/molecularSubtypeSpecific/no_trx_indel_mutations.rds')

################### Let's go Fishing

# The following function represents the fishHook command that was executed
# externally on HPC See angler.R script angler <- function(fish_hypo,
# fish_muts, fish_eligible) { fishing_pole <- fishHook::Fish(hypotheses =
# fish_hypo, events = fish_muts, eligible = fish_eligible, idcol =
# 'Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode', use_local_mut_density = FALSE, nb = TRUE,
# mc.cores = 1) return(fishing_pole) }

Look at the TMB of the eligible genome and stratify by disease stage and genomic feature Some metrics on the non-coding genome (all regions)

Code 
# Correct the overall evaluated sites for same methodology as before, no Ig or
# sex chromosomes
snv_fish_final <- snv_fish[gr.in(query = snv_fish, subject = eligible_bed)]
snv_fish_ig <- snv_fish[gr.in(query = snv_fish, subject = ig_region_mask_bed)]

complete_snv_fish_per_sample <- left_join(x = gr2dt(gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_in_hq_patients %Q%
    (VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV"), subject = snv_fish_final, qcol = c("Reference_Allele",
    "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode"))) %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode),
    y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation,
            coverage_normal, coverage_tumor, purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id),
    by = c("patient", "sample")) %>%
    dplyr::select(-c(6, 7)) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()

indel_fish_final <- indel_fish[gr.in(query = indel_fish, subject = eligible_bed)]
indel_fish_ig <- indel_fish[gr.in(query = indel_fish, subject = ig_region_mask_bed)]

complete_indel_fish_per_sample <- left_join(x = gr2dt(gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_in_hq_patients %Q%
    (VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion")), subject = indel_fish_final,
    qcol = c("Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode"))) %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode),
    y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation,
            coverage_normal, coverage_tumor, purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id),
    by = c("patient", "sample")) %>%
    dplyr::select(-c(6, 7)) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()

message("Total unique SNVs and InDels after filtering out silent mutations ...")
## Total unique SNVs and InDels after filtering out silent mutations ...
message("Unique SNVs: ", length(snv_fish_final))
## Unique SNVs: 1053256
message("InDels: ", length(indel_fish_final))
## InDels: 72176
message("This is set of mutations used in fishHook model.")
## This is set of mutations used in fishHook model.

complete_snv_fish_metrics <- complete_snv_fish_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "total_snvs") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_snvs_per_sample = median(total_snvs), range_snvs = range(total_snvs))
message("median SNVs per sample: ", complete_snv_fish_metrics$median_snvs_per_sample[1])
## median SNVs per sample: 3304
message("range of SNVs per sample: ", complete_snv_fish_metrics$range_snvs[1], " to ",
    complete_snv_fish_metrics$range_snvs[2])
## range of SNVs per sample: 13 to 13095

complete_indel_fish_metrics <- complete_indel_fish_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "total_indels") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_indels_per_sample = median(total_indels), range_indels = range(total_indels))
message("median InDels per sample: ", complete_indel_fish_metrics$median_indels_per_sample[1])
## median InDels per sample: 216
message("range of InDels per sample: ", complete_indel_fish_metrics$range_indels[1],
    " to ", complete_indel_fish_metrics$range_indels[2])
## range of InDels per sample: 8 to 1143

tmb_fish_eligbile <- rrbind(complete_snv_fish_per_sample, complete_indel_fish_per_sample) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(age)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation, coverage_normal,
        coverage_tumor, purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "total_mutation_count") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::filter(total_mutation_count > 1000) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(tumor_mutation_burden = total_mutation_count/(sum(GenomicRanges::width(eligible_bed))/1000000))
tmb_fish_eligbile$age <- as.numeric(tmb_fish_eligbile$age)

# Correct the TMB by age by fitting a linear model
message("Testing linear regression of TMB in high-confidence genome and demographic / molecular covariates")
## Testing linear regression of TMB in high-confidence genome and demographic / molecular covariates
test_tmb_lm <- lm(data = tmb_fish_eligbile, formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ age +
    igh_translocation + purity + ploidy)
summary(test_tmb_lm)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ age + igh_translocation + 
##     purity + ploidy, data = tmb_fish_eligbile)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.2191 -0.6251 -0.2178  0.4379  6.0832 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                Estimate Std. Error t value   Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                   -0.195010   0.606929  -0.321   0.748199    
## age                            0.010861   0.005913   1.837   0.067227 .  
## igh_translocationt(4;14)       0.691811   0.199557   3.467   0.000602 ***
## igh_translocationt(14;16)      0.811776   0.604795   1.342   0.180519    
## igh_translocationt(14;20)      0.255997   0.346914   0.738   0.461125    
## igh_translocationt(6;14)      -0.462020   0.474720  -0.973   0.331202    
## igh_translocationt(8;14)       0.518958   0.413404   1.255   0.210321    
## igh_translocationNot Detected  0.245714   0.154080   1.595   0.111811    
## purity                         1.162476   0.254253   4.572 0.00000703 ***
## ploidy                         0.345574   0.211603   1.633   0.103475    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 1.023 on 305 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.137,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.1115 
## F-statistic:  5.38 on 9 and 305 DF,  p-value: 0.0000007633

# tmb_lm <- lm(data = tmb_fish_eligbile %>% dplyr::mutate('t4_14' =
# case_when(igh_translocation == 't(4;14)' ~ 1, igh_translocation != 't(4;14)'
# ~ 0), 'male' = case_when(sex == 'Male' ~ 1, sex != 'Male' ~ 0)), formula =
# tumor_mutation_burden ~ male + t4_14 + purity + ploidy)
message("Final linear regression model of TMB across disease stages")
## Final linear regression model of TMB across disease stages
tmb_lm <- lm(data = tmb_fish_eligbile %>%
    dplyr::mutate(t4_14 = case_when(igh_translocation == "t(4;14)" ~ 1, igh_translocation !=
        "t(4;14)" ~ 0)), formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ t4_14 + purity + ploidy)
summary(tmb_lm)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ t4_14 + purity + ploidy, 
##     data = tmb_fish_eligbile %>% dplyr::mutate(t4_14 = case_when(igh_translocation == 
##         "t(4;14)" ~ 1, igh_translocation != "t(4;14)" ~ 0)))
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.3954 -0.6885 -0.1769  0.4359  5.9443 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value   Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)   0.3187     0.4817   0.661    0.50880    
## t4_14         0.4787     0.1688   2.836    0.00486 ** 
## purity        1.1765     0.2517   4.675 0.00000439 ***
## ploidy        0.4851     0.1919   2.528    0.01198 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 1.027 on 311 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.112,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.1034 
## F-statistic: 13.08 on 3 and 311 DF,  p-value: 0.00000004598


# Calculate age-adjusted TMB using the residuals of the linear fitting
tmb_fish_eligbile$tumor_mutation_burden_corrected <- resid(tmb_lm) + mean(tmb_fish_eligbile$tumor_mutation_burden)

# ggplot(tmb_fish_eligbile, aes(x = age)) + geom_point(aes(y =
# tumor_mutation_burden, color = 'Raw TMB')) + geom_point(aes(y =
# tumor_mutation_burden_corrected, color = 'Corrected TMB'), shape = 16, alpha
# = 0.7) + scale_color_manual(values = c('Raw TMB' = 'blue', 'Corrected TMB' =
# 'red'))

message("Median TMB across all patients: ", median(tmb_fish_eligbile$tumor_mutation_burden_corrected,
    na.rm = T))
## Median TMB across all patients: 2.15861084543219

# Plot the overall TMB by disease stage
dx_median_tmb <- tmb_fish_eligbile %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_tmb = median(na.omit(tumor_mutation_burden_corrected)),
        median_age = median(na.omit(age)))

dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = droplevels(tmb_fish_eligbile %>%
    dplyr::select(disease_stage, tumor_mutation_burden_corrected)), formula = tumor_mutation_burden_corrected ~
    disease_stage, p.adjust.method = "fdr")
dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p.adj)

tmb_by_dx <- ggplot(data = tmb_fish_eligbile, aes(x = disease_stage, y = tumor_mutation_burden)) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5), xmax = c(1.5,
        2.5, 3.5, 4.5), ymin = 0.1, ymax = 100, alpha = 0.25, fill = disease_stage_colors) +
    ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = "compactswarm", corral = "wrap") + labs(y = "TMB (muts. per Mb)",
    x = NULL) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) + scale_y_log10(labels = c("0.1",
    "1.0", "10", "100"), breaks = c(0.1, 1, 10, 100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45,
    hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans",
    size = 12), axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15), panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment", x = c(0.75, 1.75, 2.75, 3.75), xend = c(1.25,
        2.25, 3.25, 4.25), y = dx_median_tmb$median_tmb, yend = dx_median_tmb$median_tmb,
        color = "purple2", linewidth = 2.25) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = c(1,
    2, 3, 4), y = 150, label = as.character(round(dx_median_tmb$median_tmb, digits = 2)),
    size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(4:6),
    ], label = "P={p_string}", y.position = c(1.28, 1.47, 1.69), tip.length = 0.008,
    label.size = 3.75, vjust = 0.1) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(1,
    2), ], label = "{p.adj.signif}", y.position = c(0.8, 1.06), tip.length = 0.008,
    label.size = 3.75, vjust = 0.1)

# Convert to simpler representation for mutation type, and take top consequence
# for each mutation if >1 given coding (in exon) vs non-conding (not in exon as
# by ensembl)
fish_muts_hq <- gr2dt(grbind(snv_fish_final, indel_fish_final)) %>%
    dplyr::rename(patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata, by = c("patient", "sample"))

fish_muts_hq <- fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::mutate(mut_type = case_when(Variant_Type == "SNP" ~ "SNVs", Variant_Type %in%
        c("INS", "DEL") ~ "InDels"), csq = stringr::str_replace_all(string = str_split(string = fish_muts_hq$Consequence,
        pattern = ",", simplify = T)[, 1], pattern = "_", replacement = " ")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(impact = case_when(csq %in% c("splice acceptor variant", "splice donor variant",
        "stop gained", "frameshift variant", "stop lost", "start lost") ~ "High",
        csq %in% c("inframe insertion", "inframe deletion", "missense variant", "protein altering variant") ~
            "Moderate", csq == "splice region variant" ~ "Low", csq %in% c("coding sequence variant",
            "mature miRNA variant", "5 prime UTR variant", "3 prime UTR variant",
            "non coding transcript exon variant", "intron variant", "upstream gene variant",
            "downstream gene variant", "TF binding site variant", "regulatory region variant",
            "intergenic variant") ~ "Modifier"), c_vs_nc = case_when(csq %in% c("splice acceptor variant",
        "splice donor variant", "protein altering variant", "splice region variant",
        "mature miRNA variant", "5 prime UTR variant", "3 prime UTR variant", "non coding transcript exon variant",
        "intron variant", "upstream gene variant", "downstream gene variant", "TF binding site variant",
        "regulatory region variant", "intergenic variant") ~ "Non-coding", csq %in%
        c("stop gained", "frameshift variant", "stop lost", "start lost", "inframe insertion",
            "inframe deletion", "missense variant", "coding sequence variant") ~
        "Coding"))

fish_muts_hq$age <- as.numeric(fish_muts_hq$age)

# reorder csq from hig to low
fish_muts_hq$csq <- factor(fish_muts_hq$csq, levels = c("splice acceptor variant",
    "splice donor variant", "stop gained", "frameshift variant", "stop lost", "start lost",
    "inframe insertion", "inframe deletion", "missense variant", "protein altering variant",
    "splice region variant", "coding sequence variant", "mature miRNA variant", "5 prime UTR variant",
    "3 prime UTR variant", "non coding transcript exon variant", "intron variant",
    "upstream gene variant", "downstream gene variant", "TF binding site variant",
    "regulatory region variant", "intergenic variant"))

# reorder impact
fish_muts_hq$impact <- factor(fish_muts_hq$impact, levels = c("High", "Moderate",
    "Low", "Modifier"))

# order coding vs non-coding
fish_muts_hq$c_vs_nc <- factor(fish_muts_hq$c_vs_nc, levels = c("Coding", "Non-coding"))

# Plot count per consequence
disease_stage_order <- c("MGUS", "SMM", "MM", "RRMM")

# SNVs and InDels together
csq_plot_list <- list()
impact_plot_list <- list()
for (i in 1:length(disease_stage_order)) {
    csq_plt <- ggplot(data = fish_muts_hq %>%
        dplyr::group_by(csq, .drop = F) %>%
        dplyr::filter(disease_stage == disease_stage_order[i]) %>%
        dplyr::count(.drop = FALSE)) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = csq, y = n, fill = csq)) +
        ggfittext::geom_bar_text(aes(x = csq, y = n, label = n), contrast = T) +
        labs(title = disease_stage_order[i], y = NULL) + scale_y_continuous(transform = "log1p",
        labels = scales::label_comma(accuracy = 1), breaks = c(1000, 10000, 100000,
            1000000)) + scale_x_discrete(breaks = c("splice acceptor variant", "splice donor variant",
        "stop gained", "frameshift variant", "stop lost", "start lost", "inframe insertion",
        "inframe deletion", "missense variant", "protein altering variant", "splice region variant",
        "coding sequence variant", "mature miRNA variant", "5 prime UTR variant",
        "3 prime UTR variant", "non coding transcript exon variant", "intron variant",
        "upstream gene variant", "downstream gene variant", "TF binding site variant",
        "regulatory region variant", "intergenic variant")) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Consequence",
        values = c(rev(paletteer_c("ggthemes::Purple", 8)[3:8]), rev(paletteer_c("ggthemes::Brown",
            6)[3:6]), "#317197", rev(paletteer_c("ggthemes::Classic Area Green",
            14)[4:14]))) + theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.title.x = element_blank(), plot.title = element_text(size = 13))
    # Add the bar plot
    csq_plot_list <- rlist::list.append(csq_plot_list, csq_plt)

    # now add the pie chart
    impact_pie <- ggpie::ggpie(data = fish_muts_hq %>%
        dplyr::filter(disease_stage == disease_stage_order[i]), group_key = "impact",
        count_type = "full", label_info = c("count", "ratio"), label_type = "horizon",
        label_size = 6, label_pos = "out", label_threshold = 50, label_color = c("#613C5F",
            "#752825", "#295f80", "#357300")) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Impact\nGroup",
        values = c("#613C5F", "#752825", "#295f80", "#357300")) + labs(title = disease_stage_order[i]) +
        theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16))
    # Add the bar plot
    impact_plot_list <- rlist::list.append(impact_plot_list, impact_pie)

}
## Warning in geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity", color = border_color, :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.
## Warning in geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity", color = border_color, :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.
## Warning in geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity", color = border_color, :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.
## Warning in geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity", color = border_color, :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

# Combine the Consequence plots per disease stage
csq_plot_by_dx <- ggarrange(plotlist = csq_plot_list, ncol = 1, nrow = 4, common.legend = T,
    legend = "bottom")
# annotate_figure(p = csq_plot_by_dx, left = text_grob('Mutations', size = 15,
# rot = 90, hjust = -0.5)) export 8.27 x 13 landscape

# Impact pie charts ggarrange(plotlist = impact_plot_list, ncol = 2, nrow = 2,
# common.legend = T, legend = 'bottom') export 6 x 6 portrait

# data split by coding vs non-coding
c_vs_nc_dt <- fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::group_by(c_vs_nc) %>%
    dplyr::count()

coding_vs_noncoding_barplot <- ggplot(data = c_vs_nc_dt) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = c_vs_nc,
    y = n, fill = c_vs_nc), color = "black") + ggfittext::geom_bar_text(aes(x = c_vs_nc,
    y = n, label = scales::comma(n)), contrast = F) + labs(y = "Mutations") + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.02,
    0.02), labels = scales::label_comma(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1250000)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = paletteer_d("ggsci::alternating_igv")) + theme(legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))
# export 6 x 6 portrait coding_vs_noncoding_barplot

message("Percentage of Non-Coding: ", round(c_vs_nc_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(c_vs_nc == "Non-coding") %>%
    dplyr::reframe(pct = (n/sum(c_vs_nc_dt$n)) * 100) %>%
    dplyr::select(pct) %>%
    as.numeric(), digits = 2))
## Percentage of Non-Coding: 98.99

message("Percentage of Coding: ", round(c_vs_nc_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(c_vs_nc == "Coding") %>%
    dplyr::reframe(pct = (n/sum(c_vs_nc_dt$n)) * 100) %>%
    dplyr::select(pct) %>%
    as.numeric(), digits = 2))
## Percentage of Coding: 1.01

# How many intron and intergenic variants are there
message("Percentage of Intronic Variants: ", round(fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq %in% c("splice acceptor variant", "splice donor variant", "splice region variant",
        "intron variant")) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::reframe(pct = (n/nrow(fish_muts_hq)) * 100) %>%
    dplyr::select(pct) %>%
    as.numeric(), digits = 2))
## Percentage of Intronic Variants: 50.97
message("Count of Intronic Variants: ", fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq %in% c("splice acceptor variant", "splice donor variant", "splice region variant",
        "intron variant")) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::select(n) %>%
    as.numeric())
## Count of Intronic Variants: 573596


message("Percentage of Intergenic Variants: ", round(fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq %in% c("upstream gene variant", "downstream gene variant",
        "TF binding site variant", "regulatory region variant", "intergenic variant")) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::reframe(pct = (n/nrow(fish_muts_hq)) * 100) %>%
    dplyr::select(pct) %>%
    as.numeric(), digits = 2))
## Percentage of Intergenic Variants: 45.93
message("Count of Intronic Variants: ", fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq %in% c("upstream gene variant", "downstream gene variant",
        "TF binding site variant", "regulatory region variant", "intergenic variant")) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::select(n) %>%
    as.numeric())
## Count of Intronic Variants: 516904

message("Percentage of 3' UTR Variants: ", round(fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq == "3 prime UTR variant") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::reframe(pct = (n/nrow(fish_muts_hq)) * 100) %>%
    dplyr::select(pct) %>%
    as.numeric(), digits = 2))
## Percentage of 3' UTR Variants: 0.85

message("Count of 3' UTR Variants: ", fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq == "3 prime UTR variant") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::select(n) %>%
    as.numeric())
## Count of 3' UTR Variants: 9549

message("Percentage of 5' UTR Variants: ", round(fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq == "5 prime UTR variant") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::reframe(pct = (n/nrow(fish_muts_hq)) * 100) %>%
    dplyr::select(pct) %>%
    as.numeric(), digits = 2))
## Percentage of 5' UTR Variants: 0.18

message("Count of 5' UTR Variants: ", fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq == "5 prime UTR variant") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::select(n) %>%
    as.numeric())
## Count of 5' UTR Variants: 2079

Supplementary Figure 1

TMB calculation for intronic, intergenic, and coding regions

Code 
# # Load full gene annotation GTF full_genes_gtf <-
# devgru::read_gtf_file(gtf_file_path = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.108.gtf.gz')) full_genes_gtf_dt <- gr2dt(full_genes_gtf)
# # Filter to the same set of gene biotypes: protein-coding, long non-coding,
# and micro RNA # Must have at least 1 transcript record # Also filter out the
# records for selenocysteine, start codon, stop codon, and CDS as these regions
# are # covered within the exons genes_of_interest <- full_genes_gtf_dt %>%
# dplyr::filter(gene_biotype %in%
# c('IG_C_gene','IG_D_gene','IG_J_gene','IG_V_gene',
# 'lncRNA','miRNA','protein_coding','TR_C_gene','TR_D_gene',
# 'TR_J_gene','TR_V_gene') & !is.na(gene_name) & !is.na(transcript_id) & !type
# %in% c('Selenocysteine','start_codon','stop_codon','CDS')) # Grab the
# transcript name that corresponds to the gene annotation # This match is done
# using the width of the gene record and transcript record
# representative_transcript <- c() for(i in
# 1:n_distinct(genes_of_interest$gene_name)) { representative_transcript[i] <-
# genes_of_interest %>% dplyr::filter(gene_name ==
# unique(genes_of_interest$gene_name)[i] & type == 'transcript') %>%
# dplyr::arrange(desc(width)) %>% dplyr::select(transcript_id) %>%
# dplyr::first() %>% as.character() } # Subset each GR obj to only include the
# representative transcripts per_representative_transcript_gr <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(genes_of_interest %>% dplyr::filter(transcript_id %in%
# representative_transcript & type == 'transcript')))
# per_representative_transcript_exon_gr <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(genes_of_interest %>% dplyr::filter(transcript_id %in%
# representative_transcript & type == 'exon')))
# per_representative_transcript_utr_gr <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(genes_of_interest %>% dplyr::filter(transcript_id %in%
# representative_transcript & type %in%
# c('five_prime_utr','three_prime_utr')))) # Now build the CDS for each
# representative transcript per gene # subtract out 5' UTR and 3' UTR
# per_representative_transcript_cds <- data.table() for(i in
# 1:n_distinct(per_representative_transcript_utr_gr$gene_name)) {
# per_representative_transcript_cds <-
# rrbind(per_representative_transcript_cds, gr2dt(gr.setdiff(query =
# per_representative_transcript_exon_gr %Q% (gene_name ==
# unique(per_representative_transcript_utr_gr$gene_name)[i]), subject =
# per_representative_transcript_utr_gr %Q% (gene_name ==
# unique(per_representative_transcript_utr_gr$gene_name)[i]), ignore.strand =
# T))) } # Need to add in the set of genes that do not have any UTR to the CDS
# segments per_representative_transcript_cds_gr <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(rrbind(per_representative_transcript_cds,
# gr2dt(per_representative_transcript_exon_gr %Q% (!gene_name %in%
# unique(per_representative_transcript_utr_gr$gene_name)))))) # To find the
# intronic regions subtract the exonic regions from the transcript
# per_representative_transcript_intron <- data.table() for(i in
# 1:n_distinct(per_representative_transcript_exon_gr$gene_name)) {
# per_representative_transcript_intron <-
# rrbind(per_representative_transcript_intron, gr2dt(gr.setdiff(query =
# per_representative_transcript_gr %Q% (gene_name ==
# unique(per_representative_transcript_exon_gr$gene_name)[i]), subject =
# per_representative_transcript_exon_gr %Q% (gene_name ==
# unique(per_representative_transcript_exon_gr$gene_name)[i]), ignore.strand =
# T))) } # Replace the type designation to intron then convert to GR obj
# per_representative_transcript_intron$type <- 'intron'
# per_representative_transcript_intron_gr <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(per_representative_transcript_intron)) # The final
# complete gene body set is the CDS + UTR + intron complete_gene_body_gr <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(grbind(per_representative_transcript_cds_gr,
# per_representative_transcript_intron_gr,
# per_representative_transcript_utr_gr)) # Convert to DT to build the final GB
# DT complete_gene_body <- gr2dt(complete_gene_body_gr) # Final GDT / GGR #
# GRCh38 coordinates in GR obj format (seqnames, start, end, strand) # Genomic
# context of the gene body segment (type) # Prioritize gene annotations by gene
# name
# (gene_name,gene_id,gene_biotype,gene_source,transcript_id,transcript_name) #
# Some additional classifications for plotting
# (orientation,coding_v_noncoding,exonic_v_intronic) final_gene_dt <-
# complete_gene_body %>%
# dplyr::select(seqnames,start,end,type,gene_name,gene_id,gene_biotype,gene_source,transcript_id,transcript_name)
# %>% dplyr::left_join(y = gr2dt(per_representative_transcript_gr) %>%
# dplyr::select(transcript_id,strand), by = 'transcript_id') %>%
# dplyr::mutate('coding_v_noncoding' = case_when(type == 'exon' ~ 'coding',
# type != 'exon' ~ 'non_coding'), 'exonic_v_intronic' = case_when(type %in%
# c('exon','five_prime_utr','three_prime_utr') ~ 'exonic', type == 'intron' ~
# 'intronic'), 'orientation' = case_when(strand == '+' ~ 1, strand == '-' ~ 0))
# %>% dplyr::rename('segment' = type) %>%
# dplyr::select(seqnames,start,end,strand,segment,
# gene_name,gene_id,gene_biotype, gene_source,transcript_id,transcript_name,
# orientation,coding_v_noncoding,exonic_v_intronic) final_gene_gr <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(final_gene_dt))

# Save / load the final GGR saveRDS(object = final_gene_gr, file =
# paste0(base_data_path, 'completeGeneSegments.rds'))
complete_gene_segments_gr <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "completeGeneSegments.rds"))

# Set the region TMB denominator
cds_regions <- complete_gene_segments_gr %Q% (coding_v_noncoding == "coding" & gene_biotype ==
    "protein_coding")
cds_tmb_denom <- sum(GenomicRanges::width(cds_regions[gr.in(query = cds_regions,
    subject = eligible_bed)]))/1000000

five_prime_utr_regions <- complete_gene_segments_gr %Q% (segment == "five_prime_utr" &
    gene_biotype == "protein_coding")
five_prime_utr_tmb_denom <- sum(GenomicRanges::width(five_prime_utr_regions[gr.in(query = five_prime_utr_regions,
    subject = eligible_bed)]))/1000000

three_prime_utr_regions <- complete_gene_segments_gr %Q% (segment == "three_prime_utr" &
    gene_biotype == "protein_coding")
three_prime_utr_tmb_denom <- sum(GenomicRanges::width(three_prime_utr_regions[gr.in(query = three_prime_utr_regions,
    subject = eligible_bed)]))/1000000

intron_regions <- complete_gene_segments_gr %Q% (segment == "intron" & gene_biotype ==
    "protein_coding")
intron_tmb_denom <- sum(GenomicRanges::width(intron_regions[gr.in(query = intron_regions,
    subject = eligible_bed)]))/1000000

intergenic_regions <- eligible_bed %_% complete_gene_segments_gr
intergenic_tmb_denom <- sum(GenomicRanges::width(intergenic_regions))/1000000


# With the regions of interest set, now calculate the TMB for each
cds_tmb <- fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(y = rrbind(complete_snv_fish_per_sample, complete_indel_fish_per_sample) %>%
        dplyr::select(seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2, sample,
            patient), by = c("seqnames", "start", "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2",
        "sample", "patient")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation,
        purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::filter(c_vs_nc == "Coding") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(tmb = n/cds_tmb_denom, genomic_context = "CDS")

five_prime_utr_tmb <- fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(y = rrbind(complete_snv_fish_per_sample, complete_indel_fish_per_sample) %>%
        dplyr::select(seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2, sample,
            patient), by = c("seqnames", "start", "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2",
        "sample", "patient")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation,
        purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq == "5 prime UTR variant") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(tmb = n/five_prime_utr_tmb_denom, genomic_context = "5' UTR")

three_prime_utr_tmb <- fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(y = rrbind(complete_snv_fish_per_sample, complete_indel_fish_per_sample) %>%
        dplyr::select(seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2, sample,
            patient), by = c("seqnames", "start", "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2",
        "sample", "patient")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation,
        purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq == "3 prime UTR variant") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(tmb = n/three_prime_utr_tmb_denom, genomic_context = "3' UTR")

intron_tmb <- fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(y = rrbind(complete_snv_fish_per_sample, complete_indel_fish_per_sample) %>%
        dplyr::select(seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2, sample,
            patient), by = c("seqnames", "start", "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2",
        "sample", "patient")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation,
        purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq %in% c("splice acceptor variant", "splice donor variant", "splice region variant",
        "intron variant")) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(tmb = n/intron_tmb_denom, genomic_context = "Intron")

intergenic_tmb <- fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(y = rrbind(complete_snv_fish_per_sample, complete_indel_fish_per_sample) %>%
        dplyr::select(seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2, sample,
            patient), by = c("seqnames", "start", "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2",
        "sample", "patient")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation,
        purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::filter(csq %in% c("upstream gene variant", "downstream gene variant",
        "TF binding site variant", "regulatory region variant", "intergenic variant")) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(tmb = n/intergenic_tmb_denom, genomic_context = "Intergenic")

# Plot the TMB per sequence classification
genomic_context_tmb <- rrbind(cds_tmb, five_prime_utr_tmb, three_prime_utr_tmb, intron_tmb,
    intergenic_tmb) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(sex) & !is.na(age) & !is.na(igh_translocation) & !is.na(purity) &
        !is.na(ploidy))

# Correct the TMB by age by fitting a linear model
message("Testing linear regression of TMB and demographic / molecular covariates")
## Testing linear regression of TMB and demographic / molecular covariates
test_genomic_lm <- lm(data = genomic_context_tmb, formula = tmb ~ age + igh_translocation +
    purity + ploidy)
summary(test_genomic_lm)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tmb ~ age + igh_translocation + purity + ploidy, 
##     data = genomic_context_tmb)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.8541 -0.6577 -0.1394  0.4694  6.8726 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                Estimate Std. Error t value    Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                    0.389967   0.233376   1.671    0.094915 .  
## age                            0.007840   0.002327   3.369    0.000771 ***
## igh_translocationt(4;14)       0.177713   0.078599   2.261    0.023889 *  
## igh_translocationt(14;16)      1.116136   0.243823   4.578 0.000005055 ***
## igh_translocationt(14;20)      0.195324   0.134193   1.456    0.145710    
## igh_translocationt(6;14)      -0.007424   0.191286  -0.039    0.969047    
## igh_translocationt(8;14)       0.291453   0.165601   1.760    0.078599 .  
## igh_translocationNot Detected  0.162578   0.059206   2.746    0.006099 ** 
## purity                         0.526741   0.099092   5.316 0.000000121 ***
## ploidy                        -0.019297   0.077471  -0.249    0.803323    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.9224 on 1647 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.03714,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.03188 
## F-statistic: 7.058 on 9 and 1647 DF,  p-value: 0.0000000004482

# Looks to be over fit with all this, will remove 'male' as the sex chromosomes
# are already removed
message("Final linear regression model of TMB across genomic regions")
## Final linear regression model of TMB across genomic regions
genomic_tmb_lm <- lm(data = genomic_context_tmb %>%
    dplyr::mutate(t14_16 = case_when(igh_translocation == "t(14;16)" ~ 1, igh_translocation !=
        "t(14;16)" ~ 0)), formula = tmb ~ age + t14_16 + purity)
# genomic_tmb_lm <- lm(data = genomic_context_tmb %>% dplyr::mutate('t14_16' =
# case_when(igh_translocation == 't(14;16)' ~ 1, igh_translocation !=
# 't(14;16)' ~ 0), 'male' = case_when(sex == 'Male' ~ 1, sex != 'Male' ~ 0)),
# formula = tmb ~ male + age + t14_16 + purity)
summary(genomic_tmb_lm)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tmb ~ age + t14_16 + purity, data = genomic_context_tmb %>% 
##     dplyr::mutate(t14_16 = case_when(igh_translocation == "t(14;16)" ~ 
##         1, igh_translocation != "t(14;16)" ~ 0)))
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.8493 -0.6594 -0.1530  0.4758  6.8921 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value   Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.535113   0.155384   3.444   0.000588 ***
## age         0.007001   0.002305   3.038   0.002421 ** 
## t14_16      0.985308   0.240207   4.102 0.00004297 ***
## purity      0.512740   0.096969   5.288 0.00000014 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.9237 on 1653 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.03088,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.02912 
## F-statistic: 17.56 on 3 and 1653 DF,  p-value: 0.00000000003206

# Calculate age-adjusted TMB using the residuals of the linear fitting
genomic_context_tmb$tmb_corrected <- resid(genomic_tmb_lm) + mean(genomic_context_tmb$tmb)

genomic_context_median_tmb <- genomic_context_tmb %>%
    dplyr::filter(tmb_corrected > 0) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(genomic_context) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_tmb = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

genomic_context_tmb$genomic_context <- factor(genomic_context_tmb$genomic_context,
    levels = c("CDS", "3' UTR", "Intron", "5' UTR", "Intergenic"))

genomic_context_tmb_wilcox_output <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = genomic_context_tmb,
    formula = tmb_corrected ~ genomic_context)
genomic_context_tmb_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(genomic_context_tmb_wilcox_output$p.adj)

tmb_by_genomic_region <- ggplot(genomic_context_tmb, aes(x = genomic_context, y = tmb_corrected)) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5), xmax = c(1.5,
        2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5), ymin = 0.01, ymax = 100, alpha = 0.25, fill = paletteer_d("ggsci::hallmarks_light_cosmic")[1:5]) +
    ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = "compactswarm", corral = "wrap") + labs(y = "TMB (muts. per Mb)",
    x = NULL) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) + scale_y_log10(labels = c("0.01",
    "0.1", "1.0", "10", "100"), breaks = c(0.01, 0.1, 1, 10, 100), expand = c(0.03,
    0.03)) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans",
    size = 14), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans",
    size = 12), axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15), panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment", x = c(0.65, 1.65, 2.65, 3.65, 4.65), xend = c(1.35,
        2.35, 3.35, 4.35, 5.35), y = genomic_context_median_tmb$median_tmb[c(3, 1,
        5, 2, 4)], yend = genomic_context_median_tmb$median_tmb[c(3, 1, 5, 2, 4)],
        color = "green", linewidth = 2.25) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = c(1,
    2, 3, 4, 5), y = 150, label = as.character(round(genomic_context_median_tmb$median_tmb[c(3,
    1, 5, 2, 4)], digits = 2)), size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = genomic_context_tmb_wilcox_output[1,
    ], label = "P={p.adj}", y.position = 0.84, tip.length = 0.008, label.size = 3.75,
    vjust = 0.1) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = genomic_context_tmb_wilcox_output[c(2:4),
    ], label = "P={p_string}", y.position = c(1.13, 1.4, 1.64), tip.length = 0.008,
    label.size = 3.75, vjust = 0.1)

# SPLiT BY DISEASE STAGE
genomic_context_median_tmb_by_dx <- genomic_context_tmb %>%
    dplyr::filter(tmb_corrected > 0) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(genomic_context, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_tmb = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

cds_tmb_by_dx_wilcox <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = genomic_context_tmb %>%
    dplyr::filter(genomic_context == "CDS") %>%
    droplevels(), formula = tmb_corrected ~ disease_stage)

three_prime_utr_tmb_by_dx_wilcox <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = genomic_context_tmb %>%
    dplyr::filter(genomic_context == "3' UTR") %>%
    droplevels(), formula = tmb_corrected ~ disease_stage)

five_prime_utr_tmb_by_dx_wilcox <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = genomic_context_tmb %>%
    dplyr::filter(genomic_context == "5' UTR") %>%
    droplevels(), formula = tmb_corrected ~ disease_stage)

intron_tmb_by_dx_wilcox <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = genomic_context_tmb %>%
    dplyr::filter(genomic_context == "Intron") %>%
    droplevels(), formula = tmb_corrected ~ disease_stage)

intergenic_tmb_by_dx_wilcox <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = genomic_context_tmb %>%
    dplyr::filter(genomic_context == "Intergenic") %>%
    droplevels(), formula = tmb_corrected ~ disease_stage)

# regional_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p_string <-
# scientific(regional_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p.adj)

tmb_by_genomic_context_by_dx <- ggplot(genomic_context_tmb, aes(x = genomic_context,
    y = tmb_corrected, colour = disease_stage)) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
    xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5), xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5), ymin = 0.01,
    ymax = 10, alpha = 0.25, fill = paletteer_d("ggsci::hallmarks_light_cosmic")[1:5]) +
    geom_boxplot() + labs(y = "Tumor mutational burden (muts. per Mb)", x = NULL) +
    scale_y_log10(labels = c("0.01", "0.1", "1.0", "10"), breaks = c(0.01, 0.1, 1,
        10), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease\nStage",
    values = disease_stage_colors) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45,
    hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans",
    size = 12), axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 16), panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment", x = 4.1, xend = 4.3, y = 9.15, yend = 9.15,
        linewidth = 0.5) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = 4.2, y = 11.5, size = 4.5,
    angle = 0, label = paste0("P=", five_prime_utr_tmb_by_dx_wilcox[6, ]$p.adj))

Now look at results of Pan-Myeloma fishHook model

Code 
# Looking at fishHook model output Analysis of model output
snv_sushi_results <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "fishHookModels/panMyeloma/mgp1000_snv_results.sushi.rds"))
indel_sushi_results <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "fishHookModels/panMyeloma/mgp1000_indel_results.sushi.rds"))

# Visualize in a genome-wide fashion like GWAS for tiles
snv_sushi_results$mut_type <- "SNV"
indel_sushi_results$mut_type <- "InDel"

sushi_combo <- gr_refactor_seqs(grbind(dt2gr(snv_sushi_results), dt2gr(indel_sushi_results)))
sushi_for_plot <- gr_absolute_ranges(input_gr = sushi_combo, cpus = 2)
## Setting parallel cores to 2 ...

sushi_for_plot$mut_type <- factor(sushi_for_plot$mut_type, levels = c("SNV", "InDel"))

# sushi_for_plot$true_gene <- complete_gene_segments_gr$gene_name[nearest(x =
# sushi_for_plot, subject = gUtils::gr.stripstrand(complete_gene_segments_gr))]

# After manual inspection of nearest protein coding genes and expanded
# overlapping genes we updated the assignment of genes for the following MERs
# TSPOAP1 ====> MIR142 CSDE1 ====> NRAS BLOC1S5-TXNDC5.  ====> TXNDC5 FRMD8
# ====> NEAT1

# QQ plots SNVs First grab disease specific set and order by most FDR
# significance
panmy_snv_fishhook <- gr2dt(sushi_for_plot) %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", .default = nearest_gene))

# Top 10 genes
panmy_snv_top_fishhook_genes <- panmy_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get index of all tiles per top gene
panmy_snv_top_gene_idx <- which(c(panmy_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()) %in% panmy_snv_top_fishhook_genes)

# Build the color vector for each dot, highlight labels of interest and black
# for the rest
panmy_snv_color_idx <- data.table(idx = seq(1, nrow(panmy_snv_fishhook))) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(color_idx = case_when(idx %in% panmy_snv_top_gene_idx ~ "cyan",
        !idx %in% panmy_snv_top_gene_idx ~ "black"))

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
panmy_snv_gene_manhattan_labs <- panmy_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% panmy_snv_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% panmy_snv_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

# fishHook QQ plot
panmy_snv_fish_qq <- geom_fishhook_qq(fishhook_results = panmy_snv_fishhook, plot_title = "Pan-Myeloma",
    hightlight_vector = panmy_snv_color_idx$color_idx, label_vector = panmy_snv_gene_manhattan_labs$label,
    manual_lambda = 1.09, label_x_nudge = 15)

# InDels
panmy_indel_fishhook <- gr2dt(sushi_for_plot) %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~
        "TXNDC5", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

panmy_indel_top_fishhook_genes <- panmy_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

panmy_indel_top_gene_idx <- which(c(panmy_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()) %in% panmy_indel_top_fishhook_genes)

panmy_indel_color_idx <- data.table(idx = seq(1, nrow(panmy_indel_fishhook))) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(color_idx = case_when(idx %in% panmy_indel_top_gene_idx ~ "cyan",
        !idx %in% panmy_indel_top_gene_idx ~ "black"))

panmy_indel_gene_manhattan_labs <- panmy_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% panmy_indel_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% panmy_indel_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

# fishHook QQ plot
panmy_indel_fish_qq <- geom_fishhook_qq(fishhook_results = panmy_indel_fishhook,
    plot_title = "Pan-Myeloma", hightlight_vector = panmy_indel_color_idx$color_idx,
    label_vector = panmy_indel_gene_manhattan_labs$label, manual_lambda = 1.01, label_x_nudge = -2.5)

# Full genome view of SNV and InDel tiles
fishtiles <- ggplot(data = gr2dt(sushi_for_plot)) + scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(x = start_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(fdr), color = mut_type), alpha = 0.5, pointsize = 3.5) + geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05),
    linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred") + labs(y = expression("-log"[10] * "(" *
    italic("FDR") * ")")) + scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
    labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]), breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(-log10(sushi_for_plot$fdr)) +
    7), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Mutation Type", values = c("#191970",
    "#EEA9B8")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(1, linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:21]),
    xmax = linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22], ymin = 0, ymax = max(-log10(sushi_for_plot$fdr)) +
        7, alpha = 0.18, fill = linearized_hg38_strip_fill[1:22]) + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 60, hjust = 0.95), legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
# export 6 x 11.69 landscape

Check tiles across disease stage specific models

Code 
dx_sushi_combo <- data.table()
for (i in 1:length(disease_stage_order)) {
    # Read in the disease specific results
    dx_snv_sushi_results <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "fishHookModels/diseaseStageSpecific/",
        disease_stage_order[i], "_snv_results.sushi.rds"))
    dx_indel_sushi_results <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "fishHookModels/diseaseStageSpecific/",
        disease_stage_order[i], "_indel_results.sushi.rds"))

    # Add disease stage to DT
    dx_snv_sushi_results$disease_stage <- disease_stage_order[i]
    dx_indel_sushi_results$disease_stage <- disease_stage_order[i]

    # Add mutation type
    dx_snv_sushi_results$mut_type <- "SNV"
    dx_indel_sushi_results$mut_type <- "InDel"

    # Combine the data for plotting
    dx_sushi_combo <- gUtils::rrbind(dx_sushi_combo, dx_snv_sushi_results, dx_indel_sushi_results)
}

# Convert DT to GR
dx_sushi_combo_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(dx_sushi_combo))

# Add absolute range dx_sushi_for_plot <- gr_absolute_ranges(input_gr =
# dx_sushi_combo_gr, cpus = 2)
dx_sushi_for_plot <- gr2dt(dx_sushi_combo_gr)
dx_sushi_for_plot$disease_stage <- factor(dx_sushi_for_plot$disease_stage, levels = disease_stage_order)
dx_sushi_for_plot$mut_type <- factor(dx_sushi_for_plot$mut_type, levels = c("SNV",
    "InDel"))

Generate fishHook SNV QQ plots

Code 
message("fishHook SNV QQ plots")
## fishHook SNV QQ plots
# Annotate the top genes MGUS First grab disease specific set and order by most
# FDR significance
mgus_snv_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS" & mut_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

# Top 10 genes
mgus_snv_top_fishhook_genes <- mgus_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get index of all tiles per top gene
mgus_snv_top_gene_idx <- which(c(mgus_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()) %in% mgus_snv_top_fishhook_genes)

# Build the color vector for each dot, highlight labels of interest and black
# for the rest
mgus_snv_color_idx <- data.table(idx = seq(1, nrow(mgus_snv_fishhook))) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(color_idx = case_when(idx %in% mgus_snv_top_gene_idx ~ "cyan",
        !idx %in% mgus_snv_top_gene_idx ~ "black"))

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
mgus_snv_gene_manhattan_labs <- mgus_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% mgus_snv_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% mgus_snv_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

# fishHook QQ plot
mgus_snv_fish_qq <- geom_fishhook_qq(fishhook_results = mgus_snv_fishhook, plot_title = "SNVs",
    hightlight_vector = mgus_snv_color_idx$color_idx, label_vector = mgus_snv_gene_manhattan_labs$label,
    label_x_nudge = -2, manual_lambda = 1)

############### SMM
smm_snv_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "SMM" & mut_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

# Top 10 genes
smm_snv_top_fishhook_genes <- smm_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get index of all tiles per top gene
smm_snv_top_gene_idx <- which(c(smm_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()) %in% smm_snv_top_fishhook_genes)

# Build the color vector for each dot, highlight labels of interest and black
# for the rest
smm_snv_color_idx <- data.table(idx = seq(1, nrow(smm_snv_fishhook))) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(color_idx = case_when(idx %in% smm_snv_top_gene_idx ~ "cyan", !idx %in%
        smm_snv_top_gene_idx ~ "black"))

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
smm_snv_gene_manhattan_labs <- smm_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% smm_snv_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% smm_snv_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

# fishHook QQ plot
smm_snv_fish_qq <- geom_fishhook_qq(fishhook_results = smm_snv_fishhook, plot_title = "SNVs",
    hightlight_vector = smm_snv_color_idx$color_idx, label_vector = smm_snv_gene_manhattan_labs$label,
    label_x_nudge = 5, manual_lambda = 1.02)

############## MM
mm_snv_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MM" & mut_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

# Top 10 genes
mm_snv_top_fishhook_genes <- mm_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get index of all tiles per top gene
mm_snv_top_gene_idx <- which(c(mm_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()) %in% mm_snv_top_fishhook_genes)

# Build the color vector for each dot, highlight labels of interest and black
# for the rest
mm_snv_color_idx <- data.table(idx = seq(1, nrow(mm_snv_fishhook))) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(color_idx = case_when(idx %in% mm_snv_top_gene_idx ~ "cyan", !idx %in%
        mm_snv_top_gene_idx ~ "black"))

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
mm_snv_gene_manhattan_labs <- mm_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% mm_snv_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% mm_snv_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

# fishHook QQ plot
mm_snv_fish_qq <- geom_fishhook_qq(fishhook_results = mm_snv_fishhook, plot_title = "SNVs",
    hightlight_vector = mm_snv_color_idx$color_idx, label_vector = mm_snv_gene_manhattan_labs$label,
    label_x_nudge = 10, manual_lambda = 1.05)

############# RR
rrmm_snv_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "RRMM" & mut_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

# Top 10 genes
rrmm_snv_top_fishhook_genes <- rrmm_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get index of all tiles per top gene
rrmm_snv_top_gene_idx <- which(c(rrmm_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()) %in% rrmm_snv_top_fishhook_genes)

# Build the color vector for each dot, highlight labels of interest and black
# for the rest
rrmm_snv_color_idx <- data.table(idx = seq(1, nrow(rrmm_snv_fishhook))) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(color_idx = case_when(idx %in% rrmm_snv_top_gene_idx ~ "cyan",
        !idx %in% rrmm_snv_top_gene_idx ~ "black"))

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
rrmm_snv_gene_manhattan_labs <- rrmm_snv_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% rrmm_snv_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% rrmm_snv_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

# fishHook QQ plot
rrmm_snv_fish_qq <- geom_fishhook_qq(fishhook_results = rrmm_snv_fishhook, plot_title = "SNVs",
    hightlight_vector = rrmm_snv_color_idx$color_idx, label_vector = rrmm_snv_gene_manhattan_labs$label,
    label_x_nudge = 2, manual_lambda = 1.02)

Generate fishHook InDel QQ plots

Code 
# MGUS First grab disease specific set and order by most FDR significance
mgus_indel_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS" & mut_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

# Top 10 genes
mgus_indel_top_fishhook_genes <- mgus_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# fishHook QQ plot
mgus_indel_fish_qq <- geom_fishhook_qq(fishhook_results = mgus_indel_fishhook, plot_title = "InDels",
    manual_lambda = 0.99)

############### SMM
smm_indel_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "SMM" & mut_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

# Top 10 genes
smm_indel_top_fishhook_genes <- smm_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get index of all tiles per top gene
smm_indel_top_gene_idx <- which(c(smm_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()) %in% smm_indel_top_fishhook_genes)

# Build the color vector for each dot, highlight labels of interest and black
# for the rest
smm_indel_color_idx <- data.table(idx = seq(1, nrow(smm_indel_fishhook))) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(color_idx = case_when(idx %in% smm_indel_top_gene_idx ~ "cyan",
        !idx %in% smm_indel_top_gene_idx ~ "black"))

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
smm_indel_gene_manhattan_labs <- smm_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% smm_indel_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% smm_indel_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

# fishHook QQ plot
smm_indel_fish_qq <- geom_fishhook_qq(fishhook_results = smm_indel_fishhook, plot_title = "InDels",
    hightlight_vector = smm_indel_color_idx$color_idx, label_vector = smm_indel_gene_manhattan_labs$label,
    label_x_nudge = -1, manual_lambda = 0.99)

############## MM
mm_indel_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MM" & mut_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

# Top 10 genes
mm_indel_top_fishhook_genes <- mm_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get index of all tiles per top gene
