    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()) %in% mm_indel_top_fishhook_genes)

# Build the color vector for each dot, highlight labels of interest and black
# for the rest
mm_indel_color_idx <- data.table(idx = seq(1, nrow(mm_indel_fishhook))) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(color_idx = case_when(idx %in% mm_indel_top_gene_idx ~ "cyan",
        !idx %in% mm_indel_top_gene_idx ~ "black"))

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
mm_indel_gene_manhattan_labs <- mm_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% mm_indel_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% mm_indel_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

# fishHook QQ plot
mm_indel_fish_qq <- geom_fishhook_qq(fishhook_results = mm_indel_fishhook, plot_title = "InDels",
    hightlight_vector = mm_indel_color_idx$color_idx, label_vector = mm_indel_gene_manhattan_labs$label,
    label_x_nudge = 5, manual_lambda = 1.01)

############# RR
rrmm_indel_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "RRMM" & mut_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

# Top 10 genes
rrmm_indel_top_fishhook_genes <- rrmm_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get index of all tiles per top gene
rrmm_indel_top_gene_idx <- which(c(rrmm_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()) %in% rrmm_indel_top_fishhook_genes)

# Build the color vector for each dot, highlight labels of interest and black
# for the rest
rrmm_indel_color_idx <- data.table(idx = seq(1, nrow(rrmm_indel_fishhook))) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(color_idx = case_when(idx %in% rrmm_indel_top_gene_idx ~ "cyan",
        !idx %in% rrmm_indel_top_gene_idx ~ "black"))

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
rrmm_indel_gene_manhattan_labs <- rrmm_indel_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% rrmm_indel_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% rrmm_indel_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

# fishHook QQ plot
rrmm_indel_fish_qq <- geom_fishhook_qq(fishhook_results = rrmm_indel_fishhook, plot_title = "InDels",
    hightlight_vector = rrmm_indel_color_idx$color_idx, label_vector = rrmm_indel_gene_manhattan_labs$label,
    label_x_nudge = -1.5, manual_lambda = 1)

Supplementary Figure 17

fishHook manhattan plots

Code 
############# MGUS Get the top 25 labels of both SNV and InDels
mgus_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

mgus_top_fishhook_genes <- mgus_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 25) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
mgus_gene_manhattan_labs <- mgus_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% mgus_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% mgus_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

mgus_fishhook_manhattan <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = mgus_fishhook) + scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(x = start_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(fdr), color = mut_type), alpha = 0.5, pointsize = 3.5) + ggrepel::geom_label_repel(aes(x = start_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(fdr), label = mgus_gene_manhattan_labs$label), max.overlaps = 100,
    min.segment.length = 0, na.rm = T) + ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05),
    linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred") + ggplot2::labs(y = expression("-log"[10] *
    "(" * italic("FDR") * ")")) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
    labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]), breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(-log10(mgus_fishhook$fdr)) +
    3), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_color_manual(name = "Mutation Type",
    values = c("#191970", "#EEA9B8")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(1,
    linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:21]), xmax = linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22], ymin = 0,
    ymax = max(-log10(mgus_fishhook$fdr)) + 3, alpha = 0.18, fill = linearized_hg38_strip_fill[1:22]) +
    ggplot2::theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 14)) +
    ggplot2::guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))

############# SMM Get the top 25 labels of both SNV and InDels
smm_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "SMM") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

smm_top_fishhook_genes <- smm_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 25) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
smm_gene_manhattan_labs <- smm_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% smm_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% smm_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

smm_fishhook_manhattan <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = smm_fishhook) + scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(x = start_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(fdr), color = mut_type), alpha = 0.5, pointsize = 3.5) + ggrepel::geom_label_repel(aes(x = start_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(fdr), label = smm_gene_manhattan_labs$label), max.overlaps = 100,
    min.segment.length = 0, na.rm = T) + ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05),
    linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred") + ggplot2::labs(y = expression("-log"[10] *
    "(" * italic("FDR") * ")")) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
    labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]), breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(-log10(smm_fishhook$fdr)) +
    3), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_color_manual(name = "Mutation Type",
    values = c("#191970", "#EEA9B8")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(1,
    linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:21]), xmax = linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22], ymin = 0,
    ymax = max(-log10(smm_fishhook$fdr)) + 3, alpha = 0.18, fill = linearized_hg38_strip_fill[1:22]) +
    ggplot2::theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 14)) +
    ggplot2::guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))

############# MM Get the top 25 labels of both SNV and InDels
mm_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MM") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

mm_top_fishhook_genes <- mm_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 25) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
mm_gene_manhattan_labs <- mm_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% mm_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% mm_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

mm_fishhook_manhattan <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = mm_fishhook) + scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(x = start_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(fdr), color = mut_type), alpha = 0.5, pointsize = 3.5) + ggrepel::geom_label_repel(aes(x = start_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(fdr), label = mm_gene_manhattan_labs$label), max.overlaps = 100, min.segment.length = 0,
    na.rm = T) + ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed",
    color = "darkred") + ggplot2::labs(y = expression("-log"[10] * "(" * italic("FDR") *
    ")")) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
    labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]), breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(-log10(mm_fishhook$fdr)) +
    3), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_color_manual(name = "Mutation Type",
    values = c("#191970", "#EEA9B8")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(1,
    linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:21]), xmax = linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22], ymin = 0,
    ymax = max(-log10(mm_fishhook$fdr)) + 3, alpha = 0.18, fill = linearized_hg38_strip_fill[1:22]) +
    ggplot2::theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 14)) +
    ggplot2::guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))

############# RRMM Get the top 25 labels of both SNV and InDels
rrmm_fishhook <- dx_sushi_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "RRMM") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(fdr) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(true_gene_label = case_when(nearest_gene == "TSPOAP1" ~ "MIR142",
        nearest_gene == "CSDE1" ~ "NRAS", nearest_gene == "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5" ~ "TXNDC5",
        nearest_gene == "FRMD8" ~ "NEAT1", nearest_gene == "BMP6" ~ "TXNDC5", .default = nearest_gene))

rrmm_top_fishhook_genes <- rrmm_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(true_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 25) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# Get the gene labels, only 1 for each set of top tiles
rrmm_gene_manhattan_labs <- rrmm_fishhook %>%
    dplyr::mutate(top_gene_idx = !duplicated(true_gene_label)) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(label = case_when(true_gene_label %in% rrmm_top_fishhook_genes &
        top_gene_idx == T ~ true_gene_label, !true_gene_label %in% rrmm_top_fishhook_genes ~
        NA))

rrmm_fishhook_manhattan <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = rrmm_fishhook) + scattermore::geom_scattermore(aes(x = start_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(fdr), color = mut_type), alpha = 0.5, pointsize = 3.5) + ggrepel::geom_label_repel(aes(x = start_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(fdr), label = rrmm_gene_manhattan_labs$label), max.overlaps = 100,
    min.segment.length = 0, na.rm = T) + ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05),
    linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred") + ggplot2::labs(y = expression("-log"[10] *
    "(" * italic("FDR") * ")")) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
    labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]), breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(-log10(rrmm_fishhook$fdr)) +
    3), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_color_manual(name = "Mutation Type",
    values = c("#191970", "#EEA9B8")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(1,
    linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:21]), xmax = linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22], ymin = 0,
    ymax = max(-log10(rrmm_fishhook$fdr)) + 3, alpha = 0.18, fill = linearized_hg38_strip_fill[1:22]) +
    ggplot2::theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 60, hjust = 0.95), legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 14)) + ggplot2::guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))

6 The landscape of the non-coding genome in myeloma is characterized by a discrete set of mutationally enriched regions, Part 2

Grab significant tiles and annotate by closest protein-coding / miRNA / lncRNA gene

Code 
# To extract the list of genes targeted by the enrichment of non-coding
# mutations, first, subset to only tiles with FDR < 0.05 then sort by genomic
# coordinate
sig_snv_sushi <- snv_sushi_results[fdr < 0.05][order(seqnames, start)]
sig_indel_sushi <- indel_sushi_results[fdr < 0.05][order(seqnames, start)]

# How many final tiles
message("FDR Significant SNV enriched tiles: ", nrow(sig_snv_sushi))
## FDR Significant SNV enriched tiles: 4173
message("FDR Significant InDel enriched tiles: ", nrow(sig_indel_sushi))
## FDR Significant InDel enriched tiles: 275


# Create a set of stitched overlapping tiles to reduce to consensus segments
stitched_ncsnv_er <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.reduce(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_snv_sushi)))[,
    c()])
stitched_ncsnv_er$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = stitched_ncsnv_er,
    subject = gUtils::gr.stripstrand(genes))]

stitched_ncindel_er <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.reduce(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_indel_sushi)))[,
    c()])
stitched_ncindel_er$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = stitched_ncindel_er,
    subject = gUtils::gr.stripstrand(genes))]

Now work with set of stitched tiles to assign single combined P-value and gene per set of stitched regions

Code 
# Need to find all tiles that were collapsed into each stitched region then
# compute combined p-value for each stitched enriched region Need to make sure
# use exact overlap and calculate the mean

# Lets loop through each stitched mutation enriched region and grab number of
# tiles and combined p-value SNVs

ncsnv_fish_data_per_site <- data.table::data.table(k_tiles = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)),
    q_value = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)), effect_size_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)),
    count_density_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)), pred_count_density_mean = rep(NA,
        length(stitched_ncsnv_er)), eligible_size_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)))

for (i in 1:length(stitched_ncsnv_er)) {
    tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_ncsnv_er[i, ], query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_snv_sushi)),
        type = "within", qcol = c("fdr", "effectsize", "count.density", "count.pred.density",
            "eligible"))

    # Calculate the mean FDR p-value, effect size, count density, predicted
    # count density and the eligible region
    new_qval <- poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr)

    ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles[i] <- length(tile_set_to_stitch)
    ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$q_value[i] <- as.numeric(new_qval$p)
    ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$effectsize)
    ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.density)
    ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.pred.density)
    ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$eligible)
}

# Add the final metadata to the consensus sites
stitched_ncsnv_er$k_tiles <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles
stitched_ncsnv_er$q_value <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$q_value
stitched_ncsnv_er$effect_size_mean <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean
stitched_ncsnv_er$count_density_mean <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean
stitched_ncsnv_er$pred_count_density_mean <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean
stitched_ncsnv_er$eligible_size_mean <- ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean

# snv_k_and_p_dt <- data.table::data.table(k_tiles = rep(NA,
# length(stitched_ncsnv_er)), p_combine = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)))
# for(i in 1:length(stitched_ncsnv_er)) { tile_set_to_stitch <-
# gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_ncsnv_er[i,], query =
# gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_snv_sushi)), type = 'within', qcol =
# 'fdr') # now calculate the combined p-value using the Bonferroni method on
# the tile FDR adjusted p-values new_combined_pval <- poolr::bonferroni(p =
# tile_set_to_stitch$fdr) snv_k_and_p_dt$k_tiles[i] <-
# length(tile_set_to_stitch) snv_k_and_p_dt$p_combine[i] <- new_combined_pval$p
# } stitched_ncsnv_er$k_tiles <- snv_k_and_p_dt$k_tiles
# stitched_ncsnv_er$p_combine <- snv_k_and_p_dt$p_combine
stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_uniq_id <- paste0("snv_er", seq(1, length(stitched_ncsnv_er)))

# InDels
ncindel_fish_data_per_site <- data.table::data.table(k_tiles = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)),
    q_value = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)), effect_size_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)),
    count_density_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)), pred_count_density_mean = rep(NA,
        length(stitched_ncindel_er)), eligible_size_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_ncindel_er)))

for (i in 1:length(stitched_ncindel_er)) {
    tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_ncindel_er[i, ], query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_indel_sushi)),
        type = "within", qcol = c("fdr", "effectsize", "count.density", "count.pred.density",
            "eligible"))

    # Calculate the mean FDR p-value, effect size, count density, predicted
    # count density and the eligible region
    new_qval <- poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr)

    ncindel_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles[i] <- length(tile_set_to_stitch)
    ncindel_fish_data_per_site$q_value[i] <- as.numeric(new_qval$p)
    ncindel_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$effectsize)
    ncindel_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.density)
    ncindel_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.pred.density)
    ncindel_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$eligible)
}

# Add the final metadata to the consensus sites
stitched_ncindel_er$k_tiles <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles
stitched_ncindel_er$q_value <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$q_value
stitched_ncindel_er$effect_size_mean <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean
stitched_ncindel_er$count_density_mean <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean
stitched_ncindel_er$pred_count_density_mean <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean
stitched_ncindel_er$eligible_size_mean <- ncindel_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean

# indel_k_and_p_dt <- data.table::data.table(k_tiles = rep(NA,
# length(stitched_ncindel_er)), p_combine = rep(NA,
# length(stitched_ncindel_er))) for(i in 1:length(stitched_ncindel_er)) {
# tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_ncindel_er[i,],
# query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_indel_sushi)), type = 'within',
# qcol = 'fdr') # now calculate the combined p-value using the Bonferroni
# method on the tile FDR adjusted p-values new_combined_pval <-
# poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr) indel_k_and_p_dt$k_tiles[i] <-
# length(tile_set_to_stitch) indel_k_and_p_dt$p_combine[i] <-
# new_combined_pval$p } stitched_ncindel_er$k_tiles <- indel_k_and_p_dt$k_tiles
# stitched_ncindel_er$p_combine <- indel_k_and_p_dt$p_combine
stitched_ncindel_er$mut_uniq_id <- paste0("indel_er", seq(1, length(stitched_ncindel_er)))

Calculate the MER occupancy by disease stage, translocation subtype, or admixture

Code 
# What percentage of patients have mutations in these regions
ncsnv_per_ser <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = snv_fish, subject = stitched_ncsnv_er,
    qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS",
        "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Variant_Classification"),
    scol = c("nearest_gene", "mut_uniq_id"))
ncsnv_per_ser_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(ncsnv_per_ser)

ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_X_dt <- ncsnv_per_ser_dt %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, igh_translocation), by = c("patient",
        "sample"))


ncindels_per_ier <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = indel_fish, subject = stitched_ncindel_er,
    qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS",
        "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Variant_Classification"),
    scol = c("nearest_gene", "mut_uniq_id"))
ncindels_per_ier_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(ncindels_per_ier)

ncindels_per_ier_dt_by_X_dt <- ncindels_per_ier_dt %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, igh_translocation), by = c("patient",
        "sample"))

# Need to calculate the number of unique patients mutated per type of enriched
# region
mut_patients_per_ser <- ncsnv_per_ser_dt[!duplicated(ncsnv_per_ser_dt[, Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode,
    by = .(mut_uniq_id)])][, .(Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, mut_uniq_id)]

mut_patients_per_ser_count <- mut_patients_per_ser[, .(.N), by = .(mut_uniq_id)]
stitched_ncsnv_er$total_mut_patients <- mut_patients_per_ser_count$N

# Count up number of MGUS / SMM / MM / RRMM at each MER
ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_dx <- mut_patients_per_ser %>%
    dplyr::rename(patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, disease_stage) %>%
        dplyr::distinct(), by = "patient") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mut_uniq_id, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    reshape2::acast(formula = mut_uniq_id ~ disease_stage) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
    tidyr::replace_na(list(MGUS = 0, SMM = 0, MM = 0, RRMM = 0))
## Warning in dplyr::left_join(., y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(patient, : Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
## ℹ Row 17 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
## ℹ Row 38 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
## ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
##   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.
## Using n as value column: use value.var to override.

ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_trx <- mut_patients_per_ser %>%
    dplyr::rename(patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, igh_translocation) %>%
        dplyr::distinct(), by = "patient") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mut_uniq_id, igh_translocation) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    reshape2::acast(formula = mut_uniq_id ~ igh_translocation) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
    tidyr::replace_na(list(`t(11;14)` = 0, `t(11;14)` = 0, `t(4;14)` = 0, `t(14;16)` = 0,
        `t(14;20)` = 0, `t(6;14)` = 0, `t(8;14)` = 0, `Not Detected` = 0))
## Using n as value column: use value.var to override.

ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_admix <- mut_patients_per_ser %>%
    dplyr::rename(patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, super5_cluster_id) %>%
        dplyr::distinct(), by = "patient") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mut_uniq_id, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    reshape2::acast(formula = mut_uniq_id ~ super5_cluster_id) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
    tidyr::replace_na(list(`EUR Dominant` = 0, `AFR Dominant` = 0, `AMR Dominant` = 0,
        `EAS Dominant` = 0, `SAS Dominant` = 0, `Highly Admixed` = 0))
## Using n as value column: use value.var to override.

# ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_dx %>% dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id == 'snv_er47')

# Add the counts of patients mutated per disease stage
sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx <- gr2dt(stitched_ncsnv_er) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_dx, by = "mut_uniq_id")

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_mgus_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$MGUS
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_mgus_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$MGUS/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_smm_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$SMM
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_smm_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$SMM/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "SMM") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_mm_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$MM
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_mm_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$MM/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MM") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_rrmm_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$RRMM
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_rrmm_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_dx$RRMM/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "RRMM") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

# Add the counts of patients mutated per translocation
sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx <- gr2dt(stitched_ncsnv_er) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_trx, by = "mut_uniq_id")

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_t11_14_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(11;14)`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_t11_14_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(11;14)`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == "t(11;14)") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_t4_14_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(4;14)`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_t4_14_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(4;14)`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == "t(4;14)") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_t14_16_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(14;16)`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_t14_16_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(14;16)`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == "t(14;16)") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_t14_20_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(14;20)`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_t14_20_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(14;20)`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == "t(14;20)") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_t6_14_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(6;14)`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_t6_14_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(6;14)`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == "t(6;14)") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_t8_14_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(8;14)`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_t8_14_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`t(8;14)`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == "t(8;14)") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_trx_not_detected_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`Not Detected`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_trx_not_detected_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_trx$`Not Detected`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(igh_translocation == "Not Detected") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

# Add the counts of patients mutated per ancestry
sorted_snv_patient_count_by_admix <- gr2dt(stitched_ncsnv_er) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_admix, by = "mut_uniq_id")

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_EUR_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_admix$`EUR Dominant`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_EUR_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_admix$`EUR Dominant`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(super5_cluster_id == "EUR Dominant") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_AFR_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_admix$`AFR Dominant`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_EAS_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_admix$`EAS Dominant`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_EAS_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_admix$`EAS Dominant`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(super5_cluster_id == "EAS Dominant") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_SAS_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_admix$`SAS Dominant`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_SAS_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_admix$`SAS Dominant`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(super5_cluster_id == "SAS Dominant") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%

stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_highly_admixed_patients <- sorted_snv_patient_count_by_admix$`Highly Admixed`
stitched_ncsnv_er$pct_mut_highly_admixed_patients <- round(sorted_snv_patient_count_by_admix$`Highly Admixed`/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(super5_cluster_id == "Highly Admixed") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

########## INDELS ############## Now do same process for indels
mut_patients_per_ier <- ncindels_per_ier_dt[!duplicated(ncindels_per_ier_dt[, Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode,
    by = .(mut_uniq_id)])][, .(Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, mut_uniq_id)]

mut_patients_per_ier_count <- mut_patients_per_ier[, .(.N), by = .(mut_uniq_id)]
stitched_ncindel_er$total_mut_patients <- mut_patients_per_ier_count$N

ncindel_per_ier_dt_by_dx <- mut_patients_per_ier %>%
    dplyr::rename(patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, disease_stage) %>%
        dplyr::distinct(), by = "patient") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mut_uniq_id, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    reshape2::acast(formula = mut_uniq_id ~ disease_stage) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
    tidyr::replace_na(list(MGUS = 0, SMM = 0, MM = 0, RRMM = 0))
## Warning in dplyr::left_join(., y = hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::select(patient, : Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
## ℹ Row 26 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
## ℹ Row 157 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
## ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
##   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.
## Using n as value column: use value.var to override.

ncindel_per_ier_dt_by_trx <- mut_patients_per_ier %>%
    dplyr::rename(patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, igh_translocation) %>%
        dplyr::distinct(), by = "patient") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mut_uniq_id, igh_translocation) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    reshape2::acast(formula = mut_uniq_id ~ igh_translocation) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
    tidyr::replace_na(list(`t(11;14)` = 0, `t(11;14)` = 0, `t(4;14)` = 0, `t(14;16)` = 0,
        `t(14;20)` = 0, `t(6;14)` = 0, `t(8;14)` = 0, `Not Detected` = 0))
## Using n as value column: use value.var to override.

ncindel_per_ier_dt_by_admix <- mut_patients_per_ier %>%
    dplyr::rename(patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, super5_cluster_id) %>%
        dplyr::distinct(), by = "patient") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mut_uniq_id, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    reshape2::acast(formula = mut_uniq_id ~ super5_cluster_id) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
    tidyr::replace_na(list(`EUR Dominant` = 0, `AFR Dominant` = 0, `AMR Dominant` = 0,
        `EAS Dominant` = 0, `SAS Dominant` = 0, `Highly Admixed` = 0))
## Using n as value column: use value.var to override.

# Add the counts of patients mutated per disease stage
sorted_indel_patient_count_by_dx <- gr2dt(stitched_ncindel_er) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = ncindel_per_ier_dt_by_dx, by = "mut_uniq_id")

stitched_ncindel_er$mut_mgus_patients <- sorted_indel_patient_count_by_dx$MGUS
stitched_ncindel_er$pct_mut_mgus_patients <- round(sorted_indel_patient_count_by_dx$MGUS/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()) * 100, digits = 1)

stitched_ncindel_er$mut_smm_patients <- sorted_indel_patient_count_by_dx$SMM
stitched_ncindel_er$pct_mut_smm_patients <- round(sorted_indel_patient_count_by_dx$SMM/(hq_dataset_metadata %>%

Find all overlapping genes at each site

Code 
# TODO: functionalize this annotation process loop through each range and
# output all genes that overlap each region
stitched_ncsnv_overlapping_genes <- data.table()
for (i in 1:length(stitched_ncsnv_er)) {
    per_mer_gene_overlap <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = stitched_ncsnv_er[i],
        subject = complete_gene_segments_gr, qcol = "mut_uniq_id", scol = "gene_name")
    # Only use the unique set of annotations
    uniq_gene_overlap <- unique(per_mer_gene_overlap$gene_name)
    # String the genes together for overlap column
    stitched_ncsnv_overlapping_genes <- gUtils::rrbind(stitched_ncsnv_overlapping_genes,
        data.table(mut_uniq_id = stitched_ncsnv_er$mut_uniq_id[i], overlapping_genes = stringr::str_c(uniq_gene_overlap,
            collapse = ";")))
}
stitched_ncsnv_er$overlapping_genes <- stitched_ncsnv_overlapping_genes$overlapping_genes

stitched_ncindel_overlapping_genes <- data.table()
for (i in 1:length(stitched_ncindel_er)) {
    per_mer_gene_overlap <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = stitched_ncindel_er[i],
        subject = complete_gene_segments_gr, qcol = "mut_uniq_id", scol = "gene_name")
    # Only use the unique set of annotations
    uniq_gene_overlap <- unique(per_mer_gene_overlap$gene_name)
    # String the genes together for overlap column
    stitched_ncindel_overlapping_genes <- gUtils::rrbind(stitched_ncindel_overlapping_genes,
        data.table(mut_uniq_id = stitched_ncindel_er$mut_uniq_id[i], overlapping_genes = stringr::str_c(uniq_gene_overlap,
            collapse = ";")))
}
stitched_ncindel_er$overlapping_genes <- stitched_ncindel_overlapping_genes$overlapping_genes

Combine the set of SNV/InDel enriched regions into a set of Mutation Enriched Regions (MERs)

Code 
# Combine the two sets of enriched retions
mers <- gr2dt(gr_refactor_seqs(grbind(stitched_ncsnv_er, stitched_ncindel_er)))

# Sort by combined p-value and filter and then uniquely label each MER by
# ranking and calculate percentage of all patients with mutation in MER
mers <- mers %>%
    dplyr::arrange(q_value) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_total_mut_patients = round(total_mut_patients/length(unique(snv_fish$Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode)),
        digits = 3) * 100, mer_id = paste0("mer", seq(1, nrow(mers))))

# Print some metrics on MERs
message("total MERs: ", nrow(mers))
## total MERs: 307
message("Total unique genes associated to MERs: ", length(unique(mers$nearest_gene)))
## Total unique genes associated to MERs: 220

# How much of genome do MERs cover (look at redundant SNV+InDel MERs)
reduced_mers <- GenomicRanges::reduce(x = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(mers)))
message("total Genomic span of MERs in Mbp: ", sum(GenomicRanges::width(reduced_mers))/1000000)
## total Genomic span of MERs in Mbp: 5.379
message("Median span MERs in Kbp: ", median(mers$width)/1000)
## Median span MERs in Kbp: 15
message("Range span MERs in Kbp: ", range(mers$width)[1]/1000, " to ", range(mers$width)[2]/1000)
## Range span MERs in Kbp: 10 to 91

# SNV and InDel MER specifics
message("SNV enriched MERs: ", nrow(mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(mut_uniq_id, pattern = "snv_"))))
## SNV enriched MERs: 280
message("InDel enriched MERs: ", nrow(mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(mut_uniq_id, pattern = "indel_"))))
## InDel enriched MERs: 27

# After manual inspection of nearest protein coding genes and expanded
# overlapping genes we updated the assignment of genes for the following MERs
# MER12 - TSPOAP1 ====> MIR142 MER14 - CSDE1 ====> NRAS MER36 - BLOC1S5-TXNDC5
# ====> TXNDC5 MER37 - BLOC1S5-TXNDC5 ====> TXNDC5 MER83 - FRMD8 ====> NEAT1
mers$mer_gene_label <- replace(x = mers$nearest_gene, list = c(12, 14, 36, 37, 83),
    values = c("MIR142", "NRAS", "TXNDC5", "TXNDC5", "NEAT1"))

# Redundant MERs
redundant_mers <- gr_refactor_seqs(gr.findoverlaps(query = stitched_ncindel_er, subject = stitched_ncsnv_er,
    qcol = c("q_value", "mut_uniq_id")))
redundant_mers_dt <- gr2dt(redundant_mers)
redundant_mers_dt <- left_join(x = redundant_mers_dt, y = mers, by = "mut_uniq_id")

message("InDel MERs overlapping SNV MERs: ", nrow(redundant_mers_dt))
## InDel MERs overlapping SNV MERs: 5
message("Overlap at these genes: ", paste0(redundant_mers_dt$mer_gene_label, collapse = ", "))
## Overlap at these genes: TENT5C, OR2T2, BCL6, TXNDC5, PLD5P1


# Plot MERs across the genome using midpoint of each MER
mers_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(mers))
mers_gr <- gr_absolute_ranges(mers_gr)
## Setting parallel cores to 1 ...

# TODO: add this functionality to gr_absolute_ranges
mer_abs_midpoints <- c()
for (i in 1:length(mers_gr)) {
    mer_abs_midpoints[i] <- elliplot::midpoint(c(mers_gr$start_abs_linear[i], mers_gr$end_abs_linear[i]))[[1]][2]
}
mers_gr$midpoint_abs_linear <- mer_abs_midpoints
mers_gr$mer_type <- case_when(stringr::str_detect(mers_gr$mut_uniq_id, pattern = "indel_") ~
    "InDel", stringr::str_detect(mers_gr$mut_uniq_id, pattern = "snv_") ~ "SNV")
mers <- gr2dt(mers_gr)
mers$mer_type <- factor(mers$mer_type, levels = c("SNV", "InDel"))

# Label some MERs of interest
mer_of_interest_labels <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "moiLabels.txt"), header = T)

mer_manhattan <- ggplot(data = mers) + ggplot2::geom_point(aes(x = midpoint_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(q_value), color = mer_type), alpha = 0.7, size = 4) + ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(x = midpoint_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(q_value), label = mer_of_interest_labels$gene_name), max.overlaps = 100,
    min.segment.length = 0) + ggplot2::labs(y = expression("-log"[10] * "(" * italic("q-value") *
    ")"), ) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
    labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]), breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(-log10(mers$q_value)) +
    3), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_color_manual(name = "MER Type",
    values = c("#FB832D", "black")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(1,
    linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:21]), xmax = linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22], ymin = 0,
    ymax = max(-log10(mers$q_value)) + 3, alpha = 0.18, fill = linearized_hg38_strip_fill[1:22]) +
    ggplot2::theme(axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15), axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 11), legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
# export 6 x 11.69 landscape

# Write out final set of MERs
mer_cols <- c("seqnames", "start", "end", "width", "mer_id", "mer_gene_label", "mer_type",
    "k_tiles", "q_value", "effect_size_mean", "count_density_mean", "pred_count_density_mean",
    "eligible_size_mean", "total_mut_patients", "pct_total_mut_patients", "mut_mgus_patients",
    "pct_mut_mgus_patients", "mut_smm_patients", "pct_mut_smm_patients", "mut_mm_patients",
    "pct_mut_mm_patients", "mut_rrmm_patients", "pct_mut_rrmm_patients", "mut_t11_14_patients",
    "pct_mut_t11_14_patients", "mut_t4_14_patients", "pct_mut_t4_14_patients", "mut_t14_16_patients",
    "pct_mut_t14_16_patients", "mut_t14_20_patients", "pct_mut_t14_20_patients",
    "mut_t6_14_patients", "pct_mut_t6_14_patients", "mut_t8_14_patients", "pct_mut_t8_14_patients",
    "mut_trx_not_detected_patients", "pct_mut_trx_not_detected_patients", "mut_EUR_patients",
    "pct_mut_EUR_patients", "mut_AFR_patients", "pct_mut_AFR_patients", "mut_AMR_patients",
    "pct_mut_AMR_patients", "mut_EAS_patients", "pct_mut_EAS_patients", "mut_SAS_patients",
    "pct_mut_SAS_patients", "mut_highly_admixed_patients", "pct_mut_highly_admixed_patients",
    "nearest_gene", "overlapping_genes", "mut_uniq_id")

# data.table::fwrite(x = mers %>% dplyr::arrange(q_value) %>%
# dplyr::select(all_of(mer_cols)), file = 'MERs.txt', sep = '\t', row.names =
# F, col.names = T)

7 The mutational burden at MERs is increased in later disease stages and varies by ancestry

Visualize features of the MERs in different ways - SNVs

Code 
# Collect all unique mutations per patient that fall within the MERs
muts_per_mer <- gr2dt(gr_refactor_seqs(grbind(ncsnv_per_ser, ncindels_per_ier)))

muts_per_mer <- muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::mutate(mut_type = case_when(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV" ~ "SNVs", VARIANT_CLASS %in%
        c("insertion", "deletion") ~ "InDels"))

# Let's add more metadata to the mutations per mer
muts_per_mer_for_plot <- rrbind(ncsnv_per_ser_dt_by_X_dt, ncindels_per_ier_dt_by_X_dt) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = mers[, c("mut_uniq_id", "mer_id")], by = "mut_uniq_id")  #%>%
# dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata, by = c('patient', 'sample'))
# muts_per_mer_for_plot <- muts_per_mer %>% dplyr::rename('patient' =
# Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, 'sample' = Tumor_Sample_Barcode) %>%
# dplyr::left_join(y = mers[,c('mut_uniq_id','mer_id')], by = 'mut_uniq_id')
# %>% dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata, by = c('patient', 'sample'))

# Number of total unique SNVs and InDels across MERs per patient
snv_per_mer <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

indel_per_mer <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

########## SNVs #
message("Total SNVs in MERs: ", muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric())
## Total SNVs in MERs: 18199
message("Percentage of all SNVs in MERs: ", round(muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric()/length(snv_fish_final) * 100, digits = 2))
## Percentage of all SNVs in MERs: 1.73

message("Median of SNVs in MERs across patients: ", median(snv_per_mer$n))
## Median of SNVs in MERs across patients: 53
message("Range of SNVs in MERs across patients: ", paste0(range(snv_per_mer$n)[1],
    "-", range(snv_per_mer$n)[2]))
## Range of SNVs in MERs across patients: 1-291

########## InDels #
message("Total InDels in MERs: ", muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion")) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric())
## Total InDels in MERs: 440
message("Percentage of all InDels in MERs: ", round(muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion")) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric()/length(indel_fish_final) * 100, digits = 2))
## Percentage of all InDels in MERs: 0.61

message("Median of SNVs in MERs across patients: ", median(indel_per_mer$n))
## Median of SNVs in MERs across patients: 2
message("Range of SNVs in MERs across patients: ", paste0(range(indel_per_mer$n)[1],
    "-", range(indel_per_mer$n)[2]))
## Range of SNVs in MERs across patients: 1-8

Look at the absolute burden of MERs per patient

Code 
snv_per_mer_plot <- ggplot(data = snv_per_mer) + geom_violin(aes(y = n, x = "SNVs"),
    fill = "#FB832D", color = "#FB832D", width = 0.5, linewidth = 1) + geom_boxplot(aes(y = n,
    x = "SNVs"), outliers = FALSE, width = 0.15, fill = "#FB832D", color = "gray99",
    linewidth = 1.8) + labs(y = "Total SNV burden") + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95,
        size = 14), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))

# Snake plot of unique MERs per patient
uniq_snv_mers_per_patient <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
    dplyr::count(mer_id)

uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count <- uniq_snv_mers_per_patient %>%
    dplyr::select(patient, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::arrange(n)

uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$patient <- factor(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$patient,
    levels = c(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$patient))

snv_uniq_mer_plot <- ggplot(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count) + ggplot2::geom_point(aes(x = patient,
    y = n), shape = 21, color = "black", fill = "#FB832D", size = 3, alpha = 0.5) +
    ggplot2::labs(x = "Patients", y = "Unique MERs") + ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.03,
    0.03)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.015, 0.015)) + ggplot2::theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1)) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment", x = nrow(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count)/2 -
    60, xend = nrow(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count)/2 + 60, y = median(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$n),
    yend = median(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$n), color = "gray40", linewidth = 4,
    alpha = 0.9)

message("Range of Unique SNV MERs across patients: ", paste0(range(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$n)[1],
    "-", range(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$n)[2]))
## Range of Unique SNV MERs across patients: 1-133
message("Median of Unique SNV MERs across patients: ", median(uniq_snv_mers_per_patient_count$n))
## Median of Unique SNV MERs across patients: 37

# Combine the two SNV MER metric plots snv_per_mer_plot + snv_uniq_mer_plot +
# patchwork::plot_layout(nrow = 1, widths = c(1,0.5)) export 6 x 6 portrait

Visualize features of the MERs in different ways - InDels

Code 
# Number of total SNVs across MERs per patient InDels #

indel_per_mer <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
    dplyr::count()

message("Total InDels in MERs: ", muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion")) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric())
## Total InDels in MERs: 440
message("Range of InDels in MERs across patients: ", paste0(range(indel_per_mer$n)[1],
    "-", range(indel_per_mer$n)[2]))
## Range of InDels in MERs across patients: 1-8
message("Median of InDels in MERs across patients: ", median(indel_per_mer$n))
## Median of InDels in MERs across patients: 2


indel_per_mer_plot <- ggplot(data = indel_per_mer) + geom_violin(aes(y = n, x = "InDels"),
    fill = "black", color = "black", width = 0.5, linewidth = 1) + geom_boxplot(aes(y = n,
    x = "InDels"), outliers = FALSE, width = 0.15, fill = "black", color = "white",
    linewidth = 1.8) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 8)) + labs(y = "Total mutation count") +
    theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45,
        hjust = 0.95, size = 14), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))

# Snake plot of unique MERs per patient
uniq_indel_mers_per_patient <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion")) %>%
    dplyr::select(patient, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
    dplyr::count(mer_id)

uniq_indel_mers_per_patient_count <- uniq_indel_mers_per_patient %>%
    dplyr::select(patient, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::arrange(n)

uniq_indel_mers_per_patient_count$patient <- factor(uniq_indel_mers_per_patient_count$patient,
    levels = c(uniq_indel_mers_per_patient_count$patient))

indel_uniq_mer_plot <- ggplot(uniq_indel_mers_per_patient_count) + geom_jitter(aes(x = patient,
    y = n), color = "black", size = 3, alpha = 0.4, height = 0.1) + labs(x = "Patients",
    y = "Unique MERs") + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.015,
    0.015)) + theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))

# indel_per_mer_plot + indel_uniq_mer_plot + patchwork::plot_layout(nrow = 1,
# widths = c(1,0.75)) export 6 x 6 portrait

Look at the mutation burden across MERs by type and disease stage

Code 
# Gather all SNVs in MERs per sample
complete_mer_snv_per_sample <- left_join(x = gr2dt(gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_in_hq_patients %Q%
    (VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV"), subject = mers_gr %Q% (mer_type == "SNV"), qcol = c("Reference_Allele",
    "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode"),
    scol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label"))) %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode),
    y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation,
            coverage_normal, coverage_tumor, purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id),
    by = c("patient", "sample")) %>%
    dplyr::select(-c(6, 7)) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
complete_mer_snv_per_sample$age <- as.numeric(complete_mer_snv_per_sample$age)

# Gather all InDels in MERs per sample
complete_mer_indel_per_sample <- left_join(x = gr2dt(gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_in_hq_patients %Q%
    (VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion")), subject = mers_gr %Q% (mer_type ==
    "InDel"), qcol = c("Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",
    "Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode"), scol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label"))) %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode),
    y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation,
            coverage_normal, coverage_tumor, purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id),
    by = c("patient", "sample")) %>%
    dplyr::select(-c(6, 7)) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
complete_mer_indel_per_sample$age <- as.numeric(complete_mer_indel_per_sample$age)

# Calculate the broad mutational burden separate here SNVs #########
total_mer_snv_tmb <- complete_mer_snv_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(age)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation, coverage_normal,
        coverage_tumor, purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "total_mutation_count") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(tumor_mutation_burden = total_mutation_count/(sum(GenomicRanges::width(mers_gr %Q%
        (mer_type == "SNV")))/1000000))

# common_mer_snv_tmb <- complete_mer_snv_per_sample %>%
# dplyr::filter(!is.na(age) & mer_id %in% common_snv_mers_all_dx$mer_id) %>%
# dplyr::group_by(sample,disease_stage,age,sex,igh_translocation,coverage_normal,coverage_tumor,purity,ploidy,super5_cluster_id)
# %>% dplyr::count(name = 'total_mutation_count') %>% dplyr::ungroup() %>%
# dplyr::mutate('tumor_mutation_burden' = total_mutation_count /
# (sum(GenomicRanges::width(mers_gr %Q% (mer_id %in%
# common_snv_mers_all_dx$mer_id) %Q% (mer_type == 'SNV'))) / 1e6))

######## InDels #########
total_mer_indel_tmb <- complete_mer_indel_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(age)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage, age, sex, igh_translocation, coverage_normal,
        coverage_tumor, purity, ploidy, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "total_mutation_count") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(tumor_mutation_burden = total_mutation_count/(sum(GenomicRanges::width(mers_gr %Q%
        (mer_type == "InDel")))/1000000))

# common_mer_indel_tmb <- complete_mer_indel_per_sample %>%
# dplyr::filter(!is.na(age) & mer_id %in% common_indel_mers_all_dx$mer_id) %>%
# dplyr::group_by(sample,disease_stage,age,sex,igh_translocation,coverage_normal,coverage_tumor,purity,ploidy,super5_cluster_id)
# %>% dplyr::count(name = 'total_mutation_count') %>% dplyr::ungroup() %>%
# dplyr::mutate('tumor_mutation_burden' = total_mutation_count /
# (sum(GenomicRanges::width(mers_gr %Q% (mer_id %in%
# common_indel_mers_all_dx$mer_id) %Q% (mer_type == 'InDel'))) / 1e6))

# Correct the TMB by age by fitting a linear model
test_total_snv_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = total_mer_snv_tmb, formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~
    age + igh_translocation + purity + ploidy)
summary(test_total_snv_mer_tmb_lm)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ age + igh_translocation + 
##     purity + ploidy, data = total_mer_snv_tmb)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -12.681  -2.792  -0.092   1.967  33.403 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                               Estimate Std. Error t value    Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                    3.26967    2.69156   1.215      0.2253    
## age                           -0.02762    0.02696  -1.025      0.3062    
## igh_translocationt(4;14)       0.92223    0.91339   1.010      0.3134    
## igh_translocationt(14;16)      0.42210    2.84895   0.148      0.8823    
## igh_translocationt(14;20)     -1.57213    1.56673  -1.003      0.3164    
## igh_translocationt(6;14)      -0.44579    2.23428  -0.200      0.8420    
## igh_translocationt(8;14)       3.82345    1.93311   1.978      0.0488 *  
## igh_translocationNot Detected  1.23708    0.68347   1.810      0.0712 .  
## purity                         5.78293    1.14183   5.065 0.000000682 ***
## ploidy                         1.93361    0.88665   2.181      0.0299 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 4.821 on 330 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.125,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.1011 
## F-statistic: 5.237 on 9 and 330 DF,  p-value: 0.000001132


total_snv_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = total_mer_snv_tmb, formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~
    purity + ploidy)
summary(total_snv_mer_tmb_lm)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ purity + ploidy, data = total_mer_snv_tmb)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -12.236  -2.934  -0.268   2.164  34.776 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value    Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)   0.5658     2.0895   0.271     0.78673    
## purity        5.8213     1.1215   5.191 0.000000363 ***
## ploidy        2.7560     0.8365   3.295     0.00109 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 4.843 on 337 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.09857,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.09322 
## F-statistic: 18.43 on 2 and 337 DF,  p-value: 0.00000002546


# message('Testing linear regression of common MER SNV burden demographic /
# molecular covariates') test_common_snv_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data =
# common_mer_snv_tmb, formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ age + igh_translocation
# + purity + ploidy) summary(test_common_snv_mer_tmb_lm)

# common_snv_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = common_mer_snv_tmb, #%>%
# #dplyr::mutate('igh_trx' = case_when(igh_translocation == 'Not Detected' ~ 1,
# # igh_translocation != 'Not Detected' ~ 0)), formula = tumor_mutation_burden
# ~ purity + ploidy) message('Final linear regression model of common MER SNV
# burden across disease stages') summary(common_snv_mer_tmb_lm)

# Plot the overall TMB by disease stage
total_mer_snv_tmb$tmb_corrected <- resid(total_snv_mer_tmb_lm) + mean(total_mer_snv_tmb$tumor_mutation_burden)

total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb <- total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_tmb = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = droplevels(total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
    dplyr::select(disease_stage, tmb_corrected)), formula = tmb_corrected ~ disease_stage,
    p.adjust.method = "fdr")
total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p.adj)

total_mer_snv_tmb_by_dx <- ggplot(data = total_mer_snv_tmb, aes(x = disease_stage,
    y = tmb_corrected, fill = disease_stage, colour = disease_stage)) + geom_violin(linewidth = 1) +
    geom_boxplot(outliers = FALSE, width = 0.2, color = "white", linewidth = 1.1) +
    labs(y = "MER SNVs per Mb", x = NULL) + scale_y_log10(limits = c(1, 100), labels = c("1.0",
    "10", "100"), breaks = c(1, 10, 100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease\nStage",
    values = disease_stage_colors) + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease\nStage",
    values = disease_stage_colors) + theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45,
    hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans",
    size = 12), axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15), panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = alpha(col = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.2))) +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(4,
        5), ], label = "P={p_string}", y.position = c(1.71, 1.97), tip.length = 0.008,
        label.size = 4, vjust = 0.1) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[2,
    ], label = "{p.adj.signif}", y.position = 1.85, tip.length = 0.008, label.size = 4,
    vjust = 0.1) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(1,
    6), ], label = "P={p.adj}", y.position = c(1.55, 1.78), tip.length = 0.008, label.size = 4,
    vjust = 0.1)

# total_mer_snv_tmb_by_dx <- ggplot(data = total_mer_snv_tmb, aes(x =

# 1.5, 2.5, 3.5), xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5), ymin = 1, ymax = 100, alpha =

# ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data =
# total_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(1,6),], label = '{p.adj}',
# y.position = c(1.59,1.76), tip.length = 0.005, label.size = 3.5, vjust = 0.1)

# rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = droplevels(common_mer_snv_tmb %>%

# mer_snv_tmb_by_dx <- ggplot(data = common_mer_snv_tmb, aes(x = disease_stage,
# y = tmb_corrected)) + annotate(geom = 'rect', xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5),
# xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5), ymin = 1, ymax = 100, alpha = 0.25, fill =
# disease_stage_colors) + ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = 'compactswarm',
# corral = 'wrap') + labs(y = 'SNVs per Mb)',#expression('log'[10]*'(TMB)'), x
# = NULL) + scale_y_log10(labels = c('1.0', '10','100'), breaks = c(1.0,
# 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle =
# 45, hjust = 0.95, family = 'sans', size = 14), axis.ticks.x =
# element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_text(family = 'sans', size = 12),
# axis.title.y = element_text(family = 'sans', size = 16), panel.grid.major.y =
# element_line(colour = 'gray75'), panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
# panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) + annotate(geom = 'segment', x =
# c(0.75, 1.75, 2.75, 3.75), xend = c(1.25, 2.25, 3.25, 4.25), y =
# common_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb$median_tmb, yend =
# common_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb$median_tmb, color = 'purple2', linewidth = 2.25)
# + annotate(geom = 'text', x = c(1,2,3,4), y = 120, label =
# as.character(round(common_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb$median_tmb, digits = 2)),
# size = 5) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data =
# common_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(1,4),], label = 'P={p_string}',
# y.position = c(1.58,1.68), tip.length = 0.005, label.size = 3.5, vjust = 0.1)
# + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data =
# common_mer_snv_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(2,6),], label =
# '{p.adj.signif}', y.position = c(1.80,1.87), tip.length = 0.005, label.size =
# 3.5, vjust = 0.1)

########### INDELS ############ Plot the overall TMB by disease stage

# Correct the TMB by age by fitting a linear model
test_total_indel_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = total_mer_indel_tmb, formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~
    age + igh_translocation + purity + ploidy)
summary(test_total_indel_mer_tmb_lm)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ age + igh_translocation + 
##     purity + ploidy, data = total_mer_indel_tmb)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -4.8708 -2.3600 -0.8719  1.3264 13.3549 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                Estimate Std. Error t value  Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                    0.159212   2.399034   0.066    0.9472    
## age                           -0.002765   0.025350  -0.109    0.9132    
## igh_translocationt(4;14)       1.474443   0.783595   1.882    0.0613 .  
## igh_translocationt(14;16)      4.761876   2.486466   1.915    0.0568 .  
## igh_translocationt(14;20)      1.065257   1.484083   0.718    0.4737    
## igh_translocationt(6;14)       0.663725   2.466676   0.269    0.7881    
## igh_translocationt(8;14)       6.759808   1.515404   4.461 0.0000133 ***
## igh_translocationNot Detected  0.910369   0.612570   1.486    0.1387    
## purity                         2.421918   1.023932   2.365    0.0189 *  
## ploidy                         1.072104   0.763933   1.403    0.1620    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 3.414 on 209 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1393, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1022 
## F-statistic: 3.757 on 9 and 209 DF,  p-value: 0.0002108


total_indel_mer_tmb_lm <- lm(data = total_mer_indel_tmb, formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~
    purity + ploidy)
summary(total_indel_mer_tmb_lm)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tumor_mutation_burden ~ purity + ploidy, data = total_mer_indel_tmb)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -4.9670 -2.7604 -0.9166  1.5720 13.0604 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept) -0.09392    1.85150  -0.051   0.9596  
## purity       2.17307    1.04260   2.084   0.0383 *
## ploidy       1.62731    0.73523   2.213   0.0279 *
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 3.545 on 216 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.04094,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.03206 
## F-statistic:  4.61 on 2 and 216 DF,  p-value: 0.01095

# Plot the overall TMB by disease stage
total_mer_indel_tmb$tmb_corrected <- resid(total_indel_mer_tmb_lm) + mean(total_mer_indel_tmb$tumor_mutation_burden)

total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb <- total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_tmb = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = droplevels(total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
    dplyr::select(disease_stage, tmb_corrected)), formula = tmb_corrected ~ disease_stage,
    p.adjust.method = "fdr")
total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output$p.adj)


total_mer_indel_tmb_by_dx <- ggplot(data = total_mer_indel_tmb, aes(x = disease_stage,
    y = tmb_corrected, fill = disease_stage, colour = disease_stage)) + geom_violin(linewidth = 1) +
    geom_boxplot(outliers = FALSE, width = 0.2, color = "white", linewidth = 1.1) +
    labs(y = "MER InDels per Mb", x = NULL) + scale_y_log10(limits = c(-2, 100),
    labels = c("0.1", "1.0", "10", "100"), breaks = c(0.1, 1, 10, 100), expand = c(0.03,
        0.03)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease\nStage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
    scale_color_manual(name = "Disease\nStage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
    theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95,
        family = "sans", size = 14), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans",
        size = 12), axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15), panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = alpha(col = "black", alpha = 0.2))) +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[4,
        ], label = "P={p.adj}", y.position = 1.45, tip.length = 0.008, label.size = 4,
        vjust = 0.1) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(1,
    2, 6), ], label = "{p.adj.signif}", y.position = c(1.3, 1.71, 1.6), tip.length = 0.008,
    label.size = 4, vjust = 0.1)
## Warning in transform$transform(limits): NaNs produced

# total_mer_indel_tmb_by_dx <- ggplot(data = total_mer_indel_tmb, aes(x =
# disease_stage, y = tmb_corrected)) + annotate(geom = 'rect', xmin = c(0.5,
# 1.5, 2.5, 3.5), xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5), ymin = 0.1, ymax = 100, alpha =
# 0.25, fill = disease_stage_colors) + ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method =
# 'compactswarm', corral = 'wrap') + labs(y = 'MER InDels per
# Mb',#expression('log'[10]*'(TMB)'), x = NULL) + scale_y_log10(labels =
# c('0.1','1.0', '10','100'), breaks = c(0.1,1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03,
# 0.03)) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family =
# 'sans', size = 14), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.y =

# total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb$median_tmb, color = 'purple2', linewidth =
# 2.25) + annotate(geom = 'text', x = c(1,2,3,4), y = 120, label =
# as.character(round(total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb$median_tmb, digits = 2)),
# size = 5) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data =
# total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[4,], label = 'P={p.adj}',
# y.position = 1.45, tip.length = 0.005, label.size = 3.5, vjust = 0.1) +
# ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data =
# total_mer_indel_dx_median_tmb_wilcox_output[c(1,2,6),], label =
