# label.size = 3.5, vjust = 0.1)

Check SNV MER metrics when split by translocation subtype

Code 
median_snv_per_mer_by_trx <- total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
  dplyr::group_by(igh_translocation) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_tmb_corrected" = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

snv_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output <- total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
  dplyr::select(igh_translocation, tmb_corrected) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!igh_translocation %in% c("t(14;16)","t(6;14)","t(8;14)")) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = tmb_corrected ~ igh_translocation,
                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")
snv_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(snv_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output$p.adj)

message("Median MER SNVs per t(11;14): ", median_snv_per_mer_by_trx[1,2])
## Median MER SNVs per t(11;14): 9.82784441369538
message("Median MER SNVs per t(4;14): ", median_snv_per_mer_by_trx[2,2])
## Median MER SNVs per t(4;14): 11.8718445954633
message("Median MER SNVs per t(14;20): ", median_snv_per_mer_by_trx[4,2])
## Median MER SNVs per t(14;20): 7.07311629921329

# SNVs in MERs by TRX
mer_snv_by_trx <- ggplot(data = total_mer_snv_tmb, aes(x = igh_translocation, y = tmb_corrected)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5),
           xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5, 7.5),
           ymin = 1, ymax = 100,
           alpha = 0.25,
           fill = trx_colors) +
  ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = "compactswarm", corral = "wrap") +
  labs(y = "SNVs per Mb",#expression("log"[10]*"(TMB)"),
       x = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = c("1.0", "10","100"), breaks = c(1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 16),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
             x = c(0.75, 1.75, 2.75, 3.75, 4.75, 5.75, 6.75),
             xend = c(1.25, 2.25, 3.25, 4.25, 5.25, 6.25, 7.25),
             y = median_snv_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected,
             yend = median_snv_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected,
             color = "cyan",
             linewidth = 2.25) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
             x = c(1,2,3,4,5,6,7),
             y = 120,
             label = as.character(round(median_snv_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected, digits = 2)),
             size = 5)


########### INDELS #############
median_indel_per_mer_by_trx <- total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
  dplyr::group_by(igh_translocation) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_tmb_corrected" = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

indel_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output <- total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
  dplyr::select(igh_translocation, tmb_corrected) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!igh_translocation %in% c("t(14;16)","t(6;14)","t(8;14)")) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = tmb_corrected ~ igh_translocation,
                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")
indel_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(indel_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output$p.adj)

message("Median MER InDels per t(11;14): ", median_indel_per_mer_by_trx[1,2])
## Median MER InDels per t(11;14): 3.2919850388785
message("Median MER InDels per t(4;14): ", median_indel_per_mer_by_trx[2,2])
## Median MER InDels per t(4;14): 4.97552812425097
message("Median MER InDels per t(14;20): ", median_indel_per_mer_by_trx[4,2])
## Median MER InDels per t(14;20): 4.34306175054947

# InDels in MERs by TRX
mer_indel_by_trx <- ggplot(data = total_mer_indel_tmb, aes(x = igh_translocation, y = tmb_corrected)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5),
           xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5, 7.5),
           ymin = 1, ymax = 100,
           alpha = 0.25,
           fill = trx_colors) +
  ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = "compactswarm", corral = "wrap") +
  labs(y = "InDels per Mb",#expression("log"[10]*"(TMB)"),
       x = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = c("1.0", "10","100"), breaks = c(1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 16),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
             x = c(0.75, 1.75, 2.75, 3.75, 4.75, 5.75, 6.75),
             xend = c(1.25, 2.25, 3.25, 4.25, 5.25, 6.25, 7.25),
             y = median_indel_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected,
             yend = median_indel_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected,
             color = "cyan",
             linewidth = 2.25) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
             x = c(1,2,3,4,5,6,7),
             y = 120,
             label = as.character(round(median_indel_per_mer_by_trx$median_tmb_corrected, digits = 2)),
             size = 5)

Look at SNV MER metrics split by admixture ancestry and then in precursor vs late disease in these subsets

Code 
median_snv_per_mer_by_admix <- total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
  dplyr::group_by(super5_cluster_id) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_tmb_corrected" = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

snv_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output <- total_mer_snv_tmb %>%
  dplyr::select(super5_cluster_id, tmb_corrected) %>%
  #dplyr::filter(!igh_translocation %in% c("t(14;16)","t(6;14)","t(8;14)")) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = tmb_corrected ~ super5_cluster_id,
                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")
snv_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(snv_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output$p.adj)

message("Median MER SNVs per EUR Dominant ancestry: ", median_snv_per_mer_by_admix[1,2])
## Median MER SNVs per EUR Dominant ancestry: 11.1148647846224
message("Median MER SNVs per AFR Dominant ancestry: ", median_snv_per_mer_by_admix[2,2])
## Median MER SNVs per AFR Dominant ancestry: 9.55691399210662

# SNVs in MERs by TRX
mer_snv_by_admix <- ggplot(data = total_mer_snv_tmb, aes(x = super5_cluster_id, y = tmb_corrected)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5),
           xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5),
           ymin = 1, ymax = 100,
           alpha = 0.25,
           fill = super_pop_admix_cluster_colors) +
  ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = "compactswarm", corral = "wrap") +
  labs(y = "SNVs per Mb",#expression("log"[10]*"(TMB)"),
       x = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = c("1.0", "10","100"), breaks = c(1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 16),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
             x = c(0.75, 1.75, 2.75, 3.75, 4.75, 5.75),
             xend = c(1.25, 2.25, 3.25, 4.25, 5.25, 6.25),
             y = median_snv_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected,
             yend = median_snv_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected,
             color = "cyan",
             linewidth = 2.25) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
             x = c(1,2,3,4,5,6),
             y = 120,
             label = as.character(round(median_snv_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected, digits = 2)),
             size = 5) +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = snv_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output[1,],
                             label = "P={p.adj}",
                             y.position = 1.75,
                             tip.length = 0.005,
                             label.size = 3.5,
                             vjust = 0.1)# +
  #ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = snv_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output[c(1,2,6),],
  #                           label = "{p.adj.signif}",
  #                           y.position = c(1.3,1.70,1.60),
  #                           tip.length = 0.005,
  #                           label.size = 3.5,
  #                           vjust = 0.1)

############ INDELS ###################
median_indel_per_mer_by_admix <- total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
  dplyr::group_by(super5_cluster_id) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_tmb_corrected" = median(na.omit(tmb_corrected)))

indel_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output <- total_mer_indel_tmb %>%
  dplyr::select(super5_cluster_id, tmb_corrected) %>%
  #dplyr::filter(!igh_translocation %in% c("t(14;16)","t(6;14)","t(8;14)")) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = tmb_corrected ~ super5_cluster_id,
                                p.adjust.method = "fdr")
indel_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(indel_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output$p.adj)

# InDels in MERs by ancestry
mer_indel_by_admix <- ggplot(data = total_mer_indel_tmb, aes(x = super5_cluster_id, y = tmb_corrected)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
           xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5),
           xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5),
           ymin = 1, ymax = 100,
           alpha = 0.25,
           fill = super_pop_admix_cluster_colors) +
  ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm(method = "compactswarm", corral = "wrap") +
  labs(y = "InDels per Mb",
       x = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = c("1.0", "10","100"), breaks = c(1.0, 10,100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 16),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
  ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
             x = c(0.75, 1.75, 2.75, 3.75, 4.75, 5.75),
             xend = c(1.25, 2.25, 3.25, 4.25, 5.25, 6.25),
             y = median_indel_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected,
             yend = median_indel_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected,
             color = "cyan",
             linewidth = 2.25) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
             x = c(1,2,3,4,5,6),
             y = 120,
             label = as.character(round(median_indel_per_mer_by_admix$median_tmb_corrected, digits = 2)),
             size = 5) #+
  #ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = indel_per_mer_by_admix_wilcox_output[1,],
  #                           label = "P={p.adj}",
  #                           y.position = 1.75,
  #                           tip.length = 0.005,
  #                           label.size = 3.5,
  #                           vjust = 0.1)

Supplementary Figure 3

Check SNV MER metrics when split by disease stage

Code 
# Disease Stage For accurate accounting of mutations per disease stage, need to
# allow for mutations to carry over from disease stage to another for
# multi-sample patients
muts_per_sample <- muts %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample)) %Q%
    (Variant_Classification != "Silent")

# get all SNV per sample in SNV MERs
ncsnv_per_sample <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_per_sample %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS ==
    "SNV"), subject = mers_gr %Q% (mer_type == "SNV"), qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode",
    "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS", "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2",
    "VAF", "Variant_Classification"), scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id", "mut_uniq_id"))
ncsnv_per_sample_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(ncsnv_per_sample)

# Count per sample
ncsnv_per_sample_count <- ncsnv_per_sample_dt %>%
    dplyr::rename(patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

# Add metadata to DT
snv_per_mer_per_sample <- left_join(x = ncsnv_per_sample_count, y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, age), by = "sample")

# group the counts by precursor and late disease
snv_per_mer_per_sample <- snv_per_mer_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::mutate(precursor_late = case_when(disease_stage %in% c("MGUS", "SMM") ~
        "Precursor", disease_stage %in% c("MM", "RR") ~ "Late"))
snv_per_mer_per_sample$precursor_late <- factor(snv_per_mer_per_sample$precursor_late,
    levels = c("Precursor", "Late"))

# Calculate the median for each group and run stats test
# median_snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late <- snv_per_mer_per_sample %>%
# dplyr::group_by(precursor_late) %>% dplyr::summarise(median_n =
# median(na.omit(n))) snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late_wilcox_output <-
# snv_per_mer_per_sample %>% dplyr::ungroup() %>% dplyr::select(precursor_late,
# n) %>% rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = n ~ precursor_late,
# p.adjust.method = 'fdr') snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late_wilcox_output$p_string
# <- scientific(snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late_wilcox_output$p.adj)
# print('Median MER SNVs per Precursor samples:')
# median_snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late[2,2] print('Median MER SNVs per Late
# samples:') median_snv_per_mer_by_precursor_late[1,2]

median_snv_per_mer_by_dx <- snv_per_mer_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_n = median(na.omit(n)))

median_snv_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output <- snv_per_mer_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::select(disease_stage, n) %>%
    droplevels() %>%
    rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = n ~ disease_stage, p.adjust.method = "fdr")
median_snv_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(median_snv_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output$p.adj)


snvs_within_mers_by_dx_violin_plot <- ggplot(data = snv_per_mer_per_sample) + geom_violin(aes(x = disease_stage,
    y = n, fill = disease_stage), alpha = 0.6, color = "gray70") + geom_boxplot(aes(x = disease_stage,
    y = n, fill = disease_stage), outliers = FALSE, width = 0.15, color = "gray99",
    linewidth = 1.5) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 325)) + labs(y = "Total SNV count") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = disease_stage_colors) + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95,
        size = 14), legend.position = "none", panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1)) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = median_snv_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output[c(1,
    4:6), ], label = "P={p_string}", y.position = c(255, 285, 300, 315), tip.length = 0.005,
    label.size = 3.5, vjust = 0.1) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = median_snv_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output[2,
    ], label = "{p.adj.signif}", y.position = 270, tip.length = 0.005, label.size = 3.5,
    vjust = 0.1)
# export 5 x 5 portrait

Run similar analysis for InDels

Code 
# For accurate accounting of mutations per disease stage, need to allow for
# mutations to carry over from disease stage to another for multi-sample
# patients
ncindel_per_sample <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_per_sample %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS %in%
    c("insertion", "deletion")), subject = mers_gr %Q% (mer_type == "InDel"), , qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode",
    "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS", "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2",
    "VAF", "Variant_Classification"), scol = c("nearest_gene", "mer_id", "mut_uniq_id"))
ncindel_per_sample_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(ncindel_per_sample)

ncindel_per_sample_count <- ncindel_per_sample_dt %>%
    dplyr::rename(patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

indel_per_mer_per_sample <- left_join(x = ncindel_per_sample_count, y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, age), by = "sample")

median_indel_per_mer_by_dx <- indel_per_mer_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_n = median(na.omit(n)))

indel_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output <- droplevels(indel_per_mer_per_sample) %>%
    dplyr::select(disease_stage, n) %>%
    rstatix::wilcox_test(formula = n ~ disease_stage)
indel_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(indel_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output$p)


indel_within_mers_by_dx_violin_plot <- ggplot(data = indel_per_mer_per_sample) +
    geom_violin(aes(x = disease_stage, y = n, fill = disease_stage), alpha = 0.6) +
    labs(y = "Total InDel count") + scale_fill_manual(values = disease_stage_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = seq(0, 8, 2)) + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 14), legend.position = "none",
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "point",
    x = c(1, 2, 3, 4), y = median_indel_per_mer_by_dx$median_n, size = 4, shape = 21,
    color = "black", fill = "white") + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = indel_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output[c(4),
    ], label = "P={p_string}", y.position = 8, bracket.nudge.y = 0.5, size = 4.5,
    tip.length = 0.005) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = indel_per_mer_by_dx_wilcox_output[c(1,
    6), ], label = "{p.adj.signif}", y.position = c(7, 9), bracket.nudge.y = 0.5,
    size = 4.5, tip.length = 0.005)
# export 5 x 5 portrait

# Now correct for subtype, disease stage, and AA vs EA
indel_per_mer_by_trx <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("insertion", "deletion") & igh_translocation !=
        "t(8;14)") %>%
    droplevels() %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, igh_translocation) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

median_indel_per_mer_by_trx <- indel_per_mer_by_trx %>%
    dplyr::group_by(igh_translocation) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_n = median(na.omit(n)))

indel_per_mer_by_trx_wilcox_output <- indel_per_mer_by_trx %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::select(igh_translocation, n) %>%
    rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = n ~ igh_translocation, p.adjust.method = "fdr")

indel_per_mer_by_trx_plot <- ggplot(data = indel_per_mer_by_trx) + geom_violin(aes(x = igh_translocation,
    y = n, fill = igh_translocation)) + labs(y = "Total InDel count") + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,
    9), breaks = seq(0, 8, 2)) + scale_fill_manual(values = trx_colors) + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 14), legend.position = "none",
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "point",
    x = seq(1, 6, 1), y = median_indel_per_mer_by_trx$median_n, size = 3, shape = 21,
    color = "black", fill = "white")

Snake plot of percentage of unique MERs per sample

Code 
# Snake plot of unique MERs per sample First gather all the SNV MERs per sample
# per disease stage
all_uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx <- complete_mer_snv_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_count <- all_uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "uniq_snv_mers") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_mers_occupied = round((uniq_snv_mers/280 * 100), digits = 3))

# Order all records by % of SNV MERs occupied by sample per dx
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered <- data.table()
for (i in 1:length(disease_stage_order)) {
    uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_count_per_dx <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_count %>%
        dplyr::filter(disease_stage == disease_stage_order[i])

    uniq_snv_mer_pct_order_per_dx <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_count_per_dx %>%
        dplyr::arrange(pct_mers_occupied) %>%
        dplyr::select(sample) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        as.character()
    # Build ordered plot data
    uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered_per_dx <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_count_per_dx

    uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered_per_dx$sample <- factor(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered_per_dx$sample,
        levels = uniq_snv_mer_pct_order_per_dx)

    # Combine per disease stage ordered data to final set
    uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered <- rrbind(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered,
        uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered_per_dx)
}


# Calculate the medians
uniq_snv_mer_pct_occupied_median <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::summarize(median_pct = median(pct_mers_occupied)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()


uniq_snv_mer_pct_occupied_wilcox_output <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = droplevels(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered),
    formula = pct_mers_occupied ~ disease_stage, p.adjust.method = "fdr") %>%
    rstatix::add_xy_position(x = "disease_stage")
uniq_snv_mer_pct_occupied_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(uniq_snv_mer_pct_occupied_wilcox_output$p.adj)

# TESTING
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered %>%
    group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    mutate(stage_median = median(pct_mers_occupied))

uniq_snv_mer_snake_plot <- ggpubr::ggdotchart(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered,
    x = "sample", y = "pct_mers_occupied", color = "disease_stage", palette = disease_stage_colors,
    dot.size = 2.5) + scale_y_continuous(trans = "log1p", breaks = c(0, 1, 10, 100)) +
    geom_hline(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_ordered %>%
        distinct(disease_stage, stage_median), aes(yintercept = stage_median, color = disease_stage),
        linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) + labs(y = "SNV MERs mutated (%)") +
    facet_grid(~disease_stage, space = "free") + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13), axis.text.x.bottom = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x.bottom = element_blank(), legend.position = "none", strip.background.y = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 14), strip.text.x = element_text(angle = 0,
        size = 14))

Compare the occupancy of MERs across clinical, genomic, and demographic features Firt, look at disease stage.

Code 
mer_snv_by_dx_upset <- list(MGUS = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_mgus_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), SMM = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_smm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), MM = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_mm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), RRMM = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_rrmm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort())

mer_snv_by_dx_upset_plot <- upset(data = fromList(input = mer_snv_by_dx_upset), sets = rev(disease_stage_order),
    keep.order = T, order.by = "freq", mainbar.y.label = "Pair Intersection", sets.x.label = "Unique SNV MERs\nPer Disease Stage",
    point.size = 5.25, line.size = 1.8, text.scale = c(1.75, 1.5, 1.5, 1.25, 1.25,
        1.5), sets.bar.color = rev(disease_stage_colors), matrix.color = "black",
    queries = list(list(query = intersects, params = list(rev(disease_stage_order)),
        active = TRUE)))
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the UpSetR package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/hms-dbmi/UpSetR/issues>.
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the UpSetR package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/hms-dbmi/UpSetR/issues>.
## Warning: The `size` argument of `element_line()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use the `linewidth` argument instead.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the UpSetR package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/hms-dbmi/UpSetR/issues>.

common_snv_mers_all_dx <- mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_type == "SNV" & mut_mgus_patients > 0 & mut_smm_patients >
        0 & mut_mm_patients > 0 & mut_rrmm_patients > 0)

message("Percentage of SNV MERs common across MGUS + SMM + MM + RRMM: ", round(229/280 *
    100, digits = 1))
## Percentage of SNV MERs common across MGUS + SMM + MM + RRMM: 81.8
message("Percentage of SNV MERs common across SMM or MGUS + MM + RRMM: ", round(51/280 *
    100, digits = 1))
## Percentage of SNV MERs common across SMM or MGUS + MM + RRMM: 18.2


mer_indel_by_dx_upset <- list(MGUS = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_mgus_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), SMM = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_smm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), MM = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_mm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), RRMM = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_rrmm_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort())

mer_indel_by_dx_upset_plot <- upset(data = fromList(input = mer_indel_by_dx_upset),
    sets = rev(disease_stage_order), keep.order = T, order.by = "freq", mainbar.y.label = "Pair Intersection",
    sets.x.label = "Unique InDel MERs\nPer Disease Stage", point.size = 5.25, line.size = 1.8,
    text.scale = c(1.75, 1.5, 1.5, 1.25, 1.25, 1.5), sets.bar.color = rev(disease_stage_colors),
    matrix.color = "black", queries = list(list(query = intersects, params = list(rev(disease_stage_order)),
        color = "hotpink3", active = TRUE), list(query = intersects, params = list(rev(disease_stage_order[-1])),
        color = "cyan3", active = TRUE)))

common_indel_mers_all_dx <- mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_type == "InDel" & mut_mgus_patients > 0 & mut_smm_patients >
        0 & mut_mm_patients > 0 & mut_rrmm_patients > 0)

Then look at IGH translocation subtype.

Code 
mer_snv_by_trx_upset <- list(`t(11;14)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t11_14_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `t(4;14)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t4_14_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `t(14;16)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t14_16_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `t(14;20)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t14_20_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `t(6;14)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t6_14_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `t(8;14)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t8_14_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `Not Detected` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_trx_not_detected_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort())

mer_snv_by_trx_upset_plot <- upset(data = fromList(input = mer_snv_by_trx_upset),
    sets = rev(names(mer_snv_by_trx_upset)), keep.order = T, order.by = "freq", mainbar.y.label = "Pair Intersection",
    sets.x.label = "Unique SNV MERs\nPer Translocation", point.size = 5.25, line.size = 1.8,
    text.scale = c(1.75, 1.5, 1.5, 1.25, 1.25, 1.5), sets.bar.color = rev(trx_colors),
    matrix.color = "black")

common_snv_mers_all_trx <- mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_type == "SNV" & mut_t11_14_patients > 0 & mut_t4_14_patients >
        0 & mut_t14_16_patients > 0 & mut_t14_20_patients > 0 & mut_t6_14_patients >
        0 & mut_t8_14_patients > 0 & mut_trx_not_detected_patients > 0)

###### INDELS ########
mer_indel_by_trx_upset <- list(`t(11;14)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t11_14_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `t(4;14)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t4_14_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `t(14;16)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t14_16_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `t(14;20)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t14_20_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `t(6;14)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t6_14_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `t(8;14)` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_t8_14_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `Not Detected` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_trx_not_detected_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort())

mer_indel_by_trx_upset_plot <- upset(data = fromList(input = mer_indel_by_trx_upset),
    sets = rev(names(mer_indel_by_trx_upset)), keep.order = T, order.by = "freq",
    mainbar.y.label = "Pair Intersection", sets.x.label = "Unique InDel MERs\nPer Translocation",
    point.size = 5.25, line.size = 1.8, text.scale = c(1.75, 1.5, 1.5, 1.25, 1.25,
        1.5), sets.bar.color = rev(trx_colors))

common_indel_mers_all_trx <- mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_type == "InDel" & mut_t11_14_patients > 0 & mut_t4_14_patients >
        0 & mut_t14_16_patients > 0 & mut_t14_20_patients > 0 & mut_t6_14_patients >
        0 & mut_t8_14_patients > 0 & mut_trx_not_detected_patients > 0)

Then look at ancestry.

Code 
mer_snv_by_admix_upset <- list(`EUR Dominant` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_EUR_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `AFR Dominant` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_AFR_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `AMR Dominant` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_AMR_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `EAS Dominant` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_EAS_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `SAS Dominant` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_SAS_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `Highly Admixed` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_highly_admixed_patients > 0 & mer_type == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort())

mer_snv_by_admix_upset_plot <- upset(data = fromList(input = mer_snv_by_admix_upset),
    sets = rev(names(mer_snv_by_admix_upset)), keep.order = T, order.by = "freq",
    mainbar.y.label = "Pair Intersection", sets.x.label = "Unique SNV MERs\nPer Ancestry",
    point.size = 5.25, line.size = 1.8, text.scale = c(1.75, 1.5, 1.5, 1.25, 1.25,
        1.5), sets.bar.color = rev(super_pop_admix_cluster_colors))

common_snv_mers_all_adminx <- mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_type == "SNV" & mut_t11_14_patients > 0 & mut_t4_14_patients >
        0 & mut_t14_16_patients > 0 & mut_t14_20_patients > 0 & mut_t6_14_patients >
        0 & mut_t8_14_patients > 0 & mut_trx_not_detected_patients > 0)

mer_indel_by_admix_upset <- list(`EUR Dominant` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_EUR_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `AFR Dominant` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_AFR_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `AMR Dominant` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_AMR_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `EAS Dominant` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_EAS_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `SAS Dominant` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_SAS_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `Highly Admixed` = mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_highly_admixed_patients > 0 & mer_type == "InDel") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort())

mer_indel_by_admix_upset_plot <- upset(data = fromList(input = mer_indel_by_admix_upset),
    sets = rev(names(mer_indel_by_admix_upset)), keep.order = T, order.by = "freq",
    mainbar.y.label = "Pair Intersection", sets.x.label = "Unique InDel MERs\nPer Ancestry",
    point.size = 5.25, line.size = 1.8, text.scale = c(1.75, 1.5, 1.5, 1.25, 1.25,
        1.5), sets.bar.color = rev(super_pop_admix_cluster_colors))

common_indel_mers_all_adminx <- mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_type == "InDel" & mut_t11_14_patients > 0 & mut_t4_14_patients >
        0 & mut_t14_16_patients > 0 & mut_t14_20_patients > 0 & mut_t6_14_patients >
        0 & mut_t8_14_patients > 0 & mut_trx_not_detected_patients > 0)

Snake plot of unique MERs per disease stage

Code 
# Snake plot of unique MERs per patient
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, mer_id, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count(mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
    droplevels() %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$sample <- factor(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$sample,
    levels = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count %>%
        dplyr::arrange(n) %>%
        dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
        dplyr::arrange(disease_stage) %>%
        dplyr::ungroup() %>%
        dplyr::select(sample) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        as.character())

# by Dx
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_uniq_mers = median(n))

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count %>%
    rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(formula = n ~ disease_stage, p.adjust.method = "fdr")
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output$p_string <- scientific(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output$p.adj)

# violn box plot
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_violinbox <- ggplot(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count) +
    geom_violin(aes(x = disease_stage, y = n, fill = disease_stage), alpha = 0.6,
        color = "gray70") + geom_boxplot(aes(x = disease_stage, y = n, fill = disease_stage),
    outliers = FALSE, width = 0.15, color = "gray99", linewidth = 1.5) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,
    110)) + labs(y = "Unique SNV MERs") + scale_fill_manual(values = disease_stage_colors) +
    theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45,
        hjust = 0.95, size = 14), legend.position = "none", panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1)) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output[4,
    ], label = "P={p_string}", y.position = 93, tip.length = 0.005, label.size = 3.5,
    vjust = 0.1) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output[c(1,
    5), ], label = "P={p.adj}", y.position = c(85, 98), tip.length = 0.005, label.size = 3.5,
    vjust = 0.1) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output[c(2,
    6), ], label = "{p.adj.signif}", y.position = c(105, 108), tip.length = 0.005,
    label.size = 3.5, vjust = 0.1)


# find point for median bar
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group_midpoint = n/2)

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$group_midpoint +
    c(0, uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[1], sum(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:2)]),
        sum(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:3)]))

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$group_range <- c(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[1],
    sum(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:2)]), sum(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:3)]),
    sum(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:4)]))


uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_snake_plot <- ggplot(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count) +
    geom_point(aes(x = sample, y = n, color = disease_stage), size = 3) + labs(x = "Samples",
    y = "Unique SNV MERs") + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01,
    0.01), limits = c(0, max(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$n) + 15)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors) + theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 11)) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment", x = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint -
        8, xend = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint + 8, y = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians$median_uniq_mers,
        yend = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians$median_uniq_mers, color = "hotpink",
        linewidth = 2.25) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment", x = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint[2],
    xend = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint[3], y = max(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$n) +
        7, yend = max(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$n) + 7, linewidth = 0.33) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = midpoint(x = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint[2:3])[[1]][2],
        y = max(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$n) + 9, label = paste0("P=",
            uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_wilcox_output$p_string[4]), size = 4.5) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(0, uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$group_range[-4]),
        xmax = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_medians_x$group_range, ymin = 0, ymax = max(uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count$n) +
            15, alpha = 0.3, fill = c("gray75", "gray35", "gray75", "gray35"))

Check the relationship between MER SNVs and total SNV burden

Code 
# linear corr between total SNVs in MERs and unique MERs All together
uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx <- left_join(x = gr2dt(ncsnv_per_sample) %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode), y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count(mer_id)

uniq_indel_mers_per_sample_by_dx <- left_join(x = gr2dt(ncindel_per_sample) %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode), y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count(mer_id)

uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count <- uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()
## Adding missing grouping variables: `disease_stage`

uniq_indel_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count <- uniq_indel_mers_per_sample_by_dx %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()
## Adding missing grouping variables: `disease_stage`

# Goal is to describe the number of unique MERs per sample as a function of the
# total SNV burden
mer_correlations <- left_join(x = fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup(), y = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count, by = c("sample",
    "disease_stage")) %>%
    dplyr::rename(total_snv_burden = n.x, uniq_mers = n.y)

indel_mer_correlations <- left_join(x = fish_muts_hq %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup(), y = uniq_indel_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count, by = c("sample",
    "disease_stage")) %>%
    dplyr::rename(total_indel_burden = n.x, uniq_mers = n.y)

# convert mutations from raw total to per 1k
mer_correlations$total_snv_per_1k <- mer_correlations$total_snv_burden/1000

indel_mer_correlations$total_indel_per_1k <- indel_mer_correlations$total_indel_burden/1000

uniq_mer_total_burden_corr_plot <- ggplot(mer_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~
        x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 7, label.y = 10, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 7, label.y = 5, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "SNV Burden (1 k)", y = "Unique SNV MERs") + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors) + theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))

uniq_indel_mer_total_burden_corr_plot <- ggplot(indel_mer_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = total_indel_burden,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black",
    na.rm = T) + geom_smooth(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "black", alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) +
    ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 550,
        label.y = 1, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_indel_burden,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 550, label.y = 0.6, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001,
    na.rm = T) + labs(x = "InDel Burden", y = "Unique InDel MERs") + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,
    7)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
    theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))

# export 6 x 6 portrait SNVs #######
glance(mer_correlations %>%
    lm(data = ., formula = uniq_mers ~ total_snv_per_1k))
## # A tibble: 1 × 12
##   r.squared adj.r.squared sigma statistic  p.value    df logLik   AIC   BIC
##       <dbl>         <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0.182         0.179  12.5      78.0 5.08e-17     1 -1392. 2791. 2802.
## # ℹ 3 more variables: deviance <dbl>, df.residual <int>, nobs <int>

summary(mer_correlations %>%
    lm(data = ., formula = uniq_mers ~ total_snv_per_1k))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = uniq_mers ~ total_snv_per_1k, data = .)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -27.105  -9.468  -0.786   7.480  56.682 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value            Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       28.0644     1.2485   22.48 <0.0000000000000002 ***
## total_snv_per_1k   3.1230     0.3537    8.83 <0.0000000000000002 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 12.53 on 351 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1817, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1794 
## F-statistic: 77.96 on 1 and 351 DF,  p-value: < 0.00000000000000022

####### InDels ##########
glance(indel_mer_correlations %>%
    lm(data = ., formula = uniq_mers ~ total_indel_per_1k))
## # A tibble: 1 × 12
##   r.squared adj.r.squared sigma statistic  p.value    df logLik   AIC   BIC
##       <dbl>         <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0.216         0.213  1.24      62.6 1.11e-13     1  -373.  751.  761.
## # ℹ 3 more variables: deviance <dbl>, df.residual <int>, nobs <int>

summary(indel_mer_correlations %>%
    lm(data = ., formula = uniq_mers ~ total_indel_per_1k))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = uniq_mers ~ total_indel_per_1k, data = .)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.9836 -0.8448 -0.2748  0.6634  4.8856 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value          Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)          1.0141     0.1523   6.660 0.000000000204383 ***
## total_indel_per_1k   4.4190     0.5585   7.912 0.000000000000111 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 1.237 on 227 degrees of freedom
##   (124 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.2161, Adjusted R-squared:  0.2127 
## F-statistic: 62.59 on 1 and 227 DF,  p-value: 0.000000000000111

# Check this relationship in each disease stage
mer_total_snv_correlations_by_dx <- ggplot(mer_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~
        x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 7, label.y = 11, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 7, label.y = 1, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "SNV Burden (1 k)", y = "Unique SNV MERs") + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors) + facet_wrap(facets = ~disease_stage) + theme(strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 13), legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

mer_total_indel_correlations_by_dx <- ggplot(indel_mer_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = total_indel_burden,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black",
    na.rm = T) + geom_smooth(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "black", alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) +
    ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_indel_burden, y = uniq_mers), label.x = 500,
        label.y = 1.1, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_indel_burden,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 500, label.y = 0.6, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001,
    na.rm = T) + labs(x = "InDel Burden", y = "Unique InDel MERs") + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,
    7)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
    facet_wrap(facets = ~disease_stage) + theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))

# MGUS
mgus_mer_correlations <- mer_correlations %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS")

mgus_mer_corr_plot <- ggplot(mgus_mer_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~
        x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 4.5, label.y = 15, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 4.5, label.y = 10, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(title = "MGUS", x = "SNV Burden (1k)", y = "Unique SNV MERs") + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors[1]) + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none", panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# SMM
smm_mer_correlations <- mer_correlations %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "SMM")

smm_mer_corr_plot <- ggplot(smm_mer_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~
        x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 5, label.y = 8, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 5, label.y = 2, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(title = "SMM", x = "Total SNVs (1k)", y = "Unique SNV MERs") + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors[2]) + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none", panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# MM
mm_mer_correlations <- mer_correlations %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MM")

mm_mer_corr_plot <- ggplot(mm_mer_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~
        x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 6, label.y = 10, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 6, label.y = 2, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(title = "MM", x = "Total SNVs (1k)", y = "Unique SNV MERs") + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors[3]) + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none", panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# RR
rr_mer_correlations <- mer_correlations %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "RRMM")

rr_mer_corr_plot <- ggplot(rr_mer_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~
        x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 8, label.y = 20, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_snv_per_1k,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 8, label.y = 15, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(title = "RRMM", x = "Total SNVs (1k)", y = "Unique SNV MERs") + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors[4]) + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none", panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

Check if linear relationship between age and unique MERs

Code 
mer_age_correlations <- left_join(x = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, age), y = uniq_snv_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count,
    by = c("sample", "disease_stage")) %>%
    dplyr::rename(uniq_mers = n) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(age))

uniq_mer_age_corr_plot <- ggplot(mer_age_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5,
        color = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 65, label.y = 7, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 65, label.y = 2, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Unique SNV MERs") + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors[1:4]) + theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))


indel_mer_age_correlations <- left_join(x = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage, age), y = uniq_indel_mers_per_sample_by_dx_count,
    by = c("sample", "disease_stage")) %>%
    dplyr::rename(uniq_mers = n) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(age))

uniq_indel_mer_age_corr_plot <- ggplot(indel_mer_age_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5,
        color = "black", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 30, label.y = 6.9, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 30, label.y = 6.5, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Unique InDel MERs") + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors[1:4]) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 7)) +
    theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))

# export 6 x 6 portrait

# Faceted by disease stage
mer_age_correlations_by_dx <- ggplot(mer_age_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5,
        color = "#FB832D", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 70, label.y = 95, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 70, label.y = 87, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Unique SNV MERs") + scale_x_continuous(limits = c(20,
    100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03), labels = scales::label_comma(accuracy = 1)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,
    100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage", values = disease_stage_colors) +
    facet_wrap(facets = ~disease_stage) + theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

indel_mer_age_correlations_by_dx <- ggplot(indel_mer_age_correlations) + geom_point(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers, fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(aes(x = age, y = uniq_mers), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5,
        color = "black", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 30, label.y = 6.9, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age,
    y = uniq_mers), label.x = 30, label.y = 6.3, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Unique InDel MERs") + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors[1:4]) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 7)) +
    facet_wrap(facets = ~disease_stage) + theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 1))

# Confirm total SNV count is also linear with age
age_total_snv_corr <- ggplot(hq_dataset_metadata) + geom_point(aes(x = age, y = snv_count/1000,
    fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black", na.rm = T) + geom_smooth(aes(x = age,
    y = snv_count/1000), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "#FB832D",
    alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age, y = snv_count/1000),
    label.x = 55, label.y = 24.1, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age,
    y = snv_count/1000), label.x = 55, label.y = 21, size = 4.5, na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "SNV Burden (thousands)") + scale_x_continuous(limits = c(20,
    100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 25), expand = c(0.03,
    0.03), labels = scales::label_comma(accuracy = 1)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors) + facet_wrap(facets = ~disease_stage) + theme(legend.position = "top",
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

age_total_indel_corr <- ggplot(hq_dataset_metadata) + geom_point(aes(x = age, y = indel_count/1000,
    fill = disease_stage), size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black", na.rm = T) + geom_smooth(aes(x = age,
    y = indel_count/1000), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "black",
    alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = age, y = indel_count/1000),
    label.x = 60, label.y = 8.2, size = 4.5, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = age,
    y = indel_count/1000), label.x = 60, label.y = 7.2, size = 4.5, na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "InDel Burden") + scale_x_continuous(limits = c(20,
    100), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 9), expand = c(0.03,
    0.03), labels = scales::label_comma(accuracy = 1)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors) + facet_wrap(facets = ~disease_stage) + theme(legend.position = "top",
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

Supplementary Figure 4

Figure 1

8 SBS signatures at MERs have features of punctuated lineage-specific mutagenesis from endogenous mechanisms and off-target AID, Part 1

Complete SBS profile across all samples

Code 
# WITH SBSBLOOD AND WITHOUT SBS5

# For proper SBS signatures to be called, we must have sufficient number of
# SNVs Typical minimum cutoff is 1000 SNVs (2000 is default for
# SigProfilerTopography) First, collect all SNVs across all MERs
hq_dataset_sigs <- fish_muts_hq[VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV", .(sample, seqnames, start,
    Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)] %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample %in% tmb_fish_eligbile$sample)

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(hq_dataset_sigs) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
hq_dataset_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = hq_dataset_sigs,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)
# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
hq_dataset_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(hq_dataset_sig_96) + labs(title = "High-qualty genomic regions")

# Translate data for use in mmsig
hq_dataset_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(hq_dataset_sig_96)
hq_dataset_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(hq_dataset_sig_96))

# Prep the signature reference for the fitted approach
data(signature_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38)

# SBS1 & SBS5 for clock-like (priority to use SBS5 for all rather than split
# into SBS5 / SBS40) SBS2 & SBS13 for APOBEC SBS8 still unknown SBS9 for Pol
# Eta somatic hyper mutation SBS18 for Damage by reactive oxygen species (ROS)
# SBS84 & SBS85 for canonical AID (excluded in analysis) SBS99 for melphalan
# exposure (included from previous analysis as known signature) SBS45, SBS46,
# and SBS49 for sequencing artifacts (used to identify possilbe exclusion of
# samples, if not excluded via de novo extraction) Add SBSblood as more
# appropriate description of SBS5
sbs_blood <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "sbsBloodRefSig.txt"), sep = "\t",
    header = T)

sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38 <- signature_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38 %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(c("sub", "tri", "SBS1", "SBS2", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS13",
        "SBS18", "SBS84", "SBS-MM1"))) %>%
    dplyr::rename(SBS99 = `SBS-MM1`) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = sbs_blood, by = c("sub", "tri"))

# Fit the signatures Complete set of all MER mutations Break them up in to
# smaller groups to see if this works chunk 1
hq_dataset_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = hq_dataset_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
    strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE, genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE)


# SBS1, SBSblood, SBS8, SBS9, SBS84, SBS18, SBS99, SBS2, SBS13
mmsig_signames_updated <- c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18",
    "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13")
mmsig_colors <- c("#1B9E77FF", "#7570B3FF", "#E7298AFF", "#66A61EFF", "#FB8072",
    "#A6761DFF", "#666666FF", "#D95F02FF", "#E6AB02FF")

hq_dataset_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = hq_dataset_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage) %>%
        droplevels(), by = "sample") %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = c("sample", "disease_stage"), variable.name = "signature",
    value.name = "rel_contrib")

hq_dataset_sigs_order <- hq_dataset_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, disease_stage) %>%
        droplevels(), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sum_aid = SBS9 + SBS84) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(sum_aid) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::select(sample)

hq_dataset_sigs_t$sample <- factor(hq_dataset_sigs_t$sample, levels = hq_dataset_sigs_order$sample)

hq_dataset_sigs_t$signature <- factor(hq_dataset_sigs_t$signature, levels = mmsig_signames_updated)


hq_dataset_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(hq_dataset_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "Samples",
    y = "Relative Contribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))


######## RELATIONSHIPS OF SBS AND METADATA ###########


# SBS1, SBSblood, and the combined SBS1 Relative
sbs1_rel_vs_age <- hq_dataset_sigs_t %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, age), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBS1") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = age, y = rel_contrib)) + geom_point(aes(color = disease_stage)) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred",
        alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(label.x = 55, label.y = 0.17,
    size = 4.5, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_cor(label.x = 55, label.y = 0.15, size = 4.5,
    na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) + labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "SBS1 Contribution (%)") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25, 90), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03,
    0.03), labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors)

# SBS1 Absolute
sbs1_abs_vs_age <- hq_dataset_sigs_t %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, age), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBS1") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
        tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, mutations), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(rel_contrib * mutations, digits = 0)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = age, y = abs_burden)) + geom_point(aes(color = disease_stage)) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred",
        alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(label.x = 55, label.y = 600,
    size = 4.5, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_cor(label.x = 55, label.y = 570, size = 4.5,
    na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) + labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "SBS1 Burden") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25, 90), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors)

####### SBSblood Relative
sbsblood_rel_vs_age <- hq_dataset_sigs_t %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, age), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBSblood") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = age, y = rel_contrib)) + geom_point(aes(color = disease_stage)) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred",
        alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(label.x = 55, label.y = 0.17,
    size = 4.5, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_cor(label.x = 55, label.y = 0.15, size = 4.5,
    na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) + labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "SBSblood Contribution (%)") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25, 90), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03,
    0.03), labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors)

# SBSblood absolute
sbsblood_abs_vs_age <- hq_dataset_sigs_t %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, age), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBSblood") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
        tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, mutations), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(rel_contrib * mutations, digits = 0)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = age, y = abs_burden)) + geom_point(aes(color = disease_stage)) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred",
        alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(label.x = 55, label.y = 3500,
    size = 4.5, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_cor(label.x = 55, label.y = 3300, size = 4.5,
    na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) + labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "SBSblood Burden") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25, 90), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors)

####### Clock-like Relative
clock_rel_vs_age <- hq_dataset_sigs_t %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, age), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature %in% c("SBSblood", "SBS1")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, age, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(clock_like = sum(rel_contrib)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = age, y = clock_like)) + geom_point(aes(color = disease_stage)) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred",
        alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(label.x = 55, label.y = 0.17,
    size = 4.5, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_cor(label.x = 55, label.y = 0.15, size = 4.5,
    na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) + labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Clock-like (SBS1+SBSblood) Contribution (%)") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25, 90), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03,
    0.03), labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors)

########### Clock-like absolute
clock_abs_vs_age <- hq_dataset_sigs_t %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, age), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature %in% c("SBSblood", "SBS1")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, age, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(clock_like = sum(rel_contrib)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
        tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, mutations), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(clock_like * mutations, digits = 0)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = age, y = abs_burden)) + geom_point(aes(color = disease_stage)) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "darkred",
        alpha = 0.75, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(label.x = 55, label.y = 3500,
    size = 4.5, na.rm = T) + ggpubr::stat_cor(label.x = 55, label.y = 3300, size = 4.5,
    na.rm = T, p.accuracy = 0.0001) + labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Clock-like (SBS1+SBSblood) Burden") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(25, 90), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease Stage",
    values = disease_stage_colors)

# SBS9, SBS84, SBS18, SBS99, SBS2, SBS13 mmsig_signames <- c('SBS1', 'SBS5',

# c('#1B9E77FF','#7570B3FF','#E7298AFF','#66A61EFF',

Let’s look at SBS signatures in MERs

Code 
######## WITH SBSBLOOD ##########

# For proper SBS signatures to be called, we must have sufficient number of
# SNVs Typical minimum cutoff is 1000 SNVs (2000 is default for
# SigProfilerTopography) First, collect all SNVs across all MERs
mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

# Unfortunately, there aren't enough mutations per MER to assess relative
# signatures individually TODO: try spliting mers by SE overlap group
# muts_per_mer_for_plot %>% dplyr::group_by(mer_id) %>% dplyr::count() %>%
# dplyr::arrange(desc(n))

# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs First, given that
# there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different samples/patients
# etc.), remove them here
mer_sig_muts <- mer_sig_muts[!duplicated(mer_sig_muts), ]
mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- "MERs"

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = mer_sig_muts, sample.id = "sample",
    chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(mer_sig_96) + labs(title = "MERs")

# Translate data for use in mmsig
mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(mer_sig_96)
mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(mer_sig_96))

# Prep the signature reference for the fitted approach
data(signature_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38)

# SBS1 & SBS5 for clock-like (priority to use SBS5 for all rather than split
# into SBS5 / SBS40) SBS2 & SBS13 for APOBEC SBS8 still unknown SBS9 for Pol
# Eta somatic hyper mutation SBS18 for Damage by reactive oxygen species (ROS)
# SBS84 & SBS85 for canonical AID (excluded in analysis) SBS-MM1 for melphalan
# exposure (included from previous analysis as known signature) SBS45, SBS46,
# and SBS49 for sequencing artifacts (used to identify possilbe exclusion of
# samples, if not excluded via de novo extraction) Add SBSblood as more
# appropriate description of SBS5 Fit the signatures Complete set of all MER
# mutations
mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = mer_sig_96_transpose, sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
    input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL, strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE,
    genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL)

mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(mer_sigs_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood",
    "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
    geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "Samples", y = "Relative Contribution") +
    theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# mer_sig_96_p + mer_mmsig_bar_p + plot_layout(widths = c(1.1,0.075)) export 4
# x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in MERs: ")
## SBS Signatures found in MERs:
round(t(mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)
##           MERs
## SBS1      4.45
## SBS2      0.00
## SBS8      5.76
## SBS9     23.51
## SBS13     0.00
## SBS18     0.00
## SBS84     0.00
## SBS99     0.00
## SBSblood 66.28

# Convert from relative to absolute for the signatures
mer_abs_sig_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(MERs * mer_sig_96$Total, digits = 0))
# ggplot(mer_abs_sig_contrib) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = signatures, y =
# abs_burden))


######## WITH SBS5 ##########

# # For proper SBS signatures to be called, we must have sufficient number of
# SNVs # Typical minimum cutoff is 1000 SNVs (2000 is default for
# SigProfilerTopography) # First, collect all SNVs across all MERs mer_sig_muts
# <- muts_per_mer_for_plot[VARIANT_CLASS == 'SNV', .(mer_id, seqnames, start,
# Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)] # Unfortunately, there aren't enough
# mutations per MER to assess relative signatures individually # TODO: try
# spliting mers by SE overlap group muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
# dplyr::group_by(mer_id) %>% dplyr::count() %>% dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) #
# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs # First, given
# that there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different
# samples/patients etc.), remove them here mer_sig_muts <-
# mer_sig_muts[!duplicated(mer_sig_muts)] mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- 'MERs' # Now
# prepare the data for mmsig # Correct the column names for input to mmsig
# colnames(mer_sig_muts) <- c('sample','chr','pos','ref','alt') # Prep the
# input for mmsig using deconstructSig mer_sig_96 <-
# deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = mer_sig_muts, sample.id =
# 'sample', chr = 'chr', pos = 'pos', ref = 'ref', alt = 'alt', bsg =
# BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38) # Generate a 96 class SBS profile mer_sig_96_p

# mmsig mer_sig_96$Total<- rowSums(mer_sig_96) mer_sig_96_transpose<-
# as.data.frame.matrix(t(mer_sig_96)) # Prep the signature reference for the
# fitted approach data(signature_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38) # SBS1 & SBS5 for
# clock-like (priority to use SBS5 for all rather than split into SBS5 / SBS40)
# # SBS2 & SBS13 for APOBEC # SBS8 still unknown # SBS9 for Pol Eta somatic
# hyper mutation # SBS18 for Damage by reactive oxygen species (ROS) # SBS84 &
# SBS85 for canonical AID (excluded in analysis) # SBS99 for melphalan exposure

# SBS49 for sequencing artifacts (used to identify possilbe exclusion of

# signature_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38[c('sub', 'tri', 'SBS1', 'SBS2', 'SBS5',
# 'SBS8', 'SBS9', 'SBS13', 'SBS18', 'SBS84', 'SBS99')] # Fit the signatures
# mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = mer_sig_96_transpose,
# sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = 'classes',
# sample.sigt.profs = NULL, strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE, genome =
# 'hg38', dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL) mer_mmsig_bar_p <-
# mmsig::plot_signatures(sig = mer_sig_96_out$estimate, samples = F, sig_order
# = c('SBS1', 'SBS5', 'SBS2', 'SBS13', 'SBS8', 'SBS9', 'SBS18',
# 'SBS84','SBS99')) + labs(title = 'MERs') + theme(legend.position = 'right',
# panel.border = element_rect(fill = 'transparent'), plot.title =
# element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14)) #mer_sig_96_p + mer_mmsig_bar_p +
# plot_layout(widths = c(1.1,0.075)) # export 4 x 11 landscape print('SBS
# Signatures found in MERs:') round(t(mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100,
# digits = 2)

Let’s look at the signatures in eligible region of non-coding analysis excluding the MERs

Code 
# Collect all mutations that fall within the eligible regions of the genome
# (same as input for fishHook model)
fish_muts <- grbind(snv_fish, indel_fish)
muts_eligible <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = fish_muts, subject = eligible_bed,
    qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS",
        "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Variant_Classification",
        "SYMBOL"))
muts_eligible_dt <- gr2dt(muts_eligible)

# Let's look at SBS signatures in non-MER First, collect all SNVs across
# eligible region
eligible_sig_muts <- muts_eligible_dt[VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV", .(Tumor_Sample_Barcode,
    seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)]

# Now prepare the data for mmsig and compatability with MER SBS mutations
# Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(eligible_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Subtract out the mutations within the MERs from the eligible region mutations
non_mer_sig_muts <- dplyr::setdiff(x = eligible_sig_muts[, -1], y = mer_sig_muts[,
    -1])

# TODO: try to match samples to mutations in nonMERs, could gain some better
# insight depending on signal
non_mer_sig_muts$sample <- "non-MERs"

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
non_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = non_mer_sig_muts,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
non_mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(non_mer_sig_96) + labs(title = "Total Non-MERs")

# Translate data for use in mmsig
non_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(non_mer_sig_96)
non_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(non_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures
non_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = non_mer_sig_96_transpose, sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
    input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL, strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE,
    genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL)

non_mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = non_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

non_mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(non_mer_sigs_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood",
    "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(non_mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
    geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "Samples", y = "Relative Contribution") +
    theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
