# non_mer_mmsig_bar_p <- mmsig::plot_signatures(sig =
# non_mer_sig_96_out$estimate, samples = F, sig_order = c('SBS1', 'SBSblood',
# 'SBS2', 'SBS13', 'SBS8', 'SBS9', 'SBS18', 'SBS84', 'SBS99')) + labs(title =
# 'Simulated\nNon-MERs') + theme(legend.position = 'right', panel.border =
# element_rect(fill = 'transparent'), plot.title = element_text(size = 14,
# hjust = 0.5))

# Combo plot non_mer_sig_96_p + non_mer_mmsig_bar_p + plot_layout(widths =
# c(1.25,0.1)) export 4 x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in Non-MERs:")
## SBS Signatures found in Non-MERs:
round(t(non_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)
##          non-MERs
## SBS1         5.46
## SBS2         6.50
## SBS8        16.55
## SBS9        28.27
## SBS13        3.35
## SBS18        0.00
## SBS84        0.00
## SBS99        0.00
## SBSblood    39.86

9 Simulating the genomic background

Create a background genome for random sampling that is the eligible regions excluding the MERs

Code 
non_mer_eligible <- eligible_bed %_% gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))

# To control for best representation, random regions will be restricted to
# range of MERs (i.e. control the variance to the same as the observed) Look at
# the distribution of MER widths for proper simulation of similar distribution
# derived from the non-MER background mer_min_width <-
# min(GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))))

# mer_q1_width <- mer_median_width - (IQR(x =
# GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers)))) / 2)
# mer_q3_width <- mer_median_width + (IQR(x =
# GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers)))) / 2)
# mer_max_width <-
# max(GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))))

# Visualize the observed distribution
mer_median_width <- median(GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))))
mer_width_distro <- data.table::data.table(gr2dt(mers)[, 1], gr2dt(mers)[, 2], gr2dt(mers)[,
    3], width = GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers)))/1000,
    region = "MERs", simulation = 0)

mer_width_distro_plot <- ggplot(data = mer_width_distro) + ggdist::stat_slab(aes(x = width,
    y = region, fill = region), show.legend = F) + ggdist::stat_dotsinterval(aes(x = width,
    y = region, fill = region), quantiles = 200, side = "bottom", slab_linewidth = NA,
    show.legend = F) + labs(y = "MERs", x = "Width (kbp)") + scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(10,
    100, 10), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_fill_manual(values = "#9467BD") + theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, vjust = 0.5)) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = mer_median_width/1000, y = 1.07, label = paste(mer_median_width/1000,
        "kbp"))

# https://stats.stackexchange.com/questions/132652/how-to-determine-which-distribution-fits-my-data-best
# https://aursiber.github.io/fitdistrplus/articles/fitdistrplus_vignette.html#Choice
# # Let's fit the MER width distribution instead of making up from quantiles #
# First, test the observed evaluation of kurtosis and squared skewness and a
# number of bootstrap runs # to help identify the best fit of the observed data
# fitdistrplus::plotdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, histo = T, demp = T,
# discrete = F) fitdistrplus::descdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, discrete
# = F, boot = 500) maybe gamma or log-normal
width_lognorm_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, distr = "lnorm",
    discrete = F, method = "mle")
width_gamma_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, distr = "gamma",
    discrete = F, method = "mle")
width_beta_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width/100, distr = "beta",
    discrete = F, optim.method = "BFGS", method = "mle")

# Let's look at the fit summaries summary(width_lognorm_fit)
# summary(width_gamma_fit) summary(width_beta_fit)

# now some fit metrics fitdistrplus::denscomp(ft = list(width_lognorm_fit,
# width_gamma_fit), xlim = c(0, max(mer_width_distro$width)), xlab =
# 'Kilobases', plotstyle = 'ggplot', fitcol = c('#5ABCEB', '#CF4E9C')) +
# scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) fitdistrplus::cdfcomp(ft =
# list(width_lognorm_fit, width_gamma_fit), xlab = 'Kilobases', plotstyle =
# 'ggplot', fitcol = c('#5ABCEB', '#CF4E9C')) fitdistrplus::qqcomp(ft =
# list(width_lognorm_fit, width_gamma_fit), plotstyle = 'ggplot', fitcol =
# c('#5ABCEB', '#CF4E9C')) fitdistrplus::ppcomp(ft = list(width_lognorm_fit,
# width_gamma_fit), plotstyle = 'ggplot', fitcol = c('#5ABCEB', '#CF4E9C')) #
# Check the goodness of fit statistics fitdistrplus::gofstat(f =
# list(width_lognorm_fit, width_gamma_fit), discrete = F, fitnames =
# c('lognorm', 'gamma'))

Now run simulations of the background to see what they look like compared to MERs

Code 
# The regular log-normal fit is best but could be improved by limiting the
# width to same limitations as MERs (i.e. must be 10kbp or greater), This
# requires a truncated log-normal fit with a minimum of 10.0 (kbp)
# https://stackoverflow.com/questions/16947799/fitting-a-lognormal-distribution-to-truncated-data-in-r
dtruncated_log_normal <- function(x, meanlog, sdlog) {
    a_min = min(mer_width_distro$width)
    b_max = max(mer_width_distro$width)
    dtrunc(x, "lnorm", a = a_min, b = b_max, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog)
}

ptruncated_log_normal <- function(q, meanlog, sdlog) {
    a_min = min(mer_width_distro$width)
    b_max = max(mer_width_distro$width)
    ptrunc(q, "lnorm", a = a_min, b = b_max, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog)
}

qtruncated_log_normal <- function(x, meanlog, sdlog) {
    a_min = min(mer_width_distro$width)
    b_max = max(mer_width_distro$width)
    qtrunc(x, "lnorm", a = a_min, b = b_max, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog)
}

width_trunc_lognorm_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, distr = "truncated_log_normal",
    discrete = F, method = "mle", start = list(meanlog = 0, sdlog = 1))
## Warning in fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, distr =
## "truncated_log_normal", : The dtruncated_log_normal function should return a
## vector of with NaN values when input has inconsistent parameters and not raise
## an error
## Warning in fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, distr =
## "truncated_log_normal", : The ptruncated_log_normal function should return a
## vector of with NaN values when input has inconsistent parameters and not raise
## an error

summary(width_trunc_lognorm_fit)
## Fitting of the distribution ' truncated_log_normal ' by maximum likelihood 
## Parameters : 
##          estimate Std. Error
## meanlog -4.446759  12.874631
## sdlog    1.927701   1.652831
## Loglikelihood:  -922.1035   AIC:  1848.207   BIC:  1855.661 
## Correlation matrix:
##            meanlog      sdlog
## meanlog  1.0000000 -0.9992426
## sdlog   -0.9992426  1.0000000

# QQ and CDF plot
trunc_lognorm_fit_cdf <- cdfcomp(width_trunc_lognorm_fit, plotstyle = "ggplot", legendtext = "Truncated Log-Normal",
    fitcol = "#91D1C2", fitlwd = 1, datacol = "#9467BD", xlab = "Data", ylab = "Cumulative Density",
    main = "Empirical & Theoretical CDFs") + theme(legend.position = "inside", legend.position.inside = c(0.7,
    0.05), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), axis.text = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13, color = "black"))

trunc_lognorm_fit_qq <- qqcomp(width_trunc_lognorm_fit, xlim = c(10, 90), ylim = c(10,
    90), plotstyle = "ggplot", legendtext = "Truncated Log-Normal", fitcol = "#91D1C2",
    fitlwd = 4, fitpch = 18) + theme(legend.position = "inside", legend.position.inside = c(0.3,
    0.95), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
# export 5 x 5 landscape

# Let's look at the fitted distribution
trunc_lognorm_fit_testsim_dist <- EnvStats::rlnormTrunc(n = 300, meanlog = width_trunc_lognorm_fit$estimate[1],
    sdlog = width_trunc_lognorm_fit$estimate[2], min = min(mer_width_distro$width),
    max = max(mer_width_distro$width))
# ggplot() + geom_histogram(aes(x = trunc_lognorm_fit_testsim_dist))

#########################################

# R U N A L T S I M U L A T I O N

#########################################

# # Run 300 simulations of sampling non-MER background to set of MER-like
# regions and analyze their SBS composition trunc_complete_sim_distro <-
# data.table::data.table() trunc_complete_sim_sbs <- data.table::data.table()
# trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs <- data.table::data.table() for(i in 1:300) {
# message(paste0('Simulation ', i, ' ...')) # Sample the fitted distribution
# for MER-sized width to be used for random non-MER background rand_kbp_widths
# <- round(x = EnvStats::rlnormTrunc(n = nrow(mers), meanlog =
# width_trunc_lognorm_fit$estimate[1], sdlog =
# width_trunc_lognorm_fit$estimate[2], min = min(mer_width_distro$width), max =
# max(mer_width_distro$width)), digits = 1) * 1000 # Now create simulated set
# of non-MER regions that have similar size characteristics of MERs
# rand_test_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.rand(w = rand_kbp_widths, genome
# = non_mer_eligible)) # Combine all the random simulated regions and find the
# mutations within trunc_complete_sim_distro <-
# gUtils::rrbind(trunc_complete_sim_distro,
# data.table::data.table(gr2dt(rand_test_gr)[,1], gr2dt(rand_test_gr)[,2],
# gr2dt(rand_test_gr)[,3], 'width' = round(GenomicRanges::width(rand_test_gr) /
# 1000, digits = 1), 'region' = 'Simulated\nNon-MERs', 'simulation' = i)) #
# Intersect the random simulated regions with the mutations rand_test_sig_muts
# <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = snv_fish, subject = rand_test_gr, qcol =
# c('Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode', 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode', 'VARIANT_CLASS',
# 'Reference_Allele', 'Tumor_Seq_Allele2', 'Variant_Classification', 'SYMBOL'))
# # Let's look at SBS signatures in random MER-sized non-MER regions per
# simulation rand_test_sig_muts_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(rand_test_sig_muts)[,
# .(Tumor_Sample_Barcode, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele,
# Tumor_Seq_Allele2)] colnames(rand_test_sig_muts_dt) <-
# c('sample','chr','pos','ref','alt') rand_test_sig_muts_dt$sample <-
# as.character(i) # Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
# rand_test_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref =
# rand_test_sig_muts_dt, sample.id = 'sample', chr = 'chr', pos = 'pos', ref =
# 'ref', alt = 'alt', bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38) # Generate a 96 class
# SBS profile and add it to the complete list rand_test_sig_96$n_patients <-
# length(unique(rand_test_sig_muts$Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode))
# trunc_complete_sim_sbs <- gUtils::rrbind(trunc_complete_sim_sbs,
# rand_test_sig_96) # Translate data for use in mmsig rand_test_sig_96$Total<-
# rowSums(rand_test_sig_96) rand_test_sig_96_transpose<-
# as.data.frame.matrix(t(rand_test_sig_96)) # Fit the signatures
# rand_test_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input =
# rand_test_sig_96_transpose, sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
# input.format = 'classes', sample.sigt.profs = NULL, strandbias = FALSE,
# bootstrap = FALSE, genome = 'hg38', dbg = FALSE) # Capture the mmsig SBS
# estimates trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs <-
# gUtils::rrbind(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs, rand_test_sig_96_out$estimate) } #
# Save/load simulation data to save time saveRDS(object =
# trunc_complete_sim_distro, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'nonMerSimulationWidthDistro.rds')) saveRDS(object = trunc_complete_sim_sbs,
# file = paste0(base_data_path, 'nonMerSimulation96Sbs.rds')) saveRDS(object =
# trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'nonMerSimulationMmsigSbs.rds'))

trunc_complete_sim_distro <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationWidthDistro.rds"))
trunc_complete_sim_sbs <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulation96Sbs.rds"))
trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationMmsigSbs.rds"))

# Test each distribution with a wilcox test and test the entire set of random
# simulations
mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width <- gUtils::rrbind(mer_width_distro, trunc_complete_sim_distro)
mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$simulation <- as.character(mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$simulation)
mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$simulation <- factor(mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$simulation)
mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$region <- factor(mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width$region, levels = c("MERs",
    "Simulated\nNon-MERs"))

mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_pvals <- data.table(pvals = rep("NA", 300))
for (i in 1:300) {
    mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_pvals$pvals[i] <- rstatix::wilcox_test(data = mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width %>%
        dplyr::filter(simulation %in% c(0, i)) %>%
        droplevels(), formula = width ~ simulation)$p

}
mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_median_pval <- data.table(median_pval = round(median(as.numeric(mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_pvals$pvals)),
    digits = 3))

mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_distro_vln <- ggplot(data = mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width) +
    ggdist::stat_eye(aes(x = width, y = region, fill = region), scale = 0.9, show.legend = F) +
    labs(y = NULL, x = "Width (kb)") + scale_x_continuous(limits = c(10, max(mer_width_distro$width) +
    18), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "", values = c("#9467BD",
    "#91D1C2")) + theme(axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0,
    vjust = 0.5), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
    x = c(round(median(trunc_complete_sim_distro$width), digits = 0), mer_median_width/1000),
    y = c(2.07, 1.07), label = c(paste(round(median(trunc_complete_sim_distro$width),
        digits = 0), "kb"), paste(mer_median_width/1000, "kb")), color = c("black",
        "white"), size = 4) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment", x = 103, xend = 103,
    y = 1, yend = 2, linewidth = 0.33) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = 105,
    y = 1.5, size = 4.5, angle = 270, label = paste0("P=", mer_vs_sim_non_mer_width_median_pval$median_pval))
# export 4 x 6 landscape

Supplementary Figure 18

10 SBS signatures at MERs have features of punctuated lineage-specific mutagenesis from endogenous mechanisms and off-target AID, Part 2

Visualize the sim profiles

Code 
# Plots for figure panel
sbs_per_sim <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(trunc_complete_sim_sbs[-97])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "trinuc_context")
colnames(sbs_per_sim)[-1] <- paste0("sim", seq(1, 300))
sbs_per_sim_errorbars <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(trunc_complete_sim_sbs)) {
    sbs_pct_per_sim <- sbs_per_sim[, c(1, i + 1)] %>%
        dplyr::rename(count = colnames(sbs_per_sim)[i + 1])

    sbs_pct_per_sim$pct_sbs <- (sbs_pct_per_sim$count/sum(sbs_pct_per_sim$count)) *
        100

    sbs_per_sim_errorbars <- rrbind(sbs_per_sim_errorbars, data.table(trinuc_context = sbs_pct_per_sim$trinuc_context,
        sim = colnames(sbs_per_sim)[i + 1], pct_sbs = sbs_pct_per_sim$pct_sbs))
}

# Set COSMIC standard color scheme
col_96 <- c(rep("#5ABCEB", 16), rep("#050708", 16), rep("#D33C32", 16), rep("#CBCACB",
    16), rep("#ABCD72", 16), rep("#E7C9C6", 16))

per_sim_errorbars_sbs <- sbs_per_sim_errorbars %>%
    dplyr::group_by(trinuc_context) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_sbs_pct = median(pct_sbs), sd_sbs_pct = sd(pct_sbs))

per_sim_errorbars_sbs_plot <- ggplot(per_sim_errorbars_sbs) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = trinuc_context,
    y = median_sbs_pct), fill = col_96) + geom_errorbar(aes(x = trinuc_context, ymin = median_sbs_pct -
    sd_sbs_pct, ymax = median_sbs_pct + sd_sbs_pct), width = 0.3) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,
    0), limits = c(0, max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct) * 0.2 + max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct)),
    labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1, accuracy = 0.1)) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0,
    0)) + labs(title = "Simulated Non-MERs", x = "Trinucleotide Context", y = "Single Base Substitutions (%)") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90,
        size = 5, color = col_96, face = "bold"), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA)) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(0.5, 16.5, 32.5, 48.5, 64.5, 80.5),
        xmax = c(16.5, 32.5, 48.5, 64.5, 80.5, 96.5), ymin = max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct) *
            0.125 + max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct), ymax = max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct) *
            0.2 + max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct), fill = col_96[c(1, 17,
            33, 49, 65, 81)]) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = c(8, 24, 40,
    56, 72, 88), y = max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct) * 0.16 + max(per_sim_errorbars_sbs$median_sbs_pct),
    label = c("C>A", "C>G", "C>T", "T>A", "T>C", "T>G"), size = 4, color = c("black",
        "white", "black", "black", "black", "black"))

# Combo plot of SBS 96s MERs and sim Non-MERs mer_sig_96_p /
# per_sim_errorbars_sbs_plot + plot_layout(ncol = 1, nrow = 2, axes =
# 'collect') export

# Sort the simulated non-MER signatures to show APOBEC clearly detected in
# nearly all, to align well with the other supplementary figure
# non_mer_per_sim_sigs <- trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs %>%
# dplyr::mutate('cum_apobec' = SBS2 + SBS13, 'sample' =
# paste0('sim',dplyr::row_number())) %>% dplyr::arrange(cum_apobec) %>%
# dplyr::select(sample,SBS1,SBS2,SBS5,SBS8,SBS9,SBS13,SBS18,SBS84,`SBS99`)


non_mer_per_sim_sigs <- trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs %>%
    dplyr::mutate(cum_apobec = SBS2 + SBS13, sample = paste0("sim", dplyr::row_number())) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(cum_apobec) %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, SBS1, SBS2, SBSblood, SBS8, SBS9, SBS13, SBS18, SBS84,
        SBS99)


non_mer_per_sim_sigs$sample <- factor(non_mer_per_sim_sigs$sample, levels = non_mer_per_sim_sigs$sample)

non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = non_mer_per_sim_sigs, id.vars = "sample",
    variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

# non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t$signature <- factor(non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t$signature,
# levels = c('SBS1', 'SBS5', 'SBS8', 'SBS9', 'SBS84', 'SBS18', 'SBS99', 'SBS2',
# 'SBS13'))

non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t$signature <- factor(non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1",
    "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

non_mer_per_sim_mmsigs <- ggplot(non_mer_per_sim_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "Simulated Non-MERs",
    y = "Relative Contribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# non_mer_per_sim_mmsig <- mmsig::plot_signatures(sig =
# trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs, samples = F, sig_order = c('SBS1', 'SBS5', 'SBS2',
# 'SBS13', 'SBS8', 'SBS9', 'SBS18', 'SBS84', 'SBS99')) + labs(title = 'Non-MER
# Simulations') + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 15))
# export 5 x 10 landscape

# mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs <- data.table('SBS1' =
# mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS1), 'SBS2' =
# mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS2), 'SBS5' =
# mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS5), 'SBS8' =
# mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS8), 'SBS9' =
# mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS9), 'SBS13' =
# mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS13), 'SBS18' =
# mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS18), 'SBS84' =
# mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS84), 'SBS99' =
# mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$`SBS99`), 'mutations' =
# mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$mutations))

mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs <- data.table(SBS1 = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS1),
    SBS2 = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS2), SBSblood = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBSblood),
    SBS8 = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS8), SBS9 = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS9),
    SBS13 = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS13), SBS18 = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS18),
    SBS84 = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS84), SBS99 = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$SBS99),
    mutations = mean(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$mutations))


mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t$sample <- "Simulated\nNon-MERs"

mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t$signature <- factor(mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1",
    "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

mean_non_mer_sim_mmsigs <- ggplot(mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "Simulated Non-MERs",
    y = "Relative Contribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# round(mean_non_mer_sim_sigs_t$rel_contrib * 100, digits = 2)

# non_mer_mean_sim_mmsig_plot <- mmsig::plot_signatures(sig =
# mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs, samples = F, sig_order = c('SBS1', 'SBS5',
# 'SBS2', 'SBS13', 'SBS8', 'SBS9', 'SBS18', 'SBS84', 'SBS99')) + labs(title =
# 'Simulated\nNon-MERs') + theme(legend.position = 'right', panel.border =
# element_rect(fill = 'transparent'), plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,
# size = 14))

# Combo plot mer_mmsig_bar_p + non_mer_mean_sim_mmsig_plot + plot_layout(ncol =
# 2, guides = 'collect', axes = 'collect')

# Calculate the TMB per simulation compared to MERs normalize by number of
# patients contributing SNVs then get TMB
mer_tmb <- (mer_sig_96$Total/(muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()))/(sum(mers$width)/1000000)

trunc_complete_sim_distro$simulation <- factor(trunc_complete_sim_distro$simulation,
    levels = seq(1, 300))
non_mer_sim_tmb <- trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$mutations/(trunc_complete_sim_distro %>%
    dplyr::group_by(simulation) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(tot_sim_size = (sum(width) * 1000)/1000000) %>%
    dplyr::select(tot_sim_size))

# normalize by number of patients contributing SNVs then get TMB
non_mer_sim_tmb <- (trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs$mutations/trunc_complete_sim_sbs$n_patients)/(trunc_complete_sim_distro %>%
    dplyr::group_by(simulation) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(tot_sim_size = (sum(width) * 1000)/1000000) %>%
    dplyr::select(tot_sim_size))


# combine the two TMB sets for plotting
mer_vs_sim_tmb <- data.table(region = c("MERs", rep("Simulated\nNon-MERs", nrow(trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs))),
    tmb = c(mer_tmb, as.vector(t(non_mer_sim_tmb))))
mer_vs_sim_tmb$region <- factor(mer_vs_sim_tmb$region, levels = c("MERs", "Simulated\nNon-MERs"))

# now run Z-test of real obs mean vs simulated means non_mer_tmb_sim_set <-
# dplyr::slice_sample(non_mer_sim_tmb, n = 5)
mer_vs_non_mer_tmb_ztest <- BSDA::z.test(x = non_mer_sim_tmb, mu = mer_tmb, alternative = "two.sided",
    sigma.x = sd(non_mer_sim_tmb$tot_sim_size))
message("Results of testing means between MERs and Sim Non-MERs:")
## Results of testing means between MERs and Sim Non-MERs:
mer_vs_non_mer_tmb_ztest
## 
##  One-sample z-Test
## 
## data:  non_mer_sim_tmb
## z = -907, p-value < 0.00000000000000022
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 9.417861
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  2.163881 2.195164
## sample estimates:
## mean of x 
##  2.179523
mer_vs_non_mer_tmb_fc <- data.table(fc = round(mer_tmb/mean(non_mer_sim_tmb$tot_sim_size),
    digits = 2), p_string = "P<0.0001")

tmb_mer_vs_sim_plot <- ggplot(mer_vs_sim_tmb) + ggbeeswarm::geom_quasirandom(aes(x = "Genomic Region",
    y = tmb, fill = region), size = 4.25, shape = 21, color = "black") + labs(y = "TMB (muts. per Mb)",
    x = NULL) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Genomic\nRegions", values = c("#9467BD",
    "#91D1C2")) + scale_y_log10(expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + theme(legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank()) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
    x = 0.75, xend = 1.25, y = mean(non_mer_sim_tmb$tot_sim_size), yend = mean(non_mer_sim_tmb$tot_sim_size),
    color = "black", linewidth = 2.7) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = 1, y = 8,
    label = paste0("Mean FC=", mer_vs_non_mer_tmb_fc$fc, "\n", mer_vs_non_mer_tmb_fc$p_string))
# export 4 x 5 portrait

message("MER per patient TMB: ", round(mer_tmb, digits = 2))
## MER per patient TMB: 9.42
message("Simulated Non-MER per patient TMB: ", round(mean(non_mer_sim_tmb$tot_sim_size),
    digits = 2))
## Simulated Non-MER per patient TMB: 2.18

# Difference in signatures in MERs vs Non-MERs Look at distribution of SBS
# contribution
sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig <- reshape2::melt(data = trunc_complete_mmsig_sbs[, -10],
    value.name = "rel_contrib_per_sim", variable.name = "signature") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(signature) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(rel_contrib = mean(rel_contrib_per_sim) * 100)
## No id variables; using all as measure variables
sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig$region <- "Simulated\nNon-MERs"

sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig <- rrbind(sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig, data.table(signature = rownames(t(mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])),
    t(mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, region = "MERs") %>%
    dplyr::rename(rel_contrib = MERs))


sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig$region <- factor(sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig$region, levels = c("MERs",
    "Simulated\nNon-MERs"))

sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig$signature <- factor(x = sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig$signature,
    levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS2", "SBS13", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS18", "SBS84",
        "SBS99"))

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_diffs <- ggplot(data = sim_mmsig_contrib_per_sig, aes(x = signature,
    y = rel_contrib, fill = region, label = round(rel_contrib, digits = 2))) + ggplot2::geom_col(position = "dodge") +
    ggfittext::geom_bar_text(position = "dodge", outside = T, contrast = T) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Region",
    values = c("#9467BD", "#91D1C2")) + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1,
    scale = 1)) + labs(y = "Contribution (%)") + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, color = c("#1B9E77", "#7570B3",
        "#D95F02", "#E6AB02", "#E7298A", "#66A61E", "#A6761D", "#FB8072", "#666666")),
    legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 12), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
# export 6 x 10 landscape

Extracted MER de novo SBS signature SBS96A decomposition into COSMIC signatures

Code 
# Run the workflow
denovo_sbs96a_decomp_plot <- create_decomposition_figure(denovo_txt = paste0(base_data_path,
    "sigProfilerSBSandIDsigs/mers/SBS96/Suggested_Solution/SBS96_De-Novo_Solution/Signatures/SBS96_De-Novo_Signatures.txt"),
    decomposition_csv = paste0(base_data_path, "sigProfilerSBSandIDsigs/mers/SBS96/Suggested_Solution/COSMIC_SBS96_Decomposed_Solution/De_Novo_map_to_COSMIC_SBS96.csv"),
    cosmic_reference = paste0(base_data_path, "sigProfilerSBSandIDsigs/mers/SBS96/Suggested_Solution/COSMIC_SBS96_Decomposed_Solution/Signatures/COSMIC_SBS96_Signatures.txt"),
    sig_type = "SBS")
## ✔ Parsed de novo signature: Signature SBS96A
## ℹ Contributing COSMIC signatures:
##   signature percentage
## 1      SBS5      62.24
## 2      SBS3      31.88
## 3      SBS1       5.88

Supplementary Figure 5

Look at the contribution across disease stages Starting with signatures in MGUS MERs

Code 
##################################### SPLIT BY DISEASE STAGE
raw_mgus_mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV" & disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs First, given that
# there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different samples/patients
# etc.), remove them here
mgus_mer_sig_muts <- raw_mgus_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::select(-sample) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
mgus_mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- "MGUS"

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(mgus_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
mgus_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = mgus_mer_sig_muts,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
mgus_mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(mgus_mer_sig_96) + labs(title = "MGUS MERs") + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14,
    hjust = 0.5))

# Translate data for use in mmsig
mgus_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(mgus_mer_sig_96)
mgus_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(mgus_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures Complete set of all MER mutations
mgus_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = mgus_mer_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
    strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE, genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL)

mgus_mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = mgus_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

mgus_mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(mgus_mer_sigs_t$signature, levels = mmsig_signames_updated)

mgus_mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(mgus_mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "MGUS",
    y = "Relative Contribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# mer_sig_96_p + mer_mmsig_bar_p + plot_layout(widths = c(1.1,0.075)) export 4
# x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in MERs:")
## SBS Signatures found in MERs:
round(t(mgus_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)
##           MGUS
## SBS1      2.48
## SBS2      0.00
## SBS8      0.00
## SBS9     21.80
## SBS13     0.00
## SBS18     0.00
## SBS84     0.00
## SBS99     0.00
## SBSblood 75.72

# Convert from relative to absolute for the signatures
mgus_mer_abs_sig_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(mgus_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(MGUS * (mgus_mer_sig_96$Total/n_distinct(raw_mgus_mer_sig_muts$sample)),
        digits = 0), group = "MGUS")
# ggplot(mgus_mer_abs_sig_contrib) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = signatures, y =
# abs_burden))

Signatures in SMM MERs

Code 
##################################### SPLIT BY DISEASE STAGE
raw_smm_mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV" & disease_stage == "SMM") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs First, given that
# there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different samples/patients
# etc.), remove them here
smm_mer_sig_muts <- raw_smm_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::select(-sample) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
smm_mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- "SMM"

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(smm_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
smm_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = smm_mer_sig_muts,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
smm_mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(smm_mer_sig_96) + labs(title = "SMM MERs") + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14,
    hjust = 0.5))

# Translate data for use in mmsig
smm_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(smm_mer_sig_96)
smm_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(smm_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures Complete set of all MER mutations
smm_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = smm_mer_sig_96_transpose, sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
    input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL, strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE,
    genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL)

smm_mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = smm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

smm_mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(smm_mer_sigs_t$signature, levels = mmsig_signames_updated)

smm_mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(smm_mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
    geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "SMM", y = "Relative Contribution") +
    theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# mer_sig_96_p + mer_mmsig_bar_p + plot_layout(widths = c(1.1,0.075)) export 4
# x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in MERs:")
## SBS Signatures found in MERs:
round(t(smm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)
##            SMM
## SBS1      4.28
## SBS2      0.00
## SBS8      0.00
## SBS9     28.25
## SBS13     0.00
## SBS18     0.00
## SBS84     0.00
## SBS99     0.00
## SBSblood 67.47

# Convert from relative to absolute for the signatures
smm_mer_abs_sig_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(smm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(SMM * (smm_mer_sig_96$Total/n_distinct(raw_smm_mer_sig_muts$sample)),
        digits = 0), group = "SMM")
# ggplot(smm_mer_abs_sig_contrib) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = signatures, y =
# abs_burden))

Signatures in MM MERs

Code 
raw_mm_mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV" & disease_stage == "MM") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs First, given that
# there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different samples/patients
# etc.), remove them here
mm_mer_sig_muts <- raw_mm_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::select(-sample) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
mm_mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- "MM"

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(mm_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
mm_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = mm_mer_sig_muts, sample.id = "sample",
    chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
mm_mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(mm_mer_sig_96) + labs(title = "MM MERs") + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14,
    hjust = 0.5))

# Translate data for use in mmsig
mm_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(mm_mer_sig_96)
mm_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(mm_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures Complete set of all MER mutations
mm_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = mm_mer_sig_96_transpose, sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
    input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL, strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE,
    genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL)

mm_mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = mm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

mm_mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(mm_mer_sigs_t$signature, levels = mmsig_signames_updated)

mm_mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(mm_mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
    geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "MM", y = "Relative Contribution") +
    theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# mer_sig_96_p + mer_mmsig_bar_p + plot_layout(widths = c(1.1,0.075)) export 4
# x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in MERs:")
## SBS Signatures found in MERs:
round(t(mm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)
##             MM
## SBS1      4.20
## SBS2      0.00
## SBS8      0.00
## SBS9     23.97
## SBS13     2.09
## SBS18     0.00
## SBS84     0.00
## SBS99     0.00
## SBSblood 69.75

# Convert from relative to absolute for the signatures
mm_mer_abs_sig_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(mm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(MM * (mm_mer_sig_96$Total/n_distinct(raw_mm_mer_sig_muts$sample)),
        digits = 0), group = "MM")
# ggplot(mm_mer_abs_sig_contrib) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = signatures, y =
# abs_burden))

Signatures in RRMM MERs

Code 
raw_rrmm_mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV" & disease_stage == "RRMM") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs First, given that
# there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different samples/patients
# etc.), remove them here
rrmm_mer_sig_muts <- raw_rrmm_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::select(-sample) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
rrmm_mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- "RRMM"

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(rrmm_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
rrmm_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = rrmm_mer_sig_muts,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
rrmm_mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(rrmm_mer_sig_96) + labs(title = "RRMM MERs") + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14,
    hjust = 0.5))

# Translate data for use in mmsig
rrmm_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(rrmm_mer_sig_96)
rrmm_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(rrmm_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures Complete set of all MER mutations
rrmm_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = rrmm_mer_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
    strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE, genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL)

rrmm_mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = rrmm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

rrmm_mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(rrmm_mer_sigs_t$signature, levels = mmsig_signames_updated)

rrmm_mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(rrmm_mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "RRMM",
    y = "Relative Contribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# export 4 x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in MERs:")
## SBS Signatures found in MERs:
round(t(rrmm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)
##           RRMM
## SBS1      5.57
## SBS2      0.00
## SBS8      7.77
## SBS9     19.14
## SBS13     0.00
## SBS18     0.00
## SBS84     0.00
## SBS99     0.00
## SBSblood 67.52

# Convert from relative to absolute for the signatures
rrmm_mer_abs_sig_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(rrmm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(RRMM * (rrmm_mer_sig_96$Total/n_distinct(raw_rrmm_mer_sig_muts$sample)),
        digits = 0), group = "RRMM")
# ggplot(rrmm_mer_abs_sig_contrib) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = signatures, y =
# abs_burden))

Convert relative burden of MER signatures to absolute

Code 
# Need to include difference in relative change to sims
non_mer_sim_sigs_t <- as.data.frame(t(mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs[, -10])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signature") %>%
    dplyr::rename(rel_contrib = V1) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sample = "Simulated\nNon-MERs")

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta <- rbind(mgus_mer_sigs_t, smm_mer_sigs_t, mm_mer_sigs_t,
    rrmm_mer_sigs_t) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = non_mer_sim_sigs_t %>%
        dplyr::mutate(init_val = rel_contrib + .Machine$double.eps) %>%
        dplyr::select(signature, init_val), by = "signature") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_change_from_sim = round(((rel_contrib + .Machine$double.eps) -
        init_val) * 100, digits = 2)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta$sample <- factor(mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta$sample,
    levels = disease_stage_order)

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta$signature <- factor(mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta$signature,
    levels = mmsig_signames_updated)

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta_plot <- ggplot(mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta, aes(x = sample,
    y = abs_change_from_sim, fill = signature)) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
    xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5), xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5), ymin = -20, ymax = 40,
    alpha = 0.15, fill = disease_stage_colors) + geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.75) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03,
    0.03), labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1, scale = 1)) + labs(x = NULL,
    y = "Difference in contribution relative to non-MERs") + theme(legend.position = "right",
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# Look at differences between MERs and non-MERs
message("SBSblood in MERs compared to non-MERs:")
## SBSblood in MERs compared to non-MERs:
mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBSblood")
## # A tibble: 4 × 5
##   sample signature rel_contrib init_val abs_change_from_sim
##   <fct>  <fct>           <dbl>    <dbl>               <dbl>
## 1 MGUS   SBSblood        0.757    0.379                37.8
## 2 SMM    SBSblood        0.675    0.379                29.6
## 3 MM     SBSblood        0.698    0.379                31.8
## 4 RRMM   SBSblood        0.675    0.379                29.6
mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBSblood") %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_diff_pct = mean(abs_change_from_sim))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   mean_diff_pct
##           <dbl>
## 1          32.2

message("SBS9 (AID) in MERs compared to non-MERs:")
## SBS9 (AID) in MERs compared to non-MERs:
mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBS9")
## # A tibble: 4 × 5
##   sample signature rel_contrib init_val abs_change_from_sim
##   <fct>  <fct>           <dbl>    <dbl>               <dbl>
## 1 MGUS   SBS9            0.218    0.339              -12.1 
## 2 SMM    SBS9            0.282    0.339               -5.64
## 3 MM     SBS9            0.240    0.339               -9.92
## 4 RRMM   SBS9            0.191    0.339              -14.8
mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBS9") %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_diff_pct = mean(abs_change_from_sim))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   mean_diff_pct
##           <dbl>
## 1         -10.6

message("SBS1 (clock-like) in MERs compared to non-MERs:")
## SBS1 (clock-like) in MERs compared to non-MERs:
mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBS1")
## # A tibble: 4 × 5
##   sample signature rel_contrib init_val abs_change_from_sim
##   <fct>  <fct>           <dbl>    <dbl>               <dbl>
## 1 MGUS   SBS1           0.0248   0.0565               -3.17
## 2 SMM    SBS1           0.0428   0.0565               -1.37
## 3 MM     SBS1           0.0420   0.0565               -1.45
## 4 RRMM   SBS1           0.0557   0.0565               -0.08
mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBS1") %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_diff_pct = mean(abs_change_from_sim))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   mean_diff_pct
##           <dbl>
## 1         -1.52

# Calculate the absolute burden in MERs vs Non-MERs
non_mer_sim_abs_sig_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs[, -10])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(V1 * (mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs$mutations/mean(trunc_complete_sim_sbs$n_patients)),
        digits = 0), group = "Simulated\nNon-MERs")

# if raw difference from sim vs disease stage is necessary (i.e. negative
# values)
sig_abs_burden_comparison <- tibble::as_tibble(rbind(mgus_mer_abs_sig_contrib[c(1,
    3, 4)], smm_mer_abs_sig_contrib[, c(1, 3, 4)], mm_mer_abs_sig_contrib[, c(1,
    3, 4)], rrmm_mer_abs_sig_contrib[, c(1, 3, 4)], non_mer_sim_abs_sig_contrib[,
    c(1, 3, 4)]))

# sig_abs_burden_comparison$group <- factor(sig_abs_burden_comparison$group,
# levels = c('MGUS','SMM','MM','RRMM'))

sig_abs_burden_comparison$group <- factor(sig_abs_burden_comparison$group, levels = c("MGUS",
    "SMM", "MM", "RRMM", "Simulated\nNon-MERs"))

sig_abs_burden_comparison$signatures <- factor(sig_abs_burden_comparison$signatures,
    levels = mmsig_signames_updated)

sig_abs_burden_barplot <- ggplot(sig_abs_burden_comparison, aes(x = group, y = abs_burden,
    fill = signatures)) + ggplot2::geom_col(position = "dodge") + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03, 0.03), limits = c(0,
    45)) + labs(x = NULL, y = "Absolute Burden") + theme(legend.position = "right",
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# ggplot(sig_abs_burden_comparison, aes(x = group, y = abs_foldchange_from_sim,
# fill = signatures)) + annotate(geom = 'rect', xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5),
# xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5), ymin = -5, ymax = 5, alpha = 0.15, fill =
# disease_stage_colors) + ggplot2::geom_col(position = 'dodge') +
# scale_fill_manual(name = 'Signature', values = mmsig_colors) +
# scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03,0.03), limits = c(0,15)) + labs(x = NULL,
# y = 'Mutations') + theme(legend.position = 'right', axis.ticks.x =
# element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = 'transparent'))

# Look at fold change in snv burden differences between MERs and non-MERs
sig_abs_burden_fc_comparison <- rbind(mgus_mer_abs_sig_contrib[, c(1, 3, 4)], smm_mer_abs_sig_contrib[,
    c(1, 3, 4)], mm_mer_abs_sig_contrib[, c(1, 3, 4)], rrmm_mer_abs_sig_contrib[,
    c(1, 3, 4)]) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = non_mer_sim_abs_sig_contrib %>%
        dplyr::mutate(init_val = abs_burden + .Machine$double.eps) %>%
        dplyr::select(signatures, init_val), by = "signatures") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(group) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_foldchange_from_sim = ((abs_burden + .Machine$double.eps) -
        init_val)/init_val) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()


message("SBSblood in MERs compared to non-MERs:")
## SBSblood in MERs compared to non-MERs:
sig_abs_burden_fc_comparison %>%
    dplyr::filter(signatures == "SBSblood")
## # A tibble: 4 × 5
##   signatures abs_burden group init_val abs_foldchange_from_sim
##   <chr>           <dbl> <chr>    <dbl>                   <dbl>
## 1 SBSblood           43 MGUS         5                     7.6
## 2 SBSblood           25 SMM          5                     4  
## 3 SBSblood           35 MM           5                     6  
## 4 SBSblood           33 RRMM         5                     5.6
sig_abs_burden_fc_comparison %>%
    dplyr::filter(signatures == "SBSblood") %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_diff_fc = mean(abs_foldchange_from_sim))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   mean_diff_fc
##          <dbl>
## 1          5.8

message("SBS9 in MERs compared to non-MERs:")
## SBS9 in MERs compared to non-MERs:
sig_abs_burden_fc_comparison %>%
    dplyr::filter(signatures == "SBS9")
## # A tibble: 4 × 5
##   signatures abs_burden group init_val abs_foldchange_from_sim
##   <chr>           <dbl> <chr>    <dbl>                   <dbl>
## 1 SBS9               12 MGUS         4                    2   
## 2 SBS9               10 SMM          4                    1.5 
## 3 SBS9               12 MM           4                    2   
## 4 SBS9                9 RRMM         4                    1.25
sig_abs_burden_fc_comparison %>%
    dplyr::filter(signatures == "SBS9") %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_diff_fc = mean(abs_foldchange_from_sim))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   mean_diff_fc
##          <dbl>
## 1         1.69

Extracted de novo InDel signature ID83A decomposition into COSMIC signatures

Code 
# Run the workflow
denovo_id83a_decomp_plot <- create_decomposition_figure(denovo_txt = paste0(base_data_path,
    "sigProfilerSBSandIDsigs/mers/ID83/Suggested_Solution/ID83_De-Novo_Solution/Signatures/ID83_De-Novo_Signatures.txt"),
    decomposition_csv = paste0(base_data_path, "sigProfilerSBSandIDsigs/mers/ID83/Suggested_Solution/COSMIC_ID83_Decomposed_Solution/De_Novo_map_to_COSMIC_ID83.csv"),
    cosmic_reference = paste0(base_data_path, "sigProfilerSBSandIDsigs/mers/ID83/Suggested_Solution/COSMIC_ID83_Decomposed_Solution/Signatures/COSMIC_ID83_Signatures.txt"),
    sig_type = "ID")
## ✔ Parsed de novo signature: Signature ID83A
## ℹ Contributing COSMIC signatures:
##   signature percentage
## 1       ID5      57.70
## 2       ID2      22.74
## 3       ID1      19.56

Figure 2

11 The genome topography at MERs is in an accessible and active conformation

SigProfilerTopography analysis output

Code 
sigpro_topography_output <- data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "Step1_Signature_Biosample_DNAElement_PValue.txt"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)
pan_myeloma_topography <- sigpro_topography_output %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "aggregatedsubstitutions" & biosample != "K562") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(topography = str_remove(string = dna_element, pattern = "[ENCFF|RefEpigenome].*_"))

pan_myeloma_topography$biosample <- pan_myeloma_topography$biosample %>%
    dplyr::recode_factor(Myeloma = "Myeloma\nPatients")

pan_myeloma_topography$biosample <- factor(pan_myeloma_topography$biosample, levels = c("Myeloma\nPatients",
    "MM1S", "KMS11"))

pan_myeloma_topography$topography <- factor(pan_myeloma_topography$topography, levels = c("CTCF",
    "H4K20me1", "DNase", "ATAC", "H3K36me3", "H3K27me3", "H3K4me3", "H3K4me1", "H3K27ac"))

occupancy_fc_heatmap <- ggplot(data = pan_myeloma_topography, aes(x = biosample,
    y = topography, fill = fold_change, label = paste0(p_star(p_value), "\n", round(fold_change,
        digits = 2)))) + geom_tile() + labs(caption = p_signif_codes) + ggfittext::geom_fit_text(contrast = T) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(name = "Fold Change\n(Real vs. Simulated)", midpoint = 1,
        low = "#2E2A2B", high = "#CF4E9C", mid = "white") + scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
    theme(legend.position = "top", axis.title = element_blank(), plot.caption = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
# export 7 x 9 portrait

Epigenomics occupancy profiles for MM patients

Code 
# Cycle through epigenomic marks, collect signal from observed mutations and
# each simulation run
epigenomic_mark <- c("H3K27ac", "H3K4me1", "H3K4me3", "H3K27me3", "H3K36me3", "ATAC")
occupancy_enrichment_profile_list <- list()
for (i in 1:length(epigenomic_mark)) {
    # Read in observed signal
    obs_occupancy_profile <- data.table(mean_signal = data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path,
        "epigenomicOccupancy/observed/Myeloma/PanMyeloma-MER_RefEpigenome_Myeloma_",
        epigenomic_mark[i], "_AverageSignalArray.txt")) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        as.numeric(), region = "MERs", pos = seq(-1000, 1000, 1))

    # Read in each simulated signal
    total_sim_occupancy_profile <- data.table()
    for (j in 1:5) {
        per_sim_occupancy_profile <- data.table(signal = data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path,
            "epigenomicOccupancy/simulated/Myeloma/PanMyeloma-MER_", "sim", j, "_RefEpigenome_Myeloma_",
            epigenomic_mark[i], "_AverageSignalArray.txt")) %>%
            purrr::as_vector() %>%
            as.numeric(), region = "Simulated Non-MERs", pos = seq(-1000, 1000, 1))
        total_sim_occupancy_profile <- rrbind(total_sim_occupancy_profile, per_sim_occupancy_profile)
    }

    sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile <- total_sim_occupancy_profile %>%
        dplyr::group_by(region, pos) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(mean_signal = mean(signal), sd_signal = sd(signal))

    occupancy_enrichment_profile_plot <- ggplot() + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black",
        linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.75, alpha = 0.8) + geom_ribbon(data = sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile,
        aes(x = pos, ymin = mean_signal - sd_signal, ymax = mean_signal + sd_signal),
        fill = "gray70", alpha = 0.5) + geom_line(data = rrbind(sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile[,
        -4], obs_occupancy_profile), aes(x = pos, y = mean_signal, color = region),
        linewidth = 1.25) + labs(title = epigenomic_mark[i], x = "Interval around SNV (bp)",
        y = "Mean Signal") + scale_color_manual(name = "Region", values = c("#9467BD",
        "#91D1C2")) + scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

    occupancy_enrichment_profile_list[[i]] <- occupancy_enrichment_profile_plot
}

epigenomic_occupancy_enrichment_plots <- patchwork::wrap_plots(occupancy_enrichment_profile_list,
    ncol = 2)

Figure 3

Epigenomics occupancy profiles for KMS11 cell line

Code 
kms11_epigenomic_mark <- c("H3K27ac", "H3K4me1", "H3K4me3", "H3K27me3", "H3K36me3",
    "H4K20me1", "CTCF")
kms11_encode_id <- c("ENCFF874LYU", "ENCFF452IWV", "ENCFF400AMJ", "ENCFF293AAL",
    "ENCFF838NMF", "ENCFF683CGI", "ENCFF496TKW")
kms11_occupancy_enrichment_profile_list <- list()
for (i in 1:length(kms11_epigenomic_mark)) {
    # Read in observed signal
    kms11_obs_occupancy_profile <- data.table(mean_signal = data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path,
        "epigenomicOccupancy/observed/KMS11/PanMyeloma-MER_", kms11_encode_id[i],
        "_KMS11_", kms11_epigenomic_mark[i], "_AverageSignalArray.txt")) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        as.numeric(), region = "MERs", pos = seq(-1000, 1000, 1))

    # Read in each simulated signal
    kms11_total_sim_occupancy_profile <- data.table()
    for (j in 1:5) {
        kms11_per_sim_occupancy_profile <- data.table(signal = data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path,
            "epigenomicOccupancy/simulated/KMS11/PanMyeloma-MER_", "sim", j, "_",
            kms11_encode_id[i], "_KMS11_", kms11_epigenomic_mark[i], "_AverageSignalArray.txt")) %>%
            purrr::as_vector() %>%
            as.numeric(), region = "Simulated Non-MERs", pos = seq(-1000, 1000, 1))
        kms11_total_sim_occupancy_profile <- rrbind(kms11_total_sim_occupancy_profile,
            kms11_per_sim_occupancy_profile)
    }

    kms11_sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile <- kms11_total_sim_occupancy_profile %>%
        dplyr::group_by(region, pos) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(mean_signal = mean(signal), sd_signal = sd(signal))

    kms11_occupancy_enrichment_profile_plot <- ggplot() + geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
        color = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.75, alpha = 0.8) + geom_ribbon(data = kms11_sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile,
        aes(x = pos, ymin = mean_signal - sd_signal, ymax = mean_signal + sd_signal),
        fill = "gray70", alpha = 0.5) + geom_line(data = rrbind(kms11_sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile[,
        -4], kms11_obs_occupancy_profile), aes(x = pos, y = mean_signal, color = region),
        linewidth = 1.25) + labs(title = kms11_epigenomic_mark[i], x = "Interval around SNV (bp)",
        y = "Mean Signal") + scale_color_manual(name = "Region", values = c("#9467BD",
        "#43A245")) + scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

    kms11_occupancy_enrichment_profile_list[[i]] <- kms11_occupancy_enrichment_profile_plot
}

kms11_epigenomic_occupancy_enrichment_plots <- patchwork::wrap_plots(kms11_occupancy_enrichment_profile_list,
    ncol = 2)

Epigenomics occupancy profiles for MM1.S cell line

Code 
mm1s_epigenomic_mark <- c("H3K4me1", "H3K4me3", "H3K27me3", "H3K36me3", "DNase",
    "H4K20me1", "CTCF")
mm1s_encode_id <- c("ENCFF653YKZ", "ENCFF825UPB", "ENCFF577YXE", "ENCFF121HFO", "ENCFF148MKR",
    "ENCFF362XCO", "ENCFF539QXX")
mm1s_occupancy_enrichment_profile_list <- list()
for (i in 1:length(mm1s_epigenomic_mark)) {
    # Read in observed signal
    mm1s_obs_occupancy_profile <- data.table(mean_signal = data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path,
        "epigenomicOccupancy/observed/MM1S/PanMyeloma-MER_", mm1s_encode_id[i], "_MM1S_",
        mm1s_epigenomic_mark[i], "_AverageSignalArray.txt")) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        as.numeric(), region = "MERs", pos = seq(-1000, 1000, 1))

    # Read in each simulated signal
    mm1s_total_sim_occupancy_profile <- data.table()
    for (j in 1:5) {
        mm1s_per_sim_occupancy_profile <- data.table(signal = data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path,
            "epigenomicOccupancy/simulated/MM1S/PanMyeloma-MER_", "sim", j, "_",
            mm1s_encode_id[i], "_MM1S_", mm1s_epigenomic_mark[i], "_AverageSignalArray.txt")) %>%
            purrr::as_vector() %>%
            as.numeric(), region = "Simulated Non-MERs", pos = seq(-1000, 1000, 1))
        mm1s_total_sim_occupancy_profile <- rrbind(mm1s_total_sim_occupancy_profile,
            mm1s_per_sim_occupancy_profile)
    }

    mm1s_sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile <- mm1s_total_sim_occupancy_profile %>%
        dplyr::group_by(region, pos) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(mean_signal = mean(signal), sd_signal = sd(signal))

    mm1s_occupancy_enrichment_profile_plot <- ggplot() + geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
        color = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.75, alpha = 0.8) + geom_ribbon(data = mm1s_sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile,
        aes(x = pos, ymin = mean_signal - sd_signal, ymax = mean_signal + sd_signal),
        fill = "gray70", alpha = 0.5) + geom_line(data = rrbind(mm1s_sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile[,
        -4], mm1s_obs_occupancy_profile), aes(x = pos, y = mean_signal, color = region),
        linewidth = 1.25) + labs(title = mm1s_epigenomic_mark[i], x = "Interval around SNV (bp)",
        y = "Mean Signal") + scale_color_manual(name = "Region", values = c("#9467BD",
        "#91331F")) + scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

    mm1s_occupancy_enrichment_profile_list[[i]] <- mm1s_occupancy_enrichment_profile_plot
}

mm1s_epigenomic_occupancy_enrichment_plots <- patchwork::wrap_plots(mm1s_occupancy_enrichment_profile_list,
    ncol = 2)

Supplementary Figure 6

12 Distribution of MERs across functional genomic elements of the genome

Let’s look at overlap of MERs with different typese of functional genomic features including CDS, UTRs, E/SEs, Promoters, lncRNA, miRNA, Fragile Sites, and Normal Lymphocyte MERs

Code 
# There are some elements that were splintered after conversion to hg38 with
# ensembl MM1S hg18 --> hg38
liftchain_hg18 <- rtracklayer::import.chain(paste0(base_data_path, "hg18ToHg38.over.chain"))
liftchain_hg19 <- rtracklayer::import.chain(paste0(base_data_path, "hg19ToHg38.over.chain"))

mm_enhancer_elements_hg18 <- dt2gr(fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "MM1SEnhancerAndSuperEnhancers.hg18.bed"),
    sep = "\t", header = F, col.names = c("seqnames", "start", "end", "enhancer_id",
        "enhancer_type")))
mm_enhancer_elements_hg18$tissue <- "PC"

mm_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- rtracklayer::liftOver(x = mm_enhancer_elements_hg18,
    chain = liftchain_hg18)
# Unlist output to get GR
mm_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- gUtils::grl.unlist(mm_enhancer_elements_hg38)
mm_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- gr_refactor_seqs(mm_enhancer_elements_hg38[, -c(4, 5)])

# GC/DLBCL hg19 --> hg38
gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19 <- dt2gr(fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "GCandPostGCEnhancersAndSuperEnhancers.hg19.bed"),
    sep = "\t", header = F, col.names = c("seqnames", "start", "end", "tissue_original",
        "enhancer_type")))
gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19$tissue <- "GCB"
gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19$enhancer_id <- paste0(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19$tissue_original,
    rep("_", length(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19)), gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19$enhancer_type,
    rep("_", length(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19)), seq(1, length(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19)))


gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- rtracklayer::liftOver(x = gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19,
    chain = liftchain_hg19)
# Unlist output to get GR
gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- gUtils::grl.unlist(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38)
gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- gr_refactor_seqs(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38[,
    -c(1, 5, 6)])

# Combine the sets of elements
enhancer_elements <- gUtils::grbind(mm_enhancer_elements_hg38, gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38)

# # Still some splintered elements (by small bp), let's fix here
# splintered_ee_id <-
# unique(enhancer_elements[duplicated(enhancer_elements$enhancer_id)]$enhancer_id)
# stitched_ee <- data.table::data.table() for(i in 1:length(splintered_ee_id))
# { splintered_ee <- enhancer_elements %Q% (enhancer_id == splintered_ee_id[i])
# if(length(splintered_ee@seqnames@values) == 1) { collapsed_ee <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(GenomicRanges::GRanges(seqnames =
# splintered_ee@seqnames@values[1], IRanges(start =
# splintered_ee@ranges@start[1], end =
# splintered_ee@ranges@start[length(splintered_ee@ranges@start)] +
# splintered_ee@ranges@width[length(splintered_ee@ranges@width)] - 1)))
# collapsed_ee$enhancer_id <- unique(splintered_ee$enhancer_id)
# collapsed_ee$enhancer_type <- unique(splintered_ee$enhancer_type)
# collapsed_ee$tissue <- unique(splintered_ee$tissue) stitched_ee <-
# gUtils::grbind(stitched_ee, collapsed_ee) } } # Now combine the stitched with
# the others that were not splintered after conversion to hg38
# non_splintered_ee <- enhancer_elements %Q% (!enhancer_id %in%
# splintered_ee_id) complete_ee <- gUtils::grbind(stitched_ee,
# non_splintered_ee)

# Write out the complete EE list as a BED fwrite(x =
# gr2dt(complete_ee)[,c(1:3,6:8)], file = 'enhancerElements.hg38.bed', sep =
# '\t', row.names = F, col.names = T)

complete_ee <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "enhancerElements.hg38.bed"),
    has_header = T)
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

# Look at distribution of mutations across elements
all_muts_per_ee <- gr.findoverlaps(query = muts_hq, subject = complete_ee, qcol = c("VARIANT_CLASS",
    "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode",
    "SYMBOL"), scol = c("enhancer_id", "enhancer_type", "tissue"))

# Quickly visualize the percentage of mutations within E/SE
total_pct_muts_in_ee <- data.table::data.table(enhancer_type = c("SE", "E", "Other"),
    pct_muts = c(length(all_muts_per_ee %Q% (enhancer_type == "SE"))/length(muts_hq),
        length(all_muts_per_ee %Q% (enhancer_type == "E"))/length(muts_hq), length(gr.setdiff(query = muts_hq,
            subject = all_muts_per_ee))/length(muts_hq)))

Gather all other elements for the overlap analysis

Code 
# The functional genomic elements that will be assessed for overlap with MERs,
# as defined by PCAWG non-coding paper Coding Sequences - Exons of all
# protein-coding genes (from Ensembl v108) 5' UTRs - 5' UTRs of all
# protein-coding genes (from Ensembl v108) 3' UTRs - 3' UTRs of all
# protein-coding genes (from Ensembl v108) Promoters - Promoters for
# protein-coding genes (EPIraction - ENCODE ENCFF801AIE / Ensembl v106, will
# harmonize based on gene name) Enhancers/Super-Enhancers - GCB and PC sets
# from Bal et al and Loven et al Normal Lymphocyte MERs - MERs identified in
# HSCs, Naive B, and Memory B cells (Machado et al) Fragile Sites - HumCFS
# adapted from JaBbA analysis (Kumar et al) Long non-coding RNA - lncRNAs
# adapted from PCAWG (Rheinbay et al) Micro RNA - miRNAs adapted from (Rheinbay
# et al)

########## lncRNA ###########
lncrna_elements_raw <- gene_body_hg38 %Q% (gene_biotype == "lncRNA")

# Now filter the raw set based on PCAWG recommendations no sense overlap (i.e.
# no overlap on same strand)
lncrna_cds_overlap_to_exclude <- lncrna_elements_raw[gr.in(query = lncrna_elements_raw,
    subject = gene_body_hg38 %Q% (gene_biotype == "protein_coding"), ignore.strand = F)]

# lncRNA genes below 200 bp in length
lncrna_small_genes_to_exclude <- gr2dt(lncrna_elements_raw) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(gene_name) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(tot_width = sum(width)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(tot_width < 200) %>%
    dplyr::select(gene_name) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# filter to get final set
lncrna_elements_gr <- lncrna_elements_raw %Q% (!gene_name %in% c(lncrna_cds_overlap_to_exclude$gene_name,
    lncrna_small_genes_to_exclude))

########## miRNA ###########
mirna_elements_gr <- gene_body_hg38 %Q% (gene_biotype == "miRNA")

########## Promoters ########### Read in per chrom set
promoter_sites_gr <- GenomicRanges::trim(read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path,
    "functionalGenomicElements/promoters.hg38.bed"), additional_col_names = "gene_name")) %Q%
    (gene_name %in% unique(genes$gene_name))
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns
# sum(GenomicRanges::width(promoter_sites_gr)) / 1e6
promoter_sites_gr$prom_id <- paste0("prom_", promoter_sites_gr$gene_name)

########## CDS ###########
cds_elements_gr <- cds_regions

########## 5' UTR ###########
five_prime_utr_elements_gr <- five_prime_utr_regions

########## 3' UTR ###########
three_prime_utr_elements_gr <- three_prime_utr_regions

########## Introns ###########
intron_elements_gr <- intron_regions

########## Intergenic ###########
intergenic_elements_gr <- intergenic_regions

Normal lymphocyte mutationally enriched regions Extracting the mutations for the fishHook models

Code 
liftchain_hg19 <- rtracklayer::import.chain(paste0(base_data_path, "hg19ToHg38.over.chain"))

# Read in SNVs from normal lymphs
normal_sites <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "som2_indel_SNV_allDF_v2.machado_normalLymphocytes.txt.gz"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)[, -1]
# Convert to GR and then to hg38
normal_sites_gr <- dt2gr(normal_sites)
## Warning in dt2gr(normal_sites): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
normal_sites_hg38 <- rtracklayer::liftOver(x = gr.chr(normal_sites_gr), chain = liftchain_hg19)
# Unlist output to get GR
normal_sites_hg38 <- gUtils::grl.unlist(normal_sites_hg38)
normal_sites_hg38 <- gr_refactor_seqs(normal_sites_hg38)


lymphocyte_metadata <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "colonyinfo_AX001_KX001_KX002_KX003_TX001_TX002_CB001.txt"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)

hsc_colonies <- lymphocyte_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(Cell.type2 == "HSC") %>%
    dplyr::select(colony) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    unique()

naive_b_colonies <- lymphocyte_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(Cell.type2 == "Naive B") %>%
    dplyr::select(colony) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    unique()

memory_b_colonies <- lymphocyte_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(Cell.type2 == "Memory B") %>%
    dplyr::select(colony) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    unique()

########### HSCs

hsc_muts <- normal_sites_hg38 %Q% (ID %in% hsc_colonies) %Q% (!seqnames %in% c("chrX",
    "chrY"))

# hsc_maflite <- data.table::data.table('Chromosome' =
# gr2dt(hsc_muts)$seqnames, 'Start_Position' = gr2dt(hsc_muts)$start,
# 'Reference_Allele' = gr2dt(hsc_muts)$ref, 'Tumor_Seq_Allele2' =
# gr2dt(hsc_muts)$mut, 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode' = gr2dt(hsc_muts)$ID)

# fwrite(x = hsc_maflite, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/hsc.machado_normalLymphocytes.snv.indel.lite.maf'),
# sep = '\t', row.names = F, col.names = T)

########### Naive B cells

naive_b_muts <- normal_sites_hg38 %Q% (ID %in% naive_b_colonies) %Q% (!seqnames %in%
    c("chrX", "chrY"))

# naive_b_maflite <- data.table::data.table('Chromosome' =
# gr2dt(naive_b_muts)$seqnames, 'Start_Position' = gr2dt(naive_b_muts)$start,
# 'Reference_Allele' = gr2dt(naive_b_muts)$ref, 'Tumor_Seq_Allele2' =
# gr2dt(naive_b_muts)$mut, 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode' = gr2dt(naive_b_muts)$ID)

# fwrite(x = naive_b_maflite, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/naive_b.machado_normalLymphocytes.snv.indel.lite.maf'),
# sep = '\t', row.names = F, col.names = T)

########### Memory B cells

memory_b_muts <- normal_sites_hg38 %Q% (ID %in% memory_b_colonies) %Q% (!seqnames %in%
    c("chrX", "chrY"))

# memory_b_maflite <- data.table::data.table('Chromosome' =
# gr2dt(memory_b_muts)$seqnames, 'Start_Position' = gr2dt(memory_b_muts)$start,
# 'Reference_Allele' = gr2dt(memory_b_muts)$ref, 'Tumor_Seq_Allele2' =
# gr2dt(memory_b_muts)$mut, 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode' = gr2dt(memory_b_muts)$ID)

# fwrite(x = memory_b_maflite, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/memory_b.machado_normalLymphocytes.snv.indel.lite.maf'),
# sep = '\t', row.names = F, col.names = T)

# normal_lymphocyte_muts <- normal_sites_hg38 %Q% (ID %in%
# b_tissueage_colories) %Q% (!seqnames %in% c('chrX','chrY'))
# normal_lymphocyte_maflite <- data.table::data.table('Chromosome' =
# gr2dt(normal_lymphocyte_muts)$seqnames, 'Start_Position' =
# gr2dt(normal_lymphocyte_muts)$start, 'Reference_Allele' =
# gr2dt(normal_lymphocyte_muts)$ref, 'Tumor_Seq_Allele2' =
# gr2dt(normal_lymphocyte_muts)$mut, 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode' =
# gr2dt(normal_lymphocyte_muts)$ID)

# fwrite(x = normal_lymphocyte_maflite, file =
# '~/Downloads/som2_indel_SNV_allDF_v2.machado_normalLymphocytes.snv.indel.lite.maf',
# sep = '\t', row.names = F, col.names = T)


# Pick up here with the MAF file and prep for use in fishHook model
normal_lymphocyte_muts_for_model <- read_maf_file(maf_file_path = paste0(base_data_path,
    "som2_indel_SNV_allDF_v2.machado_normalLymphocytes.hq.snv.indel.maf.gz"))
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

# Subset the MAF by cell subset
hsc_muts_for_model <- normal_lymphocyte_muts_for_model %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in%
    hsc_colonies) %Q% (!seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY"))

naive_b_muts_for_model <- normal_lymphocyte_muts_for_model %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in%
    naive_b_colonies) %Q% (!seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY"))

memory_b_muts_for_model <- normal_lymphocyte_muts_for_model %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in%
    memory_b_colonies) %Q% (!seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY"))

# Separate SNVs and InDels HSC
snv_hsc_muts <- hsc_muts_for_model %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV")
snv_hsc_fish <- snv_hsc_muts %Q% (Variant_Classification != "Silent")

# saveRDS(object = snv_hsc_fish, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/hsc_snv_mutations.rds'))

# Naive B
snv_naive_b_muts <- naive_b_muts_for_model %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV")
snv_naive_b_fish <- snv_naive_b_muts %Q% (Variant_Classification != "Silent")

# saveRDS(object = snv_naive_b_fish, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/naive_b_snv_mutations.rds'))

# Memory B
snv_memory_b_muts <- memory_b_muts_for_model %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV")
snv_memory_b_fish <- snv_memory_b_muts %Q% (Variant_Classification != "Silent")

# saveRDS(object = snv_memory_b_fish, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/memory_b_snv_mutations.rds'))

snv_normal_lymphocyte_muts <- normal_lymphocyte_muts_for_model %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS ==
    "SNV")
indel_normal_lymphocyte_muts <- normal_lymphocyte_muts_for_model %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS %in%
    c("deletion", "insertion"))

snv_normal_lymphocyte_fish <- snv_normal_lymphocyte_muts %Q% (Variant_Classification !=
    "Silent")
indel_normal_lymphocyte_fish <- indel_normal_lymphocyte_muts %Q% (Variant_Classification !=
    "Silent")

# saveRDS(object = snv_normal_lymphocyte_fish, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/normal_lymphocyte_snv_mutations.rds'))
# saveRDS(object = indel_normal_lymphocyte_fish, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/normal_lymphocyte_indel_mutations.rds'))

Building the consensus regions

Code 
snv_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/normal_lymphocyte_snv_results.sushi.rds"))
indel_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/normal_lymphocyte_indel_results.sushi.rds"))

# Visualize in a genome-wide fashion like GWAS for tiles
snv_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results$mut_type <- "SNV"
indel_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results$mut_type <- "InDel"

# first, subset to only tiles with FDR < 0.05 then sort by genomic coordinate
sig_snv_lymph_sushi <- snv_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results[fdr < 0.05][order(seqnames,
    start)]
sig_indel_lymph_sushi <- indel_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results[fdr < 0.05][order(seqnames,
    start)]

# How many final tiles
message("FDR Significant SNV enriched tiles in Normal Lymphocytes: ", nrow(sig_snv_lymph_sushi))
## FDR Significant SNV enriched tiles in Normal Lymphocytes: 608
message("FDR Significant InDel enriched tiles in Normal Lymphocytes: ", nrow(sig_indel_lymph_sushi))
## FDR Significant InDel enriched tiles in Normal Lymphocytes: 153

# Create a set of stitched overlapping tiles to reduce to consensus segments
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.reduce(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_snv_lymph_sushi)))[,
    c()])
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er,
    subject = gUtils::gr.stripstrand(genes))]

stitched_lymph_ncindel_er <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.reduce(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_indel_lymph_sushi)))[,
    c()])
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = stitched_lymph_ncindel_er,
    subject = gUtils::gr.stripstrand(genes))]


# Lets loop through each stitched mutation enriched region and grab number of
# tiles and combined p-value SNVs
lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site <- data.table::data.table(k_tiles = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)),
    q_value = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)), effect_size_mean = rep(NA,
        length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)), count_density_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)),
    pred_count_density_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)), eligible_size_mean = rep(NA,
        length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)))

for (i in 1:length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)) {
    tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er[i, ],
        query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_snv_lymph_sushi)), type = "within",
        qcol = c("fdr", "effectsize", "count.density", "count.pred.density", "eligible"))

    # Calculate the mean FDR p-value, effect size, count density, predicted
    # count density and the eligible region
    new_qval <- poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr)

    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles[i] <- length(tile_set_to_stitch)
    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$q_value[i] <- as.numeric(new_qval$p)
    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$effectsize)
    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.density)
    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.pred.density)
    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$eligible)
}

# Add the final metadata to the consensus sites
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$k_tiles <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$q_value <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$q_value
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$effect_size_mean <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$count_density_mean <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$pred_count_density_mean <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$eligible_size_mean <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$mut_uniq_id <- paste0("lymph_snv_er", seq(1, length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)))

# InDels
lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site <- data.table::data.table(k_tiles = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)),
    q_value = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)), effect_size_mean = rep(NA,
        length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)), count_density_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)),
    pred_count_density_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)), eligible_size_mean = rep(NA,
        length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)))

for (i in 1:length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)) {
    tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_lymph_ncindel_er[i,
        ], query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_indel_lymph_sushi)), type = "within",
        qcol = c("fdr", "effectsize", "count.density", "count.pred.density", "eligible"))

    # Calculate the mean FDR p-value, effect size, count density, predicted
    # count density and the eligible region
    new_qval <- poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr)

    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles[i] <- length(tile_set_to_stitch)
    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$q_value[i] <- as.numeric(new_qval$p)
    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$effectsize)
    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.density)
    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.pred.density)
    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$eligible)
}

# Add the final metadata to the consensus sites
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$k_tiles <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$q_value <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$q_value
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$effect_size_mean <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$count_density_mean <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$pred_count_density_mean <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$eligible_size_mean <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$mut_uniq_id <- paste0("indel_er", seq(1, length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)))

# Combine the lymphoctye enriched regions to get the normal lymphocyte MERs
