lymphocyte_mers <- gr2dt(gr_refactor_seqs(grbind(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er, stitched_lymph_ncindel_er))) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(q_value)

Look at the breakdown of MERs based on overlap with protein-coding gene bodies (CDS / 3’ UTRs / 5’ UTRs / Introns)

Code 
# All MER muts GR
muts_per_mer_gr <- muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = mers %>%
        dplyr::select(mut_uniq_id, mer_id, mer_gene_label), by = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
    dt_to_gr()

# Loop through all MERs and find the overlap of each mutation in relation to
# protein coding gene bodies
gene_body_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table(mer_id = mers$mer_id, mer_gene_label = mers$mer_gene_label,
    cds_overlap_pct = 0, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct = 0, five_prime_utr_overlap_pct = 0,
    intron_overlap_pct = 0)

for (i in 1:n_distinct(gene_body_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
    # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
    per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(gene_body_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
    mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)

    # Find where each mutation in the MER overlaps and calculate the percentage
    # CDS
    mer_mut_cds_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = GenomicRanges::reduce(cds_elements_gr),
        subject = per_mer_muts, scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    gene_body_overlap_pct$cds_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_cds_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
    # remove the eligible muts, muts cannot be in two regions (InDels)
    if (length(mer_mut_cds_overlap) > 0) {
        per_mer_muts <- per_mer_muts[-mer_mut_cds_overlap$subject.id]
    }

    # 3' UTR
    mer_mut_three_prime_utr_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = GenomicRanges::reduce(three_prime_utr_elements_gr) %_%
        cds_elements_gr, subject = per_mer_muts, scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    gene_body_overlap_pct$three_prime_utr_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_three_prime_utr_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
    # remove the eligible muts, muts cannot be in two regions (InDels)
    if (length(mer_mut_three_prime_utr_overlap) > 0) {
        per_mer_muts <- per_mer_muts[-mer_mut_three_prime_utr_overlap$subject.id]
    }

    # 5' UTR
    mer_mut_five_prime_utr_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = GenomicRanges::reduce(five_prime_utr_elements_gr) %_%
        cds_regions %_% three_prime_utr_regions, subject = per_mer_muts, scol = c("mer_gene_label",
        "mer_id")))
    gene_body_overlap_pct$five_prime_utr_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_five_prime_utr_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
    # remove the eligible muts, muts cannot be in two regions (InDels)
    if (length(mer_mut_five_prime_utr_overlap) > 0) {
        per_mer_muts <- per_mer_muts[-mer_mut_five_prime_utr_overlap$subject.id]
    }

    # Intronic
    mer_mut_intron_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = GenomicRanges::reduce(intron_elements_gr) %_%
        cds_elements_gr %_% five_prime_utr_elements_gr %_% three_prime_utr_elements_gr,
        subject = per_mer_muts, scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    gene_body_overlap_pct$intron_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_intron_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
}

# gene body
gene_body_mers <- gene_body_overlap_pct %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mer_id, mer_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(gene_body_pct = round(sum(cds_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct,
        five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct), digits = 1)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(gene_body_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(rank = row_number(), intergenic_pct = round(100 - gene_body_pct,
        digits = 2))

# For initial plotting to see the breakdown high-level ordering based on gene
# body or intergenic
gene_body_mers$rank <- factor(gene_body_mers$rank, levels = gene_body_mers$rank)

gene_body_mers_ordered <- gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::select(-gene_body_pct) %>%
    reshape2::melt(id.vars = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label", "rank"), variable.name = "enhancer_type",
        value.name = "pct")

gene_body_mers_ordered$enhancer_type <- factor(gene_body_mers_ordered$enhancer_type,
    levels = c("cds_overlap_pct", "five_prime_utr_overlap_pct", "three_prime_utr_overlap_pct",
        "intron_overlap_pct", "intergenic_pct"))

gene_body_mer_barplot <- ggplot(gene_body_mers_ordered) + geom_col(aes(x = rank,
    y = pct, fill = enhancer_type), width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Features",
    values = alpha(paletteer_d("ggsci::hallmarks_light_cosmic")[c(1, 4, 2, 3, 5)],
        alpha = 0.7), labels = c("CDS", "5' UTR", "3' UTR", "Intron", "Intergenic")) +
    labs(y = "Overlap with feature", x = "MERs") + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1,
    scale = 1), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA))

# Now cluster the MERs based on overlap with gene body features
# clustered_gene_body_mers_dendro <- factoextra::fviz_nbclust(x =
# gene_body_mers %>% dplyr::select(mer_id,cds_overlap_pct,
# three_prime_utr_overlap_pct, five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct,
# intergenic_pct) %>% tibble::column_to_rownames('mer_id'), FUN =
# factoextra::hcut, method = 'wss')

mer_gene_body_feature_clusters <- factoextra::hcut(x = gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct, five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct) %>%
    tibble::column_to_rownames("mer_id"), k = 4, stand = F)
# factoextra::fviz_dend(mer_gene_body_feature_clusters, show_labels = T)

# Attach the MER ID to the cluster assignment
gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt <- data.table::data.table(mer_id = names(mer_gene_body_feature_clusters$cluster),
    cluster_id = mer_gene_body_feature_clusters$cluster)

# Visualize the composite of each gene body MER cluster
clustered_gene_body_mers_comp_boxplot <- gene_body_mers_ordered %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    ggplot() + geom_boxplot(aes(x = enhancer_type, y = pct, fill = enhancer_type)) +
    facet_wrap(facets = ~cluster_id, nrow = 2) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Features",
    values = alpha(paletteer_d("ggsci::hallmarks_light_cosmic")[c(1, 4, 2, 3, 5)],
        alpha = 0.7), labels = c("CDS", "5' UTR", "3' UTR", "Intron", "Intergenic")) +
    labs(y = "Overlap with feature", x = NULL) + scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CDS",
    "5' UTR", "3' UTR", "Intron", "Intergenic")) + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1,
    scale = 1)) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 50, vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA))

table(gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt$cluster_id)
## 
##   1   2   3   4 
##  96   9  31 171

# cluster 1 - Intronic MERs (n=96) cluster 2 - CDS MERs (n=9) cluster 3 - UTR
# MERs (n=31) cluster 4 - Intergenic MERs (n=171)

gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_intron_overlap = mean(intron_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_intron_overlap))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   cluster_id mean_intron_overlap
##        <int>               <dbl>
## 1          1              97.7  
## 2          3              50.2  
## 3          2              12.2  
## 4          4               0.903

gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_cds_overlap = mean(cds_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_cds_overlap))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   cluster_id mean_cds_overlap
##        <int>            <dbl>
## 1          2           81.0  
## 2          3            8.70 
## 3          1            0.627
## 4          4            0.218

gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_three_prime_utr_overlap = mean(three_prime_utr_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_three_prime_utr_overlap))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   cluster_id mean_three_prime_utr_overlap
##        <int>                        <dbl>
## 1          3                       12.9  
## 2          2                        3.06 
## 3          1                        0.196
## 4          4                        0.144

gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_five_prime_utr_overlap = mean(five_prime_utr_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_five_prime_utr_overlap))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   cluster_id mean_five_prime_utr_overlap
##        <int>                       <dbl>
## 1          3                       3.90 
## 2          1                       0.259
## 3          2                       0.231
## 4          4                       0.217

gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_intergenic_overlap = median(intergenic_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(median_intergenic_overlap))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   cluster_id median_intergenic_overlap
##        <int>                     <dbl>
## 1          4                     100  
## 2          3                      21.7
## 3          1                       0  
## 4          2                       0

# Create labels for each cluster
gene_body_mers <- gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(cluster_label = dplyr::case_when(cluster_id == 1 ~ "Intronic",
        cluster_id == 2 ~ "CDS", cluster_id == 3 ~ "UTR", cluster_id == 4 ~ "Intergenic"))
# Create the MER overlap base
mers_overlap <- mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mers %>%
        dplyr::select(mer_id, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct, five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
            intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct, gene_body_pct, cluster_label), by = "mer_id")

message("Protein-coding gene body MERs: ", mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label != "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric(), " (", round(mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label != "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric()/307 * 100, digits = 1), "%)")
## Protein-coding gene body MERs: 136 (44.3%)

message("Intergenic MERs: ", mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric(), " (", round(mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric()/307 * 100, digits = 1), "%)")
## Intergenic MERs: 171 (55.7%)

Analyze the gene body MERs more in-depth

Code 
# mers_overlap %>% dplyr::filter(cluster_label != 'Intergenic') %>%
# dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_gene_label,pct_total_mut_patients) %>%
# dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# mers_overlap %>% dplyr::filter(cluster_label == 'CDS') %>%
# dplyr::select(mer_id,mer_gene_label,pct_total_mut_patients) %>%
# dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

message("Intronic MERs: ", mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "Intronic") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric())
## Intronic MERs: 96

# mers_overlap %>% dplyr::group_by(cluster_label) %>%
# dplyr::summarise('mean_intron_overlap' = mean(intron_overlap_pct)) %>%
# dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_intron_overlap))

message("CDS MERs: ", mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "CDS") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric())
## CDS MERs: 9

# mers_overlap %>% dplyr::group_by(cluster_label) %>%
# dplyr::summarise('mean_cds_overlap' = mean(cds_overlap_pct)) %>%
# dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_cds_overlap))

message("UTR MERs: ", mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "UTR") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric())
## UTR MERs: 31

# mers_overlap %>% dplyr::group_by(cluster_label) %>%
# dplyr::summarise('mean_three_prime_utr_overlap' =
# mean(three_prime_utr_overlap_pct), 'mean_five_prime_utr_overlap' =
# mean(five_prime_utr_overlap_pct)) %>%
# dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_three_prime_utr_overlap))

# Visualize the composition of overlap after final cluster labeling
gene_body_mers$cluster_label <- factor(gene_body_mers$cluster_label, levels = c("CDS",
    "UTR", "Intronic", "Intergenic"))
# get order of MERs
gene_body_mer_order <- gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::arrange(cluster_label) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

gene_body_mers$mer_id <- factor(gene_body_mers$mer_id, levels = gene_body_mer_order)

gene_body_mers_ordered <- gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::select(-gene_body_pct, -rank, -mer_gene_label, -cluster_id) %>%
    reshape2::melt(id.vars = c("mer_id", "cluster_label"), variable.name = "enhancer_type",
        value.name = "pct")

gene_body_mer_barplot <- ggplot(gene_body_mers_ordered) + geom_col(aes(x = mer_id,
    y = pct, fill = enhancer_type), width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Features",
    values = alpha(paletteer_d("ggsci::hallmarks_light_cosmic")[c(1, 4, 2, 3, 5)],
        alpha = 0.7), labels = c("CDS", "3' UTR", "5' UTR", "Intron", "Intergenic")) +
    labs(y = "Overlap with feature", x = "MERs") + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1,
    scale = 1), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + theme(legend.position = "top", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA))

gene_body_mer_boxplot <- ggplot(gene_body_mers_ordered) + geom_boxplot(aes(x = enhancer_type,
    y = pct, fill = enhancer_type)) + facet_wrap(facets = ~cluster_label, nrow = 2) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Features", values = alpha(paletteer_d("ggsci::hallmarks_light_cosmic")[c(1,
        4, 2, 3, 5)], alpha = 0.7), labels = c("CDS", "3' UTR", "5' UTR", "Intron",
        "Intergenic")) + labs(y = "Overlap with feature", x = NULL) + scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CDS",
    "3' UTR", "5' UTR", "Intron", "Intergenic")) + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1,
    scale = 1)) + theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 50,
    vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA))

# gene_body_mer_barplot + gene_body_mer_boxplot + plot_layout(ncol = 2, nrow =
# 1, axis_titles = 'collect_y', widths = c(0.6,0.4))

Assess the intersection of driver genes with protein-coding gene body MERs

Code 
# Read in driver gene list
mm_driver_genes <- fread(paste0(base_data_path, "mgpDriverGenes.txt"), header = F,
    col.names = "gene")

# Overlap of MERs and driver genes
driver_gene_mer_class <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label %in% mm_driver_genes$gene) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label, cluster_label, mer_id)

# CDS
driver_gene_cds_mers <- driver_gene_mer_class %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "CDS") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

message("Driver gene CDS MERs: ", paste0(driver_gene_cds_mers, collapse = ", "))
## Driver gene CDS MERs: NRAS, TENT5C, BRAF, KRAS

mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label %in% driver_gene_cds_mers) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label, cds_overlap_pct, mer_id)
##    mer_gene_label cds_overlap_pct mer_id
##            <char>           <num> <char>
## 1:           NRAS           97.44  mer14
## 2:         TENT5C           62.96  mer70
## 3:         TENT5C           65.52 mer182
## 4:           BRAF           72.73  mer93
## 5:           KRAS           75.86  mer32

mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label %in% driver_gene_cds_mers) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label, cds_overlap_pct, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(muts_cds_pct_range = range(cds_overlap_pct))
##   muts_cds_pct_range
## 1              62.96
## 2              97.44

# UTR
driver_gene_utr_mers <- driver_gene_mer_class %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "UTR") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

message("Driver gene UTR MERs: ", paste0(driver_gene_utr_mers, collapse = ", "))
## Driver gene UTR MERs: DUSP2, EGR1, CCND1

mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label %in% driver_gene_utr_mers) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct, five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        mer_id)
##    mer_gene_label three_prime_utr_overlap_pct five_prime_utr_overlap_pct mer_id
##            <char>                       <num>                      <num> <char>
## 1:          DUSP2                        7.41                       0.00 mer263
## 2:           EGR1                        0.00                      16.67 mer141
## 3:          CCND1                        0.00                       8.72 mer155

mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label %in% driver_gene_utr_mers) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct, five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(muts_three_prime_pct_range = range(three_prime_utr_overlap_pct),
        muts_five_prime_pct_range = range(five_prime_utr_overlap_pct))
##   muts_three_prime_pct_range muts_five_prime_pct_range
## 1                       0.00                      0.00
## 2                       7.41                     16.67

# Intronic
driver_gene_intron_mers <- driver_gene_mer_class %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "Intronic") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

message("Driver gene Intronic MERs: ", paste0(driver_gene_intron_mers, collapse = ", "))
## Driver gene Intronic MERs: RFTN1, LTB, ZFP36L1

mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label %in% driver_gene_intron_mers) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label, intron_overlap_pct, mer_id)
##    mer_gene_label intron_overlap_pct mer_id
##            <char>              <num> <char>
## 1:          RFTN1              87.93  mer48
## 2:            LTB              88.30  mer13
## 3:        ZFP36L1              92.21   mer5

mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label %in% driver_gene_intron_mers) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label, intron_overlap_pct, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(muts_intron_pct_range = range(intron_overlap_pct))
##   muts_intron_pct_range
## 1                 87.93
## 2                 92.21

# Intergenic
driver_gene_intergenic_mers <- driver_gene_mer_class %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

message("Driver gene Intergenic MERs: ", driver_gene_intergenic_mers)
## Driver gene Intergenic MERs: CYLD

mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::mutate(driver_status = case_when(mer_id %in% driver_gene_mer_class$mer_id ~
        "driver_gene", !mer_id %in% driver_gene_mer_class$mer_id ~ NA))

mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label == driver_gene_intergenic_mers) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_gene_label, intergenic_pct, mer_id)
##    mer_gene_label intergenic_pct mer_id
##            <char>          <num> <char>
## 1:           CYLD           78.6 mer135


# what is the context of the mutations, in the driver genes?  i.e. which are
# likely picked up due to coding mutations mm_driver_muts_context <-
# muts_per_mer %>% dplyr::left_join(y =
# mers[,c('mut_uniq_id','mer_gene_label')], by = 'mut_uniq_id') %>%
# dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id %in% mm_driver_gene_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
# dplyr::group_by(mer_gene_label) %>% dplyr::select(Variant_Classification) %>%
# dplyr::ungroup() %>% table() mm_driver_nc_muts_pct <- data.table('gene' =
# rownames(mm_driver_muts_context), 'pct' = rep('NA',
# length(rownames(mm_driver_muts_context)))) for(i in
# 1:length(rownames(mm_driver_muts_context))) { mm_driver_nc_muts_pct[i,2] <-
# as.data.table(mm_driver_muts_context) %>% dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label ==
# rownames(mm_driver_muts_context)[i] & Variant_Classification %in%
# c('3'Flank', '3'UTR', '5'Flank', '5'UTR', 'IGR', 'Intron')) %>%
# dplyr::reframe(pct_nc_muts = round(sum(N) /
# rowSums(mm_driver_muts_context)[i], digits = 2)) %>% purrr::as_vector() }
# mm_driver_nc_muts_pct <- mm_driver_nc_muts_pct %>% dplyr::arrange(pct)

VAF of nc mutations and missense mutations in driver genes

Code 
mm_driver_nc_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene %in% c("EGR1", "CCND1", "LTB", "ZFP36L1") & Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode %in%
        c("NIH012B", "52148-DNA-N", "NIH041B", "IID_H201428_N01_01_WG01", "27522_Skin_10X_N",
            "382803-N", "50050-DNA-N", "370474-N", "78208-DNA-N", "97033-DNA-N",
            "IID_H196061_N01_01_WG01", "IID_H201447_N01_01_WG01", "MMRC0335_V0D57G_N",
            "NIH010B", "NIH035B", "PD26434b_N", "PD47563b_N", "596699-N"))

mm_driver_nc_mer_muts$Variant_Classification <- factor(mm_driver_nc_mer_muts$Variant_Classification,
    levels = c("Missense_Mutation", "Intron", "3'Flank", "5'Flank", "5'UTR", "Splice_Region"),
    labels = c("Missense Mutation", "Intron", "3'Flank", "5'Flank", "5'UTR", "Splice Region"))

variant_class_colors <- c("#E31A1C", "#2C3E50", "#18BC9C", "#CCBE93", "#1F78B4",
    "#FF7F00")

# EGR1
egr1_window <- "chr5:138465600-138466200"

gene_track_egr1 <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = egr1_window, flank = 0, genes_of_interest = "EGR1",
    gene_name_side = "five_prime", gene_label_nudge = 50, gene_label_size = 4) +
    labs(y = "EGR1") + theme(axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, vjust = 0.5,
    size = 15))

scale_track_egr1 <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = egr1_window, flank = 0)

mutation_track_egr1 <- mm_driver_nc_mer_muts %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene == "EGR1" & Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode == c("NIH012B")) %>%
    ggplot() + geom_point(aes(x = start, y = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, color = Variant_Classification,
    size = VAF)) + scale_size_binned(breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1), range = c(1,
    4), limits = c(0, 1)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Variant\nClassification",
    values = variant_class_colors[c(1, 2, 5)]) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = get_locus_axis(input_window = egr1_window,
    input_flank = 0)$name, labels = get_locus_axis(input_window = egr1_window, input_flank = 0)$labels,
    limits = get_locus_axis(input_window = egr1_window, input_flank = 0)$limits,
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 2.5, color = "black"), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(2,
        units = "mm"), plot.margin = unit(c(0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), legend.text = element_text(size = 11), legend.position = "none")

egr1_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(scale_track_egr1, mutation_track_egr1,
    gene_track_egr1), ncol = 1, guides = "collect", heights = c(0.07, 0.09, 0.07))


# CCND1
ccnd1_window <- "chr11:69640000-69644000"

gene_track_ccnd1 <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = ccnd1_window, flank = 0,
    genes_of_interest = "CCND1", gene_name_side = "five_prime", gene_label_nudge = 50,
    gene_label_size = 4) + labs(y = "CCND1") + theme(axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0,
    vjust = 0.5, size = 15))

scale_track_ccnd1 <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = ccnd1_window, flank = 0)

mutation_track_ccnd1 <- mm_driver_nc_mer_muts %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene == "CCND1" & Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode %in% c("370474-N",
        "78208-DNA-N", "97033-DNA-N", "IID_H196061_N01_01_WG01", "IID_H201447_N01_01_WG01",
        "MMRC0335_V0D57G_N", "NIH010B", "NIH035B", "PD26434b_N", "PD47563b_N")) %>%
    ggplot() + geom_point(aes(x = start, y = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, color = Variant_Classification,
    size = VAF)) + scale_size_binned(breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1), range = c(1,
    4), limits = c(0, 1)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Variant\nClassification",
    values = variant_class_colors[c(1, 2, 4, 5)]) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = get_locus_axis(input_window = ccnd1_window,
    input_flank = 0)$name, labels = get_locus_axis(input_window = ccnd1_window, input_flank = 0)$labels,
    limits = get_locus_axis(input_window = ccnd1_window, input_flank = 0)$limits,
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 2.5, color = "black"), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(2,
        units = "mm"), plot.margin = unit(c(0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), legend.text = element_text(size = 11))

ccnd1_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(scale_track_ccnd1, mutation_track_ccnd1,
    gene_track_ccnd1), ncol = 1, guides = "collect", heights = c(0.07, 0.17, 0.07))

# LTB
ltb_window <- "chr6:31581300-31582100"

gene_track_ltb <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = ltb_window, flank = 0, genes_of_interest = "LTB",
    gene_name_side = "five_prime", gene_label_nudge = 50, gene_label_size = 4) +
    labs(y = "LTB") + theme(axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, vjust = 0.5, size = 15))

scale_track_ltb <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = ltb_window, flank = 0)

mutation_track_ltb <- mm_driver_nc_mer_muts %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene == "LTB" & Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode %in% c("52148-DNA-N",
        "NIH041B", "27522_Skin_10X_N")) %>%
    ggplot() + geom_point(aes(x = start, y = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, color = Variant_Classification,
    size = VAF)) + scale_color_manual(name = "Variant\nClassification", values = variant_class_colors[c(1,
    2, 6)]) + scale_size_binned(breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1), range = c(1, 4),
    limits = c(0, 1)) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = get_locus_axis(input_window = ltb_window,
    input_flank = 0)$name, labels = get_locus_axis(input_window = ltb_window, input_flank = 0)$labels,
    limits = get_locus_axis(input_window = ltb_window, input_flank = 0)$limits, expand = c(0.01,
        0.01)) + ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 2.5,
        color = "black"), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(2, units = "mm"), plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,
        0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10), axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11), legend.position = "none")

ltb_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(scale_track_ltb, mutation_track_ltb,
    gene_track_ltb), ncol = 1, guides = "collect", heights = c(0.07, 0.09, 0.07))

# ZFP36L1

gene_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window, flank = 1,

    2)]) + scale_size_binned(breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1), range = c(1, 4),

zfp36l1_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(scale_track_zfp36l1, mutation_track_zfp36l1,
    gene_track_zfp36l1), ncol = 1, guides = "collect", heights = c(0.07, 0.09, 0.07))

Supplementary Figure 7

Now look at the MER mutation overlap with lncRNA and miRNA gene bodies

Code 
# Loop through all MERs and find the overlap of each mutation in relation to
# protein coding gene bodies
ncgene_body_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table(mer_id = mers$mer_id, mer_gene_label = mers$mer_gene_label,
    lncrna_overlap_pct = 0, mirna_overlap_pct = 0)

for (i in 1:n_distinct(ncgene_body_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
    # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
    per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(ncgene_body_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
    mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)

    # Find where each mutation in the MER overlaps and calculate the percentage
    # miRNA
    mer_mut_mirna_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = mirna_elements_gr,
        subject = per_mer_muts, qcol = "gene_name", scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    ncgene_body_overlap_pct$mirna_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_mirna_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)

    # lncRNA
    mer_mut_lncrna_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = lncrna_elements_gr,
        subject = per_mer_muts, qcol = "gene_name", scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    ncgene_body_overlap_pct$lncrna_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_lncrna_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
}

# Add the lncRNA and miRNA gene bodies to MER overlap
mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = ncgene_body_overlap_pct, by = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label"))

ncgene_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
        cluster_label, gene_body_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct, mirna_overlap_pct, overlapping_genes) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# All lncRNA
mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(lncrna_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_gene_label, cluster_label, gene_body_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, overlapping_genes)
##     mer_id mer_gene_label cluster_label gene_body_pct lncrna_overlap_pct
##     <char>         <char>        <char>         <num>              <num>
##  1:  mer98           FLG2           CDS          93.7             100.00
##  2: mer241         PCDH10    Intergenic           0.0             100.00
##  3:  mer81          PRSS3           UTR          48.2             100.00
##  4: mer112         PITRM1    Intergenic           0.0             100.00
##  5: mer171         GPR158    Intergenic           0.0             100.00
##  6: mer298        WASHC2A    Intergenic           0.0             100.00
##  7:  mer63        ZNF705A    Intergenic           0.0             100.00
##  8: mer172         PIEZO2    Intergenic           0.0             100.00
##  9: mer138          RUBCN    Intergenic           0.0              94.44
## 10:  mer12         MIR142    Intergenic           1.4              90.54
## 11:  mer62         RABL2A    Intergenic           0.0              82.05
## 12:  mer96         CLEC6A    Intergenic           0.0              79.31
## 13:  mer51           TFRC    Intergenic           0.0              78.72
## 14:  mer83          NEAT1    Intergenic           0.0              77.78
## 15: mer280           EBF1    Intergenic          25.7              71.43
## 16:  mer39            LPP    Intergenic           0.0              60.67
## 17: mer277        DEFB115    Intergenic           0.0              52.49
## 18: mer206        CCDC74A    Intergenic           0.0              50.00
## 19:  mer43         KCNJ12    Intergenic           2.4              48.39
## 20: mer158         TUBA3C    Intergenic           0.0              46.67
## 21:  mer92         SAMD11    Intergenic           0.0              34.48
## 22: mer259           CES1    Intergenic           0.0              24.24
## 23: mer203        FAM90A1    Intergenic          10.4              24.14
## 24: mer192          NBPF1    Intergenic          16.7              22.22
## 25: mer293          PCGF3      Intronic         100.0              17.65
## 26:  mer56          NBPF1    Intergenic           0.0              17.39
## 27: mer218       CNTNAP3B    Intergenic           0.0              15.52
## 28:   mer6           BTG2           UTR          76.5               9.41
## 29:  mer58           BTG1           UTR          73.5               8.82
## 30: mer125           TNK2    Intergenic           0.0               7.14
## 31: mer288         TSPEAR      Intronic         100.0               4.55
## 32: mer161         DGCR6L    Intergenic           0.0               1.10
##     mer_id mer_gene_label cluster_label gene_body_pct lncrna_overlap_pct
##     <char>         <char>        <char>         <num>              <num>
##     mirna_overlap_pct                           overlapping_genes
##                 <num>                                      <char>
##  1:               0.0                                FLG-AS1;FLG2
##  2:               0.0                                      SNHG27
##  3:               0.0                            PRSS3;UBE2R2-AS1
##  4:               0.0                                   LINC02669
##  5:               0.0                                   LINC00836
##  6:               0.0                                     FAM21EP
##  7:               0.0                                      FAM66C
##  8:               0.0                                   LINC01887
##  9:               0.0                                      SDHAP4
## 10:               2.7 TSPOAP1;MIR142HG;MIR142;MIR4736;TSPOAP1-AS1
## 11:               0.0                            MIR1302-3;WASH2P
## 12:               0.0                                   LINC00937
## 13:               0.0                                      SDHAP1
## 14:               0.0                                 FRMD8;NEAT1
## 15:               0.0                              EBF1;LINC02202
## 16:               0.0                                   LINC01991
## 17:               0.0                                     FAM242A
## 18:               0.0                                    CDRT15P3
## 19:               0.0                            KCNJ12;LINC02693
## 20:               0.0                                     FAM230C
## 21:               0.0                  FAM87B;LINC00115;LINC01128
## 22:               0.0                                      CES1P1
## 23:               0.0                              FAM66C;FAM90A1
## 24:               0.0                               NBPF1;CROCCP2
## 25:               0.0                             PCGF3;PCGF3-AS1
## 26:               0.0                                   LINC01783
## 27:               0.0                                     FAM242F
## 28:               0.0                                BTG2-DT;BTG2
## 29:               0.0                      LINC01619;BTG1;BTG1-DT
## 30:               0.0                                      SDHAP1
## 31:               0.0                           TSPEAR;TSPEAR-AS1
## 32:               0.0                                     FAM230G
##     mirna_overlap_pct                           overlapping_genes
##                 <num>                                      <char>

# mean overlap of all
lncrna_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::mutate(lncrna = case_when(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0 ~ "lncrna", lncrna_overlap_pct <
        0 ~ NA))
mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_lncrna_overlap = mean(lncrna_overlap_pct))
##   mean_lncrna_overlap
## 1            56.84844

# Co-localization of protein-coding and lncRNA gene body MERs
ncgene_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0 & cluster_label != "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(lncrna_overlap_pct))
##    mer_id mer_gene_label total_mut_patients pct_total_mut_patients
##    <char>         <char>              <int>                  <num>
## 1:  mer81          PRSS3                 38                   12.9
## 2:  mer98           FLG2                 35                   11.9
## 3: mer293          PCGF3                 16                    5.4
## 4:   mer6           BTG2                 63                   21.4
## 5:  mer58           BTG1                 24                    8.2
## 6: mer288         TSPEAR                 21                    7.1
##    cluster_label gene_body_pct lncrna_overlap_pct mirna_overlap_pct
##           <char>         <num>              <num>             <num>
## 1:           UTR          48.2             100.00                 0
## 2:           CDS          93.7             100.00                 0
## 3:      Intronic         100.0              17.65                 0
## 4:           UTR          76.5               9.41                 0
## 5:           UTR          73.5               8.82                 0
## 6:      Intronic         100.0               4.55                 0
##         overlapping_genes
##                    <char>
## 1:       PRSS3;UBE2R2-AS1
## 2:           FLG-AS1;FLG2
## 3:        PCGF3;PCGF3-AS1
## 4:           BTG2-DT;BTG2
## 5: LINC01619;BTG1;BTG1-DT
## 6:      TSPEAR;TSPEAR-AS1

# lncRNA specific MERs (i.e. no protein coding overlap)
mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0 & cluster_label == "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(lncrna_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_gene_label, cluster_label, gene_body_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, overlapping_genes)
##     mer_id mer_gene_label cluster_label gene_body_pct lncrna_overlap_pct
##     <char>         <char>        <char>         <num>              <num>
##  1: mer241         PCDH10    Intergenic           0.0             100.00

##  7: mer138          RUBCN    Intergenic           0.0              94.44
##  8:  mer12         MIR142    Intergenic           1.4              90.54

##                 <num>                                      <char>

# How many FAM genes
mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0 & cluster_label == "Intergenic" & str_detect(string = overlapping_genes,
        pattern = "FAM")) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(lncrna_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_gene_label, cluster_label, gene_body_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, overlapping_genes)
##    mer_id mer_gene_label cluster_label gene_body_pct lncrna_overlap_pct
##    <char>         <char>        <char>         <num>              <num>
## 1: mer298        WASHC2A    Intergenic           0.0             100.00
## 2:  mer63        ZNF705A    Intergenic           0.0             100.00
## 3: mer277        DEFB115    Intergenic           0.0              52.49
## 4: mer158         TUBA3C    Intergenic           0.0              46.67

## 6: mer203        FAM90A1    Intergenic          10.4              24.14
## 7: mer218       CNTNAP3B    Intergenic           0.0              15.52
## 8: mer161         DGCR6L    Intergenic           0.0               1.10

## 1:                 0                    FAM21EP
## 2:                 0                     FAM66C
## 3:                 0                    FAM242A
## 4:                 0                    FAM230C
## 5:                 0 FAM87B;LINC00115;LINC01128
## 6:                 0             FAM66C;FAM90A1
## 7:                 0                    FAM242F
## 8:                 0                    FAM230G

# miRNA MERs
mirna_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::mutate(mirna = case_when(mirna_overlap_pct > 0 ~ "mirna", mirna_overlap_pct ==
        0 ~ NA))
mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mirna_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mirna_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_gene_label, cluster_label, gene_body_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, overlapping_genes)
##    mer_id mer_gene_label cluster_label gene_body_pct lncrna_overlap_pct
##    <char>         <char>        <char>         <num>              <num>
## 1: mer207         COL5A1    Intergenic           2.5               0.00
## 2: mer126          HLA-B           UTR          83.3               0.00
## 3:  mer12         MIR142    Intergenic           1.4              90.54
##    mirna_overlap_pct
##                <num>
## 1:             12.50
## 2:              3.33
## 3:              2.70
##                                                          overlapping_genes
##                                                                     <char>
## 1: COL5A1;MIR3689C;MIR3689A;MIR3689D1;MIR3689B;MIR3689D2;MIR3689E;MIR3689F
## 2:                                                           HLA-B;MIR6891
## 3:                             TSPOAP1;MIR142HG;MIR142;MIR4736;TSPOAP1-AS1

Enhancers/Super-Enhancers and Promoters

Code 
# Loop through all MERs and find the overlap of each mutation in relation to
# protein coding gene bodies
cre_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table(mer_id = mers$mer_id, mer_gene_label = mers$mer_gene_label,
    enhancer_overlap_pct = 0, enhancer_id = NA, tissue = NA, enhancer_type = NA,
    promoter_overlap_pct = 0, prom_id = NA)

for (i in 1:n_distinct(cre_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
    # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
    per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(cre_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
    mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)

    # E/SEs
    mer_mut_ee_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = complete_ee,
        subject = per_mer_muts, qcol = c("enhancer_id", "tissue", "enhancer_type"),
        scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))

    cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$subject.id))/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)

    # Get cell line specific EE ID
    if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)) > 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_id[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)),
            collapse = ",")
    } else if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)) == 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_id[i] <- unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)
    }

    # Get cell line
    if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)) > 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$tissue[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)),
            collapse = ",")
    } else if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)) == 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$tissue[i] <- unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)
    }

    # Get EE enhancer_type
    if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)) > 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_type[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)),
            collapse = ",")
    } else if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)) == 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_type[i] <- unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)
    }

    # Promoters

    mer_mut_promoter_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = promoter_sites_gr,
        subject = per_mer_muts, qcol = "prom_id", scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    cre_overlap_pct$promoter_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_promoter_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)

    # Get all overlapping promoters
    if (length(unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)) > 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$prom_id[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)),
            collapse = ",")
    } else if (length(unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)) == 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$prom_id[i] <- unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)
    }
}

# Add the Enhancer / Promoter to MER overlap
mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = cre_overlap_pct, by = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label"))

# E/SE MERs
e_se_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(tissue))
e_se_mers_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(e_se_mers))

# How many MERs / genes
message("Number of significant MERs that overlap E/SE elements: ", length(unique(e_se_mers$mer_id)))
## Number of significant MERs that overlap E/SE elements: 50
message("Number of unique coding genes associated: ", length(unique(e_se_mers$nearest_gene)))
## Number of unique coding genes associated: 39

e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_enhancer_overlap = mean(enhancer_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_enhancer_overlap))
##   mean_enhancer_overlap
## 1               85.0808

# How many mutations in E/SE-MERs
message("Total SNVs in E/SE-MERs: ", muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
    dplyr::count())
## Total SNVs in E/SE-MERs: 4257
message("% of all MER SNVs in E/SEs: ", (muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
    dplyr::count())/(muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs") %>%
    dplyr::count()) * 100)
## % of all MER SNVs in E/SEs: 23.3913951316006

message("Total InDels in E/SE-MERs: ", muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
    dplyr::count())
## Total InDels in E/SE-MERs: 162
message("% of all MER InDels in E/SEs: ", (muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
    dplyr::count())/(muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels") %>%
    dplyr::count()) * 100)
## % of all MER InDels in E/SEs: 36.8181818181818

# Range of % mutated patients
message("Range of Percentage of patients mutated at E/SE-MERs: ", paste0(range(e_se_mers$pct_total_mut_patients),
    collapse = " - "))
## Range of Percentage of patients mutated at E/SE-MERs: 5.4 - 66

LOLA analysis of SE

Code 
##################################################### TEST 1: ARE SEs ENRICHED
##################################################### IN MERs TEST 2: ARE SEs
##################################################### ENRICHED IN SIMULATED
##################################################### RANDOM NON-MERs (negative
##################################################### control)

# query will be SEs universe will be all E/SE elements ref set database will be
# MERs

e_se_universe <- LOLA::readBed(paste0(base_data_path, "enhancerElements.hg38.bed"))
se_query <- e_se_universe[str_detect(string = names(e_se_universe), pattern = "SE_")]

# Use simulated Non-MERs in LOLA as negative control Write them all to single
# BED then split into per simulation BEDs to be tested fwrite(x =
# trunc_complete_sim_distro[c(1:3,6)], file = 'simNonMERs.bed', sep = '\t',
# col.names = F) LOLA::splitFileIntoCollection(filename =
# paste0(base_data_path, 'simNonMERs.bed'), splitCol = 4, collectionFolder =
# paste0(base_data_path, 'lolaAnalysis/mers/collection2/regions/'),
# filenamePrepend = 'simulatedNonMERs-')

# Run the LOLA analysis
mer_and_non_mer_db <- loadRegionDB(dbLocation = paste0(base_data_path, "lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/"))
## Reading collection annotations:
##  collection1: found collection annotation:../../data/lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/collection1/collection.txt

## 296: ../../data/lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/collection2/regions/simulatedNonMERs-95.bed
## 297: ../../data/lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/collection2/regions/simulatedNonMERs-96.bed
## 298: ../../data/lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/collection2/regions/simulatedNonMERs-97.bed
## 299: ../../data/lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/collection2/regions/simulatedNonMERs-98.bed
## 300: ../../data/lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/collection2/regions/simulatedNonMERs-99.bed
# mer_and_non_mer_se_lola <- runLOLA(userSets = se_query, userUniverse =
# e_se_universe, regionDB = mer_and_non_mer_db) saveRDS(object =
# mer_and_non_mer_se_lola, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/mer_vs_non_mer_se_lola.rds'))
mer_and_non_mer_se_lola <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/mer_vs_non_mer_se_lola.rds"))

# convert log10 p-value to empirical and then add correction
message("SE-MER overlap FDR adj. P-value: ", scales::scientific(x = mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$qValue[1]))
## SE-MER overlap FDR adj. P-value: 1.12e-30
message("Odds ratio of SE-MER overlap: ", round(mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$oddsRatio[1],
    digits = 1))
## Odds ratio of SE-MER overlap: 33
message("SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap median P-value: ", round(median(mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$qValue[-1]),
    digits = 3))
## SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap median P-value: 0.156
message("Odds ratio of SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap: ", round(median(mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$oddsRatio[-1]),
    digits = 1))
## Odds ratio of SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap: 2.9

# Transform LOLA output for plotting
mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot <- mer_and_non_mer_se_lola %>%
    dplyr::group_by(antibody) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_or = median(oddsRatio), sd_or = sd(oddsRatio))
message("95% CI of SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap odds ratio: ", mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$median_or[2] -
    mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$sd_or[2], " - ", mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$median_or[2] +
    mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$sd_or[2])
## 95% CI of SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap odds ratio: 0.762415005377327 - 5.02329829113012

Look at the genes related to each E/SE-MER group

Code 
# GCB MERs
gcb_se_mers <- e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB")

message("Number of GCB E/SE-MERs: ", length(unique(gcb_se_mers$mer_id)))
## Number of GCB E/SE-MERs: 25
message("Number of unique coding genes associated: ", length(unique(gcb_se_mers$nearest_gene)))
## Number of unique coding genes associated: 18

gcb_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_type, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
        seqnames, start, end, cluster_label, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, enhancer_overlap_pct, tissue, enhancer_type, promoter_overlap_pct,
        prom_id) %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label %in% c("RPIA", "SERPINA9", "ZCCHC7", "LTB", "DTX1",
        "TCL1A", "PAX5", "CXCR4", "LPP", "RFTN1", "BTG1", "CD83")) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# PC MERs

    dplyr::filter(tissue == "PC")

message("Number of PC E/SE-MERs: ", length(unique(pc_se_mers$mer_id)))

pc_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(enhancer_type == "SE") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_type, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,

Look at the BCL6 locus for comparison to Dalla Favera’s DLBCL analysis

Code 
bcl6_intron_window <- "chr3:187744850-187745350"

# Create window labels for additional layers bcl6_intron_woi <-
# data.table('win_start' = c(187744872, 187745199, 187744678), 'win_end' =
# c(187744917, 187745246, 187744698), 'win_name' = c(window_labeler(window =
# 'chr3:187744872-187744917', padding = 0, name_of_window = 'Normal MBC Hotspot
# 1\n(Iqbal et al. 2007)'), window_labeler(window = 'chr3:187745199-187745246',
# padding = 0, name_of_window = 'Normal MBC Hotspot 2\n(Iqbal et al. 2007)'),
# window_labeler(window = 'chr3:187744678-187744698', padding = 0,
# name_of_window = 'DLBCL Hotspot\n(Bal et al. 2022)')), 'win_color' =
# c('#9BD08A', '#C2EFB4', '#DC766F'))

bcl6_intron_woi <- data.table(win_start = c(187744872, 187745199, 187744678), win_end = c(187744917,
    187745246, 187744698), win_name = c("Normal MBC Hotspot 1*", "Normal MBC Hotspot 2**",
    "DLBCL Hotspot***"), win_color = c("#9BD08A", "#C2EFB4", "#DC766F"))

bcl6_intron_woi$win_name <- factor(bcl6_intron_woi$win_name, levels = bcl6_intron_woi$win_name)

ideogram_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_single_chrom_ideogrom(window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250)
## Scale for x is already present.
## Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.

scale_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250)

gene_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, genes_of_interest = "BCL6", gene_name_side = "five_prime", gene_label_nudge = 30,
    gene_label_size = 4) + labs(y = "BCL6 Intron 1")

gene_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = gene_track_bcl6_intron,
    insert_layer_after = 0, ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi, aes(xmin = win_start,
        xmax = win_end, ymin = 0, ymax = 2, fill = win_name), alpha = 0.3)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows",
    values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color)

enhancer_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_bed_track(data = complete_ee, window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, aes_y_column = "tissue", aes_fill_column = "enhancer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",
    legend_title = "Element", aes_fill_colors = "#DC9445") + labs(y = "Enhancers") +
    ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744872, xmax = 187744917, ymin = 0, ymax = 2),
        alpha = 0.3, fill = "#9BD08A", color = alpha("#9BD08A", alpha = 0.2)) + ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187745199,
    xmax = 187745246, ymin = 0, ymax = 2), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#C2EFB4", color = alpha("#C2EFB4",
    alpha = 0.2)) + ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744678, xmax = 187744698, ymin = 0,
    ymax = 2), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#DC766F", color = alpha("#DC766F", alpha = 0.2))

mer_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_bed_track(data = mers_gr, window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, aes_y_column = "mer_type", aes_fill_column = "mer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",
    aes_fill_colors = c("black", "#FB832D")) + labs(y = "MERs") + ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744872,
    xmax = 187744917, ymin = 0, ymax = 3), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#9BD08A", color = alpha("#9BD08A",
    alpha = 0.06)) + ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187745199, xmax = 187745246, ymin = 0,
    ymax = 3), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#C2EFB4", color = alpha("#C2EFB4", alpha = 0.06)) +
    ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744678, xmax = 187744698, ymin = 0, ymax = 3),
        alpha = 0.2, fill = "#DC766F", color = alpha("#DC766F", alpha = 0.06))

# create SNV track layer to add on top of
snv_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_count_mutations(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#191970", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("Myeloma\nSNVs") + scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3),
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = snv_track_bcl6_intron,
    insert_layer_after = 0, ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi, aes(xmin = win_start,
        xmax = win_end, ymin = 0, ymax = max(snv_track_bcl6_intron$data$n) + 1, fill = win_name),
        alpha = 0.3)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows", values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) +
    scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.01,
        0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

# snv_vaf_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = snv_muts_hq_dt,
# window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window, flank = 250, contrast = F,
# x_axis_theme = 'blank') + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = 'Density',
# colours = rev(paletteer_c('grDevices::GnBu', 15)))

# snv_vaf_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base =
# snv_vaf_track_bcl6_intron, insert_layer_after = 0, ggplot2::geom_rect(data =
# bcl6_intron_woi, aes(xmin = win_start, xmax = win_end, ymin = 0, ymax = 1,
# fill = win_name), alpha = 0.2)) + scale_fill_manual(name = 'Windows',values =
# bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) + theme(legend.position = 'none')

indel_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_count_mutations(data = indel_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#EEA9B8", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("Myeloma\nInDels") + scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3),
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

indel_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = indel_track_bcl6_intron,
    insert_layer_after = 0, ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi, aes(xmin = win_start,
        xmax = win_end, ymin = 0, ymax = max(indel_track_bcl6_intron$data$n) + 1,
        fill = win_name), alpha = 0.3)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows", values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) +
    scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.01,
        0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_count_mutations(data = gr2dt(normal_lymphocyte_muts_for_model) %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV"), window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "gray", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("Lymphocyte\nSNVs") + scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3),
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron,
    insert_layer_after = 0, ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi, aes(xmin = win_start,
        xmax = win_end, ymin = 0, ymax = max(normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron$data$n) +
            1, fill = win_name), alpha = 0.3)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows",
    values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) + scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3),
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.


bcl6_intron_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(ideogram_track_bcl6_intron,
    scale_track_bcl6_intron, normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines, indel_track_bcl6_intron_lines,
    snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines, enhancer_track_bcl6_intron, mer_track_bcl6_intron,
    gene_track_bcl6_intron_lines), ncol = 1, guides = "collect", heights = c(0.01,
    0.015, 0.03, 0.03, 0.03, 0.015, 0.015, 0.01))
# 9 x 14 portrait


# bcl6_intron_locus_view <-
# patchwork::wrap_plots(list(ideogram_track_bcl6_intron,
# scale_track_bcl6_intron, normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron,
# snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines, snv_vaf_track_bcl6_intron_lines,
# enhancer_track_bcl6_intron, mer_track_bcl6_intron,
# gene_track_bcl6_intron_lines), ncol = 1, guides = 'collect', heights =
# c(0.1,0.075,0.15,0.15,0.15,0.075,0.1,0.075))

muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id %in% c("snv_er42", "indel_er7", "snv_er43", "snv_er44")) %>%
    dplyr::select(Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::count()/n_distinct(muts_per_mer$Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode)
##            n
##        <num>
## 1: 0.7687075

Look at MER-promoter overlap

Code 
# Main gene set list will include any B cell related pathway from the
# immunesigdb bcell_c7_immunesigdb_pathways <-
# fgsea::gmtPathways(paste0(base_data_path,
# 'functionalGenomicElements/c7.bcell.immunesigdb.v2025.1.Hs.symbols.gmt'))

prom_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(promoter_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# mean overlap of all
prom_mers %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_promotor_overlap = mean(promoter_overlap_pct))
##   mean_promotor_overlap
## 1              20.67854

message("Number of Promoter MERs: ", length(unique(prom_mers$mer_id)))
## Number of Promoter MERs: 41

prom_mers_genes <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(promoter_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients)) %>%
    dplyr::select(prom_id) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    stringr::str_split(pattern = ",") %>%
    unlist() %>%
    stringr::str_remove(pattern = "prom_")

message("Number of Genes linked to Promoter MERs: ", length(unique(prom_mers_genes)))
## Number of Genes linked to Promoter MERs: 41

# All promoter - E/SE-MER overlap
prom_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(enhancer_id)) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_type, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
        seqnames, start, end, cluster_label, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, enhancer_overlap_pct, tissue, enhancer_type, promoter_overlap_pct,
        prom_id, overlapping_genes) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))
##     mer_id mer_type mer_gene_label total_mut_patients pct_total_mut_patients

# Just GCB E/SE-MERs
prom_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_type, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
        seqnames, start, end, cluster_label, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, enhancer_overlap_pct, tissue, enhancer_type, promoter_overlap_pct,
        prom_id, overlapping_genes) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# Both GCB/PC E/SE-MERs
prom_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB,PC") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_type, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
        seqnames, start, end, cluster_label, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, enhancer_overlap_pct, tissue, enhancer_type, promoter_overlap_pct,
        prom_id, overlapping_genes) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))
##    mer_id mer_type mer_gene_label total_mut_patients pct_total_mut_patients
##    <char>   <fctr>         <char>              <int>                  <num>
## 1:   mer7      SNV          BACH2                 70                   23.8
## 2:   mer6      SNV           BTG2                 63                   21.4
## 3:   mer5      SNV        ZFP36L1                 63                   21.4
## 4:  mer15      SNV           RHOH                 56                   19.0
## 5:  mer12      SNV         MIR142                 52                   17.7
## 6:  mer33      SNV           IRF8                 47                   16.0
##    seqnames     start       end cluster_label cds_overlap_pct
##      <char>     <int>     <int>        <char>           <num>
## 1:     chr6  90286001  90306500           UTR            0.00
## 2:     chr1 203296001 203313000           UTR            3.53
## 3:    chr14  68782501  68800000      Intronic            1.95
## 4:     chr4  40190001  40207000      Intronic            0.00
## 5:    chr17  58322001  58342000    Intergenic            0.00
## 6:    chr16  85890001  85909500      Intronic            2.41
##    three_prime_utr_overlap_pct five_prime_utr_overlap_pct intron_overlap_pct
##                          <num>                      <num>              <num>
## 1:                           0                       8.38              78.44
## 2:                           0                       1.18              71.76
## 3:                           0                       3.25              92.21
## 4:                           0                       0.00              97.47
## 5:                           0                       1.35               0.00
## 6:                           0                       1.20              86.75
##    intergenic_pct lncrna_overlap_pct mirna_overlap_pct enhancer_overlap_pct
##             <num>              <num>             <num>                <num>
## 1:           13.2               0.00               0.0                  100
## 2:           23.5               9.41               0.0                  100
## 3:            2.6               0.00               0.0                  100
## 4:            2.5               0.00               0.0                  100
## 5:           98.6              90.54               2.7                  100
## 6:            9.6               0.00               0.0                  100
##    tissue enhancer_type promoter_overlap_pct      prom_id
##    <char>        <char>                <num>       <char>
## 1: GCB,PC          E,SE                16.77   prom_BACH2
## 2: GCB,PC            SE                25.88    prom_BTG2
## 3: GCB,PC          E,SE                40.91 prom_ZFP36L1
## 4: GCB,PC            SE                 5.06    prom_RHOH
## 5: GCB,PC            SE                 1.35 prom_TSPOAP1
## 6: GCB,PC          E,SE                49.40    prom_IRF8
##                              overlapping_genes
##                                         <char>
## 1:                                       BACH2
## 2:                                BTG2-DT;BTG2
## 3:                                     ZFP36L1
## 4:                                        RHOH
## 5: TSPOAP1;MIR142HG;MIR142;MIR4736;TSPOAP1-AS1
## 6:                                        IRF8

# Just PC E/SE-MERs
prom_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(tissue == "PC") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_type, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
        seqnames, start, end, cluster_label, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, enhancer_overlap_pct, tissue, enhancer_type, promoter_overlap_pct,
        prom_id, overlapping_genes) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))
##    mer_id mer_type mer_gene_label total_mut_patients pct_total_mut_patients
##    <char>   <fctr>         <char>              <int>                  <num>
## 1: mer155      SNV          CCND1                 36                   12.2
## 2: mer235      SNV       ZDHHC11B                 22                    7.5
##    seqnames    start      end cluster_label cds_overlap_pct
##      <char>    <int>    <int>        <char>           <num>
## 1:    chr11 69631501 69651500           UTR           11.05
## 2:     chr5   778001   789000           UTR            0.00
##    three_prime_utr_overlap_pct five_prime_utr_overlap_pct intron_overlap_pct
##                          <num>                      <num>              <num>
## 1:                           0                       8.72              65.12
## 2:                           0                       3.85              42.31
##    intergenic_pct lncrna_overlap_pct mirna_overlap_pct enhancer_overlap_pct
##             <num>              <num>             <num>                <num>
## 1:           15.1                  0                 0                90.70
## 2:           53.8                  0                 0                30.77
##    tissue enhancer_type promoter_overlap_pct       prom_id overlapping_genes
##    <char>        <char>                <num>        <char>            <char>
## 1:     PC             E                30.81    prom_CCND1             CCND1
## 2:     PC             E                23.08 prom_ZDHHC11B          ZDHHC11B

# driver gene promoter MERs
mm_driver_genes[which(mm_driver_genes$gene %in% prom_mers_genes), ]
##       gene
##     <char>
## 1:    EGR1
## 2:   DUSP2
## 3:   CCND1
## 4:     LTB
## 5:   RFTN1
## 6: ZFP36L1

Assess the overlap of MERs and normal lymphocyte MERs

Code 
lymph_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table(mer_id = mers$mer_id, mer_gene_label = mers$mer_gene_label,
    lymphocyte_overlap_pct = 0)

for (i in 1:n_distinct(lymph_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
    # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
    per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(lymph_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
    mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)

    # Normal Lymphocyte MER overlap
    mer_mut_lymph_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(lymphocyte_mers)),
        subject = per_mer_muts, qcol = "nearest_gene", scol = c("mer_gene_label",
            "mer_id")))
    lymph_overlap_pct$lymphocyte_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(unique(mer_mut_lymph_overlap$subject.id))/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
}

# Add the Enhancer / Promoter to MER overlap
mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = lymph_overlap_pct, by = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label"))

lymph_mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(lymphocyte_overlap_pct > 0)

# mean overlap of all
lymph_mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_lymphocyte_overlap = mean(lymphocyte_overlap_pct))
##   mean_lymphocyte_overlap
## 1                96.32842

message("Number of Lymphocyte overlap MERs: ", length(unique(lymph_mers_overlap$mer_id)))
## Number of Lymphocyte overlap MERs: 19

# E/SE-MERs that overlap with normal lymphocyte MERs
lymph_mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_type, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
        seqnames, start, end, cluster_label, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, enhancer_overlap_pct, tissue, enhancer_type, promoter_overlap_pct,
        prom_id, overlapping_genes, lymphocyte_overlap_pct) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

    dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB,PC") %>%

Visualize the distribution of MERs across different genomic feature sets

Code 
mer_distro_by_protein_coding <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, cluster_label) %>%
    dplyr::rename(feature = cluster_label) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "Protein-coding")

mer_distro_by_enhancer <- e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(enhancer_id) ~ "Enhancers")) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, feature) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "Enhancers")

mer_distro_by_promoter <- prom_mers %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(prom_id) ~ "Promoters")) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, feature) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "Promoters")

mer_distro_by_lncrna <- lncrna_mers %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(lncrna) ~ "lncRNA")) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(lncrna)) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, feature) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "lncRNA")

mer_distro_by_mirna <- mirna_mers %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(mirna) ~ "miRNA")) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(mirna)) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, feature) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "miRNA")

mer_distro_by_lymphocyte <- lymph_mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = "Normal Lymphocytes") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, feature) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "Normal Lymphocytes")

mer_distro_by_feature <- rbind(mer_distro_by_protein_coding, mer_distro_by_enhancer,
    mer_distro_by_promoter, mer_distro_by_lncrna, mer_distro_by_mirna, mer_distro_by_lymphocyte)

mer_distro_by_feature$group <- factor(mer_distro_by_feature$group, levels = c("Protein-coding",
    "Enhancers", "Promoters", "lncRNA", "miRNA", "Normal Lymphocytes"))
mer_distro_by_feature$feature <- factor(mer_distro_by_feature$feature, levels = c("CDS",
    "Intronic", "UTR", "Intergenic", "Enhancers", "Promoters", "lncRNA", "miRNA",
    "Normal Lymphocytes"))

mer_distro_by_feature_plot <- ggplot(mer_distro_by_feature, aes(x = midpoint_abs_linear,
    y = -log10(q_value), color = feature)) + ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3.5) +
    facet_wrap(facets = ~group, ncol = 1) + ggplot2::labs(y = expression("-log"[10] *
    "(" * italic("q-value") * ")")) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
    labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]), breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(name = "Genomic\nFeature", values = paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[1:9]) +
    ggplot2::theme(axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15), axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 11), legend.position = "top",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 13))


# Pie chart for each facet plot

mer_distro_pie_protein_coding <- ggpie::ggpie(data = mer_distro_by_feature %>%
    dplyr::filter(group == "Protein-coding"), group_key = "feature", count_type = "full",
    label_info = c("count", "ratio"), label_type = "horizon", label_size = 6, label_pos = "out",
    border_color = NA) + scale_fill_manual(values = paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[1:4]) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
## Warning in geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity", color = border_color, :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

mer_distro_pie_enhnacer <- ggpie::ggpie(data = mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(enhancer_id) ~ "Enhancers", is.na(enhancer_id) ~
        "non-Enhancers")) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, feature), group_key = "feature", count_type = "full", label_info = c("count",
    "ratio"), label_type = "horizon", label_size = 6, label_pos = "out", border_color = NA) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray85", paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[5])) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
## Warning in geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity", color = border_color, :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

mer_distro_pie_promoter <- ggpie::ggpie(data = mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(prom_id) ~ "Promoters", is.na(prom_id) ~
        "non-Promoters")) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, feature), group_key = "feature", count_type = "full", label_info = c("count",
    "ratio"), label_type = "horizon", label_size = 6, label_pos = "out", border_color = NA) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray85", paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[6])) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
## Warning in geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity", color = border_color, :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

mer_distro_pie_lncrna <- ggpie::ggpie(data = lncrna_mers %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(lncrna) ~ "lncRNA", is.na(lncrna) ~
        "non-lncRNA")) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, feature), group_key = "feature", count_type = "full", label_info = c("count",
    "ratio"), label_type = "horizon", label_size = 6, label_pos = "out", border_color = NA) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[7], "gray85")) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
## Warning in geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity", color = border_color, :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

mer_distro_pie_mirna <- ggpie::ggpie(data = mirna_mers %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(mirna) ~ "miRNA", is.na(mirna) ~ "non-miRNA")) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, feature), group_key = "feature", count_type = "full", label_info = c("count",
    "ratio"), label_type = "horizon", label_size = 6, label_pos = "out", border_color = NA) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray85", paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[8])) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
## Warning in geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity", color = border_color, :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

mer_distro_pie_lymphocyte <- ggpie::ggpie(data = mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(lymphocyte_overlap_pct > 0 ~ "Lymphocyte",
        lymphocyte_overlap_pct <= 0 ~ "non-Lymphocyte")) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, feature), group_key = "feature", count_type = "full", label_info = c("count",
    "ratio"), label_type = "horizon", label_size = 6, label_pos = "out", border_color = NA) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("gray85", paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[9])) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
## Warning in geom_bar(width = 1, stat = "identity", color = border_color, :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

# mer_distro_pie_protein_coding / mer_distro_pie_enhnacer /
# mer_distro_pie_promoter / mer_distro_pie_lncrna / mer_distro_pie_mirna /
# mer_distro_pie_lymphocyte

# UpSet plot of overlap between all types
mer_by_feature_upset <- list(CDS = mer_distro_by_protein_coding %>%
    dplyr::filter(feature == "CDS") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), Intronic = mer_distro_by_protein_coding %>%
    dplyr::filter(feature == "Intronic") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), UTR = mer_distro_by_protein_coding %>%
    dplyr::filter(feature == "UTR") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), Intergenic = mer_distro_by_protein_coding %>%
    dplyr::filter(feature == "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), Enhancers = mer_distro_by_enhancer %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), Promoters = mer_distro_by_promoter %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), lncRNA = mer_distro_by_lncrna %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), miRNA = mer_distro_by_mirna %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort(), `Normal Lymphocytes` = mer_distro_by_lymphocyte %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character() %>%
    gtools::mixedsort())

mer_by_feature_upset_order <- c("CDS", "Intronic", "UTR", "Intergenic", "Enhancers",
    "Promoters", "lncRNA", "miRNA", "Normal Lymphocytes")

mer_by_feature_upset_plot <- upset(data = fromList(input = mer_by_feature_upset),
    sets = rev(mer_by_feature_upset_order), keep.order = T, order.by = "freq", mainbar.y.label = "Intersection",
    sets.x.label = "MERs annotated by feature", point.size = 5.25, line.size = 1.8,
    text.scale = c(1.75, 1.5, 1.5, 1.25, 1.25, 1.5), sets.bar.color = rev(paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[1:9]),
    matrix.color = "black")

Investigate the mutation specifics of the E/SE MERs including signatures

Code 
muts_per_grouped_mer_for_plot <- muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::rename(patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode, sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = e_se_mers[, c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label", "mut_uniq_id",
        "enhancer_id", "tissue", "enhancer_type")], by = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata, by = c("patient", "sample")) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(mer_id) | !is.na(tissue))

# # Before doing sig analysis, find total mutations across all patients # SNVs
