Figure 4

Supplementary Figure 8

Results - Kataegis and Omikli are associated with E/SE-MERs

Analyze MM genomes for clustered mutation scars Collect all clustered mutations per sample and calculate

Code 
# Read in the total set of clustered vs non-clustered mutations
sigpro_clusters_all_clustered <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/clusters_clustered_vaf.txt"),
    header = T, sep = "\t")

sigpro_clusters_non_clustered <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/clusters_nonClustered.txt"),
    header = F, sep = "\t", skip = 1, col.names = colnames(sigpro_clusters_all_clustered)[-14])

# Extract the high-quality samples
hq_sigpro_clusters_all_clustered <- sigpro_clusters_all_clustered %>%
    dplyr::mutate(patient = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,
        2], sample = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,
        1]) %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample))

hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered <- sigpro_clusters_non_clustered %>%
    dplyr::mutate(patient = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,
        2], sample = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,
        1]) %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample))

# Calculate the clustered mutational burden and percentage clustered vs non
# clustered
hq_clustered_metrics <- hq_sigpro_clusters_all_clustered %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::rename(tot_clustered = n) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered %>%
        dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
        dplyr::count() %>%
        dplyr::ungroup() %>%
        dplyr::rename(tot_non_clustered = n), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(clustered_tmb = tot_clustered/2800, clustered_pct = round((tot_clustered/(tot_clustered +
        tot_non_clustered)) * 100, digits = 1)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, SBS5, SBS9), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(age_group = case_when(age < 65 ~ "Younger than 65", age >= 65 ~
        "65 and older"))

# TODO: work on this snake plot
hq_clustered_metrics$sample <- factor(hq_clustered_metrics$sample, levels = hq_clustered_metrics %>%
    dplyr::arrange(clustered_pct) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character())

# Calculate some median All hq_clustered_metrics %>% dplyr::group_by(patient)
# %>% dplyr::reframe('median_pct_clustered' = median(clustered_pct))

# by Dx
clustered_muts_by_dx_medians <- hq_clustered_metrics %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_pct_clustered = median(clustered_pct))

# find point for median bar
clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x <- hq_clustered_metrics %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group_midpoint = n/2)

clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint <- clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_midpoint +
    c(0, clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[1], sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:2)]),
        sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:3)]))

clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_range <- c(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[1],
    sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:2)]), sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:3)]),
    sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:4)]))

clustered_pct_by_dx_snake_plot <- ggplot(hq_clustered_metrics) + geom_point(aes(x = sample,
    y = clustered_pct, color = disease_stage), size = 3.5) + labs(x = "Samples",
    y = "Mutations in\nclustered events (%)") + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease\nStage",
    values = disease_stage_colors) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01,
    0.01), limits = c(0, 38)) + theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
    x = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint - 7, xend = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint +
        7, y = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians$median_pct_clustered, yend = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians$median_pct_clustered,
    color = "red", linewidth = 2.25) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(0,
    clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_range[-4]), xmax = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_range,
    ymin = 0, ymax = 38, alpha = 0.3, fill = c("gray75", "gray35", "gray75", "gray35"))

# Read in each set of clustered event type
cluster_subclass <- c("DBS", "MBS", "Omikli", "KataegisProcessiveKetones", "KataegisNonProcessive",
    "KataegisProcessiveStrong", "KataegisProcessivePyramidine", "Other")
cluster_mainclass <- c("DBS", "MBS", "Omikli", "Kataegis", "Kataegis", "Kataegis",
    "Kataegis", "Other")
sigpro_clusters_output <- data.table()
for (i in 1:length(cluster_subclass)) {
    cluster_subclass_data <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/subclasses/",
        list.files(path = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/subclasses"),
            pattern = paste0("*", cluster_subclass[i], ".txt"))), fill = T)[, c(1:15)] %>%
        dplyr::mutate(seqnames = paste0("chr", chr), sample = str_split(string = samples,
            pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[, 1], patient = str_split(string = samples,
            pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[, 2], subclass = cluster_subclass[i],
            mainclass = cluster_mainclass[i]) %>%
        dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, ref, alt, patient, sample, group, IMD,
            `VAF/CCF`, subclass, mainclass)

    sigpro_clusters_output <- rrbind(sigpro_clusters_output, cluster_subclass_data)
}

# Tally the composition of each subclass of clustered event
per_patient_cluster_composition <- sigpro_clusters_output %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_clustered_metrics, by = c("patient", "sample")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, sample, disease_stage, mainclass) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "n_per_class") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, sample, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sum_clustered_muts = sum(n_per_class)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_per_class = round(n_per_class/sum_clustered_muts * 100, digits = 2)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

# Make same organization as snake plot
per_patient_cluster_composition$sample <- factor(per_patient_cluster_composition$sample,
    levels = hq_clustered_metrics$sample)
per_patient_cluster_composition$mainclass <- factor(per_patient_cluster_composition$mainclass,
    levels = c("DBS", "MBS", "Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other"))

clustered_events_colors <- c("#000000", "#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#D55E00",
    "#767F8B")

clustered_muts_composition_barplot <- ggplot(per_patient_cluster_composition) + geom_col(aes(x = sample,
    y = pct_per_class, fill = mainclass), width = 1) + labs(x = "Samples", y = "Relative\nContribution (%)") +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Clustered\nEvent\nSubclass", values = clustered_events_colors[2:6]) +
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

# Calculate some median
per_class_median_by_dx <- per_patient_cluster_composition %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage, mainclass) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(sum_n_per_class_dx = sum(n_per_class)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = per_patient_cluster_composition %>%
        dplyr::select(disease_stage, patient, sample, sum_clustered_muts) %>%
        unique() %>%
        dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(sum_clustered_dx = sum(sum_clustered_muts)), by = "disease_stage") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_per_class_dx = round(sum_n_per_class_dx/sum_clustered_dx *
        100, digits = 2))

# ggplot(per_class_median_by_dx) + geom_col(aes(x = disease_stage, y =
# pct_per_class_dx, fill = mainclass)) + scale_fill_manual(name =
# 'Clustered\nEvent\nSubclass', values = clustered_events_colors[2:6])


per_patient_cluster_composition %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()/(per_patient_cluster_composition %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct())
## [1] 0.952381

# What is the burden of clustered events outside of the IG regions
hq_sigpro_clusters_muts <- sigpro_clusters_output %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample))
hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_sigpro_clusters_muts))

# clustered_event_regions <- data.table() # Nest loop, first find unique sample
# then each group within (group IDs are repeated) for(i in
# 1:length(unique(hq_sigpro_clusters_muts$sample))) { cluster_event_per_sample
# <- hq_sigpro_clusters_muts %>% dplyr::filter(sample ==
# unique(hq_sigpro_clusters_muts$sample)[i]) # need to loop through each
# subclass for(j in 1:length(unique(cluster_event_per_sample$subclass))) {
# per_sample_cluster_event_by_sublcass <- cluster_event_per_sample %>%
# dplyr::filter(subclass == unique(cluster_event_per_sample$subclass)[j]) #
# Nested loop through the grouped muts to tally individual events for(j in
# 1:length(unique(per_sample_cluster_event_by_sublcass$group))) {
# per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group <-
# per_sample_cluster_event_by_sublcass %>% dplyr::filter(group ==
# unique(per_sample_cluster_event_by_sublcass$group)[j])
# clustered_event_regions <- rrbind(clustered_event_regions,
# data.table('seqnames' =
# unique(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group$seqnames), 'start' =
# dplyr::first(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group$start), 'end' =
# dplyr::last(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group$start), 'sample' =
# unique(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group$sample), 'patient' =
# unique(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group$patient), 'mainclass' =
# unique(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group$mainclass), 'subclass' =
# unique(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group$subclass), 'group' =
# unique(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group$group), 'mut_count' =
# nrow(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group), 'vaf_median' =
# median(per_sample_cluster_subclass_event_group$`VAF/CCF`))) } } }

# saveRDS(object = clustered_event_regions, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'clusteredMutationsAnalysis/clusteredEvents.rds'))
clustered_event_regions <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/clusteredEvents.rds"))

# Convert to GR obj
clustered_event_regions_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(clustered_event_regions))

# Restrict to autosomes
hq_clustered_event_regions_gr <- clustered_event_regions_gr %Q% (!seqnames %in% c("chrX",
    "chrY"))

# Remove any duplicated event per patient as analysis is not across disease
# stage
uniq_hq_clustered_event_regions_gr <- hq_clustered_event_regions_gr[!duplicated(hq_clustered_event_regions_gr[,
    2])]

# Now mask regions that coincide with Ig regions , annotate with nearest gene
# and convert to DT
uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(gr.setdiff(query = uniq_hq_clustered_event_regions_gr,
    subject = ig_region_mask_bed))
uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_gr$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_gr,
    subject = gr.stripstrand(genes))]
uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt <- gr2dt(uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_gr)

# Count the events and compare to the total with Ig regions
uniq_ig_clustered_events_gr <- gr.findoverlaps(query = uniq_hq_clustered_event_regions_gr,
    subject = ig_region_mask_bed, qcol = c("sample", "patient", "mainclass", "subclass",
        "group", "mut_count", "vaf_median"))
uniq_ig_clustered_events_dt <- gr2dt(uniq_ig_clustered_events_gr)

message("Percentage of clustered events in Ig and non-Ig regions:")
## Percentage of clustered events in Ig and non-Ig regions:
uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mainclass) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "non_ig") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = uniq_ig_clustered_events_dt %>%
        dplyr::group_by(mainclass) %>%
        dplyr::count(name = "ig") %>%
        dplyr::ungroup(), by = "mainclass") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_non_ig_clustered_events = round((non_ig/(non_ig + ig)) * 100,
        digits = 2))
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   mainclass non_ig    ig pct_non_ig_clustered_events
##   <chr>      <int> <int>                       <dbl>
## 1 DBS         4386    56                        98.7
## 2 Kataegis    1757  4602                        27.6
## 3 MBS           66     1                        98.5
## 4 Omikli     28416  4161                        87.2
## 5 Other      27319  3669                        88.2

What do non-Ig region kataegis look like compare to Ig

Code 
# How many patients
uniq_ig_clustered_events_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()/294
## [1] 0.9013605

uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()/294

# median / range of kat events
message("Range and median number of kataegis events per patient:")
## Range and median number of kataegis events per patient:
uniq_ig_clustered_events_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_ig_kataegis_events = median(n), range_ig_kataegis_events = range(n))
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   median_ig_kataegis_events range_ig_kataegis_events
##                       <int>                    <int>
## 1                        15                        1
## 2                        15                      129

uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_non_ig_kataegis_events = median(n), range_non_ig_kataegis_events = range(n))
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   median_non_ig_kataegis_events range_non_ig_kataegis_events
##                           <dbl>                        <int>
## 1                             4                            1
## 2                             4                          106

# What difference in size
print("Range and median width of kataegis events:")
## [1] "Range and median width of kataegis events:"
uniq_ig_clustered_events_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(width) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_span_ig_kataegis = median(width), range_span_ig_kataegis = range(width))
##   median_span_ig_kataegis range_span_ig_kataegis
## 1                     551                      1
## 2                     551                  16204

uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(width) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_span_non_ig_kataegis = median(width), range_span_non_ig_kataegis = range(width))
##   median_span_non_ig_kataegis range_span_non_ig_kataegis
## 1                        1117                         27
## 2                        1117                      30879

kataegis_width_distro <- rrbind(uniq_ig_clustered_events_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(width) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(region = "Ig"), uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(width) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(region = "Non-Ig")) %>%
    ggplot() + ggplot2::geom_density(aes(x = width, fill = region), alpha = 0.6) +
    labs(x = "Genomic Span (bp)", y = "Density") + scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01,
    0.01), labels = scales::label_comma()) + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_scientific()) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Kataegis\nRegion", values = c("#BA9B00", "#006BB8")) +
    theme(legend.position = "right", legend.text = element_text(size = 15), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) +
    ggforce::facet_zoom(x = width > 10000, ylim = c(0, 0.00001), horizontal = F,
        zoom.size = 0.75)

# How many over 10 Kb
rrbind(uniq_ig_clustered_events_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(width) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(region = "Ig"), uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(width) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(region = "Non-Ig")) %>%
    dplyr::filter(width > 10000) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(region) %>%
    dplyr::count("kataegis_events_span_over_10kb") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()
## # A tibble: 2 × 3
##   region `"kataegis_events_span_over_10kb"`     n
##   <chr>  <chr>                              <int>
## 1 Ig     kataegis_events_span_over_10kb        10
## 2 Non-Ig kataegis_events_span_over_10kb        42

# range snvs per event
message("Range of SNV count involved in kataegis events:")
## Range of SNV count involved in kataegis events:
uniq_ig_clustered_events_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(mut_count) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_burden_ig_kataegis = median(mut_count), range_burden_ig_kataegis = range(mut_count))
##   median_burden_ig_kataegis range_burden_ig_kataegis
## 1                         7                        4
## 2                         7                       56

uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(mut_count) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_burden_non_ig_kataegis = median(mut_count), range_burden_non_ig_kataegis = range(mut_count))
##   median_burden_non_ig_kataegis range_burden_non_ig_kataegis
## 1                             5                            4
## 2                             5                           60

Identify the most recurrent regions of clustered mutations

Code 
# To find the consensus regions of clustered mutations, first need to define
# the consensus per main class of clustered event DBS, MBS, Omikli, Kataegis,
# Other Loop through the non-Ig clustered events by main class and find
# consensus regions
consensus_clustered_event_regions <- data.table()
for (i in 1:length(c("Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other"))) {
    class_specific_clustered_events <- uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
        dplyr::filter(mainclass == c("Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other")[i]) %>%
        dplyr::rename(chrom = seqnames, sample_name = patient) %>%
        dplyr::select(chrom, start, end, sample_name)

    # peakCombiner workflow 1. Input preparation, computes center genomic
    # coordinate per chromosome
    prep_class_specific_clustered_events <- peakCombiner::prepare_input_regions(data = class_specific_clustered_events,
        show_messages = F)

    # 2. Add small flank to each region using defined value or default of
    # median span of all input will use half of the median of all sizes
    prep_class_specific_clustered_events_expand <- peakCombiner::center_expand_regions(data = prep_class_specific_clustered_events,
        expand_by = median(GenomicRanges::width(dt2gr(prep_class_specific_clustered_events))) *
            0.25, show_messages = F)

    # 3. Consensus peak calling found_in_samples = threshold of minimumn sample
    # overlap for outpuut combined_center = nearest - the 'center' of the input
    # region that is closest to mean of the 'center's of all overlapping input
    # regions
    consensus_class_specific_clustered_events <- peakCombiner::combine_regions(data = prep_class_specific_clustered_events_expand,
        found_in_samples = 2, combined_center = "nearest", annotate_with_input_names = T,
        combined_sample_name = c("Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other")[i], show_messages = F)

    # Add to the final combined set of peaks
    consensus_clustered_event_regions <- rrbind(consensus_clustered_event_regions,
        consensus_class_specific_clustered_events)
}
## Warning in dt2gr(prep_class_specific_clustered_events): Warning: Coercing to
## GRanges via non-standard columns
## Warning in dt2gr(prep_class_specific_clustered_events): Warning: Coercing to
## GRanges via non-standard columns
## Warning in dt2gr(prep_class_specific_clustered_events): Warning: Coercing to
## GRanges via non-standard columns

# Tabulate the consensus set of each class
consensus_clustered_event_regions_dt <- consensus_clustered_event_regions %>%
    dplyr::mutate(patient_count = stringr::str_count(string = input_names, pattern = ";") +
        1, pct_patients = round(patient_count/294 * 100, digits = 1)) %>%
    dplyr::rename(event_id = name, event_type = sample_name, patient_events_at_region = input_names) %>%
    dplyr::select(chrom, start, end, event_type, event_id, patient_events_at_region,
        patient_count, pct_patients)

message("Number of consensus regions of omikli, kataegis, and other class events:")
## Number of consensus regions of omikli, kataegis, and other class events:
table(consensus_clustered_event_regions_dt$event_type)
## 
## Kataegis   Omikli    Other 
##       30      252      135

consensus_clustered_event_regions_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(consensus_clustered_event_regions_dt))
## Warning in dt2gr(consensus_clustered_event_regions_dt): Warning: Coercing to
## GRanges via non-standard columns

Contextualize the consensus clustered regions with MERs and other important regions

Code 
all_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap <- gr.findoverlaps(query = consensus_clustered_event_regions_gr,
    subject = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers_overlap)), qcol = c("event_type",
        "event_id", "patient_events_at_region", "patient_count", "pct_patients"),
    scol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label", "cluster_label", "tissue", "enhancer_type",
        "prom_id", "overlapping_genes"))
all_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap_dt <- gr2dt(all_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap)

all_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.na(tissue))

# Look at overlap between E/SE-MERs and consensus clustered regions
e_se_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap_gr <- gr.findoverlaps(query = consensus_clustered_event_regions_gr,
    subject = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(e_se_mers)), qcol = c("event_type",
        "event_id", "patient_events_at_region", "patient_count", "pct_patients"),
    scol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label", "tissue", "enhancer_type"))
e_se_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap_dt <- gr2dt(e_se_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap_gr)

# How many E/SE-MERs
n_distinct(e_se_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap_dt$mer_id)

unique(e_se_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap_dt$mer_gene_label)

    dplyr::group_by(mer_gene_label, event_type) %>%

message("Percentage of Other clustered events that overlap with E/SE-MERs: ", e_se_mer_consensus_clustered_event_overlap_dt %>%

Investigate the percentage of mutations within E/SE-MERs are from clustered events and relate to SBS signatures

Code 
# Collect all unique clustered mutations
uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr <- hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr[!duplicated(hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr[,
    2])]

# Collect all unique non-clustered mutations
hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered))
## Warning in dt2gr(hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered): Warning: Coercing to
## GRanges via non-standard columns
uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr <- hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr[!duplicated(hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr[,
    2])]


# All MERs
all_mers_non_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers_overlap)),
    subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr, qcol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label",
        "cluster_label", "tissue", "enhancer_type", "prom_id", "overlapping_genes"),
    scol = c("patient", "sample", "ref", "alt"))
all_mers_non_clustered_muts$mainclass <- "Non-clustered"

all_mers_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers_overlap)),
    subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr, qcol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label",
        "cluster_label", "tissue", "enhancer_type", "prom_id", "overlapping_genes"),
    scol = c("patient", "sample", "ref", "alt", "mainclass"))
# How many MERs have clustered mutations
n_distinct(all_mers_clustered_muts$mer_id)/307
## [1] 0.8729642

gr2dt(all_mers_clustered_muts) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, cluster_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::count(cluster_label)
##    cluster_label     n
##           <char> <int>
## 1:           CDS     8
## 2:    Intergenic   152
## 3:      Intronic    79
## 4:           UTR    29


# GCB E/SE-MERs
gcb_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = e_se_mers_gr %Q% (tissue ==
    "GCB"), subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr, qcol = c("mer_id",
    "mer_gene_label", "tissue", "enhancer_type"), scol = c("patient", "sample", "ref",
    "alt"))
gcb_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts$mainclass <- "Non-clustered"

gcb_e_se_mers_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = e_se_mers_gr %Q% (tissue ==
    "GCB"), subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr, qcol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label",
    "tissue", "enhancer_type"), scol = c("patient", "sample", "ref", "alt", "mainclass"))
# How many GCB E/SE-MERs have clustered mutations
n_distinct(gcb_e_se_mers_clustered_muts$mer_id)
## [1] 25

gcb_e_se_mers_cluster_composition <- gr2dt(grbind(gcb_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts,
    gcb_e_se_mers_clustered_muts)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mainclass, tissue) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "mut_count") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_per_class = round(mut_count/sum(mut_count) * 100, digits = 3))

gcb_e_se_mers_cluster_composition$mainclass <- factor(gcb_e_se_mers_cluster_composition$mainclass,
    levels = c("Non-clustered", "DBS", "MBS", "Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other"))
# Total clustered vs non %
gcb_e_se_mers_cluster_composition %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass %in% c("DBS", "Kataegis", "Omikli", "Other")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(total_pct_clustered = sum(pct_per_class))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   total_pct_clustered
##                 <dbl>
## 1                71.9

# Dual GCB/PC E/SE-MERs
dual_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = e_se_mers_gr %Q% (tissue ==
    "GCB,PC"), subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr, qcol = c("mer_id",
    "mer_gene_label", "tissue", "enhancer_type"), scol = c("patient", "sample", "ref",
    "alt"))
dual_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts$mainclass <- "Non-clustered"

dual_e_se_mers_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = e_se_mers_gr %Q% (tissue ==
    "GCB,PC"), subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr, qcol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label",
    "tissue", "enhancer_type"), scol = c("patient", "sample", "ref", "alt", "mainclass"))
n_distinct(dual_e_se_mers_clustered_muts$mer_id)
## [1] 11

dual_e_se_mers_cluster_composition <- gr2dt(grbind(dual_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts,
    dual_e_se_mers_clustered_muts)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mainclass, tissue) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "mut_count") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_per_class = round(mut_count/sum(mut_count) * 100, digits = 3))

dual_e_se_mers_cluster_composition$mainclass <- factor(dual_e_se_mers_cluster_composition$mainclass,
    levels = c("Non-clustered", "DBS", "MBS", "Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other"))
dual_e_se_mers_cluster_composition %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass %in% c("DBS", "Kataegis", "Omikli", "Other")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(total_pct_clustered = sum(pct_per_class))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   total_pct_clustered
##                 <dbl>
## 1                58.3

# PC E/SE-MERs
pc_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = e_se_mers_gr %Q% (tissue ==
    "PC"), subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr, qcol = c("mer_id",
    "mer_gene_label", "tissue", "enhancer_type"), scol = c("patient", "sample", "ref",
    "alt"))
pc_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts$mainclass <- "Non-clustered"

pc_e_se_mers_clustered_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = e_se_mers_gr %Q% (tissue ==
    "PC"), subject = uniq_hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr, qcol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label",
    "tissue", "enhancer_type"), scol = c("patient", "sample", "ref", "alt", "mainclass"))
n_distinct(pc_e_se_mers_clustered_muts$mer_id)
## [1] 11

pc_e_se_mers_cluster_composition <- gr2dt(grbind(pc_e_se_mers_non_clustered_muts,
    pc_e_se_mers_clustered_muts)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mainclass, tissue) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "mut_count") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_per_class = round(mut_count/sum(mut_count) * 100, digits = 3))

pc_e_se_mers_cluster_composition$mainclass <- factor(pc_e_se_mers_cluster_composition$mainclass,
    levels = c("Non-clustered", "DBS", "MBS", "Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other"))
pc_e_se_mers_cluster_composition %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass %in% c("DBS", "Kataegis", "Omikli", "Other")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(total_pct_clustered = sum(pct_per_class))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   total_pct_clustered
##                 <dbl>
## 1                36.9

# GCB
gcb_e_se_mers_cluster_comp_plot <- ggplot(gcb_e_se_mers_cluster_composition) + geom_col(aes(x = tissue,
    y = pct_per_class, fill = mainclass), width = 1) + labs(x = NULL, y = "Relative\nContribution (%)") +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Clustered\nEvent\nSubclass", values = clustered_events_colors[c(1:2,
        4:6)]) + theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

# Dual
dual_e_se_mers_cluster_comp_plot <- ggplot(dual_e_se_mers_cluster_composition) +
    geom_col(aes(x = tissue, y = pct_per_class, fill = mainclass), width = 1) + labs(x = NULL,
    y = "Relative\nContribution (%)") + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01,
    0.01)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Clustered\nEvent\nSubclass", values = clustered_events_colors[c(1:2,
    4:6)]) + theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.line.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank())

# PC
pc_e_se_mers_cluster_comp_plot <- ggplot(pc_e_se_mers_cluster_composition) + geom_col(aes(x = tissue,
    y = pct_per_class, fill = mainclass), width = 1) + labs(x = NULL, y = "Relative\nContribution (%)") +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Clustered\nEvent\nSubclass", values = clustered_events_colors[c(1:2,
        4:6)]) + theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.line.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank())

Link the clustered mutation spectrum to mutational signatures in E/SE-MERs

Code 
# First, given that there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different
# samples/patients etc.), remove them here
e_se_mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_grouped_mer_for_plot[VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV", .(tissue,
    seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)]
e_se_mer_sig_muts <- e_se_mer_sig_muts[!duplicated(e_se_mer_sig_muts)]

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(e_se_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# ALL GCB
gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = e_se_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample == "GCB"), sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos",
    ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Dual GCB/PC
dual_e_se_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = e_se_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample == "GCB,PC"), sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos",
    ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# ALL PC
pc_e_se_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = e_se_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample == "PC"), sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos",
    ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)


# Generate a 96 class SBS profile plot_sbs96(grouped_mer_sig_96)
gcb_e_se_mer_96class_plot <- plot_sbs96(gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96)
dual_e_se_mer_96class_plot <- plot_sbs96(dual_e_se_mer_sig_96)
pc_e_se_mer_96class_plot <- plot_sbs96(pc_e_se_mer_sig_96)

# Translate data for use in mmsig GCB
gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96)
gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96))

# Dual
dual_e_se_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(dual_e_se_mer_sig_96)
dual_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(dual_e_se_mer_sig_96))

# PC
pc_e_se_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(pc_e_se_mer_sig_96)
pc_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(pc_e_se_mer_sig_96))


# Fit the signatures GCB
gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
    strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = TRUE, iterations = 10, genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE,
    force_include = NULL)
## 
  |                                                                            
  |                                                                      |   0%
  |                                                                            
  |======================================================================| 100%

gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t <- reshape2::melt(data = gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature <- factor(gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1",
    "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

gcb_e_se_mmsig_barplot <- ggplot(gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "E/SE-MER Group",
    y = "Relative\nContribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

message("AID-associated SBS in GCB E/SE-MERs: ")
## AID-associated SBS in GCB E/SE-MERs:
gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature %in% c("SBS9", "SBS84")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(sum = sum(rel_contrib))
##         sum
## 1 0.6429508

# Dual
dual_e_se_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = dual_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
    strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = TRUE, iterations = 10, genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE,
    force_include = NULL)
## 
  |                                                                            
  |                                                                      |   0%
  |                                                                            
  |======================================================================| 100%

dual_e_se_mer_sig_t <- reshape2::melt(data = dual_e_se_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

dual_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature <- factor(dual_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1",
    "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

dual_e_se_mmsig_barplot <- ggplot(dual_e_se_mer_sig_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "E/SE-MER Group",
    y = "Relative\nContribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

message("AID-associated SBS in Dual GCB/PC E/SE-MERs: ")
## AID-associated SBS in Dual GCB/PC E/SE-MERs:
dual_e_se_mer_sig_t %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature %in% c("SBS9", "SBS84")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(sum = sum(rel_contrib))
##         sum
## 1 0.6284863

# PC
pc_e_se_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = pc_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
    strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = TRUE, iterations = 10, genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE,
    force_include = NULL)
## 
  |                                                                            
  |                                                                      |   0%
  |                                                                            
  |======================================================================| 100%

pc_e_se_mer_sig_t <- reshape2::melt(data = pc_e_se_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

pc_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature <- factor(pc_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1",
    "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

pc_e_se_mmsig_barplot <- ggplot(pc_e_se_mer_sig_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "E/SE-MER Group",
    y = "Relative\nContribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

message("AID-associated SBS in PC E/SE-MERs: ")
## AID-associated SBS in PC E/SE-MERs:
pc_e_se_mer_sig_t %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature %in% c("SBS9", "SBS84")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(sum = sum(rel_contrib))
##         sum
## 1 0.3434842

message("SBSblood in PC E/SE-MERs: ")
## SBSblood in PC E/SE-MERs:
pc_e_se_mer_sig_t %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBSblood") %>%
    dplyr::select(rel_contrib)
##   rel_contrib
## 1   0.5776352

Mutational signature of clustered mutations in E/SE-MERs

Code 
clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_muts <- gr2dt(gcb_e_se_mers_clustered_muts) %>%
    dplyr::select(tissue, seqnames, start, ref, alt)

clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_muts <- clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_muts[!duplicated(clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_muts)]

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# ALL GCB
clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_muts,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
clust_gcb_e_se_mer_96class_plot <- plot_sbs96(clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96)

# Translate data for use in mmsig
clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96)
clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures
clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", genome = "hg38")

clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t <- reshape2::melt(data = clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature <- factor(clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature,
    levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2",
        "SBS13"))

clust_gcb_e_se_mmsig_barplot <- ggplot(clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t, aes(x = sample,
    y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "E/SE-MER Group",
    y = "Relative\nContribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# PC E/SE-MERs
clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_muts <- gr2dt(pc_e_se_mers_clustered_muts) %>%
    dplyr::select(tissue, seqnames, start, ref, alt)

clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_muts <- clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_muts[!duplicated(clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_muts)]

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# ALL GCB
clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_muts,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
clust_pc_e_se_mer_96class_plot <- plot_sbs96(clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_96)

# Translate data for use in mmsig
clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_96)
clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures
clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", genome = "hg38")

clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_t <- reshape2::melt(data = clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature <- factor(clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1",
    "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

clust_pc_e_se_mmsig_barplot <- ggplot(clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "E/SE-MER Group",
    y = "Relative\nContribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))


# Dual
clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_muts <- gr2dt(dual_e_se_mers_clustered_muts) %>%
    dplyr::select(tissue, seqnames, start, ref, alt)

clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_muts <- clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_muts[!duplicated(clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_muts)]

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# ALL dual
clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_muts,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
clust_dual_e_se_mer_96class_plot <- plot_sbs96(clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_96)

# Translate data for use in mmsig
clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_96)
clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures
clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", genome = "hg38")

clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_t <- reshape2::melt(data = clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature <- factor(clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature,
    levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2",
        "SBS13"))

clust_dual_e_se_mmsig_barplot <- ggplot(clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_t, aes(x = sample,
    y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "E/SE-MER Group",
    y = "Relative\nContribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# AID associated
message("AID-associated SBS in GCB E/SE-MERs: ")
## AID-associated SBS in GCB E/SE-MERs:
clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature %in% c("SBS9", "SBS84")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(sum = sum(rel_contrib) * 100)
##        sum
## 1 59.61901

message("AID-associated SBS in Dual GCB/PC E/SE-MERs: ")
## AID-associated SBS in Dual GCB/PC E/SE-MERs:
clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_t %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature %in% c("SBS9", "SBS84")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(sum = sum(rel_contrib) * 100)
##        sum
## 1 67.73485

message("AID-associated SBS in PC E/SE-MERs: ")
## AID-associated SBS in PC E/SE-MERs:
clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_t %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature %in% c("SBS9", "SBS84")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(sum = sum(rel_contrib) * 100)
##        sum
## 1 95.07436

message("SBSblood in GCB E/SE-MERs: ")
## SBSblood in GCB E/SE-MERs:
clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBSblood") %>%
    dplyr::select(rel_contrib) * 100
##   rel_contrib
## 1    40.38099

message("SBSblood in Dual GCB/PC E/SE-MERs: ")
## SBSblood in Dual GCB/PC E/SE-MERs:
clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_t %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBSblood") %>%
    dplyr::select(rel_contrib) * 100
##   rel_contrib
## 1    32.26515

Supplementary Figure 9

13 E/SE-MERs are linked to recurrent structural variants and complex rearrangements

Incorporate HQ SV breakpoints and check for concordance with previous CoMMpass hotspots

Code 
############# SV HOTSPOTS

# Read in CoMMpass hotspots on hg19 from Rustad et al. 2020 paper
commpass_sv_hotspots_hg19 <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "svHotspotAnalysis/commpassSvHotspots.txt"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)

# # Convert SV hotspots to hg38 commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_grl <-
# rtracklayer::liftOver(x = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(commpass_sv_hotspots_hg19)),
# chain = liftchain_hg19) # Cycle through list and combine regions to single
# site and add annotations commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr <-
# GenomicRanges::GRanges() for(i in 1:length(commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_grl)) {
# liftover_segments <- gr_refactor_seqs(commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_grl[[i]]) #
# There are situations where the segment is on different chromosome, remove
# those here # and only use GRanges on chromosome specified in hotspot id
# liftover_hotspot_segments <- liftover_segments %Q% (seqnames ==
# str_extract(string = unique(liftover_segments$hotspot.id), pattern =
# 'chr[\\dX]+')) # If many segments after liftover, collapse all segmented
# liftovers and add annotations if(length(liftover_hotspot_segments) > 1) {
# stitched_liftover_hotspot_string <-
# paste0(first(as.character(GenomicRanges::seqnames(liftover_hotspot_segments))),
# ':', first(as.character(GenomicRanges::start(liftover_hotspot_segments))),
# '-', last(as.character(GenomicRanges::end(liftover_hotspot_segments))))
# stitched_liftover_hotspot <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(parse.gr(stitched_liftover_hotspot_string))
# mcols(stitched_liftover_hotspot) <- mcols(liftover_hotspot_segments)[1,] }
# else { stitched_liftover_hotspot <- liftover_hotspot_segments } # Update
# hotspot ID to hg38 colnames(mcols(stitched_liftover_hotspot))[c(1,16)] <-
# c('hotspot.id.hg19','references_pmid') # Update length
# stitched_liftover_hotspot$length <-
# GenomicRanges::width(stitched_liftover_hotspot) # Finally, add to final hg38
# GR obj commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr <- grbind(commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr,
# stitched_liftover_hotspot) }

######################## COMPLEX EVENT HOTSPOTS

commpass_complex_hotspots_hg19 <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "svHotspotAnalysis/commpassComplexEventHotspots.txt"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)

# # Convert SV hotspots to hg38 commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_grl <-
# rtracklayer::liftOver(x =
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg19)), chain =
# liftchain_hg19) # Cycle through list and combine regions to single site and
# add annotations commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr <- GenomicRanges::GRanges()
# for(i in 1:length(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_grl)) { liftover_segments <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_grl[[i]]) # There are
# situations where the segment is on different chromosome, remove those here #
# and only use GRanges on chromosome specified in hotspot id
# liftover_hotspot_segments <- liftover_segments %Q% (seqnames ==
# str_extract(string = unique(liftover_segments$hotspot.id), pattern =
# 'chr[\\dX]+')) # If many segments after liftover, collapse all segmented
# liftovers and add annotations if(length(liftover_hotspot_segments) > 1) {
# stitched_liftover_hotspot_string <-
# paste0(first(as.character(GenomicRanges::seqnames(liftover_hotspot_segments))),
# ':', first(as.character(GenomicRanges::start(liftover_hotspot_segments))),
# '-', last(as.character(GenomicRanges::end(liftover_hotspot_segments))))
# stitched_liftover_hotspot <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(parse.gr(stitched_liftover_hotspot_string))
# mcols(stitched_liftover_hotspot) <- mcols(liftover_hotspot_segments)[1,] }
# else { stitched_liftover_hotspot <- liftover_hotspot_segments } # Update
# hotspot ID to hg38 colnames(mcols(stitched_liftover_hotspot))[1] <-
# 'complex.hotspot.id.hg19' # Update length stitched_liftover_hotspot$length <-
# GenomicRanges::width(stitched_liftover_hotspot) # Finally, add to final hg38
# GR obj commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr <-
# grbind(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr, stitched_liftover_hotspot) }

# Save copy of hg38 versions saveRDS(object = commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr,
# file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'svHotspotAnalysis/sv_hotspots.CoMMpass.hg38.rds')) saveRDS(object =
# commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'svHotspotAnalysis/complex_hotspots.CoMMpass.hg38.rds'))
commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "svHotspotAnalysis/sv_hotspots.CoMMpass.hg38.rds"))
commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "svHotspotAnalysis/complex_hotspots.CoMMpass.hg38.rds"))

# Write out BEDs for circos fwrite(x = gr2dt(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr)
# %>% dplyr::mutate('complex_event_type' = case_when(str_detect(string =
# complex.hotspot.id.hg19, pattern = 'chromothripsis') ~ 'Chromothripsis',
# str_detect(string = complex.hotspot.id.hg19, pattern = 'templated_ins') ~
# 'Templated Insertion')) %>%
# dplyr::select(seqnames,start,end,complex_event_type), file =
# paste0(base_data_path, 'circosPlot/complex_hotspots.bed'), sep = '\t',
# col.names = T)

Find the occupancy of SV hotspots by SVs detected in HQ data

Code 
# Read in chromosome contig information by arm
chr_arm_lengths <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "chromosome_per_arm_coordinates.hg38.txt"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)

############################################## split SV BEDPE into data table
############################################## of breakpoints start by reading
############################################## in bedpe of all SVs from mgp1000
############################################## as junctions Set seqinfo named
############################################## vector of hg38 chrom names and
############################################## lengths
ref_seq_info <- c(248956422, 242193529, 198295559, 190214555, 181538259, 170805979,
    159345973, 145138636, 138394717, 133797422, 135086622, 133275309, 114364328,
    107043718, 101991189, 90338345, 83257441, 80373285, 58617616, 64444167, 46709983,
    50818468, 156040895, 57227415)
names(ref_seq_info) <- paste0("chr", c(seq(1, 22, 1), "X", "Y"))
# #total_sv_junc <- gGnome::jJ(rafile = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'mgp1000.hq.union.consensus.somatic.sv.bedpe'), # chr.convert = F, # hg =
# 'hg38', # keep.features = T, # seqlengths = ref_seq_info) # There is an
# enrichment of FP DELs that are small and found within the MSKCC dataset #
# They pass the consensus of 2 or more callers but appear to only be called due
# to lack of read depth # but inspection of these sites show little to no read
# support across the junction # and a number of reads with badly aligned
# clipped ends # Filter those out here mskcc_darakrd_hq_samples <-
# hq_dataset_metadata %>% dplyr::filter(cohort == 'MSKCC_DaraKRd') %>%
# dplyr::select(sample) %>% purrr::as_vector() %>% as.character()
# mskcc_darakrd_fp_small_dels <- total_sv_junc |> plyranges::mutate('span' =
# start2 - start1) |> plyranges::filter(type == 'DEL' & span < 100 & sample
# %in% mskcc_darakrd_hq_samples) #mskcc_darakrd_fp_small_dels <-
# total_sv_junc[which(total_sv_junc$dt$name == 'DEL' & total_sv_junc$span < 200
# & total_sv_junc$dt$`NA..3` %in% mskcc_darakrd_hq_samples)]
# total_sv_juncs_fpfilter_gi <- gGnome::setdiff(x = total_sv_junc, y =
# mskcc_darakrd_fp_small_dels) total_sv_juncs_fpfilter <- gi_to_grl(gi =
# total_sv_juncs_fpfilter_gi, keep_metadata = T) # split into BP format with
# BP_order which is 1 or 2 for each BP column total_sv_bp_unlisted <-
# gUtils::grl.unlist(total_sv_juncs_fpfilter) #total_sv_bp_unlisted <-
# gUtils::grl.unlist(total_sv_juncs_fpfilter$grl) total_sv_bp_gr <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(total_sv_bp_unlisted) total_sv_bp_gr <-
# gr_absolute_ranges(input_gr = total_sv_bp_gr, cpus = 2) total_sv_bp_dt <-
# gUtils::gr2dt(total_sv_bp_gr) # reorg DT a bit before renaming # might add
# disease stage here for spliting analysis like wee might do for fishhook
# total_sv_bp_dt <- total_sv_bp_dt[,-c('width','score','id')] #total_sv_bp_dt
# <- total_sv_bp_dt[,-c(5,7,9)] colnames(total_sv_bp_dt)[1:10] <- c('seqnames',
# 'start', 'end', 'strand', 'class', 'support', 'patient', 'sample', 'sv_id',
# 'bp_order') # filter to only calls by 2 or more hq_total_sv_bp_dt <-
# total_sv_bp_dt %>% dplyr::filter(!support %in%
# c('manta','svaba','delly','igcaller')) # Gather high-coverage, high-quality
# samples hq_total_sv_bp_dt <- hq_total_sv_bp_dt %>% dplyr::filter(patient %in%
# unique(hq_dataset_metadata$patient) & !seqnames %in% c('chrX','chrY')) #
# Collapse all BPs per patient (i.e. remove possible inflation due to same BP
# repeated across samples) hq_total_sv_bp_dt <-
# hq_total_sv_bp_dt[!duplicated(hq_total_sv_bp_dt[,c(1:2,4:7)])]
# hq_total_sv_bp_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt))

# saveRDS(object = hq_total_sv_bp_dt, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'hq_total_sv_bp_dt.rds'))
hq_total_sv_bp_dt <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "hq_total_sv_bp_dt.rds"))
# saveRDS(object = hq_total_sv_bp_gr, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'hq_total_sv_bp_gr.rds'))
hq_total_sv_bp_gr <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "hq_total_sv_bp_gr.rds"))

message("Total HQ SVs: ", length(hq_total_sv_bp_gr))
## Total HQ SVs: 15822

# Some simple metrics on SVs per patient
hq_sv_by_class_per_patient <- hq_total_sv_bp_dt %>%
    dplyr::select(patient, sample, sv_id, class) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, class) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "sv_count") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

hq_sv_total_per_patient <- hq_sv_by_class_per_patient %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(total_sv = sum(sv_count))

message("Median HQ SVs per patient: ", median(hq_sv_total_per_patient$total_sv))
## Median HQ SVs per patient: 16
message("Range of HQ SVs per patient: ", paste0(range(hq_sv_total_per_patient$total_sv),
    collapse = " - "))
## Range of HQ SVs per patient: 1 - 507

# look at relationship between TMB and SV count
hq_sv_total_per_sample <- hq_total_sv_bp_dt %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, sv_id) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "sv_count") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

hq_sv_total_and_tmb_per_sample <- hq_sv_total_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = tmb_fish_eligbile %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, tumor_mutation_burden), by = "sample")

# plot linear relationship between TMB and SV count
# ggplot(hq_sv_total_and_tmb_per_sample) + geom_point(aes(x =
# tumor_mutation_burden, y = sv_count), na.rm = T) + geom_smooth(aes(x =
# tumor_mutation_burden, y = sv_count), method = 'lm', na.rm = T, formula = y ~
# x)
message("SVs and TMB - Linear Regression")
## SVs and TMB - Linear Regression
glance(hq_sv_total_and_tmb_per_sample %>%
    lm(data = ., formula = sv_count ~ tumor_mutation_burden))
## # A tibble: 1 × 12
##   r.squared adj.r.squared sigma statistic p.value    df logLik   AIC   BIC
##       <dbl>         <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1    0.0280        0.0249  35.3      8.93 0.00304     1 -1554. 3113. 3124.
## # ℹ 3 more variables: deviance <dbl>, df.residual <int>, nobs <int>

summary(hq_sv_total_and_tmb_per_sample %>%
    lm(data = ., formula = sv_count ~ tumor_mutation_burden))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = sv_count ~ tumor_mutation_burden, data = .)
## 
## Residuals:
##    Min     1Q Median     3Q    Max 
## -40.65 -14.54  -8.71   1.89 413.12 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                       Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)   
## (Intercept)             11.642      4.775   2.438  0.01533 * 
## tumor_mutation_burden    5.519      1.847   2.988  0.00304 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 35.28 on 310 degrees of freedom
##   (35 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.02799,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.02485 
## F-statistic: 8.926 on 1 and 310 DF,  p-value: 0.003037

# look at relationship between muts in MERs and SV count
message("SVs and MERs - Linear Regression")
## SVs and MERs - Linear Regression
hq_sv_total_and_mer_muts_per_sample <- hq_sv_total_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
        dplyr::select(sample, mer_id) %>%
        dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
        dplyr::count(mer_id) %>%
        dplyr::ungroup() %>%
        dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(total_per_mer = sum(n)), by = "sample")

# Log transformed ggplot(hq_sv_total_and_mer_muts_per_sample) +
# geom_point(aes(x = log10(total_per_mer), y = log10(sv_count)), na.rm = T) +
# geom_smooth(aes(x = log10(total_per_mer), y = log10(sv_count)), method =
# 'lm', na.rm = T, formula = y ~ x)

sv_and_mer_muts_corr <- ggplot(hq_sv_total_and_mer_muts_per_sample) + geom_point(aes(x = total_per_mer,
    y = sv_count), na.rm = T, size = 3, color = "#79A8A4") + geom_smooth(aes(x = total_per_mer,
    y = sv_count), method = "lm", na.rm = T, formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "cyan",
    alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = total_per_mer, y = sv_count),
    label.x = 150, label.y = 425, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = total_per_mer,
    y = sv_count), label.x = 150, label.y = 400, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Total MER mutations", y = "Total SVs") + theme(legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))

# look at relationship between kataegis muts and SV count
message("SVs and Kataegis - Linear Regression")
## SVs and Kataegis - Linear Regression
hq_sv_total_and_kataegis_muts_per_sample <- hq_sv_total_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_sigpro_clusters_muts %>%
        dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
        dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
        dplyr::count(name = "kataegis_mut_count") %>%
        dplyr::ungroup(), by = "sample")

sv_and_kataegis_muts_corr <- ggplot(hq_sv_total_and_kataegis_muts_per_sample) + geom_point(aes(x = kataegis_mut_count,
    y = sv_count), na.rm = T, size = 3, color = "#AD8082") + geom_smooth(aes(x = kataegis_mut_count,
    y = sv_count), method = "lm", na.rm = T, formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "cyan",
    alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = kataegis_mut_count, y = sv_count),
    label.x = 50, label.y = 400, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = kataegis_mut_count,
    y = sv_count), label.x = 50, label.y = 375, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Total Kataegis mutations", y = "Total SVs") + theme(legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))

glance(hq_sv_total_and_kataegis_muts_per_sample %>%
    lm(data = ., formula = sv_count ~ kataegis_mut_count))
## # A tibble: 1 × 12
##   r.squared adj.r.squared sigma statistic  p.value    df logLik   AIC   BIC
##       <dbl>         <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0.187         0.185  31.4      76.8 9.93e-17     1 -1629. 3264. 3276.
## # ℹ 3 more variables: deviance <dbl>, df.residual <int>, nobs <int>

# look at relationship between omikli muts and SV count
message("SVs and Omikli - Linear Regression")
## SVs and Omikli - Linear Regression
hq_sv_total_and_omikli_muts_per_sample <- hq_sv_total_per_sample %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_sigpro_clusters_muts %>%
        dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Omikli") %>%
        dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
        dplyr::count(name = "omikli_mut_count") %>%
        dplyr::ungroup(), by = "sample")

sv_and_omikli_muts_corr <- ggplot(hq_sv_total_and_omikli_muts_per_sample) + geom_point(aes(x = omikli_mut_count,
    y = sv_count), na.rm = T, size = 3, color = "#DEC18C") + geom_smooth(aes(x = omikli_mut_count,
    y = sv_count), method = "lm", na.rm = T, formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5, color = "cyan",
    alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = omikli_mut_count, y = sv_count),
    label.x = 750, label.y = 425, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = omikli_mut_count,
    y = sv_count), label.x = 750, label.y = 400, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Total Omikli mutations", y = "Total SVs") + theme(legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))

glance(hq_sv_total_and_omikli_muts_per_sample %>%
    lm(data = ., formula = sv_count ~ omikli_mut_count))
## # A tibble: 1 × 12
##   r.squared adj.r.squared sigma statistic   p.value    df logLik   AIC   BIC
##       <dbl>         <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1    0.0520        0.0493  33.5      18.9 0.0000178     1 -1710. 3426. 3437.
## # ℹ 3 more variables: deviance <dbl>, df.residual <int>, nobs <int>

Look at omikli and kataegis events at CCND1

Code 
driver_genes <- get_gene_set(path_to_file = paste0(base_data_path, "geneSets/mgpDriverGenes.txt"),
    file_format = "txt", has_header = F, gene_set_name = "MM Drivers")
## Building custom gene set at ../../data/geneSets/mgpDriverGenes.txt ...
## mgpDriverGenes gene set built succsessfully ...
## This file is unfiltered ...
## Recommend review ...
## Rows: 3
## Columns: 3
## $ V1        <chr> "KRAS", "NRAS", "DIS3"
## $ source_id <chr> "mgpDriverGenes", "mgpDriverGenes", "mgpDriverGenes"
## $ gene_set  <chr> "MM Drivers", "MM Drivers", "MM Drivers"
colnames(driver_genes)[1] <- "GENE_SYMBOL"

# Clustered events in TRX region CCND1 t(11;14)
ccnd1_clustered_mut_scars <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_gr,
    subject = gUtils::parse.gr("chr11:68460752-69654474"), qcol = c("sample", "patient",
        "mainclass", "group", "mut_count", "vaf_median"))

ccnd1_mer_clustered <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = ccnd1_clustered_mut_scars,
    subject = e_se_mers_gr, qcol = c("sample", "patient", "mainclass", "group", "mut_count",
        "vaf_median"), scol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label", "tissue", "enhancer_type"))


ccnd1_clustered_mut_scars_dt <- gr2dt(ccnd1_mer_clustered) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, igh_translocation), by = c("patient",
        "sample"))

ccnd1_mer_kataegis <- ccnd1_clustered_mut_scars_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, patient, mainclass, disease_stage, igh_translocation) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()

ccnd1_mer_omikli <- ccnd1_clustered_mut_scars_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Omikli") %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, patient, mainclass, disease_stage, igh_translocation) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()

rbind(ccnd1_mer_kataegis, ccnd1_mer_omikli) %>%
    dplyr::select(patient, mainclass, disease_stage, igh_translocation) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
##                            patient mainclass disease_stage igh_translocation
##                             <char>    <char>        <fctr>            <fctr>
##  1:                       382803-N  Kataegis           SMM          t(11;14)
##  2:              MMRC0455_V0D936_N  Kataegis            MM          t(11;14)
##  3:        IID_H201439_N01_01_WG01  Kataegis            MM          t(11;14)
##  4:                        NIH010B  Kataegis           SMM          t(11;14)
##  5:                     PD26420b_N  Kataegis          RRMM          t(11;14)
##  6:                        NIH035B  Kataegis           SMM          t(11;14)
##  7:                    97033-DNA-N  Kataegis          MGUS          t(11;14)
##  8:        IID_H196061_N01_01_WG01  Kataegis          MGUS          t(11;14)
##  9:                       370474-N  Kataegis            MM          t(11;14)
## 10:                     PD26434b_N  Kataegis            MM          t(11;14)
## 11:        IID_H201447_N01_01_WG01  Kataegis            MM          t(11;14)
## 12:                       240750-N  Kataegis            MM          t(11;14)
## 13:              MMRC0335_V0D57G_N    Omikli          RRMM          t(11;14)
## 14:        IID_H201439_N01_01_WG01    Omikli            MM          t(11;14)
## 15:        IID_H196061_N01_01_WG01    Omikli          MGUS          t(11;14)
## 16:                       571299-N    Omikli            MM          t(11;14)
## 17:                     PD47563b_N    Omikli          MGUS          t(11;14)
## 18:        IID_H201447_N01_01_WG01    Omikli            MM          t(11;14)
## 19:                     PD26420b_N    Omikli          RRMM          t(11;14)
## 20:                     PD47575b_N    Omikli          MGUS          t(11;14)
## 21:                     PD26428b_N    Omikli          RRMM          t(11;14)
## 22:                    78208-DNA-N    Omikli           SMM          t(11;14)
## 23:                       382803-N    Omikli           SMM          t(11;14)
## 24:                    97033-DNA-N    Omikli          MGUS          t(11;14)
## 25:                       240750-N    Omikli            MM          t(11;14)
## 26: P01-014-S210108-XXXXX-PBMC-DNA    Omikli            MM          t(11;14)
## 27:         P13205-S35406-PBMC-DNA    Omikli            MM           t(8;14)
##                            patient mainclass disease_stage igh_translocation
##                             <char>    <char>        <fctr>            <fctr>

rbind(ccnd1_mer_kataegis, ccnd1_mer_omikli) %>%
    dplyr::select(patient, mainclass, disease_stage, igh_translocation) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::select(disease_stage) %>%
    table()
## disease_stage
## MGUS  SMM   MM RRMM  PCL   WM 
##    6    5   12    4    0    0

message("Kataegis at CCND1 E/SE-MER across disease stages: ")
## Kataegis at CCND1 E/SE-MER across disease stages:
ccnd1_mer_kataegis %>%
    dplyr::select(patient, mainclass, disease_stage, igh_translocation) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::select(disease_stage) %>%
    table()
## disease_stage
## MGUS  SMM   MM RRMM  PCL   WM 
##    2    3    6    1    0    0

# 1:n_distinct(ccnd1_mer_kataegis$patient)) { ccdn1_kataegis_mers_dist <-

# as.numeric(gr.dist(gr1 = ccnd1_mer_clustered %Q% (#mainclass == 'Kataegis' &
# patient == unique(ccnd1_mer_kataegis$patient)[i]), gr2 =
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(canon_igh_trx_mask)) %Q% (patient ==
# unique(ccnd1_mer_kataegis$patient)[i] & seqnames == 'chr11'))))) }

# VAF of these muts scales with disease stage ccnd1_clustered_mut_scars_dt %>%
# dplyr::filter(mainclass == 'Kataegis') %>%
# dplyr::select(patient,disease_stage,vaf_median) %>% ggplot() +
# geom_boxplot(aes(x = disease_stage, y = vaf_median))

# ccnd1_clustered_mut_scars_dt %>% dplyr::filter(mainclass == 'Omikli') %>%
# dplyr::select(patient,disease_stage,vaf_median) %>% ggplot() +
# geom_boxplot(aes(x = disease_stage, y = vaf_median))

# MYC t(8;14)
myc_clustered_mut_scars <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_gr,
    subject = gUtils::parse.gr("chr8:125091860-129786624"), qcol = c("sample", "patient",
        "mainclass", "group", "mut_count", "vaf_median"))

myc_clustered_mut_scars_dt <- gr2dt(myc_clustered_mut_scars) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, igh_translocation), by = c("patient",
        "sample"))

myc_clustered_mut_scars_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(mainclass == "Kataegis") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient, mainclass, disease_stage, igh_translocation) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::select(disease_stage) %>%
    table()
## disease_stage
## MGUS  SMM   MM RRMM  PCL   WM 
##    0    0    6    2    0    0

Evaluate the overlap and composition of SV BPs to hotspots

Code 
############# SV HOTSPOTS

# Calculate the percentage of patients with SVs within the hotspot
commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr$pct_patients_with_SV.hg19 <- round(commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr$patients_with_SV/752 *
    100, digits = 2)

# Calculate the overlap of SV BPs with hotspots
sv_hotspot_overlap <- gr.findoverlaps(query = hq_total_sv_bp_gr, subject = commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr,
    qcol = c("class", "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "bp_order"), scol = "hotspot.id.hg19")
sv_hotspot_overlap_dt <- gr2dt(sv_hotspot_overlap)

message("Total occupancy of SV hotspots: ", length(unique(sv_hotspot_overlap$hotspot.id.hg19)))
## Total occupancy of SV hotspots: 69
message("Fraction of all SV hotspots are occupied: ", length(unique(sv_hotspot_overlap$hotspot.id.hg19))/length(commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr %Q%
    (seqnames != "chrX")))
## Fraction of all SV hotspots are occupied: 0.985714285714286

# Hotspots with no SVs in our dataset
commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr %Q% (hotspot.id.hg19 == "down_chr1_92.8Mb")
## GRanges object with 1 range and 17 metadata columns:
##       seqnames            ranges strand |  hotspot.id.hg19    length
##          <Rle>         <IRanges>  <Rle> |      <character> <integer>
##   [1]     chr1 92317444-92428443      * | down_chr1_92.8Mb    111000
##          cytoband patients_with_SV chromoplexy chromothripsis   complex
##       <character>        <integer>   <integer>      <integer> <integer>
##   [1]       p22.1                8           0              0         1
##       templated_insertion       DUP       TRA       DEL       INV hotspot_class
##                 <integer> <integer> <integer> <integer> <integer>   <character>
##   [1]                   0         0         0         5         2          LOSS
##       driver_status genes_involved references_pmid pct_patients_with_SV.hg19
##         <character>    <character>     <character>                 <numeric>
##   [1]         KNOWN           RPL5        27909306                      1.06
##   -------
##   seqinfo: 24 sequences from an unspecified genome

# Compare the pct of patients with SVs in hotspots
sv_hotspot_overlap_comp <- sv_hotspot_overlap_dt %>%
    dplyr::group_by(hotspot.id.hg19) %>%
    dplyr::select(hotspot.id.hg19, patient) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::group_by(hotspot.id.hg19) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "patients_with_SV") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_patients_with_SV = round(patients_with_SV/294 * 100, digits = 2)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gr2dt(commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr) %>%
        dplyr::select(hotspot.id.hg19, pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), by = "hotspot.id.hg19") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_delta = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19 - pct_patients_with_SV)

# Metrics on correlation of pct patients
message("Median difference in % of patients with SV at hotspots: ", median(abs(sv_hotspot_overlap_comp$pct_delta)))
## Median difference in % of patients with SV at hotspots: 0.83


cor_test(data = sv_hotspot_overlap_comp, vars = c("pct_patients_with_SV", "pct_patients_with_SV.hg19"))
## # A tibble: 1 × 8
##   var1                 var2     cor statistic        p conf.low conf.high method
##   <chr>                <chr>  <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr> 
## 1 pct_patients_with_SV pct_p…  0.95      24.6 2.66e-35    0.919     0.968 Pears…

sv_hotspot_pct_corr <- ggplot(sv_hotspot_overlap_comp) + geom_point(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), na.rm = T, size = 3, color = "#606372") + geom_smooth(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), method = "lm", na.rm = T, formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5,
    color = "thistle1", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), label.x = 0.5, label.y = 35, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), label.x = 0.5, label.y = 32, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Query Dataset\nPatients with SVs at hotspots (%)", y = "CoMMpass Dataset\nPatients with SVs at hotspots (%)") +
    theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))

######################## COMPLEX EVENT HOTSPOTS

# Calculate the percentage of patients with SVs within the hotspot
commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr$pct_patients_with_SV.hg19 <- round(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr$no.samples/752 *
    100, digits = 2)

# Calculate the overlap of SV BPs with hotspots
complex_hotspot_overlap <- gr.findoverlaps(query = hq_total_sv_bp_gr, subject = commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr,
    qcol = c("class", "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "bp_order"), scol = "complex.hotspot.id.hg19")
complex_hotspot_overlap_dt <- gr2dt(complex_hotspot_overlap)

message("Total occupancy of Complex hotspots: ", length(unique(complex_hotspot_overlap$complex.hotspot.id.hg19)))
## Total occupancy of Complex hotspots: 33
message("Fraction of all Complex hotspots are occupied: ", length(unique(complex_hotspot_overlap$complex.hotspot.id.hg19))/length(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr))
## Fraction of all Complex hotspots are occupied: 1

# Compare the pct of patients with SVs in hotspots
complex_hotspot_overlap_comp <- complex_hotspot_overlap_dt %>%
    dplyr::group_by(complex.hotspot.id.hg19) %>%
    dplyr::select(complex.hotspot.id.hg19, patient) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::group_by(complex.hotspot.id.hg19) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "patients_with_SV") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_patients_with_SV = round(patients_with_SV/294 * 100, digits = 2)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gr2dt(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr) %>%
        dplyr::select(complex.hotspot.id.hg19, pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), by = "complex.hotspot.id.hg19") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_delta = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19 - pct_patients_with_SV)

# Metrics on correlation of pct patients
message("Median difference in % of patients at Complex hotspots: ", median(abs(complex_hotspot_overlap_comp$pct_delta)))
## Median difference in % of patients at Complex hotspots: 5.65

cor_test(data = complex_hotspot_overlap_comp, vars = c("pct_patients_with_SV", "pct_patients_with_SV.hg19"))
## # A tibble: 1 × 8
##   var1                 var2      cor statistic       p conf.low conf.high method
##   <chr>                <chr>   <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr> 
## 1 pct_patients_with_SV pct_pa…  0.59      4.02 3.44e-4    0.303     0.773 Pears…

complex_hotspot_pct_corr <- ggplot(complex_hotspot_overlap_comp) + geom_point(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), na.rm = T, size = 3, color = "#B2AD8F") + geom_smooth(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), method = "lm", na.rm = T, formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5,
    color = "thistle1", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), label.x = 3, label.y = 17, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), label.x = 3, label.y = 15, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Query Dataset\nPatients with Complex SVs at hotspots (%)", y = "CoMMpass Dataset\nPatients with Complex SVs at hotspots (%)") +
    theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))

Supplementary Figure 10

Overlap of SV hotspots and MERs

Code 
# Look at overlap of all MERs
all_mer_sv_hotspot_overlap <- gr.findoverlaps(query = dt_to_gr(mers_overlap), subject = commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr,
    qcol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label", "pct_total_mut_patients", "tissue", "enhancer_type",
        "cluster_label", "driver_status"), scol = c("hotspot.id.hg19", "chromoplexy",
        "chromothripsis", "complex", "templated_insertion", "DUP", "DEL", "TRA",
        "INV", "hotspot_class", "genes_involved", "pct_patients_with_SV.hg19"))
unique(all_mer_sv_hotspot_overlap$hotspot.id.hg19)
## [1] "nonClustered_chr1_118.2Mb" "nonClustered_chr1_150.5Mb"
## [3] "nonClustered_chr1_203.2Mb" "down_chr6_7.7Mb"          
## [5] "down_chr10_103.9Mb"        "down_chr11_69Mb"          
## [7] "nonClustered_chr14_69.3Mb" "down_chr16_50.6Mb"        
## [9] "down_chr20_32.4Mb"
n_distinct(all_mer_sv_hotspot_overlap$hotspot.id.hg19)
## [1] 9
unique(all_mer_sv_hotspot_overlap$mer_id)
##  [1] "mer70"  "mer182" "mer98"  "mer6"   "mer36"  "mer37"  "mer275" "mer210"
##  [9] "mer155" "mer5"   "mer135" "mer181"
unique(all_mer_sv_hotspot_overlap$mer_gene_label)
##  [1] "TENT5C"   "FLG2"     "BTG2"     "TXNDC5"   "PPRC1"    "C10orf95"
##  [7] "CCND1"    "ZFP36L1"  "CYLD"     "PIGU"
n_distinct(all_mer_sv_hotspot_overlap$mer_id)
## [1] 12

# Add to MERs overlap
mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gr2dt(all_mer_sv_hotspot_overlap) %>%
        dplyr::select(mer_id, hotspot.id.hg19, pct_patients_with_SV.hg19, hotspot_class,
            genes_involved), by = "mer_id")

all_mer_complex_hotspot_overlap <- gr.findoverlaps(query = mers_gr, subject = commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr,
    qcol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label", "pct_total_mut_patients"), scol = c("complex.hotspot.id.hg19",
        "no.samples"))
unique(all_mer_complex_hotspot_overlap$complex.hotspot.id.hg19)

Evidence of patients with mutations in MERs and SV breakpoints in complex event hotspots

Code 
e_se_mer_at_complex_hotspots <- gr2dt(e_se_mer_complex_hotspot_overlap) %>%
    dplyr::select(complex.hotspot.id.hg19, mer_id, mer_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()

complex_hotspot_overlap_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(complex.hotspot.id.hg19 %in% c("chromothripsis_chr1_111.2Mb", "templated_ins_chr1_118.2Mb")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(complex.hotspot.id.hg19, patient) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "patient_sv_count") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(y = muts_per_grouped_mer_for_plot %>%
        dplyr::filter(mer_id %in% c("mer70", "mer182")) %>%
        dplyr::group_by(patient) %>%
        dplyr::count(name = "patient_mer_mut_count") %>%
        dplyr::ungroup(), by = "patient")
