Assess the proximity relationship between kataegis and SV breakpoints at complex event hotspots

Code 
# Proximity profile of kataegis and omikli mutations to SV BPs will use
# non-clustered mutations as the background Correct for Ig region kataegis and
# SVs by removing them

# hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr %Q% (mainclass == 'Kataegis' & !seqnames %in%
# c('chrX','chrY')) %_% ig_region_mask_bed

# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt)) %_% ig_region_mask_bed

kataegis_sv_prox_dist <- GenomicDistributions::calcFeatureDist(query = hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr %Q%
    (mainclass == "Kataegis" & !seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY")) %_% ig_region_mask_bed,
    features = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt)) %_% ig_region_mask_bed)

omikli_sv_prox_dist <- GenomicDistributions::calcFeatureDist(query = hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr %Q%
    (mainclass == "Omikli" & !seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY")) %_% ig_region_mask_bed,
    features = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt)) %_% ig_region_mask_bed)

non_clustered_background_dist <- GenomicDistributions::calcFeatureDist(query = hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr %Q%
    (!seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY")) %_% ig_region_mask_bed, features = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt)) %_%
    ig_region_mask_bed)

# Plot proximity distros
proxi_distro_to_svs <- list(Kataegis = kataegis_sv_prox_dist, Omikli = omikli_sv_prox_dist,
    `Non-Clustered` = non_clustered_background_dist)
proxi_distro_to_svs_plot <- GenomicDistributions::plotFeatureDist(dists = proxi_distro_to_svs,
    size = 100000, nbins = 40) + scale_color_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#009E73",
    "#000000")) + scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#009E73", "#000000")) +
    labs(title = NULL, x = "Distance to proximal SV breakpoint") + theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 14),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12), legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = unit(c(6, 6, 6, 6), "mm"), panel.spacing.x = unit(3, "lines"))
## Scale for x is already present.
## Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.
# export 5 x 10 landscape

# quantify the distances and look at 5 Kb window around SV BPs
kataegis_binned_dists_around_bp <- GenomicDistributions:::cutDists(dists = kataegis_sv_prox_dist,
    size = 100000, nbins = 40)
omikli_binned_dists_around_bp <- GenomicDistributions:::cutDists(dists = omikli_sv_prox_dist,
    size = 100000, nbins = 40)
nonclustered_binned_dists_around_bp <- GenomicDistributions:::cutDists(dists = non_clustered_background_dist,
    size = 100000, nbins = 40)

kataegis_5k_window_around_bp <- kataegis_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
    dplyr::filter(cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct = Freq/sum(!is.na(kataegis_sv_prox_dist)))
message("Percentage of kataegis muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:")
## Percentage of kataegis muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:
round(sum(kataegis_5k_window_around_bp$pct) * 100, digits = 1)
## [1] 27.2

omikli_5k_window_around_bp <- omikli_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
    dplyr::filter(cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct = Freq/sum(!is.na(omikli_sv_prox_dist)))
message("Percentage of omikli muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:")
## Percentage of omikli muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:
round(sum(omikli_5k_window_around_bp$pct) * 100, digits = 1)
## [1] 5.4

nonclustered_5k_window_around_bp <- nonclustered_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
    dplyr::filter(cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct = Freq/sum(!is.na(non_clustered_background_dist)))
message("Percentage of non-clustered muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:")
## Percentage of non-clustered muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:
round(sum(nonclustered_5k_window_around_bp$pct) * 100, digits = 1)
## [1] 1.9

# Fisher test of 5kb vs other distances
kataegis_5k_window_fish_tbl <- data.table(Kataegis = c(sum(kataegis_5k_window_around_bp$Freq),
    kataegis_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
        dplyr::filter(!cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
        dplyr::select(Freq) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        sum()), `Non-Clustered` = c(sum(nonclustered_5k_window_around_bp$Freq), nonclustered_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
    dplyr::filter(!cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
    dplyr::select(Freq) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    sum()))
rownames(kataegis_5k_window_fish_tbl) <- c("+/- 2.5 Kb", "Beyond +/- 2.5 Kb")
glance(fisher.test(x = kataegis_5k_window_fish_tbl))
## # A tibble: 1 × 6
##   estimate p.value conf.low conf.high method                         alternative
##      <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>                          <chr>      
## 1     19.0       0     18.1      19.9 Fisher's Exact Test for Count… two.sided


omikli_5k_window_fish_tbl <- data.table(Omikli = c(sum(omikli_5k_window_around_bp$Freq),
    omikli_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
        dplyr::filter(!cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
        dplyr::select(Freq) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        sum()), `Non-Clustered` = c(sum(nonclustered_5k_window_around_bp$Freq), nonclustered_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
    dplyr::filter(!cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
    dplyr::select(Freq) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    sum()))
rownames(omikli_5k_window_fish_tbl) <- c("+/- 2.5 Kb", "Beyond +/- 2.5 Kb")
glance(fisher.test(x = omikli_5k_window_fish_tbl))
## # A tibble: 1 × 6
##   estimate p.value conf.low conf.high method                         alternative
##      <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>                          <chr>      
## 1     2.91       0     2.81      3.02 Fisher's Exact Test for Count… two.sided

Build barplots of clustered event composition and signature contribution for E/SE-MERs in GCB, dual, and PC samples

Code 
clust_comp_barplot_data <- rbind(gcb_e_se_mers_cluster_composition, dual_e_se_mers_cluster_composition,
    pc_e_se_mers_cluster_composition)

clust_comp_barplot_data$tissue <- factor(clust_comp_barplot_data$tissue, levels = c("GCB",
    "GCB,PC", "PC"))


clust_comp_barplot <- ggplot(clust_comp_barplot_data) + geom_col(width = 0.8, aes(x = tissue,
    y = pct_per_class/100, fill = mainclass), width = 1) + labs(x = NULL, y = "Relative Contribution") +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Clustered\nEvent\nSubclass", values = clustered_events_colors[c(1:2,
        4:6)]) + theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45,
    hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
## Warning: Duplicated aesthetics after name standardisation: width

clust_sigs_barplot_data <- rbind(clust_gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t, clust_dual_e_se_mer_sig_t,
    clust_pc_e_se_mer_sig_t)

clust_sigs_barplot_data$sample <- factor(clust_sigs_barplot_data$sample, levels = c("GCB",
    "GCB,PC", "PC"))

clust_sigs_barplot <- ggplot(data = clust_sigs_barplot_data, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 0.8) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01,
    0.01)) + labs(y = "Relative Contribution") + theme(legend.position = "right",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, family = "sans", size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

Figure 5

Pair the query proximal transloation partner junction with the distal (other chromosomes) partner for analysis

Code 
# Grab all distal and proximal interchromosomal breakpoints
interchrom_juncs <- hq_total_sv_bp_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(class == "TRA")
interchrom_juncs$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs)),
    subject = gr.stripstrand(genes))]

# Need to remove all the MM canonical TRX BPs to extract true signal first find
# all BP that fall within the expected IGH range + 50 kbp pad
igh_trx_expected_range <- parse.gr("chr14:105586858-106879851") + 25000

igh_trx_bp <- gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs)),
    subject = igh_trx_expected_range, qcol = c("patient", "sample", "sv_id", "nearest_gene"))

# now that we have the IDs of any BP that occurs in IGH range, find all
# partners
igh_trx_partners <- interchrom_juncs %>%
    dplyr::filter(sv_id %in% igh_trx_bp$sv_id) %>%
    dplyr::filter(seqnames != "chr14")

# Next, find only those that are canonical partners
mm_igh_canon_expected_range <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path,
    "canonicalSubtypePartnerTranslocation.hg38.bed"), has_header = F)
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

# grab the SV IDs as these will be excluded from counting
mm_igh_canon_partners <- gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(igh_trx_partners)),
    subject = mm_igh_canon_expected_range, qcol = c("patient", "sample", "sv_id",
        "nearest_gene"))

canon_igh_trx_mask <- interchrom_juncs[interchrom_juncs$sv_id %in% mm_igh_canon_partners$sv_id]

# Mask the canonical IGH TRX from input
interchrom_juncs_igh_masked <- dplyr::setdiff(x = interchrom_juncs, y = canon_igh_trx_mask)

# Finally remove any IGK or IGL TRX
other_ig_region_mask <- GenomicRanges::reduce(ig_region_mask_bed + 25000) %Q% (seqnames %in%
    c("chr2", "chr22"))

other_ig_trx_bp <- gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs)),
    subject = other_ig_region_mask, qcol = c("patient", "sample", "sv_id", "nearest_gene"))

# now that we have the IDs of any BP that occurs in IGK/IGL range, find all
# partners
other_ig_trx_partners <- interchrom_juncs %>%
    dplyr::filter(sv_id %in% other_ig_trx_bp$sv_id) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!seqnames %in% c("chr2", "chr22"))

other_ig_trx_mask <- interchrom_juncs[interchrom_juncs$sv_id %in% other_ig_trx_partners$sv_id]

interchrom_juncs_fully_masked <- dplyr::setdiff(x = interchrom_juncs_igh_masked,
    y = other_ig_trx_mask)

Look at the density of breakpoints around mid of MERs and various comparisons

Code 
######################################################### RUN DENSITY CALC

# PARAMETERS FOR DENSITY\ - Flank of MER: +/- 500 Kbp (5e5) - bin size for
# density: 1 Kbp (1e3) - canonical IGH TRX BPs are excluded

interchrom_juncs_igh_masked_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs_fully_masked))
# interchrom_juncs_igh_masked_gr <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs_igh_masked))

# Look at the density of breakpoints from mid of MERs of: All MERs, each SE
# subset, and simulated non-MERs BCL6 should be negative control (i.e. should
# have none or very little TRX BP)
bcl6_mer_sv_bp_control <- e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label == "BCL6") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()
bcl6_control_sv_bp_dists <- proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr = interchrom_juncs_igh_masked_gr,
    gr_of_interest = dt2gr(e_se_mers[mer_id == bcl6_mer_sv_bp_control[1]]), proximal_dist_threshold = 500000)
## Setting parallel cores to 1 ...
## Setting parallel cores to 1 ...
bcl6_control_sv_bp_dens <- window_density(data_to_count = bcl6_control_sv_bp_dists,
    window_size = 500000 * 2, bin_size = 1000)
bcl6_control_sv_bp_dens$mer <- "BCL6"

bcl6_control_sv_bp_dens$scaled_count <- bcl6_control_sv_bp_dens$count * (1 + (1/sqrt(1)))

# FULL MER set loop through all mm_mer_dist <- data.table() for(i in
# 1:length(mers$mer_id)) { distances_to_add <- proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr =
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs)), gr_of_interest = dt2gr(mers[mer_id
# == mers$mer_id[i]]), proximal_dist_threshold = 1500000) mm_mer_dist <-
# gUtils::rrbind(mm_mer_dist, distances_to_add) } mm_mer_dens <-
# window_density(data_to_count = mm_mer_dist, window_size = 10e6, bin_size =
# 1e4) mm_mer_dens$mer <- 'MM'

# GC/DLBCL MER set need to exclude E/SE-MER with overlapping SNV/InDel MERs so
# same BP aren't counted twice Any E/SE-MERs within 500 kb of each other are
# combined gr.dist(gr1 = dt_to_gr(e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue ==
# 'GCB'))) / 5e5

gcb_mer_sv_bp_set <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "gcbMersForTrxDensity.txt"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)

# gcb_mer_sv_bp_set <- e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue == 'GCB') %>%
# dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% dplyr::filter(!mer_id %in%
# c('mer2','mer66','mer159','mer11','mer39','mer215', 'mer18','mer22')) %>% #
# BCL6, RPIA, LPP, ZCCHC7/PAX5 overlap purrr::as_vector()

gcb_mer_sv_bp_dists <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set)) {

    distances_to_add <- proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr = interchrom_juncs_igh_masked_gr,
        gr_of_interest = dt_to_gr(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set[i]), proximal_dist_threshold = 500000)
    gcb_mer_sv_bp_dists <- gUtils::rrbind(gcb_mer_sv_bp_dists, distances_to_add)
}

# Remove any duplicated BPs as result of MERs in close proximity but not same
# gene (BCL6 and LPP, SERPINA9 and TCL1A, ZCCHC7 and PAX5)
# uniq_gcb_mer_sv_bp_dists <-
# gcb_mer_sv_bp_dists[!duplicated(gcb_mer_sv_bp_dists[,c(1:3,6:9)]),]

gcb_mer_sv_bp_dens <- window_density(data_to_count = gcb_mer_sv_bp_dists, window_size = 500000 *
    2, bin_size = 1000)
gcb_mer_sv_bp_dens$mer <- "GCB"

# add prefactor to account for difference in size of MER groups (i.e. more MERs
# == more change to find BP) 1 / sqrt(# of MERs)
gcb_mer_sv_bp_dens$scaled_count <- gcb_mer_sv_bp_dens$count * (1 + (1/sqrt(length(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set))))

# dual MER set gr.dist(gr1 = dt_to_gr(e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue ==
# 'GCB,PC'))) / 5e5

dual_mer_sv_bp_set <- e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB,PC") %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, mer_gene_label, mer_id) %>%
    dplyr::rename(mer_gene_label_overlap = mer_gene_label, mer_id_overlap = mer_id)

# dual_mer_sv_bp_set <- e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue == 'GCB,PC') %>%
# dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector()
dual_mer_sv_bp_dists <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(dual_mer_sv_bp_set)) {

    distances_to_add <- proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr = interchrom_juncs_igh_masked_gr,
        gr_of_interest = dt_to_gr(dual_mer_sv_bp_set[i]), proximal_dist_threshold = 500000)
    dual_mer_sv_bp_dists <- gUtils::rrbind(dual_mer_sv_bp_dists, distances_to_add)
}

dual_mer_sv_bp_dens <- window_density(data_to_count = dual_mer_sv_bp_dists, window_size = 500000 *
    2, bin_size = 1000)
dual_mer_sv_bp_dens$mer <- "GCB,PC"

dual_mer_sv_bp_dens$scaled_count <- dual_mer_sv_bp_dens$count * (1 + (1/sqrt(length(dual_mer_sv_bp_set))))

# PC MER set need to exclude E/SE-MER with overlapping SNV/InDel MERs so same
# BP aren't counted twice gr.dist(gr1 = dt_to_gr(e_se_mers %>%
# dplyr::filter(tissue == 'PC'))) / 5e5

pc_mer_sv_bp_set <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "pcMersForTrxDensity.txt"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)

# pc_mer_sv_bp_set <- e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue == 'PC') %>%
# dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% dplyr::filter(!mer_id %in% c('mer37', 'mer182'))
# %>% # TXNDC5 and TENT5C overlap purrr::as_vector()

pc_mer_sv_bp_dists <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(pc_mer_sv_bp_set)) {

    distances_to_add <- proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr = interchrom_juncs_igh_masked_gr,
        gr_of_interest = dt_to_gr(pc_mer_sv_bp_set[i]), proximal_dist_threshold = 500000)
    pc_mer_sv_bp_dists <- gUtils::rrbind(pc_mer_sv_bp_dists, distances_to_add)
}

# Remove any duplicated BPs as result of MERs in close proximity but not same
# gene (TNK2 and RUBCN) uniq_pc_mer_sv_bp_dists <-
# pc_mer_sv_bp_dists[!duplicated(pc_mer_sv_bp_dists[,c(1:3,6:9)]),]

pc_mer_sv_bp_dens <- window_density(data_to_count = pc_mer_sv_bp_dists, window_size = 500000 *
    2, bin_size = 1000)
pc_mer_sv_bp_dens$mer <- "PC"

pc_mer_sv_bp_dens$scaled_count <- pc_mer_sv_bp_dens$count * (1 + (1/sqrt(length(pc_mer_sv_bp_set))))

# Set of Random non-MERs of similar size to grouped MERs (n=15) Run 5 tests and
# take median of density counts all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_set <- data.table()
# all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists <- data.table()
# all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens <- data.table() for(i in
# 1:length(sim_non_mers_for_density_eval)) { # randomize the non-MER for
# comparison to PC E/SE MERs, add to total set for record rand_sim_non_mer_set
# <- trunc_complete_sim_distro %>% dplyr::filter(simulation ==
# sim_non_mers_for_density_eval[i]) %>% dplyr::slice_sample(n = 15)
# rand_sim_non_mer_set$test <- i all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_set <-
# rrbind(all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_set, rand_sim_non_mer_set) # Now cycle
# through non-MERs to test for Tx BP distances rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists <-
# data.table() for(j in 1:nrow(rand_sim_non_mer_set)) { distances_to_add <-
# proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr =
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs_igh_masked)), gr_of_interest =
# dt2gr(rand_sim_non_mer_set[j,]), proximal_dist_threshold = 5e5)
# rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists <-
# gUtils::rrbind(rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists, distances_to_add) }
# rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists$test <- i all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists <-
# rrbind(all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists, rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists) #
# Calculate the density of Tx BP across the window rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens
# <- window_density(data_to_count = rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists, window_size
# = 5e5 * 2, bin_size = 1e3) rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens$mer <-
# 'Simulated\nNon-MERs' rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens$test <- i
# all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens <- rrbind(all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens,
# rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens) }

# Save/load the simulation run data for reproducible runs saveRDS(object =
# all_rand_sim_non_mer_set, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'nonMerSimulationTrxDistro.rds')) saveRDS(object =
# all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'nonMerSimulationTrxDistances.rds')) saveRDS(object =
# all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'nonMerSimulationTrxDistanceDensity.rds'))
all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_set <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationTrxDistro.rds"))
all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dists <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationTrxDistances.rds"))
all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationTrxDistanceDensity.rds"))

# Build final rand sim non-MER density per bin
median_all_rand_sim_non_mer_dens <- all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens %>%
    dplyr::group_by(bin) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_count = median(count)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

final_rand_sim_non_mer_sv_bp_dens <- all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens %>%
    dplyr::select(bin, midpoint_for_plot, mer) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = median_all_rand_sim_non_mer_dens, by = "bin") %>%
    dplyr::rename(count = median_count)

final_rand_sim_non_mer_sv_bp_dens$scaled_count <- final_rand_sim_non_mer_sv_bp_dens$count *
    (1 + (1/sqrt(nrow(all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_set))))

# # BOTH MER set pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_set <- e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue
# %in% c('GCB,PC', 'PC')) %>% dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector()
# pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_dists <- data.table() for(i in
# 1:length(pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_set)) { distances_to_add <-
# proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr =
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs_igh_masked)), gr_of_interest =
# dt2gr(mers[mer_id == pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_set[i]]), proximal_dist_threshold =
# 5e5) pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_dists <- gUtils::rrbind(pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_dists,
# distances_to_add) } pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_dens <- window_density(data_to_count =
# pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_dists, window_size = 5e5 * 2, bin_size = 1e3)
# pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_dens$mer <- 'All PC E/SE'
# pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_dens$scaled_count <- pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_dens$count * (1 + (1
# / sqrt(length(pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_set)))) Let's plot the SV BP density rough
# window-step count ggplot(data = gUtils::rrbind(gcb_mer_sv_bp_dens,
# both_mer_sv_bp_dens, pc_mer_sv_bp_dens, final_rand_sim_non_mer_sv_bp_dens)) +
# geom_line(aes(x = midpoint_for_plot, y = count, color = mer))

# Smoothed density
bp_density_for_plot <- gUtils::rrbind(gcb_mer_sv_bp_dens, dual_mer_sv_bp_dens, pc_mer_sv_bp_dens,
    final_rand_sim_non_mer_sv_bp_dens, bcl6_control_sv_bp_dens)
bp_density_for_plot$mer <- factor(bp_density_for_plot$mer, levels = c("GCB", "GCB,PC",
    "PC", "Simulated\nNon-MERs", "BCL6"))
mer_trx_bp_density <- ggplot(data = bp_density_for_plot) + geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
    color = "darkgray", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.8) + geom_smooth(aes(x = midpoint_for_plot,
    y = count, color = mer), method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = F, span = 0.17,
    linewidth = 1) + scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-500000, 500000), expand = c(0.02,
    0.02), labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.1, scale = 0.001)) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01,
    0.01)) + scale_color_manual(name = "MER Set", values = c("royalblue3", "#540046",
    "orangered3", "#91D1C2", "black")) + labs(x = "Interval around midpoint (kb)",
    y = "Breakpoint density") + theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13),
    panel.border = element_rect(linewidth = 1, fill = NA))
# export at 5 x 7 landscape

# Alt plot of all PC SE vs others alt_bp_density_for_plot <-
# gUtils::rrbind(gcb_mer_sv_bp_dens, pc_e_se_mer_sv_bp_dens,
# rand_sim_non_mer_sv_bp_dens, bcl6_control_sv_bp_dens)
# alt_bp_density_for_plot$mer <- factor(alt_bp_density_for_plot$mer, levels =
# c('All PC E/SE', 'GCB', 'Simulated\nNon-MERs', 'BCL6')) ggplot(data =
# alt_bp_density_for_plot) + geom_smooth(aes(x = midpoint_for_plot, y = count,
# color = mer), method = 'loess', formula = y ~ x, se = F, span = 0.17,
# linewidth = 1.25) + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = 'darkgray', linetype =
# 'dashed', linewidth = 2) + scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.5e6,0.5e6),
# expand = c(0.02,0.02), labels = scales::label_bytes(accuracy = 0.1)) +
# scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01,0.01)) + scale_color_manual(name = 'MER
# Group', values = c('orangered','blue','#540046','black')) + labs(x =
# 'Interval around Midpoint of MERs (bp)', y = 'BP count density') +
# theme(panel.border = element_rect(linewidth = 1, fill = NA))

# Put some stats on the enrichment of BPs in PC MERs vs. simulated Non-MERs To
# do this, will emulate the process done in SigProfilerTopography 1. Calculate
# the mean of the loess density of SV BP for real data (i.e. PC MERs) 2.
# Calculate the mean of the loess density of simulated non-MER sets
# (iterations=10) 3. Z-test one mean (real) with means of n simulations
loess_dens_pc_bp <- loess(data = bp_density_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer == "PC"), formula = scaled_count ~ midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17)
real_mean_loess_dens_pc_mer_bp <- mean(loess_dens_pc_bp$fitted)

# alt_loess_dens_pc_bp <- loess(data = alt_bp_density_for_plot %>%
# dplyr::filter(mer == 'All PC E/SE'), formula = scaled_count ~
# midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17) alt_real_mean_loess_dens_pc_mer_bp <-
# mean(alt_loess_dens_pc_bp$fitted)

# run simulation to get the means to compare observed value to
# sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer <- data.table() for(i in
# 1:n_distinct(all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens$test)) {
# message(paste('Simulation', i, '...', sep = ' ')) sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens <-
# all_rand_sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens %>% dplyr::filter(test == i)
# sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens$scaled_count <- sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens$count * (1 +
# (1 / sqrt(nrow(sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens)))) sim_non_mer_trx_bp_loess_density
# <- loess(data = sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens, formula = scaled_count ~
# midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17) sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer <-
# rrbind(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer, data.table('simulation' = i,
# 'mean_loess_density' = mean(sim_non_mer_trx_bp_loess_density$fitted))) }

# saveRDS(object = sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer, file =
# paste0(base_data_path, 'nonMerSimulationTrxLoessDensity.rds'))
sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationTrxLoessDensity.rds"))

###### From SigProfilerTopography ########## Author: Burcak Otlu Always ztest
###### one sample or two_sample?  I decided to use one sample because for
###### simulations I will get vertical vector and average of that vertical
###### vector must be equal to avg_simulated_signal, there is a way to self
###### verification Comparing one mean with means of n simulations gives a more
###### realistic p-value.  In case of comparison of two samples, ztest and
###### ttest gives either 0 or very low p-values.  zstat, pvalue =
###### ztest(expectedValues, value=observedValue) results in very small
###### p-values therefore we are not calling in this way.  if there is only one
###### simulation mean in simulationsHorizontalMeans, then pvalue is nan

# also use BOTH, GCB group and BCL6 as comparative
loess_dens_dual_bp <- loess(data = bp_density_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer == "GCB,PC"), formula = scaled_count ~ midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17)
real_mean_loess_dens_dual_mer_bp <- mean(loess_dens_dual_bp$fitted)

loess_dens_gcb_bp <- loess(data = bp_density_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer == "GCB"), formula = scaled_count ~ midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17)
real_mean_loess_dens_gcb_mer_bp <- mean(loess_dens_gcb_bp$fitted)

loess_dens_bcl6_bp <- loess(data = bp_density_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer == "BCL6"), formula = scaled_count ~ midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17)
real_mean_loess_dens_bcl6_mer_bp <- mean(loess_dens_bcl6_bp$fitted)

# now run Z-test of real obs mean vs simulated means
pc_sptbo_ztest_trx_bp_density <- BSDA::z.test(x = sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density,
    mu = real_mean_loess_dens_pc_mer_bp, alternative = "two.sided", sigma.x = sd(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density))

dual_sptbo_ztest_trx_bp_density <- BSDA::z.test(x = sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density,
    mu = real_mean_loess_dens_dual_mer_bp, alternative = "two.sided", sigma.x = sd(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density))

gcb_sptbo_ztest_trx_bp_density <- BSDA::z.test(x = sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density,
    mu = real_mean_loess_dens_gcb_mer_bp, alternative = "two.sided", sigma.x = sd(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density))
# Fold change of means
message("Fold-change BP density at PC E/SE-MERs compared to simulated Non-MER background: ",
    round(real_mean_loess_dens_pc_mer_bp/mean(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density),
        digits = 2))
## Fold-change BP density at PC E/SE-MERs compared to simulated Non-MER background: 4.25

message("Fold-change BP density at Dual GCB/PC E/SE-MERs compared to simulated Non-MER background: ",
    round(real_mean_loess_dens_dual_mer_bp/mean(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density),
        digits = 2))
## Fold-change BP density at Dual GCB/PC E/SE-MERs compared to simulated Non-MER background: 2.45

message("Fold-change BP density at GCB E/SE-MERs compared to simulated Non-MER background: ",
    round(real_mean_loess_dens_gcb_mer_bp/mean(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density),
        digits = 2))
## Fold-change BP density at GCB E/SE-MERs compared to simulated Non-MER background: 3.09

Look at all intrachromosomal breakpoints

Code 
intrachrom_juncs <- hq_total_sv_bp_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(class != "TRA")
intrachrom_juncs$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(intrachrom_juncs)),
    subject = gr.stripstrand(genes))]

# Convert to GR obj for assessment of overlap with MERs
intrachrom_ranges <- dt_to_gr(intrachrom_juncs)

# find E/SE-MER simple SVs by class GCB
gcb_mer_simple_sv <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set)) {

    simple_sv_to_add <- gr.findoverlaps(query = dt_to_gr(intrachrom_juncs), subject = dt_to_gr(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set[i]),
        qcol = c("class", "support", "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "nearest_gene"),
        scol = c("mer_gene_label_overlap", "mer_id_overlap"))

    gcb_mer_simple_sv_per_mer <- data.table(mer_gene_label_overlap = rep(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_gene_label_overlap[i],
        3), mer_id_overlap = rep(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_id_overlap[i], 3), class = c("DEL",
        "INV", "DUP"), count = c(0, 0, 0))

    if (length(simple_sv_to_add) > 0) {

        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DEL") > 0) {
            gcb_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[1] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DEL") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "INV") > 0) {
            gcb_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[2] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "INV") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DUP") > 0) {
            gcb_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[3] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DUP") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
    }

    gcb_mer_simple_sv <- gUtils::rrbind(gcb_mer_simple_sv, gcb_mer_simple_sv_per_mer)
}

# PC
pc_mer_simple_sv <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(pc_mer_sv_bp_set)) {

    simple_sv_to_add <- gr.findoverlaps(query = dt_to_gr(intrachrom_juncs), subject = dt_to_gr(pc_mer_sv_bp_set[i]),
        qcol = c("class", "support", "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "nearest_gene"),
        scol = c("mer_gene_label_overlap", "mer_id_overlap"))

    pc_mer_simple_sv_per_mer <- data.table(mer_gene_label_overlap = rep(pc_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_gene_label_overlap[i],
        3), mer_id_overlap = rep(pc_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_id_overlap[i], 3), class = c("DEL",
        "INV", "DUP"), count = c(0, 0, 0))

    if (length(simple_sv_to_add) > 0) {

        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DEL") > 0) {
            pc_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[1] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DEL") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "INV") > 0) {
            pc_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[2] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "INV") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DUP") > 0) {
            pc_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[3] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DUP") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
    }

    pc_mer_simple_sv <- gUtils::rrbind(pc_mer_simple_sv, pc_mer_simple_sv_per_mer)
}

# Dual
dual_mer_simple_sv <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(dual_mer_sv_bp_set)) {

    simple_sv_to_add <- gr.findoverlaps(query = dt_to_gr(intrachrom_juncs), subject = dt_to_gr(dual_mer_sv_bp_set[i]),
        qcol = c("class", "support", "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "nearest_gene"),
        scol = c("mer_gene_label_overlap", "mer_id_overlap"))

    dual_mer_simple_sv_per_mer <- data.table(mer_gene_label_overlap = rep(dual_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_gene_label_overlap[i],
        3), mer_id_overlap = rep(dual_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_id_overlap[i], 3), class = c("DEL",
        "INV", "DUP"), count = c(0, 0, 0))

    if (length(simple_sv_to_add) > 0) {

        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DEL") > 0) {
            dual_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[1] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DEL") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "INV") > 0) {
            dual_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[2] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "INV") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DUP") > 0) {
            dual_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[3] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DUP") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
    }

    dual_mer_simple_sv <- gUtils::rrbind(dual_mer_simple_sv, dual_mer_simple_sv_per_mer)
}

# # Need to get the correct ranges for overlap intrachrom_ranges_dt <-

# = per_intrachrom_bps$end[2], 'class' = per_intrachrom_bps$class[1], 'support'

# per_intrachrom_bps$nearest_gene[1])) } }

Figure 6

14 Chromosomal rearrangement of E/SE-MERs recurrently involves MYC

Find the partners of all grouped MERs

Code 
############################################### Find the partners of all
############################################### grouped MERs

# first grab all the non-overlapping MER IDs
all_se_mer_query_set <- rbind(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set, dual_mer_sv_bp_set, pc_mer_sv_bp_set)
mer_prox_bp_dist <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(all_se_mer_query_set)) {
    moi_prox_bp_dist <- proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr = interchrom_juncs_igh_masked_gr,
        gr_of_interest = dt_to_gr(all_se_mer_query_set[i]), proximal_dist_threshold = 500000)

    if (nrow(moi_prox_bp_dist) > 0) {
        mer_prox_bp_dist <- rrbind(mer_prox_bp_dist, cbind(moi_prox_bp_dist, e_se_mers %>%
            dplyr::filter(mer_id %in% as.character(str_split(string = all_se_mer_query_set$mer_id_overlap[i],
                pattern = ";", simplify = T)[1])) %>%
            dplyr::select(mer_gene_label, mer_id, enhancer_id, tissue, enhancer_type)))
    }
}

## Setting parallel cores to 1 ...

# There are SV BP that are likely counted twice as some MERs are in within the
# same +/- 1.5 Mbp window Remove duplicated pair IDs so each is only counted
# once per gene
mer_trx_partners_nodup <- mer_prox_bp_dist %>%
    dplyr::group_by(nearest_gene_distal_partner) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!duplicated(pair_id)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

top_mer_trx_partners <- mer_trx_partners_nodup %>%
    dplyr::group_by(nearest_gene_distal_partner) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_of_mer_trx_bp = round((n/nrow(mer_trx_partners_nodup)) * 100,
        digits = 2))

top_mer_trx_partners_by_se <- mer_trx_partners_nodup %>%
    dplyr::group_by(nearest_gene_distal_partner, tissue) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(n))

# How many MER TRX
message("Total translocations at E/SE-MERs: ")
## Total translocations at E/SE-MERs:
print(mer_trx_partners_nodup %>%
    dplyr::group_by(tissue) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup())
##   tissue  n
## 1    GCB 45
## 2 GCB,PC 26
## 3     PC 57

# What percentage of all TRX BP are linked to partners of interest MYC /
# POU5F1B this is pct of all SE-MER TRX BPs
myc_pou51b_pct_grouped_mers <- mer_trx_partners_nodup %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene_distal_partner %in% c("MYC", "POU5F1B")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(nearest_gene_distal_partner) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(cumulative_pct_of_mer_trx_bp = round((sum(n)/nrow(mer_trx_partners_nodup)) *
        100, digits = 2))

top_mer_trx_partners_by_se %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene_distal_partner %in% c("MYC", "POU5F1B"))
## # A tibble: 4 × 3
## # Groups:   nearest_gene_distal_partner, tissue [4]
##   nearest_gene_distal_partner tissue     n
##   <chr>                       <chr>  <int>
## 1 MYC                         PC        13
## 2 MYC                         GCB        9
## 3 MYC                         GCB,PC     2
## 4 POU5F1B                     PC         1

# find SV IDs
myc_pou51b_trx_ids <- mer_trx_partners_nodup %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene_distal_partner %in% c("MYC", "POU5F1B")) %>%
    dplyr::select(pair_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()

# to correctly calculate the pct, need to count single SV events not each BP
# for an SV (i.e. use total BP divided by 2)
message("Total TRX BP in E/SE MERs to MYC: ", length(myc_pou51b_trx_ids))
## Total TRX BP in E/SE MERs to MYC: 25
message("Percentage of all TRX BP have junction from E/SE MER to MYC: ", round((length(myc_pou51b_trx_ids)/n_distinct(interchrom_juncs_fully_masked$sv_id)) *
    100, digits = 2))
## Percentage of all TRX BP have junction from E/SE MER to MYC: 1.15

# how many patients have these TRX
myc_pou51b_partners <- interchrom_juncs_fully_masked %>%
    dplyr::filter(sv_id %in% myc_pou51b_trx_ids)

message("Number of patients with E/SE MER to MYC junction: ", n_distinct(myc_pou51b_partners$patient))
## Number of patients with E/SE MER to MYC junction: 18
message("Percentage of patients with E/SE MER to MYC junction: ", round((n_distinct(myc_pou51b_partners$patient)/294) *
    100, digits = 2))
## Percentage of patients with E/SE MER to MYC junction: 6.12

# MYC to PC E/SE-MERs
myc_pou51b_partners %>%
    dplyr::filter(!nearest_gene %in% c("MYC", "POU5F1B")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(seqnames) %>%
    dplyr::select(nearest_gene) %>%
    table()
## Adding missing grouping variables: `seqnames`
##         nearest_gene
## seqnames ATP2B4 BLOC1S5-TXNDC5 BMP6 EEF1E1-BLOC1S5 EIF2AK3 IGLL5 RPIA RSPH14
##    chr1       1              0    0              0       0     0    0      0
##    chr2       0              0    0              0       1     0    4      0
##    chr22      0              0    0              0       0     1    0      3
##    chr6       0              8    2              2       0     0    0      0
##         nearest_gene
## seqnames TENT5C THNSL2
##    chr1       2      0
##    chr2       0      1
##    chr22      0      0
##    chr6       0      0

# GCB E/SE MER to MYC junctions
print("Number of TRX junctions from GCB E/SE-MERs from RPIA to MYC")
## [1] "Number of TRX junctions from GCB E/SE-MERs from RPIA to MYC"
myc_pou51b_partners %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene == "RPIA") %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, strand, class, support, patient, sample,
        nearest_gene, sv_id) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = mer_trx_partners_nodup %>%
        dplyr::select(pair_id, distance_proximal, chrom_distal_partner, nearest_gene_distal_partner) %>%
        dplyr::rename(sv_id = pair_id), by = "sv_id")
##    seqnames    start      end strand  class           support
##      <char>    <int>    <int> <char> <char>            <char>
## 1:     chr2 88716883 88716883      -    TRA manta,delly,svaba
## 2:     chr2 88725724 88725724      +    TRA manta,delly,svaba
## 3:     chr2 88788959 88788959      +    TRA manta,delly,svaba
## 4:     chr2 88801275 88801275      +    TRA manta,delly,svaba
##                    patient                  sample nearest_gene sv_id
##                     <char>                  <char>       <char> <int>
## 1:                501550-N             CG20-4476-T         RPIA 11158
## 2:            PD26416b_2_N              PD26416d_T         RPIA 37191
## 3: IID_H130608_N01_01_WG01 IID_H130608_T02_02_WG01         RPIA 16210
## 4: IID_H201558_N01_01_WG01 IID_H201558_T01_01_WG01         RPIA 24543
##    distance_proximal chrom_distal_partner nearest_gene_distal_partner
##                <int>               <fctr>                      <char>
## 1:            103117                 chr8                         MYC
## 2:             94276                 chr8                         MYC
## 3:             31041                 chr8                         MYC
## 4:             18725                 chr8                         MYC

# All MYC to PC E/SE-MERs
message("Number of MYC TRX junctions from PC E/SE-MERs: ", myc_pou51b_partners %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene %in% c("BLOC1S5-TXNDC5", "EEF1E1-BLOC1S5", "BMP6",
        "TENT5C")) %>%
    dplyr::count())
## Number of MYC TRX junctions from PC E/SE-MERs: 14

# TXNDC5 BPs by patients
myc_pou51b_partners %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene %in% c("BLOC1S5-TXNDC5", "EEF1E1-BLOC1S5", "BMP6")) %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()
## [1] 8

myc_pou51b_partners %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene == "TENT5C") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::n_distinct()
## [1] 1

interchrom_juncs_fully_masked %>%
    dplyr::filter(sv_id %in% (mer_trx_partners_nodup %>%
        dplyr::filter(nearest_gene_distal_partner == "IGHV3OR16-17") %>%
        dplyr::select(pair_id) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        as.numeric()))

## 10: CG21-1951-T 12695        2       2549399476     2549399476        TEX14


interchrom_juncs_fully_masked %>%
    dplyr::filter(sv_id %in% (mer_trx_partners_nodup %>%
        dplyr::filter(nearest_gene_distal_partner == "MTA1") %>%
        dplyr::select(pair_id) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        as.numeric()))

interchrom_juncs_fully_masked %>%
    dplyr::filter(sv_id %in% (mer_trx_partners_nodup %>%
        dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label == "TENT5C") %>%
        dplyr::select(pair_id) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        as.numeric()))
##     seqnames     start       end strand  class           support
##       <char>     <int>     <int> <char> <char>            <char>
##  1:     chr1 117619818 117619818      +    TRA manta,delly,svaba
##  2:     chr1 117632452 117632452      -    TRA manta,delly,svaba
##  3:     chr1 117670680 117670680      -    TRA manta,delly,svaba
##  4:     chr1 117676317 117676317      +    TRA manta,delly,svaba
##  5:     chr1 117713639 117713639      +    TRA manta,delly,svaba
##  6:     chr1 117794462 117794462      -    TRA manta,delly,svaba
##  7:     chr2 231779428 231779428      +    TRA manta,delly,svaba
##  8:     chr2 232567639 232567639      +    TRA manta,delly,svaba
##  9:     chr3 169908707 169908707      +    TRA manta,delly,svaba
## 10:     chr3 169984441 169984441      -    TRA manta,delly,svaba
## 11:     chr8 127273729 127273729      +    TRA manta,delly,svaba
## 12:     chr8 127824871 127824871      -    TRA manta,delly,svaba
##                     patient                  sample sv_id bp_order
##                      <char>                  <char> <int>    <int>
##  1:    I-H-130718-N1-1-D1-2    I-H-130718-T1-1-D1-2 14033        1
##  2: IID_H201444_N01_01_WG01 IID_H201444_T01_01_WG01 20997        1
##  3:    I-H-130718-N1-1-D1-2   I-H-130718-T1-10-D1-2 14152        1
##  4: IID_H201444_N01_01_WG01 IID_H201444_T01_01_WG01 20999        1
##  5:                463633-N             CG19-4092-T 10062        1
##  6:                463633-N             CG19-4092-T 10063        1
##  7: IID_H201444_N01_01_WG01 IID_H201444_T01_01_WG01 20997        2
##  8: IID_H201444_N01_01_WG01 IID_H201444_T01_01_WG01 20999        2
##  9:                463633-N             CG19-4092-T 10062        2
## 10:                463633-N             CG19-4092-T 10063        2
## 11:    I-H-130718-N1-1-D1-2    I-H-130718-T1-1-D1-2 14033        2
## 12:    I-H-130718-N1-1-D1-2   I-H-130718-T1-10-D1-2 14152        2
##     start_abs_linear end_abs_linear nearest_gene
##                <num>          <num>       <char>
##  1:        117619818      117619818       TENT5C
##  2:        117632452      117632452       TENT5C
##  3:        117670680      117670680       TENT5C
##  4:        117676317      117676317       TENT5C
##  5:        117713639      117713639       TENT5C
##  6:        117794462      117794462        GDAP2
##  7:        480735850      480735850        PDE6D
##  8:        481524061      481524061       EIF4E2
##  9:        661058658      661058658        SAMD7
## 10:        661134392      661134392        SEC62
## 11:       1518624005     1518624005      POU5F1B
## 12:       1519175147     1519175147          MYC

Look at BCL6 partner TRX

Code 
bcl6_trx_sv_id <- mer_trx_partners_nodup %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label == "BCL6") %>%
    dplyr::select(pair_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()

bcl6_trx_sv_pair <- hq_total_sv_bp_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(sv_id == bcl6_trx_sv_id)
print("BCL6 TRX pair:")
## [1] "BCL6 TRX pair:"
bcl6_trx_sv_pair %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, strand, class, support, patient, sample,
        bp_order)
##    seqnames     start       end strand  class     support           patient
##      <char>     <int>     <int> <char> <char>      <char>            <char>
## 1:     chr3 187327566 187327566      -    TRA manta,delly MMRC0338_V0D58S_N
## 2:    chr20  41618396  41618396      +    TRA manta,delly MMRC0338_V0D58S_N
##               sample bp_order
##               <char>    <int>
## 1: MMRC0338_V0D58G_T        1
## 2: MMRC0338_V0D58G_T        2

print("BCL6 junction partner distance from MAFB (in bp):")
## [1] "BCL6 junction partner distance from MAFB (in bp):"
as.numeric((bcl6_trx_sv_pair %>%
    dplyr::filter(seqnames == "chr20") %>%
    dplyr::select(start) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()) - gr2dt(complete_gene_segments_gr %Q% (gene_name == "MAFB" &
    segment == "five_prime_utr"))$start)
## [1] 929545

Is TXNDC5 and TENT5C expressed in normal plasma cells and myeloma cells?

Look at CoMMpass paired WGS/RNA/Survival for analysis of TXNDC5 and TENT5C E/SE-MERs effect

Code 
txndc5_myc_trx_patients <- myc_pou51b_partners %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene %in% c("BLOC1S5-TXNDC5", "EEF1E1-BLOC1S5", "BMP6")) %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

muts_txndc5_myc_trx_partner_mer <- muts_per_grouped_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label == "TXNDC5" & patient %in% txndc5_myc_trx_patients)


tent5c_myc_trx_patients <- myc_pou51b_partners %>%
    dplyr::filter(nearest_gene == "TENT5C") %>%
    dplyr::select(patient) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

muts_tent5c_myc_trx_partner_mer <- muts_per_grouped_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label %in% c("TXNDC5", "TENT5C") & patient %in% c(txndc5_myc_trx_patients,
        tent5c_myc_trx_patients))

# Read in CoMMpass MER muts
commpass_mer_bed <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "DEPRECIATEDfunSeqAnalysis/commpass.funseq.anno.bed.gz"),
    has_header = F, additional_col_names = c("ref", "alt", "ID", "non_coding_score"))
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

commpass_mer_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = commpass_mer_bed, subject = mers_gr,
    qcol = c("ID", "ref", "alt"), scol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label"))

commpass_mer_muts_dt <- gr2dt(commpass_mer_muts) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode = str_remove(string = ID, pattern = ".*--"),
        Tumor_Sample_Barcode = str_remove(string = ID, pattern = "--.*")) %>%
    dplyr::rename(Reference_Allele = ref, Tumor_Seq_Allele2 = alt)

# CoMMpass MYC events from Even's paper
commpass_sv_hg19 <- data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "mmrf_delly_mapq_60_filtered_baseline.txt")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sample_short_id = paste0(sample, "_CD138pos"))

# expected ranges from Aneta's paper
commpass_myc_txndc5_events <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 8 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 126000000 & pos1 < 131000000 &
        chrom2 == 6 & pos2 > 7600000 & pos2 < 8400000)

commpass_myc_tent5c_events <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 8 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 126000000 & pos1 < 131000000 &
        chrom2 == 1 & pos2 > 118100000 & pos2 < 118500000)

commpass_myc_igh_events <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 14 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 105000000 & pos1 < 107500000 &
        chrom2 == 8 & pos2 > 126000000 & pos2 < 131000000)

commpass_myc_igk_events <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 8 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 126000000 & pos1 < 131000000 &
        chrom2 == 2 & pos2 > 88800000 & pos2 < 90200000)

commpass_myc_igl_events <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 22 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 22500000 & pos1 < 24500000 &
        chrom2 == 8 & pos2 > 126000000 & pos2 < 131000000)

commpass_myc_other_events1 <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 8 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 126000000 & pos1 < 131000000)

commpass_myc_other_events2 <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom2 == 8 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos2 > 126000000 & pos2 < 131000000)

Survival analysis with CoMMpass

Subset the data into MYC events to TXNDC5 / TENT5C and Ig loci

Call:
survival::survdiff(formula = Surv(OS_months, OS_status) ~ myc_trx, 
    data = myc_event_test)

n=640, 74 observations deleted due to missingness.

                        N Observed Expected (O-E)^2/E (O-E)^2/V
myc_trx=TXNDC5/TENT5C  26        9     6.92     0.628     0.654
myc_trx=IGH/IGK/IGL    53       18    14.35     0.929     1.012
myc_trx=No event      561      151   156.73     0.210     1.759

 Chisq= 1.8  on 2 degrees of freedom, p= 0.4

RNA analysis of TXNDC5/TENT5C E/SE-MER events compared to no event in CoMMpass

Supplementary Figure 11

Epilogos in MERs to quantify the % make-up for chromatin state

Code 
# Epilogo top class Read in Epilogo output mm_epilogos <-
# aggregate_these(path_to_files = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'epiLogoAnalysis/results/'), pattern_to_grab = '*_mm_epilogos.*.txt.gz',
# delim = '\t', has_header = F)
chromhmm_metadata <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "epiLogoAnalysis/metadata.tsv"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)
# colnames(mm_epilogos) <- c('chrom', 'start', 'end',
# chromhmm_metadata$long_name)

# saveRDS(object = mm_epilogos, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'epiLogoAnalysis/mm_epilogos.rds'))
mm_epilogos <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "epiLogoAnalysis/mm_epilogos.rds"))

# Subset the epilogos to overlap with MERs find epilogo proportion for example
# MERs
bcl6_control_epilogos <- epilogo_prop_per_region(roi = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_id == "mer1"))), roi_id = "BCL6-1")
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
bcl6_alt_control_epilogos <- epilogo_prop_per_region(roi = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_id == "mer2"))), roi_id = "BCL6-2")
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns

# Get the mean of the whole set of MERs per group similar to breakpoint density
# GC/DLBCL MER set
gcb_mer_epilogo_set <- gcb_mer_sv_bp_set
# gcb_mer_epilogo_set <- e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue == 'GCB' & mer_id
# != 'mer159') %>% # Don't count the same region more than once
# dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector()
gcb_mer_epilogo_props <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(gcb_mer_epilogo_set)) {
    moi <- as.character(str_split(string = gcb_mer_epilogo_set$mer_id_overlap[i],
        pattern = ";", simplify = T)[1])

    epilogos_to_add <- epilogo_prop_per_region(roi = mers_gr %Q% (mer_id == moi),
        roi_id = moi)
    gcb_mer_epilogo_props <- gUtils::rrbind(gcb_mer_epilogo_props, epilogos_to_add)
}
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
gcb_mer_epilogo_props$e_se_group <- "GCB"
mean_gcb_mer_epilogo_props <- gcb_mer_epilogo_props %>%
    dplyr::group_by(state, e_se_group) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_prop = mean(prop))

# Dual GCB,PC MER set
dual_mer_epilogo_set <- dual_mer_sv_bp_set
# dual_mer_epilogo_set <- e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue == 'GCB,PC') %>%
# dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector()
dual_mer_epilogo_props <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(dual_mer_epilogo_set)) {
    moi <- as.character(str_split(string = dual_mer_epilogo_set$mer_id_overlap[i],
        pattern = ";", simplify = T)[1])

    epilogos_to_add <- epilogo_prop_per_region(roi = mers_gr %Q% (mer_id == moi),
        roi_id = moi)
    dual_mer_epilogo_props <- gUtils::rrbind(dual_mer_epilogo_props, epilogos_to_add)
}
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
dual_mer_epilogo_props$e_se_group <- "GCB,PC"
mean_dual_mer_epilogo_props <- dual_mer_epilogo_props %>%
    dplyr::group_by(state, e_se_group) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_prop = mean(prop))

# PC MER set
pc_mer_epilogo_set <- pc_mer_sv_bp_set
# pc_mer_epilogo_set <- e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue == 'PC' & !mer_id
# %in% c('mer36','mer182')) %>% dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% purrr::as_vector()
pc_mer_epilogo_props <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(pc_mer_epilogo_set)) {
    moi <- as.character(str_split(string = pc_mer_epilogo_set$mer_id_overlap[i],
        pattern = ";", simplify = T)[1])

    epilogos_to_add <- epilogo_prop_per_region(roi = mers_gr %Q% (mer_id == moi),
        roi_id = moi)
    pc_mer_epilogo_props <- gUtils::rrbind(pc_mer_epilogo_props, epilogos_to_add)
}
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
pc_mer_epilogo_props$e_se_group <- "PC"
mean_pc_mer_epilogo_props <- pc_mer_epilogo_props %>%
    dplyr::group_by(state, e_se_group) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_prop = mean(prop))

e_se_mer_epilogos_props <- rrbind(gcb_mer_epilogo_props, dual_mer_epilogo_props,
    pc_mer_epilogo_props)
e_se_mer_epilogos_props$state <- stringr::str_replace_all(string = e_se_mer_epilogos_props$state,
    pattern = "_", replacement = " ")



mean_e_se_mer_epilogos_props <- rrbind(mean_gcb_mer_epilogo_props, mean_dual_mer_epilogo_props,
    mean_pc_mer_epilogo_props)
mean_e_se_mer_epilogos_props$state <- stringr::str_replace_all(string = mean_e_se_mer_epilogos_props$state,
    pattern = "_", replacement = " ")
mean_e_se_mer_epilogos_props$state <- factor(mean_e_se_mer_epilogos_props$state,
    levels = c("Heterochromatin Low Signal", "H3K27me3 Repressed", "H3K9me3 Repressed",
        "Weak Transcription", "Transcription Elongation", "Transcription Transition",
        "Weak Enhancer", "Strong Enhancer2", "Strong Enhancer1", "Poised Promoter",
        "Weak Promoter", "Active Promoter"))
chromhmm_state_colors <- c("#260F99", "#6551CC", "#BFB2FF", "#0F6B99", "#51A3CC",
    "#B2E5FF", "#6B990F", "#A3CC51", "#E5FFB2", "#99540F", "#CC8E51", "#FFD8B2")

# Plot the mean Epilogos per E/SE-MER gropu
e_se_mer_chromstate_composition <- ggplot2::ggplot(mean_e_se_mer_epilogos_props,
    aes(x = state, y = mean_prop, label = round(mean_prop, digits = 2), fill = state)) +
    ggplot2::geom_col() + ggfittext::geom_bar_text() + ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(name = "ChromHMM\nStates",
    values = rev(chromhmm_state_colors)) + ggplot2::coord_flip() + ggplot2::facet_wrap(facets = ~e_se_group) +
    ggplot2::labs(y = "Mean Proportion", x = "ChromHMM States") + ggplot2::theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    legend.position = "none", panel.spacing.x = unit(1.5, "lines"))

Epigenomic analysis of TXNDC5 MERs

Code 
mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label == "TXNDC5") %>%
    dplyr::select(intron_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_intron = mean(intron_overlap_pct), mean_three_prime = mean(three_prime_utr_overlap_pct))
##   mean_intron mean_three_prime
## 1       66.33            26.84

# TRX Junctions
txndc5_starburst_dists <- proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs_fully_masked)),
    gr_of_interest = dt2gr(e_se_mers[mer_id == "mer36"]), proximal_dist_threshold = 500000)
## Setting parallel cores to 1 ...
## Setting parallel cores to 1 ...

# message('Total TRX BP at E/SE MER associated with TXNDC5:')
# nrow(txndc5_starburst_dists) message('Partners of TXNDC5 MER TRX junctions:')
# table(txndc5_starburst_dists$nearest_gene_distal_partner)


# Epilogos
txndc5_epilogos <- epilogo_prop_per_region(roi = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_id == "mer37"))), roi_id = "TXNDC5")
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
message("Chromatin state composition of TXNDC5: ")
## Chromatin state composition of TXNDC5:
txndc5_epilogos
##                          state        prop    roi
##                         <fctr>       <num> <char>
##  1:            Active_Promoter 0.004610717 TXNDC5
##  2:           Strong_Enhancer1 0.149226295 TXNDC5
##  3:              Weak_Promoter 0.000000000 TXNDC5
##  4:            Poised_Promoter 0.000000000 TXNDC5
##  5:              Weak_Enhancer 0.000000000 TXNDC5
##  6:           Strong_Enhancer2 0.014557975 TXNDC5
##  7:   Transcription_Transition 0.609743555 TXNDC5
##  8:         Weak_Transcription 0.027339007 TXNDC5
##  9:   Transcription_Elongation 0.194522452 TXNDC5
## 10:          H3K9me3_Repressed 0.000000000 TXNDC5
## 11: Heterochromatin_Low_Signal 0.000000000 TXNDC5
## 12:         H3K27me3_Repressed 0.000000000 TXNDC5

message("Percentatge of Transcription chromatin states (Transcription_Transition + Transcription_Elongation): ",
    txndc5_epilogos %>%
        dplyr::filter(state %in% c("Transcription_Transition", "Transcription_Elongation")) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(combined_prop = round(sum(prop) * 100, digits = 1)))
## Percentatge of Transcription chromatin states (Transcription_Transition + Transcription_Elongation): 80.4

message("Percentatge of Enhancer chromatin states (Strong_Enhancer1 + Strong_Enhancer2): ",
    txndc5_epilogos %>%
        dplyr::filter(state %in% c("Strong_Enhancer1", "Strong_Enhancer2")) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(combined_prop = round(sum(prop) * 100, digits = 1)))
## Percentatge of Enhancer chromatin states (Strong_Enhancer1 + Strong_Enhancer2): 16.4

# Mutation VAF
txndc5_muts <- muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id %in% c("snv_er72", "indel_er9"))

message("Percentage of Subclonal (VAF <0.25) mutations (SNVs + InDels) at TXNDC5 MERs: ",
    round(as.numeric(txndc5_muts %>%
        dplyr::filter(VAF < 0.25) %>%
        dplyr::count()/nrow(txndc5_muts)) * 100, digits = 1))
## Percentage of Subclonal (VAF <0.25) mutations (SNVs + InDels) at TXNDC5 MERs: 41.4
message("Min subclonal VAF: ", round(min(txndc5_muts$VAF), digits = 3))
## Min subclonal VAF: 0.042

message("Percentage of highly Clonal (VAF >=0.5) mutations (SNVs + InDels) at TXNDC5 MERs: ",
    round(as.numeric(txndc5_muts %>%
        dplyr::filter(VAF >= 0.5) %>%
        dplyr::count()/nrow(txndc5_muts)) * 100, digits = 1))
## Percentage of highly Clonal (VAF >=0.5) mutations (SNVs + InDels) at TXNDC5 MERs: 18.1
message("Max Clonal VAF: ", round(max(txndc5_muts$VAF), digits = 3))
## Max Clonal VAF: 0.966

Epigenomic analysis of TENT5C MERs

Code 
mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label == "TENT5C") %>%
    dplyr::select(, cds_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_cds = mean(cds_overlap_pct), mean_intron = mean(intron_overlap_pct),
        mean_three_prime = mean(three_prime_utr_overlap_pct))
##   mean_cds mean_intron mean_three_prime
## 1    64.24        10.6            12.71

# TENT5C
tent5c_starburst_dists <- proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs_fully_masked)),
    gr_of_interest = dt2gr(e_se_mers[mer_id == "mer70"]), proximal_dist_threshold = 500000)
## Setting parallel cores to 1 ...
## Setting parallel cores to 1 ...

# print('Total TRX BP at E/SE MER associated with TENT5C:')
# nrow(tent5c_starburst_dists) print('Partners of TENT5C MER TRX junctions:')
# table(tent5c_starburst_dists$nearest_gene_distal_partner)

# Epilogos
tent5c_epilogos <- epilogo_prop_per_region(roi = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_id == "mer70"))), roi_id = "TENT5C")
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
message("Chromatin state composition of TENT5C: ")
## Chromatin state composition of TENT5C:
tent5c_epilogos
##                          state        prop    roi
##                         <fctr>       <num> <char>
##  1:            Active_Promoter 0.026157991 TENT5C
##  2:           Strong_Enhancer1 0.406305599 TENT5C
##  3:              Weak_Promoter 0.002870861 TENT5C
##  4:            Poised_Promoter 0.000000000 TENT5C
##  5:              Weak_Enhancer 0.002474422 TENT5C
##  6:           Strong_Enhancer2 0.150000549 TENT5C
##  7:   Transcription_Transition 0.412190577 TENT5C
##  8:         Weak_Transcription 0.000000000 TENT5C
##  9:   Transcription_Elongation 0.000000000 TENT5C
## 10:          H3K9me3_Repressed 0.000000000 TENT5C
## 11: Heterochromatin_Low_Signal 0.000000000 TENT5C
## 12:         H3K27me3_Repressed 0.000000000 TENT5C

message("Percentatge of Enhancer chromatin states (Strong_Enhancer1 + Strong_Enhancer2): ",
    tent5c_epilogos %>%
        dplyr::filter(state %in% c("Strong_Enhancer1", "Strong_Enhancer2")) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(combined_prop = round(sum(prop) * 100, digits = 1)))
## Percentatge of Enhancer chromatin states (Strong_Enhancer1 + Strong_Enhancer2): 55.6


message("Percentatge of Transcription Transition chromatin state: ", tent5c_epilogos %>%
    dplyr::filter(state == "Transcription_Transition") %>%
    dplyr::reframe(combined_prop = round(sum(prop) * 100, digits = 1)))
## Percentatge of Transcription Transition chromatin state: 41.2


# Mutation VAF
tent5c_muts <- muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id %in% c("indel_er2", "snv_er16"))

Supplementary Figure 12

Epigenomic analysis of ZFP36L1 MER

Code 
mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label == "ZFP36L1") %>%
    dplyr::select(, cds_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_cds = mean(cds_overlap_pct), mean_intron = mean(intron_overlap_pct),
        mean_three_prime = mean(three_prime_utr_overlap_pct))
##   mean_cds mean_intron mean_three_prime
## 1     1.95       92.21                0

# Intra-chromosomal deletions or inversions for chr14 MERs Get only the
# intra-chromosomal events
intrachrom_juncs <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(class != "TRA")))
# Annotate each partner junction by nearest coding gene
intrachrom_juncs$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = intrachrom_juncs, subject = gr.stripstrand(genes))]

# deletions/inversions that occur between ZFP36L1 / SERPINA9 / TCL1A and IGH
# locus Let's grab all DELs / INVs on chr14q
intrachrom_chr14q <- intrachrom_juncs %Q% (seqnames == "chr14" & start > chr_arm_lengths$q_start[14] &
    class %in% c("DEL", "INV"))

# workflow is to find all BP that are within the 'effective' range from the
# gene/MER of interest This range is slightly different for DELs vs INVs For
# DELs, first BP must be just outside gene/MER on centromeric side but can be
# variable on the IGH locus side due to number of SEs at that region For INVs,
# fist BP can be on either side of gene/MER

# start of IGH region (using beginning of IGHA2) chr14:105583731 ZFP36L1
# chr14:68787662-68793067 SERPINA9 chr14:94462721-94476197 TCL1A
# chr14:95709947-95714125

#################### D E L s #################### ZFP36L1
zfp36l1_final_dels <- data.table()
zfp36l1_dels_end1 <- gr.findoverlaps(query = parse.gr(paste0("chr14:68793067-", 68793067 +
    2500000)), subject = intrachrom_chr14q %Q% (class == "DEL"), scol = c("class",
    "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "bp_order", "nearest_gene"))

# loop through all the deletion BPs that fall in the effective range use these
# to find the corresponding BP and check if in the IGH region
for (i in seq(along = zfp36l1_dels_end1$sv_id)) {
    zfp36l1_dels_end2 <- intrachrom_juncs %Q% (sv_id == zfp36l1_dels_end1$sv_id[i]) %Q%
        (bp_order != zfp36l1_dels_end1$bp_order[i])

    # If in the IGH range, keep the SV ID
    if (length(zfp36l1_dels_end2) > 0) {
        if (gr.in(query = zfp36l1_dels_end2, igh_trx_expected_range)) {
            zfp36l1_final_dels <- rrbind(zfp36l1_final_dels, gr2dt(intrachrom_juncs %Q%
                (sv_id == zfp36l1_dels_end1$sv_id[i])))
        }
    }
}
message("Intra-chromosomal deletions downstream of ZFP36L1 to near IGH region:")
## Intra-chromosomal deletions downstream of ZFP36L1 to near IGH region:
zfp36l1_final_dels %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, strand, class, support, patient, sample,
        bp_order)
##   seqnames     start       end strand class                    support
## 1    chr14  68795182  68795182      +   DEL       manta,delly,igcaller
## 2    chr14 105859665 105859665      -   DEL       manta,delly,igcaller
## 3    chr14  68795184  68795184      +   DEL manta,delly,svaba,igcaller
## 4    chr14 105859690 105859690      -   DEL manta,delly,svaba,igcaller
##       patient       sample bp_order
## 1 60828-DNA-N 60828-DNA-T2        1
## 2 60828-DNA-N 60828-DNA-T2        2
## 3 60828-DNA-N  60828-DNA-T        1
## 4 60828-DNA-N  60828-DNA-T        2

#################### I N V s #################### ZFP36L1
zfp36l1_final_invs <- data.table()
zfp36l1_invs_end1 <- gr.findoverlaps(query = grbind(parse.gr(paste0("chr14:68793067-",
    68793067 + 2500000)), parse.gr(paste0("chr14:", 68787662 - 2500000, "-68787662"))),
    subject = intrachrom_chr14q %Q% (class == "INV"), scol = c("class", "patient",
        "sample", "sv_id", "bp_order", "nearest_gene"))

for (i in seq(along = zfp36l1_invs_end1$sv_id)) {
    zfp36l1_invs_end2 <- intrachrom_juncs %Q% (sv_id == zfp36l1_invs_end1$sv_id[i]) %Q%
        (bp_order != zfp36l1_invs_end1$bp_order[i])

    # If in the IGH range, keep the SV ID
    if (length(zfp36l1_invs_end2) > 0) {

        if (gr.in(query = zfp36l1_invs_end2, igh_trx_expected_range)) {
            zfp36l1_final_invs <- rrbind(zfp36l1_final_invs, gr2dt(intrachrom_juncs %Q%
                (sv_id == zfp36l1_invs_end1$sv_id[i])))
        }
    }
}
message("Intra-chromosomal inversions downstream of ZFP36L1 to near IGH region")
## Intra-chromosomal inversions downstream of ZFP36L1 to near IGH region
zfp36l1_final_dels %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, strand, class, support, patient, sample,
        bp_order)
##   seqnames     start       end strand class                    support
## 1    chr14  68795182  68795182      +   DEL       manta,delly,igcaller
## 2    chr14 105859665 105859665      -   DEL       manta,delly,igcaller
## 3    chr14  68795184  68795184      +   DEL manta,delly,svaba,igcaller
## 4    chr14 105859690 105859690      -   DEL manta,delly,svaba,igcaller
##       patient       sample bp_order
## 1 60828-DNA-N 60828-DNA-T2        1
## 2 60828-DNA-N 60828-DNA-T2        2
## 3 60828-DNA-N  60828-DNA-T        1
## 4 60828-DNA-N  60828-DNA-T        2

# Highly recurrent SNV sites first check if their are duplicated mutations
# patients
muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id == "snv_er177") %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    duplicated() %>%
    sum()
## [1] 0

zfp36l1_snv_dups <- duplicated(muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id == "snv_er177") %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, Reference_Allele))
zfp36l1_snv_dup_sites_start <- muts_per_mer[mut_uniq_id == "snv_er177"][zfp36l1_snv_dups,
    .(start)]
zfp36l1_snv_foci_counts <- muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(start %in% zfp36l1_snv_dup_sites_start$start) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(seqnames, start) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(n))

zfp36l1_snv_foci_counts %>%
    dplyr::filter(n >= 3)
## # A tibble: 9 × 3
## # Groups:   seqnames, start [9]
##   seqnames    start     n
##   <chr>       <int> <int>
## 1 chr14    68792290     5
## 2 chr14    68792571     4
## 3 chr14    68792345     3
## 4 chr14    68792472     3
## 5 chr14    68792555     3
## 6 chr14    68792572     3
## 7 chr14    68792680     3
## 8 chr14    68792688     3
## 9 chr14    68792812     3

# Epilogos
zfp36l1_epilogos <- epilogo_prop_per_region(roi = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_id == "mer5"))), roi_id = "ZFP36L1")
## Warning in dt2gr(mm_epilogos): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns

message("Chromatin state composition of ZFP36L1: ")
## Chromatin state composition of ZFP36L1:
zfp36l1_epilogos
##                          state       prop     roi
##                         <fctr>      <num>  <char>
##  1:            Active_Promoter 0.17942665 ZFP36L1
##  2:           Strong_Enhancer1 0.17377907 ZFP36L1
##  3:              Weak_Promoter 0.01783478 ZFP36L1
##  4:            Poised_Promoter 0.00000000 ZFP36L1
##  5:              Weak_Enhancer 0.04954167 ZFP36L1
##  6:           Strong_Enhancer2 0.05661767 ZFP36L1
##  7:   Transcription_Transition 0.07794423 ZFP36L1
##  8:         Weak_Transcription 0.17926197 ZFP36L1
##  9:   Transcription_Elongation 0.05974441 ZFP36L1
## 10:          H3K9me3_Repressed 0.00000000 ZFP36L1
## 11: Heterochromatin_Low_Signal 0.16116801 ZFP36L1
## 12:         H3K27me3_Repressed 0.04468154 ZFP36L1

message("Percentatge of Enhancer chromatin states (Strong_Enhancer1 + Strong_Enhancer2): ",
    zfp36l1_epilogos %>%
        dplyr::filter(state %in% c("Strong_Enhancer1", "Strong_Enhancer2")) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(combined_prop = round(sum(prop) * 100, digits = 1)))
## Percentatge of Enhancer chromatin states (Strong_Enhancer1 + Strong_Enhancer2): 23

message("Percentatge of Promoter chromatin state: ", zfp36l1_epilogos %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% c("Active_Promoter", "Weak_Promoter", "Poised_Promoter")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(combined_prop = round(sum(prop) * 100, digits = 1)))
## Percentatge of Promoter chromatin state: 19.7

ZFP36L1 locus view

Code 
zfp36l1_window <- "chr14:68784501-68797000"

ideogram_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_single_chrom_ideogrom(window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window,
    flank = 0)
## Scale for x is already present.
## Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.

scale_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window, flank = 0)

gene_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window, flank = 0,
    genes_of_interest = "ZFP36L1", gene_name_side = "five_prime", gene_label_nudge = 2500,
    gene_label_size = 3.5) + labs(y = "ZFP36L1")

enhancer_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_bed_track(data = complete_ee, window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "tissue", aes_fill_column = "enhancer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",
    legend_title = "Element", aes_fill_colors = c("#3DA873", "#DC9445")) + labs(y = "Enhancers")

mer_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_bed_track(data = mers_gr, window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "mer_type", aes_fill_column = "mer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",
    aes_fill_colors = "#FB832D") + labs(y = "MERs") + theme(legend.position = "none")

snv_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_count_mutations(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#191970", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("Myeloma\nSNVs") + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density", colours = rev(paletteer_c("grDevices::GnBu",
    15)))

snv_vaf_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window,
    flank = 0, contrast = F, x_axis_theme = "blank") + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density",
    colours = paletteer_c("grDevices::GnBu", 15)[1:12])
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.


normal_snv_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_count_mutations(data = gr2dt(normal_lymphocyte_muts_for_model) %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV"), window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window, flank = 0,
    aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "gray", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("Lymphocyte\nSNVs")

epilogos_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_epilogo_col(data = mm_epilogos, window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window,
    flank = 0, x_axis_theme = "blank", contrast = F)
## Warning in dt2gr(data): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

breakpoint_track_zfp36l1 <- geom_sv_breakpoints(data = hq_total_sv_bp_dt, window_of_interest = zfp36l1_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "patient", aes_fill_column = "class", aes_shape_column = "class",
    x_axis_theme = "blank")

zfp36l1_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(ideogram_track_zfp36l1, scale_track_zfp36l1,
    breakpoint_track_zfp36l1, normal_snv_track_zfp36l1, snv_track_zfp36l1, snv_vaf_track_zfp36l1,
    epilogos_track_zfp36l1, enhancer_track_zfp36l1, mer_track_zfp36l1, gene_track_zfp36l1),
    ncol = 1, guides = "collect", heights = c(0.1, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.2, 0.2, 0.15,
        0.1, 0.1, 0.1))
