BTG2 locus view

Code 
btg2_window <- "chr1:203296001-203313000"

gene_track_btg2 <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = btg2_window, flank = 2500,
    genes_of_interest = "BTG2", gene_name_side = "five_prime", gene_label_nudge = 1000,
    gene_label_size = 4) + labs(y = "BTG2")

ideogram_track_btg2 <- geom_single_chrom_ideogrom(window_of_interest = btg2_window,
    flank = 2500)
## Scale for x is already present.
## Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.

scale_track_btg2 <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = btg2_window, flank = 2500)

enhancer_track_btg2 <- geom_bed_track(data = complete_ee, window_of_interest = btg2_window,
    flank = 2500, aes_y_column = "tissue", aes_fill_column = "enhancer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",
    legend_title = "Element", aes_fill_colors = "#DC9445") + labs(y = "Enhancers")

mer_track_btg2 <- geom_bed_track(data = mers_gr, window_of_interest = btg2_window,
    flank = 2500, aes_y_column = "mer_type", aes_fill_column = "mer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",
    aes_fill_colors = "#FB832D") + labs(y = "MERs") + theme(legend.position = "none")

snv_track_btg2 <- geom_count_mutations(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = btg2_window,
    flank = 2500, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#191970",
    x_axis_theme = "blank") + ylab("SNVs")

snv_vaf_track_btg2 <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = btg2_window,
    flank = 2500, contrast = F, x_axis_theme = "blank") + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density",
    colours = paletteer_c("grDevices::GnBu", 15)[1:12])
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.

epilogos_track_btg2 <- geom_epilogo_col(data = mm_epilogos, window_of_interest = btg2_window,
    flank = 2500, x_axis_theme = "blank", contrast = F)
## Warning in dt2gr(data): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

btg2_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(ideogram_track_btg2, scale_track_btg2,
    snv_track_btg2, snv_vaf_track_btg2, epilogos_track_btg2, enhancer_track_btg2,
    mer_track_btg2, gene_track_btg2), ncol = 1, guides = "collect", heights = c(0.1,
    0.1, 0.2, 0.2, 0.15, 0.1, 0.1, 0.1))

Compare Chromatin states between the E/SE groups

Code 
# Look at Enhancer signal
enhancer_epilogo_group <- e_se_mer_epilogos_props %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% c("Strong Enhancer1", "Strong Enhancer2", "Weak Enhancer")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(state_group = "Enhancer") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group, roi) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(group_state_sum_prop = sum(prop))

enhancer_epilogo_group_mean <- enhancer_epilogo_group %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_group_state_sum_prop = mean(group_state_sum_prop))
print("Enhancer states proportion")
## [1] "Enhancer states proportion"
enhancer_epilogo_group_mean
## # A tibble: 3 × 3
##   e_se_group state_group mean_group_state_sum_prop
##   <chr>      <chr>                           <dbl>
## 1 GCB        Enhancer                        0.230
## 2 GCB,PC     Enhancer                        0.515
## 3 PC         Enhancer                        0.153

# Use Dunn test to perform pairwise Kruskal-Wallis test of means
enhancer_epilogo_group_pval <- rstatix::dunn_test(data = enhancer_epilogo_group,
    formula = group_state_sum_prop ~ e_se_group)
enhancer_epilogo_group_pval$p.adj <- round(x = enhancer_epilogo_group_pval$p.adj,
    digits = 3)

enhancer_epilogo_group_plot <- ggplot(enhancer_epilogo_group_mean) + geom_col(aes(x = e_se_group,
    y = mean_group_state_sum_prop, fill = e_se_group)) + labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean Proportion") +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "E/SE-MER\nGroup", values = c("royalblue3", "#540046",
        "orangered3")) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.73)) + theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.background = element_rect(fill = ggplot2::alpha(colour = "#ffcd17",
        alpha = 0.15)), panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = enhancer_epilogo_group_pval[c(1, 3), ], label = "P={p.adj}",
        y.position = c(0.62, 0.65), bracket.nudge.y = 0.01, size = 4.5, tip.length = 0.015) +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = enhancer_epilogo_group_pval[2, ], label = "{p.adj.signif}",
        y.position = 0.57, bracket.nudge.y = 0.01, size = 4.5, tip.length = 0.015)

# Look at Transcription signal
transcription_epilogo_group <- e_se_mer_epilogos_props %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% c("Transcription Transition", "Transcription Elongation",
        "Weak Transcription")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(state_group = "Transcription") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group, roi) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(group_state_sum_prop = sum(prop))

transcription_epilogo_group_mean <- transcription_epilogo_group %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_group_state_sum_prop = mean(group_state_sum_prop))

print("Transcription states proportion")
## [1] "Transcription states proportion"
transcription_epilogo_group_mean
## # A tibble: 3 × 3
##   e_se_group state_group   mean_group_state_sum_prop
##   <chr>      <chr>                             <dbl>
## 1 GCB        Transcription                    0.0742
## 2 GCB,PC     Transcription                    0.121 
## 3 PC         Transcription                    0.434

# Use Dunn test to perform pairwise Kruskal-Wallis test of means
transcription_epilogo_group_pval <- rstatix::dunn_test(data = transcription_epilogo_group,
    formula = group_state_sum_prop ~ e_se_group)
transcription_epilogo_group_pval$p.adj <- round(x = transcription_epilogo_group_pval$p.adj,
    digits = 3)

transcription_epilogo_group_plot <- ggplot(transcription_epilogo_group_mean) + geom_col(aes(x = e_se_group,
    y = mean_group_state_sum_prop, fill = e_se_group)) + labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean Proportion") +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "E/SE-MER\nGroup", values = c("royalblue3", "#540046",
        "orangered3")) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.53)) + theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.background = element_rect(fill = ggplot2::alpha(colour = "#4fe609",
        alpha = 0.15)), panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = transcription_epilogo_group_pval[2, ], label = "P={p.adj}",
        y.position = 0.4, bracket.nudge.y = 0.01, size = 4.5, tip.length = 0.015) +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = transcription_epilogo_group_pval[c(1, 3), ],
        label = "{p.adj.signif}", y.position = c(0.45, 0.48), bracket.nudge.y = 0.01,
        size = 4.5, tip.length = 0.015)

# Look at Repressed / Heterochromatin signal
repressed_epilogo_group <- e_se_mer_epilogos_props %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% c("H3K9me3 Repressed", "H3K27me3 Repressed", "Heterochromatin Low Signal")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(state_group = "Transcription") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group, roi) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(group_state_sum_prop = sum(prop))

repressed_epilogo_group_mean <- repressed_epilogo_group %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_group_state_sum_prop = mean(group_state_sum_prop))

repressed_epilogo_group_pval <- rstatix::dunn_test(data = repressed_epilogo_group,
    formula = group_state_sum_prop ~ e_se_group)
repressed_epilogo_group_pval$p.adj <- round(x = repressed_epilogo_group_pval$p.adj,
    digits = 3)

repressed_epilogo_group_plot <- ggplot(repressed_epilogo_group_mean) + geom_col(aes(x = e_se_group,
    y = mean_group_state_sum_prop, fill = e_se_group)) + labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean Proportion") +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "E/SE-MER\nGroup", values = c("royalblue3", "#540046",
        "orangered3")) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.73)) + theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.background = element_rect(fill = ggplot2::alpha(colour = "gray40",
        alpha = 0.15)), panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = repressed_epilogo_group_pval[1, ], label = "P={p.adj}",
        y.position = 0.62, bracket.nudge.y = 0.01, size = 4.5, tip.length = 0.015) +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = repressed_epilogo_group_pval[c(2, 3), ], label = "{p.adj.signif}",
        y.position = c(0.57, 0.67), bracket.nudge.y = 0.01, size = 4.5, tip.length = 0.015)

# Look at Promoter signal
promoter_epilogo_group <- e_se_mer_epilogos_props %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% c("Active Promoter", "Weak Promoter", "Poised Promoter")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(state_group = "Promoter") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group, roi) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(group_state_sum_prop = sum(prop))

promoter_epilogo_group_mean <- promoter_epilogo_group %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_group_state_sum_prop = mean(group_state_sum_prop))

promoter_epilogo_group_pval <- rstatix::dunn_test(data = promoter_epilogo_group,
    formula = group_state_sum_prop ~ e_se_group)

promoter_epilogo_group_plot <- ggplot(promoter_epilogo_group_mean) + geom_col(aes(x = e_se_group,
    y = mean_group_state_sum_prop, fill = e_se_group)) + labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean Proportion") +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "E/SE-MER\nGroup", values = c("royalblue3", "#540046",
        "orangered3")) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.23)) + theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.background = element_rect(fill = ggplot2::alpha(colour = "#d13885",
        alpha = 0.15)), panel.grid = element_blank()) + ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = promoter_epilogo_group_pval,
    label = "{p.adj.signif}", y.position = c(0.17, 0.15, 0.19), bracket.nudge.y = 0.01,
    size = 4.5, tip.length = 0.015)

Figure 7

TXNDC5 locus view

Code 
# GRO-seq track plus lp1_gro_seq_plus_hg19 <-
# rtracklayer::import(BigWigFile(paste0(base_data_path,'GSM1755012_LP1_GROseq_plus_hg19.bigWig')),
# as = 'GRanges')

# lp1_gro_seq_plus_hg38 <- gr_refactor_seqs(grl.unlist(rtracklayer::liftOver(x
# = lp1_gro_seq_plus_hg19, chain = liftchain_hg19)))

# saveRDS(object = lp1_gro_seq_plus_hg38, file =
# paste0(base_data_path,'GSM1755012_LP1_GROseq_plus_hg38.rds'))
lp1_gro_seq_plus_hg38 <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "GSM1755012_LP1_GROseq_plus_hg38.rds"))

# minus lp1_gro_seq_minus_hg19 <-
# rtracklayer::import(BigWigFile(paste0(base_data_path,'GSM1755012_LP1_GROseq_minus_hg19.bigWig')),
# as = 'GRanges')

# lp1_gro_seq_minus_hg38 <- grl.unlist(rtracklayer::liftOver(x =
# lp1_gro_seq_minus_hg19, chain = liftchain_hg19)) lp1_gro_seq_minus_hg38$score
# <- -lp1_gro_seq_minus_hg38$score

# saveRDS(object = lp1_gro_seq_minus_hg38, file =
# paste0(base_data_path,'GSM1755012_LP1_GROseq_minus_hg38.rds'))
lp1_gro_seq_minus_hg38 <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "GSM1755012_LP1_GROseq_minus_hg38.rds"))

# More focused on gene
txndc5_window <- "chr6:7850000-8000000"

ideogram_track_txndc5 <- geom_single_chrom_ideogrom(window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0)
## Scale for x is already present.
## Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.

scale_track_txndc5 <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = txndc5_window, flank = 0)

gene_track_txndc5 <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = txndc5_window, flank = 0,
    genes_of_interest = c("TXNDC5", "BMP6", "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5"), gene_name_side = "three_prime",
    gene_label_nudge = 1000000, gene_label_size = 3)

enhancer_track_txndc5 <- geom_bed_track(data = complete_ee, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "tissue", aes_fill_column = "enhancer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",
    aes_fill_colors = c("#3DA873", "#DC9445"), legend_title = "Element") + labs(y = "Enhancers") +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

mer_track_txndc5 <- geom_bed_track(data = mers_gr, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "mer_type", aes_fill_column = "mer_type", aes_fill_colors = c("black",
        "#FB832D"), x_axis_theme = "blank") + labs(y = "MERs") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

gro_seq_antisense_track_txndc5 <- geom_ridge_track(data = lp1_gro_seq_plus_hg38,
    window_of_interest = txndc5_window, flank = 0, aes_y_column = "score", fill_color = "darkred",
    x_axis_theme = "blank") + ylab(" \n \nGRO-Seq") + scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

gro_seq_sense_track_txndc5 <- geom_ridge_track(data = lp1_gro_seq_minus_hg38, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "score", fill_color = "dodgerblue", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab(NULL) + scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

snv_track_txndc5 <- geom_count_mutations(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#191970", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("SNVs") + scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

snv_vaf_track_txndc5 <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, contrast = F, x_axis_theme = "blank") + ylab("SNV\nVAF") + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density",
    colours = paletteer_c("grDevices::GnBu", 15)[1:12]) + theme(legend.position = "none") +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

indel_track_txndc5 <- geom_count_mutations(data = indel_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#EEA9B8", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("InDels") + scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

indel_vaf_track_txndc5 <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = indel_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, contrast = F, x_axis_theme = "blank") + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density",
    colours = rev(paletteer_c("grDevices::Lajolla", 15)[3:14])) + ylab("InDel\nVAF") +
    theme(legend.position = "none") + scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

epilogos_track_txndc5 <- geom_epilogo_col(data = mm_epilogos, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, x_axis_theme = "blank", contrast = F) + theme(legend.position = "none")
## Warning in dt2gr(data): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

breakpoint_track_txndc5 <- geom_sv_breakpoints(data = hq_total_sv_bp_dt, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "patient", aes_fill_column = "class", aes_shape_column = "class",
    x_axis_theme = "blank") + theme(legend.position = "none")

txndc5_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(ideogram_track_txndc5, scale_track_txndc5,
    breakpoint_track_txndc5, indel_track_txndc5, indel_vaf_track_txndc5, snv_track_txndc5,
    snv_vaf_track_txndc5, epilogos_track_txndc5, gro_seq_antisense_track_txndc5,
    gro_seq_sense_track_txndc5, enhancer_track_txndc5, mer_track_txndc5, gene_track_txndc5),
    ncol = 1, heights = c(0.01, 0.015, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02,
        0.02, 0.015, 0.015, 0.025))

TENT5C locus view

Code 
tent5c_window <- "chr1:117585000-117675000"

gene_track_tent5c <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = tent5c_window, flank = 0,
    genes_of_interest = c("TENT5C", "TENT5C-DT"), gene_name_side = "three_prime",
    gene_label_nudge = 12000, gene_label_size = 3) + labs(y = " ")

ideogram_track_tent5c <- geom_single_chrom_ideogrom(window_of_interest = tent5c_window,
    flank = 0)
## Scale for x is already present.
## Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.

scale_track_tent5c <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = tent5c_window, flank = 0)

enhancer_track_tent5c <- geom_bed_track(data = complete_ee, window_of_interest = tent5c_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "tissue", aes_fill_column = "enhancer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",
    aes_fill_colors = "#DC9445", legend_title = "Element") + labs(y = " ")

mer_track_tent5c <- geom_bed_track(data = mers_gr, window_of_interest = tent5c_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "mer_type", aes_fill_column = "mer_type", aes_fill_colors = c("black",
        "#FB832D"), x_axis_theme = "blank") + labs(y = " ") + theme(legend.position = "none")

gro_seq_sense_track_tent5c <- geom_ridge_track(data = lp1_gro_seq_plus_hg38, window_of_interest = tent5c_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "score", fill_color = "dodgerblue", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    labs(y = " ")

gro_seq_antisense_track_tent5c <- geom_ridge_track(data = lp1_gro_seq_minus_hg38,
    window_of_interest = tent5c_window, flank = 0, aes_y_column = "score", fill_color = "darkred",
    x_axis_theme = "blank") + labs(y = " ")

snv_track_tent5c <- geom_count_mutations(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = tent5c_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#191970", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    labs(y = " ")

snv_vaf_track_tent5c <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = tent5c_window,
    flank = 0, contrast = F, x_axis_theme = "blank") + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density",
    colours = paletteer_c("grDevices::GnBu", 15)[1:12]) + labs(y = " ")
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.

indel_track_tent5c <- geom_count_mutations(data = indel_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = tent5c_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#EEA9B8", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    labs(y = " ")

indel_vaf_track_tent5c <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = indel_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = tent5c_window,
    flank = 0, contrast = F, x_axis_theme = "blank") + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density",
    colours = rev(paletteer_c("grDevices::Lajolla", 15)[3:14])) + labs(y = " ")
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.

epilogos_track_tent5c <- geom_epilogo_col(data = mm_epilogos, window_of_interest = tent5c_window,
    flank = 0, x_axis_theme = "blank", contrast = F) + labs(y = " ")
## Warning in dt2gr(data): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

breakpoint_track_tent5c <- geom_sv_breakpoints(data = hq_total_sv_bp_dt, window_of_interest = tent5c_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "patient", aes_fill_column = "class", aes_shape_column = "class",
    x_axis_theme = "blank") + labs(y = " ")

tent5c_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(ideogram_track_tent5c, scale_track_tent5c,
    breakpoint_track_tent5c, indel_track_tent5c, indel_vaf_track_tent5c, snv_track_tent5c,
    snv_vaf_track_tent5c, epilogos_track_tent5c, gro_seq_sense_track_tent5c, gro_seq_antisense_track_tent5c,
    enhancer_track_tent5c, mer_track_tent5c, gene_track_tent5c), ncol = 1, guides = "collect",
    heights = c(0.01, 0.015, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.015,
        0.02, 0.02))

15 Supplementary Tables

Table S1: Mutationally enriched regions (MERs)

Code 
table_s1 <- mers %>%
    dplyr::select(-start_abs_linear, -end_abs_linear, -midpoint_abs_linear) %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, width, mer_id, mer_gene_label, mer_type,
        k_tiles, q_value, effect_size_mean, count_density_mean, pred_count_density_mean,
        eligible_size_mean, mut_uniq_id, overlapping_genes, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
        mut_mgus_patients, pct_mut_mgus_patients, mut_smm_patients, pct_mut_smm_patients,
        mut_mm_patients, pct_mut_mm_patients, mut_rrmm_patients, pct_mut_rrmm_patients,
        mut_t11_14_patients, pct_mut_t11_14_patients, mut_t4_14_patients, pct_mut_t4_14_patients,
        mut_t14_16_patients, pct_mut_t14_16_patients, mut_t14_20_patients, pct_mut_t14_20_patients,
        mut_t6_14_patients, pct_mut_t6_14_patients, mut_t8_14_patients, pct_mut_t8_14_patients,
        mut_trx_not_detected_patients, pct_mut_trx_not_detected_patients, mut_EUR_patients,
        pct_mut_EUR_patients, mut_AFR_patients, pct_mut_AFR_patients, mut_AMR_patients,
        pct_mut_AMR_patients, mut_EAS_patients, pct_mut_EAS_patients, mut_SAS_patients,
        pct_mut_SAS_patients, mut_highly_admixed_patients, pct_mut_highly_admixed_patients)

Table S2: Overlap of genomic features and mutationally enriched region (MERs)

Code 
table_s2 <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_gene_label, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct, gene_body_pct,
        cluster_label, lncrna_overlap_pct, mirna_overlap_pct, enhancer_overlap_pct,
        enhancer_id, tissue, enhancer_type, promoter_overlap_pct, prom_id, lymphocyte_overlap_pct,
        pct_patients_with_SV.hg19, hotspot.id.hg19, complex.hotspot.id.hg19, hotspot_class,
        genes_involved, driver_status)

Table S3: Consensus reigons of kataegis, omikli, and other clustered mutation events across multiple myeloma

Code 
table_s3 <- consensus_clustered_event_regions_dt %>%
    dplyr::rename(seqnames = chrom)

Table S5: Sample Metadata

Code 
table_s4 <- hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::select(patient, sample, study, dataset_id, disease_stage, age, sex, self_identified_race,
        coverage_normal, coverage_tumor, contamination, concordance, igh_translocation,
        hyperdiploidy, purity, ploidy, snv_count, indel_count, total_mutation_count,
        EAS, SAS, EUR, AMR, AFR, super5_cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
        tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
        dplyr::select(-mutations), by = "sample")
